quotes.quote: The presence of central sources seems clear for NGC 2748, NGC 3949, NGC 4030 and NGC 4303, where a well sampled The surface brightness profile for NGC 157 also shows an excess above the Nuker fit, but in this analysis the excess is defined by a single point only.

Central sources for all of the remaining galaxies were previously reported in S04, though only the cluster in NGC 4030 was identified as resolved in that paper Note that in S04 point sources were defined to be those that have FWHM (1.5 FWHM _ PS ), where FWHM _ PS means for a point source.
In 4 cases; NGC 2748, NGC 3949, NGC 4030 and NGC 4303 there appears to be an excess which can not be accounted for by the Nuker profile alone.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4030 through SDSS surface photometry, noting its visible disk structure and using its radial profile features to classify the galaxy structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 4030 : J120023.4-010603 Two superimposed bright stars are masked. According to it is an isolated galaxy with a satellite system (largest UGC 06970, SB(s)m, with =-17.7 ). The galaxy shows two prominent spiral arms beyond a more flocculent center starting at 30 , followed by a single arm extending slightly further out towards south-west.

gmw_00003102.name: NGC 5247
gmw_00003102.overview: NGC 5247 is a prototype spiral galaxy characterized by a clean, open, two-armed logarithmic spiral pattern [arXiv:1204.5599]. The galaxy features a weak central bar, a transition zone, and a small phase shift in its main spiral structure [arXiv:1204.5599].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5247 as a prototype clean open two-armed logarithmic spiral with weak central structures, a transition zone, a phase shift, and peaked azimuthal profiles.

quotes.quote: NGC 5247: The spiral pattern of this galaxy, as for that of NGC 1566, is a prototype clean, open m=2 logarithmic spiral. There is evidence of both a central bar within 5 \ and a weak spiral pattern reaching 8 \ that has the opposite winding of the main spiral. After a transition zone, the main spiral pattern follows a logarithmic spiral with ( a_2 , i ) = (0.6, 34.4 ) in the range 40 \ to 121 . There is a small phase shift around 100 . The azimuthal profile is peaked with significant amplitudes of higher-order even harmonics.

sources.summary: The paper builds dynamical models of NGC 5247 and finds unstable disks form simulated spiral patterns matching its observed morphology, with gas extending the spiral lifetime.

quotes.quote: Using observational data, we build numerical N-body, hydrodynamical and combined equilibrium models for the spiral galaxy NGC 5247. The models turn out to be unstable towards spiral structure formation. We simulate scenarios of spiral structure formation for different sets of equilibrium rotation curves, radial velocity dispersion profiles and disk thickness and demonstrate that in all cases a simulated spiral pattern qualitatively agrees with the observed morphology of NGC 5247. We also demonstrate that an admixture of a gaseous component with mass of about a few percent of the total mass of the disk increases a lifetime of a spiral pattern by approximately 30 .

gmw_00003103.name: NGC 7424
gmw_00003103.overview: NGC 7424 is a grand-design, face-on SABcd spiral galaxy [arXiv:2402.09512] that lacks a bulge [arXiv:1810.05467] and features a central bar with tight, symmetric spiral arms [arXiv:1204.5599]. Its star-forming disk hosts multiple ultraluminous X-ray sources [arXiv:2402.09512] and was the site of the Type-IIb supernova SN 2001ig [arXiv:2402.09512].

sources.summary: The paper derives NGC 7424 mass models from its 3d Barolo rotation curve and WISE photometry, finding no prominent bulge and modeling disk components.

quotes.quote: NGC 7424 The mass models for NGC 7424 were derived using its 3d Barolo rotation curve and WISE Wright2010 W1 ( 3.4 m ) band photometry, sensitive to the old stellar populations of galaxies. The W1 light profile of the galaxy presented in fig:light-profile , along with the galaxy's optical image, suggests the absence of a prominent bulge. An attempt to decompose the light profile in the left panel of fig:light-profile further confirmed a negligible bulge. We therefore consider the stellar disk and the gas disk as the only baryonic components contributing to the observed rotation curve.

We used the model implemented in the 's task , with NGC 7424. As stated above, the stellar disk's M/L was first fixed to the WISE inferred value, then let free. tb:massmod-n7424 summarises the different values derived. The M/L value obtained in the best fit is 0.18 0.57 , which is consistent with the WISE inferred value, and shows that the two fits are in agreement.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7424 as a grand-design face-on SABcd spiral with a 10.8 Mpc adopted distance, modest star formation, SN 2001ig, and a star-forming disk hosting multiple ULXs.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we investigate two ULXs in the grand-design, face-on spiral galaxy NGC 7424 (Figure 1), with morphological classification SABcd. In the absence of Cepheid distances to this galaxy, we adopt the average between the Hubble Flow distance (10.1 Mpc) and the Tully-Fisher distance (11.5 Mpc), both listed in the NASA/IPAC Extragalactic Database (NED) https://ned.ipac.caltech.edu . ; thus, we take d = 10.8 Mpc (distance modulus 30.17 mag, scale of 52 pc arcsec ^ 1 ). The star formation rate is 0.2--0.3 M_ yr ^ 1 ( larsen02,iglesias06 , accounting for the slightly lower distance assumed here). NGC 7424 was host to the Type-IIb supernova SN 2001ig; some of the observational datasets used for this work were originally collected for a study of that SN.
The 5 5 5 5 field of view of GMOS covers most of the star-forming disk of NGC 7424 and is well suited for a search of the optical counterparts to the Chandra sources (Figure 1).

A more detailed analysis of the star-formation properties of NGC 7424 will be presented in a follow-up work currently in preparation, together with a comprehensive study of the radio sources (SNRs and H II regions) detected in our ATCA observations.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7424 as the closest sample galaxy, lacking strong dust lanes or star-forming regions and hosting a bar with tight symmetric spiral arms.

quotes.quote: NGC 7424: This galaxy is the closest in the sample with a distance of 9.5 Mpc. With a linear resolution of 20 pc, most sources are likely to be associated with individual clusters. There are no traces of either significant dust lanes or large star-forming regions. Its bar extends to 38 , where a tight spiral emerges at an angle of almost 90 . The main set of symmetric arms starts around 49 \ with ( a_2 , i ) = (0.3, 12.3 ) and terminates at 77 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 7424 as the nearby late-type spiral host of SN 2001ig, at 11.5 Mpc, with no prior recorded supernovae and low-metallicity outskirts.

quotes.quote: Supernova 2001ig was discovered visually by evans01 on 2001 Dec 10.43 UT in the outskirts of the nearby late-type spiral galaxy NGC 7424 ( D=11.5 Mpc; tu88 ). No supernovae have been recorded previously in this galaxy.

At least 40 of solar neighbourhood W-R stars are in binary systems with hot companions mof86,vdh88 , and the fraction may be even higher in low-metallicity environments ds95 , such as the outskirts of NGC 7424.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 7424 has prominent Balmer emission, uncertain individual metallicity robustness, and a young nuclear-age classification among the studied spiral nuclei.

quotes.quote: Three of the spectra (NGC 1042, NGC 7418, NGC 7424) have prominent Balmer emission lines, which are less broad than the absorption lines.
However, the case of NGC 7424 (see Figure f:chiplane ) shows that our metallicity results may not be very robust for every single object.
Thus, both suggest a distinction into two groups of nuclei: those that are 0.1 Gyr (NGC 1042, NGC 1493, NGC 2139, NGC 7418, NGC 7424, NGC 7793) and those that are 1 Gyr (NGC 300, NGC 428, NGC 3423).

gmw_00003104.name: IC391
gmw_00003104.overview: IC391 is a spiral galaxy that exhibits recognizable rotational motion [arXiv:2405.13471]. The galaxy features star-forming clumps and experiences more intense star formation events than IC1151 [arXiv:2405.13471].

sources.summary: The paper uses IC391 as an eCALIFA example spiral with star formation clumps, stronger events than IC1151, similar PCA structural intensities, and recognizable rotation.

quotes.quote: IC391 and IC1151 are both spiral galaxies exhibiting SF, while NGC1167 is classified as an S0 galaxy with an AGN. In the PCA 0 maps, SF clumps are prominent features for IC391 and IC1151. Notably, IC391 exhibits more intense SF events compared to IC1151. In contrast, NGC1167 displays minimal SF, primarily at the periphery, and is predominantly quenched.

Examining the PCA 1 map, both IC391 and IC1151 show similar intensities, reflecting the structural similarities observed in the PCA 0 map, which modulate the presentation of SF events. However, an intensity peak at the centre of NGC1167 in the PCA 1 map signals the presence of the AGN. Turning to PCA 3, which represents gas kinematics, we observe the rotational motion of the galaxies in IC391 and NGC1167.

gmw_00003105.name: IC1151
gmw_00003105.overview: IC1151 is a spiral galaxy that exhibits active star formation [arXiv:2405.13471]. Although star-forming clumps are visible across its structure, the galaxy lacks a directly recognizable rotational gas kinematics pattern [arXiv:2405.13471].

sources.summary: The paper uses IC1151 as an eCALIFA example spiral with star-forming clumps, weaker events than IC391, similar PCA structural intensity, and no directly recognizable rotation pattern.

quotes.quote: IC391 and IC1151 are both spiral galaxies exhibiting SF, while NGC1167 is classified as an S0 galaxy with an AGN. In the PCA 0 maps, SF clumps are prominent features for IC391 and IC1151. Notably, IC391 exhibits more intense SF events compared to IC1151. In contrast, NGC1167 displays minimal SF, primarily at the periphery, and is predominantly quenched.

Examining the PCA 1 map, both IC391 and IC1151 show similar intensities, reflecting the structural similarities observed in the PCA 0 map, which modulate the presentation of SF events. However, an intensity peak at the centre of NGC1167 in the PCA 1 map signals the presence of the AGN. Turning to PCA 3, which represents gas kinematics, we observe the rotational motion of the galaxies in IC391 and NGC1167. However, other PCA components also influence the kinematics, as we cannot recognise the rotation pattern in IC1151 directly.

gmw_00003106.name: SN1997ey host
gmw_00003106.overview: The host galaxy of SN1997ey is a normal, late-type disk galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.575 [arXiv:0707.3505, arXiv:astro-ph/0312111]. It exhibits a small residual star-formation activity in the optical [arXiv:0707.3505] and shows no signs of morphological disturbance [arXiv:astro-ph/0312111].

sources.summary: The paper finds the z=0.575 SN1997ey host is a late-type, optically weakly star-forming galaxy with unexplained submillimetre flux and no detected CO emission.

quotes.quote: Accepted ; Received firstpage - lastpage 2007 We study the properties and the molecular content of the host of a type-Ia supernova (SN1997ey). This z=0.575 host is the brightest submillimetre source of the sample of type-Ia supernova hosts observed at 450 m and 850 m by Farrah:2004 . Observations were performed at IRAM-30m to search for CO(2-1) and CO(3-2) lines in good weather conditions but no signal was detected.

We reach the conclusion that this galaxy is a late-type system (0.7 L^B_* ), with a small residual star-formation activity (0.2 M _ yr ^ 1 ) detected in the optical. No source of heating (AGN or starburst) is found to explain the submillimetre-continuum flux and the non-CO detection excludes the presence of a large amount of cold gas. We thus suggest that either the star formation activity is hidden in the nucleus (with A_V 4 ) or this galaxy is passive or anemic and this flux might be associated with a background galaxy. supernovae: individual: SN1997ey -- radio lines: galaxies -- submillimetre -- galaxies: general -- methods: observational Standardised type-Ia supernovae (SN) have been extensively studied to probe the expansion of the Universe for the past decade (e.g.

sources.summary: The paper examines the host galaxy of SN1997ey, detected at 450 and 850 microns, and argues its sub-millimeter flux is unlikely to come from a starburst because HST shows an undisturbed disk.

quotes.quote: We now examine the dust properties in the host galaxy of SN1997ey, which was detected at 450 m and 850 m. One possibility is that this galaxy harbours a dust enshrouded starburst that produces the sub-mm flux; such a system would be unsuitable for examining the properties of dust in normal galaxies at z=0.5 . This is however very unlikely, for two reasons. Firstly, as described earlier, none of the optical spectra of the supernova host galaxies from either the SCP or the HZT, from which this galaxy is selected, show signs of an AGN or starburst. Secondly, the HST STIS image for this galaxy, taken after the supernova had faded and presented in Figure fig1 , shows a disk galaxy with no signs of morphological disturbance that are seen in local sur,far1,bush or high redshift far2 starburst galaxies.
We therefore conclude that the observed sub-mm flux comes from the host galaxy of SN 1997ey, and that this galaxy is a normal disk galaxy.

gmw_00003107.name: SDSS J03302-0532
gmw_00003107.overview: SDSS J03302-0532 is a broad-line active galactic nucleus, also classified as a Seyfert 1 galaxy [arXiv:0806.3470]. Its optical spectrum exhibits broadened hydrogen-alpha and hydrogen-beta lines, which indicate a central black hole mass of approximately 72 million solar masses [arXiv:0806.3470].

sources.summary: The paper treats SDSS J03302-0532 as a broad-line AGN, fitting its optical spectrum to derive broad H-line properties, black-hole mass, and Eddington ratio.

quotes.quote: Broad-Line AGNs (Seyfert 1 Galaxies) SDSS J03302-0532 (J033013.26-053235.9) : The optical spectrum shows broadened H and H lines. We fit multi-Gaussian models to the spectrum. Galaxy subtraction is performed using the principle component analysis code of Hao2005 , and the narrow lines are modeled as outlined in GreeneandHo2004 ; see also Ho1997c . Following GreeneandHo2005 , the AGN luminosity is measured from the H line and the velocity dispersion is measured from the H line width (for details see GreeneandHo2007 ). The black hole masses for the SDSS objects are estimated from the broad H luminosity and line width using the relation M_ BH = 2.0 10^6 ( L_ H 10^ 42 erg s^ 1 )^ 0.55 ( FWHM_ H 10^3 km s^ 1 )^ 2.06 M_ following GreeneandHo2005 . We find: FWHM(H ) = 5340 km/s, log( L (H )) = 40.83 erg s ^ 1 , log( L [ OIII ] ) = 39.50 erg s ^ 1 , M_ BH = 7.2 10 ^ 7 M_ , L_ bol /L_ Edd = 0.006.

gmw_00003108.name: 4U 1344-60
gmw_00003108.overview: The active galactic nucleus 4U 1344-60 is classified as an intermediate Seyfert galaxy [arXiv:0806.3470] that exhibits a bright X-ray continuum [arXiv:1209.4068]. Its spectrum features a broad H-alpha emission line [arXiv:0806.3470] and a narrow iron line likely originating from distant matter [arXiv:1209.4068].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes 4U 1344-60 as a bright Seyfert galaxy whose X-ray continuum, absorption, iron line, and reflection signatures changed between archival and new observations.

quotes.quote: 4U 1344-60 is a bright Seyfert galaxy which revealed relativistic reflection features in the archival observation. We present the spectroscopic results of new data obtained with the satellite and compare them with the previous observation. The X-ray continuum of 4U 1344-60 can be well described by a power-law component with the photon index 1.7 modified by a fully and a partially covering local absorbers. We measured a substantial decrease of the fraction of the partially absorbed radiation from around 45 in the observation to less than 10 in the observation while the power-law slope remains constant within uncertainties. The iron line in the spectrum is relatively narrow, =(0.08 0.02) keV, without any suggestion for relativistic broadening.

The X-ray continuum spectrum of the Seyfert galaxy 4U 1344-60 is dominated by a power law with a standard value of the photon index 1.7 0.1 . A narrow fluorescent line with equivalent width 100 eV is an apparent feature in the spectra. The width of the iron line is =(0.08 0.02) keV, i.e. 9000 km/s of FWHM, suggesting its origin from a distant matter not affected by relativistic smearing. The outermost parts of an accretion disc, broad line region clouds or torus are possible candidates for the reflector.

sources.summary: The paper classifies 4U 1344-60 as an intermediate Seyfert with broad H emission but no broadened H line, reporting H and X-ray fluxes.

quotes.quote: 4U 1344-60 : Piconcelli2006 report a broad H line with a FWHM of 4400 km/s. They classify it as an intermediate Seyfert (S1.5) due to the absence of a broadened H line. They report a de-reddened broad H flux of F (H ) = 3.3 0.8 10 ^ 12 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 , and a 2-10 keV X-ray flux of F_ 2-10keV = 3.6 10 ^ 11 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 .

gmw_00003109.name: MRK 607
gmw_00003109.overview: Mrk 607 is an edge-on Sa spiral galaxy harboring a Seyfert 2 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2111.10036, arXiv:2103.08736]. Its central region features complex star formation histories with young, ring-like stellar structures [arXiv:2203.07484], while the gas in the galaxy counter-rotates relative to its stars [arXiv:2103.08736, arXiv:2107.07635].

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 1320 is a Seyfert 2 AGN whose hard X-ray spectrum is reflection dominated, Compton-thick, and well modeled jointly across two consistent observations.

quotes.quote: We present X-ray spectral analyses for three Seyfert 2 active galactic nuclei, NGC 424, NGC 1320, and IC 2560, observed by in the 3--79 keV band. The high quality hard X-ray spectra allow detailed modeling of the Compton reflection component for the first time in these sources. Using quasi-simultaneous and data, as well as archival data, we find that all three nuclei are obscured by Compton-thick material with column densities in excess of 5 10^ 24 cm ^ 2 , and that their X-ray spectra above 3 keV are dominated by reflection of the intrinsic continuum on Compton-thick material. Due to the very high obscuration, absorbed intrinsic continuum components are not formally required by the data in any of the sources. We constrain the intrinsic photon indices and the column density of the reflecting medium through the shape of the reflection spectra.

NGC 1320 was simultaneously observed with and twice. As no significant differences are apparent between the two observations, we model both epochs simultaneously and list the best-fit parameters of those fits in Table tab:model_parameters . The best-fit photon index is hard ( =1.3 0.1 ), however, assuming a higher cut-off energy for the intrinsic continuum brings it closer to the typical value: e.g., for a cut-off at 500 keV, the best fit is obtained for =1.5 0.1 .

sources.summary: The paper finds Mrk 607 has low-sigma patches, high h3 values, and h3 anti-correlation with the velocity field, consistent with bulge stars lagging disk rotation.

quotes.quote: Patches of low- are seen for Mrk 607, NGC 2110, NGC 3516, NGC 4051, NGC 4235 and NGC 5899, while three objects (NGC 1052, NGC 3227 and NGC 4388) show a centrally peaked distribution. These rings and partial rings are located at distances from the nucleus ranging between 150 and 250 pc. Nuclear values for each galaxy are presented in the last column of Table tab_mod , obtained by fitting the nuclear spectrum integrated within a circular aperture with radius of 75 pc. Most galaxies show low h_ 3 values ( 0.10 <h_ 3 < 0.10), suggesting that their LOSVDs are well represented by a Gaussian velocity distribution. Exceptions are Mrk 607, Mrk1066 and NGC 4051 that show h_ 3 values larger than 0.15. For 8 galaxies (NGC 1052, NGC 2110, NGC 3227, NGC 3516, NGC 4051, NGC 5506, Mrk 607 and Mrk 1066), the h_ 3 map is anti-correlated with the velocity field, with positive h_ 3 values seen at locations where rotation velocities are observed in blueshifts and negative h_ 3 values associated to regions where the rotation velocities are observed in redshifts.

The velocity dispersion maps show low- ( 50-80 ) rings for 4 galaxies (Mrk 1066, Mrk 1157, NGC 5929 and NGC 788) or ``patches" of low- structures (for Mrk 607, NGC 2110, NGC 3516, NGC 4051, NGC 4235 and NGC 5899) at typical distances of 200 pc, interpreted as being originated in young/intermediate age stellar populations. Centrally peaked maps are observed for three galaxies (NGC 1052, NGC 3227 and NGC 4388). The h_ 3 moment is anti-correlated with the velocity field for 8 galaxies (NGC 1052, NGC 2110, NGC 3227, NGC 3516, NGC 4051, NGC 5506, Mrk 607 and Mrk 1066) -- positive h_ 3 values seen at locations where the velocity field shows blueshifts and h_ 3 <0 for locations where the velocity field shows redshifts. The presence of these wings are attributed to the contribution of stars from the galaxy bulge that present lower rotation velocities. The observed velocity fields are well reproduced by a rotating disk model, with deprojected velocity amplitudes in the range 60--340 .

sources.summary: The paper describes Mrk 607 as an edge-on Seyfert 2 spiral with counter-rotating gas, compact radio emission, equatorial outflows, and near-IR emission aligned with the disc.

quotes.quote: Mrk 607 Mrk 607 is an edge-on spiral galaxy ( i=70^ ) classified as Sa?, hosts a Seyfert 2 nucleus and is located at 37 Mpc veron-cetty06 . Hubble Space Telescope (HST) observations show that the [ O iii ] emission is well aligned to the galaxy's major axis ( PA 43^ ), being more extended along the north-east side ferruit00,schmitt03 . Patches of low stellar velocity dispersion produced by young to intermediate age stellar populations are observed at distances of 200 pc from the centre rogemar_stellar . Optical and near-IR IFS observations show that the stellar kinematics in the inner kpc of Mrk 607 is dominated by rotation in the plane of the disc.

The gas also presents a clear rotation pattern, but in the opposite direction of the stars Freitas18,rogemar_stellar,astor19 . In addition, equatorial ionised outflows (perpendicular to the ionization cone) are observed in the optical emission lines Freitas18 . Radio emission is seen only from a nuclear compact source, with no evidence of a radio jet at kpc scales colbert96,nagar99 . For Mrk 607 (Fig. fig:M607 ), we find that the molecular and ionised gas emission is more extended along the orientation of line of nodes derived from the stellar velocity line of nodes 138^ 3^ ; rogemar_stellar .

sources.summary: The paper fits NGC 1320 as part of the Compton-thick AGN sample, finding no apec component, extra Fe-complex Gaussian terms, and a lower FPMB normalization.

quotes.quote: Our sample consists of 10 local ( z<0.1 ) CT AGN observed by : NGC 424, NGC 1068, 2MFGC 2280, NGC 1320, NGC 1386, NGC 3079, IC 2560, Mrk 34, NGC 4945 and the Circinus galaxy. With the exception of 2MFGC 2280, these sources were selected to be known CT AGN and to cover a wide range in X-ray luminosity. Particular attention was paid to selecting sources in the low ( 10^ 42 ) and high ( 10^ 44 ) luminosity regimes, thus enveloping the bulk of the local CT AGN population, which is at moderate luminosities, so that our sample could be as representative as possible, despite its small size.

The spectra of NGC 424 and NGC 1320 also did not require the apec model. NGC 1068 has a well known Fe complex at 6-7 keV, consisting of neutral Fe K and emission, plus ionised emission at 6.7 and 6.96 keV. While the torus model accounts for the neutral emission, we add Gaussian components fixed at the energies of the ionised emission to account for these lines, where the widths are fixed at small values (1 eV). As found by and for Circinus and for NGC 4945, we also find that the \ spectra of these AGN require an additional Gaussian component each to model lines in the Fe complex, fixed at 6.7 keV in NGC 4945 and allowed to vary around 6.6 keV for Circinus, as required by the data. Additional Gaussian components at the energies of the Fe complex were required to fit the spectra of NGC 1320, NGC 1386 and IC 2560. We summarize all the models used for each source in Table table_cnomo . We allow the cross-normalization between the two \ focal plane modules (FPMs) to vary in the fitting to account for instrumental cross-calibration. We also allow the cross-normalization between the FPMs and \ to vary to account for instrumental cross-calibration and the fact that the the \ observations are not simultaneous with . We fix the other parameters to each other since there is a known agreement between spectral parameters determined from \ and \ e.g. walton13,walton14 . We present the cross-normalizations in Table table_cnomo . The observations are mainly consistent with the cross-normalization between the FPMs being unity, with the exceptions being NGC 1068, NGC 1320 and Circinus. NGC 1068 and Circinus both show that the normalization of FPMB to be 3 greater than the normalization of the FPMA. For NGC 1320 the FPMB normalization is 9 lower.

sources.summary: The paper maps Mrk 607 as a Seyfert 2 AGN host whose electron temperatures vary only modestly and are reproduced by standard photoionization rather than outflows.

quotes.quote: We present maps for the electron temperature in the inner kpc of three luminous Seyfert galaxies: Mrk 79, Mrk 348, and Mrk 607 obtained from Gemini GMOS-IFU observations at spatial resolutions of 110--280 pc. We study the distributions of electron temperature in active galaxies and find temperatures varying in the range from 8 000 to 30 000 K. Shocks due to gas outflows play an important role in the observed temperature distributions of Mrk 79 and Mrk 348, while standard photoionization models reproduce the derived temperature values for Mrk 607. In Mrk 79 and Mrk 348, we find direct evidence for shock-ionization with overall orientation orthogonal to the ionization axis, where shocks can be easily observed as the AGN radiation field is shielded by the nuclear dusty torus. This also indicates that even when the ionization cones are narrow, the shocks can be much wider-angle. galaxies: Seyfert -- galaxies: active -- galaxies: abundances -- galaxies: ISM Active Galactic Nuclei (AGN) present in their spectra strong emission lines whose relative intensities can be used to estimate or characterize the physical and chemical properties of the gas phase in these objects, such as the nature and strength of the interstellar radiation fields, chemical abundance, local temperature, and gas density. The high luminosity of the AGN continuum and these emission lines have made AGN essential in the studies of chemical evolution of galaxies across the Hubble time. Photoionization models of the Narrow Line Region (NLR) have often been used for determining chemical abundances of heavy elements in AGN host galaxies. The pioneering work by ferland83 , who used the first version of the photoionization Cloudy code, showed that NLR exhibit metallicities in the range 0.1 (Z/ Z_ ) 1.0 . Thereafter, several studies exploiting photoionization models have been carried out aiming to estimate the metallicity of AGN host galaxies in the local universe e.g. thaisa98, feltre16, castro17 and at high redshifts e.g. dors18, mignoli19, guo20 . In spite of the wide use of photoionization models in the estimation of gas metallicities, their application in deriving elemental abundances has several limitations. Firstly, it is necessary to assume an incident Spectral Energy Distribution as one of the input parameters of the models, which is generally represented by the power law component of the non-thermal X-ray radiation with continuum between 2 keV and 2 500 with a spectral index _ ox marchese12,dors19 . Detailed photoionization models used to derive O and N abundances dors17 and bayesian-like approach enrique19 have predicted _ ox to be higher than 1.2 for Seyfert 2 nuclei. Conversely, measurements of _ ox by miller11 indicate that most AGN have _ ox of the order of 1.4 and even lower values ( 2.0 ) can be derived for these objects. This indicates that an extra physical process is missing in the models probably shocks, e.g., contini19 . Additionally, the use of emission line intensities observed in a limited spectral range can produce a degeneracy in the models e.g. davies14 and, consequently, uncertainties in the chemical abundance values. Direct determination of abundances, known as the T_ e method which is based on the observational determination of the electron temperature ( T_ e ), yields more reliable abundance estimations because it circumvents the aforementioned problems of photoionization models. Recently, dors20 presented a new methodology for the T_ e method to be applied to NLRs of Seyfert 2 nuclei, introducing a new relation between temperatures of the low ( t_ 2 ) and high ( t_ 3 ) ionization gas zones derived from photoionization models. This method produces O/H abundances slightly lower (about 0.2 dex) than those derived from detailed photoionization models. However, previous studies have questioned the use of the T_ e method in AGN. For example, stasisnka84 argued that in the NLR the intensity of the [ O iii ] ( 4949+ 5007)/ 4363 line ratio, used to derive t_ 3 e.g. hagele08 , is enhanced by emissions from clouds with high gas density ( N_ e 10^ 5 cm^ 3 ), which precludes any direct determination of abundances based on the T_ e method. nagao01 found evidence that the [ O iii ] 4363 line is emitted in denser ( N_ e 10^ 5-7 cm^ 3 ) and obscured gas regions than those emitting [ O iii ] 5007. Shocks from AGN outflows may also affect the ionization structure and T_ e distribution, particularly in regions where the AGN ionizing photons are shielded by nuclear obscuration contini01,zakamska14 , where the contribution of photoionization to gas ionization is expected to be lower. Spatially resolved measurements of electron temperature and density are important to constrain the AGN contribution to gas ionization and to infer the gas physical conditions. Observational data that enable measurements of weak auroral lines, from which T_ e can be estimated, and spatially resolved studies of T_ e in AGN are seldom found in the literature but see revalski18,dahmer19,dagostino19 . In this Letter, we present two-dimensional (2D) maps of electron temperature and electron density in the NLR, in the case in one Seyfert 1 (Mrk 79) and two Seyfert 2 (Mrk 348 and Mrk 607) galaxies, obtained from integral field spectroscopy. A thorough analysis of T_ e and N_ e could be performed with the use of 2D spectroscopy at spatial resolutions of a few hundred parsecs in these 3 AGN hosts. In Sect. datasec , we describe the data and techniques to derive T_ e and N_ e . In Sect. resulsec we present and discuss the results, while the conclusions are given in Sect. concsec . We use the Gemini Multi-Object Spectrograph GMOS, gmos Integral Field Unit (IFU) data to map the T_ e and N_ e in the inner few hundred parsecs of the nearby luminous Seyfert galaxies Mrk 79 (Sy 1, SBb), Mrk 348 (Sy 2, SA(s)0/a) and Mrk 607 (Sy 2, Sa).

The T_ e [ O III ] and T_ e [ N II ] maps for Mrk 607 show only small variations in temperature, with T_ e [ O III ] 20 000 K and T_ e [ N II ] 10 000 K. No clear evidence of outflows is seen in this galaxy Freitas18 . Figure golden shows the T_ e [ N II ] versus T_ e [ O III ] plot with the observed values for Mrk 607 and Mrk 348, which are compared to predictions of AGN photoionization models built with the Cloudy code ferland13 by dors20 and to integrated measurements for Seyfert 2 galaxies. While the points for Mrk 607 are seen in the same region occupied by the model predictions and by the integrated spectra, the points for Mrk 348 are located in a very distinct position, towards higher temperatures.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1320 as an unusual Seyfert 2 where a high scaling factor only partly removes the northwest filament, implying an additional central near-UV component.

quotes.quote: NGC 1320 (Sy2) This galaxy is an interesting case. In order to try to remove the filament to northwest, we have used f = 0.6, which is twice the average value obtained for other cases. Some structure is still seen, but applying a higher factor will introduce strong negative residuals next to the nucleus which are difficult to justify. The value of f as high as 0.6 might be justified as possibly due to a smaller [ OIII ] /H ratio in this galaxy, but still it does not explain all the emission. Most likely, it has a regular f ( 0.3) and a further contribution in the central region.

sources.summary: The paper maps ionized and molecular gas in Mrk 607, identifying it as an Sa galaxy with a Seyfert 2 nucleus, low warm and cold H2 masses, and peaked ionized-gas profiles.

quotes.quote: firstpage - lastpage We have used the Gemini Near-Infrared Integral Field Spectrograph (NIFS) in the J and K bands to map the distribution, excitation and kinematics of the ionized H ii and warm molecular gas H _2 , in the inner few 100 pc of 6 nearby active galaxies: NGC 788, Mrk 607, NGC 3227, NGC 3516, NGC 5506, NGC 5899.

Table tabmass shows that the integrated mass of warm H _2 gas (within typically 100 -- 400 pc from the nucleus) ranges from 106 M _ for Mrk 607 to 820 M _ for NGC 5506, while the estimated mass of cold molecular gas ranges from 8 10 ^7 M _ for Mrk 607 to 6 10 ^ 8 M _ for NGC 5506. The masses of ionized gas range from 1.8 10 ^5 M _ for NGC 3516 to 1.9 10 ^7 M _ for NGC 5506. We point out again that the uncertainties in the calculated masses, presented in Tab. tabmass , were obtained only by propagating the line flux uncertainties and are thus lower limits, considering that there are additional uncertainties in adopted physical properties -- e.g. on the M_ H_2 cold /M_ H_2 ratio, H _2 vibrational temperatures and electron densities. In this section we discuss individually each galaxy presenting a brief review of previous results from the literature, trying to relate them with ours . We also discuss and analyze the masses of ionized and molecular gas, as well as their surface mass density distributions and the global properties of the gas kinematics, leaving the kinematic modelling and the analysis of the gas kinematics and excitation to Paper B . NGC 788 NGC 788 (Fig. ngc788 ) was identified as a Seyfert galaxy by huchra1982 , and has been observed in the optical hamuy1987,wagner1988,kay1994,cruz1994 , radio ulvestad1989 , and millimetre heckman1989 wavebands. This galaxy is an early-type spiral with faint arms visible up to 30 from the nucleus evans1996 , most conspicuous to the north-west of the nucleus with a string of bright compact H ii regions, while a complex of fainter H ii regions is associated with a southern arm evans1996 . The kinematics major axis PA 125 ^ was determined from the V_ LOS fields (Fig. ngc788 ), with a value close to that obtained by riffel2017 for the stellar kinematics of 130 ^ . The velocity amplitude observed for [ Fe ii ] and Pa , of 150 km s ^ 1 is much higher than that for H _2 , of only 50 km s ^ 1 . These higher velocities are associated with a more collimated emission and higher velocity dispersions suggesting the presence of a bipolar outflow along the east-west direction, as suggested from the morphology of the gas emission in the [ Fe ii ] flux map. The E(B-V) map from Fig. ngc788 has the highest values to southwest of the nucleus, which coincide with a dust lane seen in the HST - F606W optical continuum image from . Mrk 607 Mrk 607 (Fig. mrk607 ) is an Sa galaxy hosting a Seyfert 2 nucleus. A continuum image from ferruit2000 shows a high ellipticity of 0.60, yielding an inclination i 67 ^ ), with major axis PA of 43^ , which agrees with our estimate of the kinematic major axis orientation of 38^ from the H _2 velocity field (Paper B). Our data reveal that the three velocity fields -- [ Fe ii ] , Pa and H _2 - show what seems to be a rotation pattern, very steep in the center in H _2 and less steep in the first two lines. Spiral dust lanes are clearly visible in the HST F547 continuum image of the inner 8 ^ (1.4 kpc) ferruit2000 . The high inclination of the galaxy may explain the large velocity dispersions observed. In the central 5 ^ (875 pc) it presents weak radio emission extended along PA 180 ^ colbert1996,nagar1999 which is not aligned with the axis of the most extended gas emission, which is observed along the galaxy major axis. The E(B-V) map from Fig. mrk607 has high values ( 5) to southeast/west of the nucleus, which coincide with a dust lane seen in the HST - F606W optical continuum image from . NGC 3227 NGC 3227 (Fig. ngc3227 ) is a well studied barred galaxy, with a Seyfert 1.5 nucleus ho1997 and in interaction with the dwarf elliptical galaxy NGC 3226 mundell2004 . The galactic disc has an inclination of 56 ^ , with an outer photometric major-axis at a position angle PA = 158 ^ mundell1995 . This value agrees with the PA 155 ^ we have obtained from the fit of the H _2 V _ LOS velocity field (Paper B), and with the value of 156 ^ found by riffel2017 from the stellar kinematics. The three emission-line velocity fields show a rotation pattern, that is nevertheless distorted due to the presence of additional components, mainly to the north-east, east and south-east. These components are most conspicuous in [ Fe ii ] and Pa velocity maps and are associated with regions of enhanced velocity dispersion. The central region has been mapped in ^ 12 CO(1-0) and ^ 12 CO(2-1) by schinnerer2000 , who detected molecular gas very close to the nucleus (within 13 pc), in agreement with our results that show a large molecular gas concentration towards the centre (see the surface mass density profile in the third line of Fig. mass ). In addition, schinnerer2000 found an asymmetric nuclear ring with a diameter of about 3 0 (220 pc), which seems to correspond to the structure delineated in the line ratio maps [ Fe ii ] /Pa and H _2 /Br of Fig. ngc3227 . The inner kiloparsec hosts a radio jet at PA 10^ and a conical NLR outflow at PA 15 ^ mundell1995 , while arribas1994 report an H outflow at PA 50 ^ . The extent of the [ Fe ii ] emission to the north, as well as its enhanced velocity dispersion may be related to the radio emission, while the outflows observed at PAs between 15 ^ and 50 ^ can explain the deviation from rotation observed in the velocity fields of [ Fe ii ] and Pa to the north-east. Signatures of gas outflows in NGC 3227 have also been observed by davies2014 in the inner 1^ 2^ using near-IR IFS observations with the instrument SINFONI at the VLT. A V-H colour map shows dust lanes to the south-west of the nucleus martini2003,davies2014 , which are co-spatial with the highest E(B-V) values seen in Fig. ngc3227 . NGC 3516 The Seyfert 1 nucleus of this SB0 galaxy (Fig. ngc3516 ) shows variable ultraviolet absorption lines voit1987,kriss1996 as well as variable broad emission lines and continuum koratkar1996 . Spectroscopic studies of the gaseous kinematics using either long-slit ulrich1980,goad1987,goad1988,mulchaey1992 or integral field spectroscopy veilleux1993,aoki1994,arribas1994 have outlined multiple spectral components displaying strong deviations from ``normal'' galaxy rotation. Two general models have been proposed to explain the morphology and the kinematics of the emission-line gas that presents a Z-shaped structure covering the inner 5 0: a bent bipolar mass outflow, first suggested by goad1987 and further developed by mulchaey1992 and veilleux1993 , and a precessing twin-jet model by veilleux1993 . The stellar rotation curve obtained by arribas1997 yields a systemic velocity of 2593 15 km s ^ 1 , close to that derived by riffel2017 of 2631 km s ^ 1 . The kinematic major axis obtained by arribas1997 has PA= 53 ^ 5^ and is consistent with our results for the stellar kinematics riffel2017 and also with the orientation of the kinematic major axis of the molecular gas of 60 ^ . NGC 5506 The nucleus of NGC 5506 (Fig. ngc5506 ) is classified as a Sy1.9 based on the detection of broad wings in the Pa profile blanco1990 . However, more recently, nagar2002 presented evidence that NGC 5506 is an obscured narrow-line Sy1, via the detection of the permitted O i 1.129 m line, together with a broad pedestal of Pa and rapid X-ray variability. The galaxy nucleus is very compact in the mid-IR, with an apparent optical depth of ^ app _ 10 m 1.4 roche1991,roche2007,siebenmorgen2004 , although NGC 5506 also shows variations in its silicate absorption depth on parsec scales roche2007 . The optical spectrum has strong [ O iii ] and [ N ii ] narrow lines zaw2009 . The log( [ O iii ] /H ) and log( [ N ii ] /H ) line ratios, 0.88 and 0.09 respectively kewley2001 , place it firmly in the Seyfert region of the BPT diagram. NGC 5506 is close to edge-on, with an inclination of 75 ^ , above and below which ionized gas is in outflow within cones with an opening angle of 80 ^ maiolino1994 . These outflows are consistent with the distribution of enhanced sigma values we observe in the ionized gas in a vertical structure crossing the galaxy plane and opening to the north and south of it (Fig. ngc5506 ). The outflow to the north is also well traced by the [ Fe ii ] flux distribution (its highest emission shows an approximately conical shape), while the H _2 flux distribution seems to better trace the gas in the galaxy plane, as it is oriented along this direction. As observed in Fig. espectros , the nucleus of NGC 5506 shows a steep red continuum over the 2.1-2.4 m spectral range that can be attributed to a blackbody source with temperature 1000 K. Although large scale dust is observed in the central region of this galaxy, possibly due to its high inclination and as seen in the V-H dust map from martini2003 , the origin of the K-band continuum is most probably hot dust emission from the AGN torus, as already observed for other active galaxies e.g. riffel09-4151,burtscher15,diniz18 . NGC 5899 This inclined SAB(rs) galaxy (Fig. ngc5899 ) is reported to be in a pair and presents an optical Seyfert 2 spectrum devaucouleurs91 . The stellar kinematics derived by riffel2017 gives a PA 24 ^ , that is somewhat distinct from that obtained by us for the molecular gas (Paper B), of 4 ^ . The kinematics of the ionized gas is very different from that of the molecular gas (Fig. ngc5899 ): while the later seems to be dominated by rotation in the inclined galaxy plane, with blueshifts to the north and redshifts to the south, the ionized gas kinematics shows velocities that are opposite to that observed in the H _2 velocity field. A possible interpretation is that the ionized gas is tracing an outflow at PA 0 ^ , as supported also by the north-south elongation observed mostly in the [ Fe ii ] flux map and by the enhanced velocity dispersion observed at these locations. Global properties Surface mass density distributions Fig. mass shows that the molecular gas seems to be more evenly distributed over the field of view than the ionized gas, which is more concentrated towards the nucleus and shows a more patchy and sometimes collimated mass distribution. The more peaked distribution of the ionized gas is clearly seen in the average surface mass density profiles shown in the third column of Fig. mass for the galaxies NGC 788, Mrk 607, NGC 3227 and NGC 5506. In the cases of NGC 3516 and NGC 5899, the average profiles of the molecular and ionized gas surface mass densities are similar to each other. The prevalence of more concentrated ionized gas profiles than those in H _2 can be attributed to the fact that the neutral gas rapidly absorbs the ionizing photons from the AGN, thus concentrating in its vicinity and/or along the ionization axis -- the preferred direction of escape of the AGN radiation. The H _2 , on the other hand, has another source of excitation, as shown in our previous works e.g. ilha2016 , where the typical temperature is 2000 K and the line ratios suggest thermal excitation. The heating to excite the rotational and vibrational modes of the H _2 molecule can be attributed to X-rays originating in the AGN. These X-rays penetrate deep along the galaxy plane in every direction heating the region and exciting the H _2 molecule. In addition, in the regions closest to the AGN, where the temperature can reach 15.000 K, the H _2 molecules are destroyed, and we only see those more deeply embedded in the circumnuclear dust, where they are shielded from the strongest AGN radiation. We thus conclude that the distinct nature of the excitation of the ionized and the molecular gas and different physical conditions of the regions where they originate can explain the difference in the gas surface mass density profiles. Gas kinematics In this section we discuss briefly the global properties of the gas kinematics, with the goal of just highlighting apparent correlations among properties and differences between the molecular and ionized gas kinematics. As we already pointed out, we will leave the modeling of the velocity fields, the quantification of possible inflows and outflows, as well as the determination of the impact of the outflows on the host galaxy (feedback) to Paper B. A global property of the gas kinematics, as seen in Figs. ngc788 - ngc5899 , is that the warm H _2 velocity field is dominated by rotation in the plane of the galaxy. Although the ionized gas velocity fields show also signatures of rotation, the rotation pattern is distorted due to the presence of additional components that can be attributed to outflows and which are usually associated with an increase in the velocity dispersion. The above results are in agreement with those of previous studies by our AGNIFS group, in which we have additionally reported, for H _2 , the presence of inflows in a number of galaxies. We have also found that outflows are most easily seen in the ionized gas thaisa2019 and that this gas shows a more centrally concentrated flux distribution than that of H _2 , also in agreement with what we found in the present paper. The most frequent presence of outflows in the ionized gas than in the molecular gas can be understood as due to the fact that the ionized gas is mostly concentrated along the ionization axis, where it can be easily pushed by an AGN outflow. The molecular gas, on the other hand, is usually destroyed by the hard AGN radiation at these locations. Thus, although the H _2 flux distributions appear also surrounding the nucleus, it is not co-spatial with the ionized gas, as it originates in gas at temperatures much lower (2000K) than those characteristic of the ionized gas (10000K). This is also supported by the lower velocity dispersion of H _2 . Clear examples are the cases of NGC 4151 storchi2009 and NGC 1068 barbosa14 that show H _2 flux distributions surrounding the nucleus but avoiding the ionization axis. On the other hand, molecular gas outflows are observed in a number of cases, and in distant AGN hosts emonts2017 . A possibility in these cases is that a nuclear outflow can push the surrounding gas including the H _2 gas which may be shielded from the ionizing radiation by dust, for example. We hope to re-visit this point in Paper B. Regarding the presence of outflows, we have found signatures of them in most galaxies of our sample, most clearly observed in the [ Fe ii ] kinematics: (1) in NGC 788, along the east-west direction; (2) in NGC 3227, towards the northeast, as suggested by distortions in the velocity field and patterns in h_3 and h_4 ; (3) in NGC 3516, from the south-east to the north-west, as suggested by the disturbed velocity field and enhanced velocity dispersion; (4) in NGC 5506, along north-south, as suggested by the enhanced velocity dispersions; (5) and in NGC 5899, towards north and south, as indicated by the opposite velocity field between the ionized and molecular gas. Another global property of the sample is the fact that the h_3 Gauss-Hermite moment shows an inverse correlation with the V _ LOS in a number of cases: (1) in the Pa line for NGC 788; (2) in Pa and [ Fe II ] for Mrk 607; (3) in Pa and H _2 in NGC 3227; (4) In Pa and H _2 for NGC 3516; (5) in Br and H _2 in NGC 5506; (6) in H _2 in NGC 5899.

sources.summary: The paper maps Mrk 607's central ionized gas, describing its strong-emission region, velocity field, and signs of nuclear outflow, rotation, density structure, or inflow.

quotes.quote: The SER in this Seyfert 2 galaxy is most extended to the NW along the PMA which also coincides with the kinematic major axis (KMA). Freitas2018 have shown that the gas in this galaxy counter-rotates relative to the stars, suggesting an external origin. An increase in is observed in a 200 pc wide band crossing the nucleus perpendicularly to the PMA. This increase is approximately co-spatial with the increase in the gas density and in the gas velocity dispersion as previously reported by and tentatively attributed to an equatorial outflow related to the AGN and its radio emission, as the ionisation axis seems to be along the PMA. schoenell2019 reach similar conclusions, reporting an equatorial outflow of molecular gas, traced by the H _2 emission.

sources.summary: Mrk 607 is an Sa Seyfert 2 galaxy with weak nuclear featureless continuum, older circumnuclear populations, nuclear reddening, disk-like stellar kinematics, and metallicity rising toward the field edges.

quotes.quote: Mrk 607 Mrk 607 is an Sa galaxy harboring a Seyfert 2 nucleus. We detected a very weak ( < 10 ) FC contribution in the nucleus, which slightly rises to the upper and lower regions of the datacube. The stellar population is dominated by older components in the circumnuclear region, with a combination of old and young stellar populations in the inner 1 0. The dust reddening peaks at 1 mag in the nucleus, and decreases to the edges of the datacube. The stellar kinematics are characterized by blueshifts to the southeast and redshifts to the northwest, as well as a lower velocity dispersion in the center if compared to the borders of the datacube.

sources.summary: The paper describes Mrk 607 as having a complex central star formation history, young and intermediate ring-like components, older nuclear starlight, and dominant nuclear hot dust.

quotes.quote: Mrk 607: The SFH of the central region of this galaxy is very complex and all the SPCs are necessary to explain the continuum emission of this galaxy. The two youngest SPCs ( xy and xyo ) when taken together present a partial ring, from south towards west up to north (130 pc < < 150 pc). The xiy SPC shows another ring-like structure (100 pc << 150 pc), reaching up to 50 percent of the emission. The xio SPC is spread over the FoV, and summed with xo contributes up to 40 percent of the stellar emission in the nuclear region ( < 50 pc). The clear dominant nuclear emission in this galaxy is due to the hot dust emission, reaching more than 50 percent in the centre, and falling to zero in the outer locations.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 1320 has two frequency-shifted maser groups, no systemic maser near the recession velocity, and a four-parameter fit yielding mass-to-distance ratio and black-hole mass.

quotes.quote: The masers of NGC 1320 have been found in just two distinct frequency shifted groups. The maser distribution shows an east-west orientation with a P.A. of 75 10 . By considering our results, we could not recognize any systemic maser feature because there are no masers with a speed near the recession velocity of 2825 km s ^ 1 . For this system we estimated 4 parameters: The BH x- and y- offsets, the recession velocity and the mass-to-distance ratio M/D=1.50 0.07 10^ 5 M_ /Mpc . In this case, the corresponding BH mass reads M=(5.12 0.37) 10^ 6 M_ .

sources.summary: The paper re-analyzes NGC 1320 as the longest and most complex appendix exposure, with evidence for transmission and reflection components in its X-ray spectrum.

quotes.quote: NGC 1320 Among the sources observed by \ and presented in this Appendix, NGC 1320 is the one with the longest exposure time ( 11.5 ks) and most complex \ spectrum, as shown in Fig. A.1 (bottom-right panel). The \ data for this source are highly suggestive of the presence of both a transmission and reflection component. While the transmitted component is well parameterized by a =1.8 power-law continuum absorbed by thick matter (with a column density of 2.4 10^ 24 cm ^ 2 ), for the reflection component the parameters are basically unconstrained, calling for observations with higher photon statistics and data above 10 keV to properly model and constrain the two spectral components. The best-fitting model requires also the presence of a power-law ( 2.9 ) at low energies and a \ emission line at an energy of 930 eV ( EW 50 eV).

sources.summary: The paper reports a centrally peaked [ FeII ] line-to-continuum profile in NGC 1320, non-rotational or non-planar gas motions, and marked species-dependent velocity-dispersion differences across its central kiloparsec.

quotes.quote: The H _2 line-to-continuum ratio is constant or drops significantly towards the nucleus in most galaxies, except NGC 2273, NGC 5273, and NGC 7450. For Br , in contrast, the ratio peaks towards the nucleus in many galaxies, consistent with stronger ionization mechanisms towards the active nucleus. These two results both explain the observed central drop of the H _2 /Br ratio. Of the three emission lines, the [ FeII ] line-to-continuum ratio shows the most distinct radial variations. Its profile is centrally peaked in Mrk 1, Mrk 3, NGC 262, NGC 1320, NGC 2273, NGC 3081, NGC 5273, and NGC 7450. Several sources show two maxima (or a central drop, e.g. NGC 1667, NGC 3185, NGC 4151, NGC 4253, NGC 4388, and NGC 5506). Finally, Mrk 1066, NGC 931, NGC 2110, NGC 2992, and NGC 4051 show peculiar distributions, e.g. the distribution in NGC 2110 shows three separate peaks.

However, the gas velocity curves are similar to those of the stars, which define the galaxy disk. This is the case in Mrk 1, Mrk 1066, NGC 262, NGC 931, NGC 1667, NGC 2110, NGC 2273, NGC 3081, NGC 3185, NGC 4388, NGC 5273, and NGC 5506. In these galaxies, all three emission lines display a velocity curve that follows that of the stars and is reminiscent of rotation, thus strongly implying that the line-emitting gas is located in the disk of the galaxy and rotating with the disk. Other galaxies (Mrk 3, NGC 1320, NGC 2992, NGC 4051, NGC 4151, NGC 4253, and NGC 7450) display velocity curves different from the stellar velocity curve in one or more emission lines, which may be a signature of non-rotational motion, either in- or outflows or other non-planar motion of the gas.

The radial dispersion profiles in Mrk 3, Mrk 1066, NGC 2110, NGC 2273, NGC 4051, NGC 4151, NGC 4253, NGC 5273, and NGC 5506 have a central peak, which indicates a higher kinetic gas temperature in the nucleus. Either the ISM is more turbulent (possibly caused by a jet- or wind-driven outflow), and/or more intensely heated by the incident radiation field. In either case, a peaked dispersion distribution is a strong indication for the AGN governing the physical state of the gas at the nucleus. The dispersion peak is most often present in [ FeII ] (indicating dust destruction close to the nucleus), and/or in Br (indicating a strong radiation field), while the H _2 line shows the least-pronounced central rise. Examples for flat velocity dispersion profiles are NGC 3081, NGC 3185, and NGC 4388, which show no significant change over up to 1.2 kpc in diameter. The velocity dispersion in all three sources is of order 100 km/s, well within the range observed for star forming regions, which is consistent with star formation being the dominant the gas excitation mechanism. Finally, the dispersion profiles in Mrk 1, NGC 262, NGC 931, NGC 1320, NGC 1667, NGC 2992, and NGC 7450 show marked differences between the three emission lines, with variations of 100 km/s or more at a given radius. This indicates that the ionized atomic and molecular gas is responding differently to the central AGN in these sources, perhaps due to the presences of asymmetric energy input (a jet or shocks) together with a stratified medium, e.g. a thin molecular disk in a thick ionized medium.

sources.summary: The paper maps the inner 0.4-1.1 kpc of Mrk 607 with GMOS-IFU data and finds an electron-temperature fluctuation value t_A^2 of 0.015.

quotes.quote: We use Gemini GMOS-IFU observations of three luminous nearby Seyfert galaxies (Mrk 79, Mrk 348 and Mrk 607) to estimate the electron temperature ( T_ e ) fluctuations in the inner 0.4--1.1 kpc region of these galaxies. Based on T_ e determinations through the [ O iii ] 5007 / 4363 emission line ratio of each spaxel, temperature variations are quantified by computing the integrated value of the temperature fluctuation parameter ( t^ 2 ) projected in the plane of the sky t_ A ^ 2 , for the first time in Active Galactic Nuclei. We find t_ A ^ 2 values of 0.135, 0.039, and 0.015 for Mrk 79, Mrk 348, and Mrk 607, respectively, which are of the same order or larger than the maximum values reported in star-forming regions and planetary nebulae. Taking into account that t_ A ^ 2 should be considered a lower limit of the total t^2 in the nebular volume, the results suggest that the impact of such fluctuations on chemical abundance determinations can be important in some AGNs.

sources.summary: The paper places MRK 607 in the Seyfert 2 region from optical line ratios, gives its [OIII] luminosity, and notes circumnuclear water masers.

quotes.quote: MRK 607 (NGC 1320) : The log( [ OIII ] /H ) and log( [ NII ] /H ) line ratios for MRK 607, 1.0458 and -0.1498 respectively Vaceli1997 , place it in the Seyfert 2 region of the BPT diagram. Shu2007 report an [ OIII ] luminosity, log( L [ OIII ] ) = 41.05 erg s ^ 1 . MRK 607 also hosts circumnuclear H _2 O masers.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes X-ray spectra of NGC 1320, describing its best-fit components, Fe K emission or flux behavior, and comparison with previous modeling.

quotes.quote: NGC 1320 The XMM-Newton spectrum to be best fit by an absorbed double power law component with a thermal component and a Gaussian component to accommodate the Fe K emission. Greenhill et al. (2008) used a cold reflection model (PEXRAV) as well as two thermal components (using MEKAL whereas we used APEC) for the soft emission; a Gaussian had also been included to model the Fe K emission.

sources.summary: The paper characterizes NGC 1320 as an edge-on Sy2 warm galaxy with relatively high infrared luminosity, while noting no comparable star-formation records at the observed scales.

quotes.quote: NGC 1320 is a edge-on galaxy with a Sy2 nucleus. This source is a "warm galaxy" with a relatively high IR luminosity Robertis86 . We did not find records of SF in other works at the scales tracer with our observations.

gmw_00003110.name: NGC 2989
gmw_00003110.overview: NGC 2989 is identified as an HII emission-line galaxy [arXiv:0806.3470]. Based on its flux ratios, it may alternatively be a composite active galactic nucleus and starburst transitioning object with a limited active galactic nucleus contribution [arXiv:0806.3470].

sources.summary: The paper says NGC 2989 is identified as an HII galaxy but may be a composite AGN-starburst object with a limited AGN luminosity contribution.

quotes.quote: Non-AGN Emission Line Galaxies NGC 2989 : VCV identifies NGC 2989 as an HII galaxy. NGC2989 (NB: Co-authors are Veron-Cetty and Veron) classify this as a pure HII galaxy, based on flux ratios, log( [ OIII ] /H ) = -0.2304, log( [ NII ] /H ) = -0.2757. This falls within but almost on the theoretical star formation line developed by IC5169 . It may be a composite AGN-Starburst (transitioning) object with AGN contribution to the total luminosity 20 .

gmw_00003111.name: NGC 7591
gmw_00003111.overview: NGC 7591 is a massive spiral galaxy [arXiv:1112.5975] classified as a luminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:0806.3470] with a composite nucleus [arXiv:1004.1364]. Its face-on spiral arms exhibit bright PAH emission [arXiv:1004.1364], while its soft X-ray emission is dominated by star formation [arXiv:2004.02884].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 7591 as a massive, gas-normal Pegasus spiral whose high oxygen abundance is attributed to the mass-metallicity relation rather than contradicting the H I deficiency trend.

quotes.quote: However, NGC 7591 bears discussion as it only marginally conforms. This object is more than a magnitude brighter than the other galaxies considered, and its circular velocity is the highest of our sample (see Table targettab ). zaritsky94 establish metallicity dependences on V_C and M_ B -both of which are tracers of mass--for spiral galaxies. Several more recent studies e.g. tremonti04,mouhcine07,finlator08 have confirmed the observational and theoretical veracity of the so-called ``mass-metallicity relationship'' (MZR).

Given the higher mass indicated by M_B and V_C , the MZR appears to be a likely reason for the otherwise anomalously high O/H content of NGC 7591 for its value of DEF. Indeed, similar scatter can be seen for the Virgo cluster, with NGC 4501 (DEF = 0.55) having a higher abundance (log(O/H) = 9.32) than the fainter, more gas-poor NGC 4689 (DEF = 0.90, log(O/H) = 9.28) skillman96,gavazzi05 . We therefore conclude that the relatively high log(O/H) observed for NGC 7591 does not contradict the correlation between H I content and nebular abundance observed for the other galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 7591 as composite and resolves its face-on spiral arms in low-resolution maps, with nuclear continuum concentration and deepest silicate absorption at the nucleus.

quotes.quote: NGC 7591 The nuclear activity of NGC 7591 is classified as composite AAH09PMAS . Only in the low spectral resolution maps we can clearly distinguish the spiral arms of this face-on galaxy. The continuum emission is concentrated at the nucleus, while we can see bright PAH and fine structure lines emission in the galaxy arms. The nucleus is the region with the deepest silicate absorption feature.

sources.summary: NGC 7591 was not detected in hard X-rays, and its soft X-ray thermal spectrum and luminosity imply X-ray emission dominated by star formation.

quotes.quote: NGC 7591 NGC 7591 was not detected by . We determined a 3 upper limit to the observed 3-24 keV luminosity of <2.83 10^ 40 erg s ^ 1 . The soft X-ray spectrum from this source is well fit by a thermal plasma model ( tbabs apec ), resulting in C-Stat/DOF=18/19. The X-ray luminosity is L_ 2-10 keV =9.2_ 4.3 ^ +4.4 10^ 39 erg s^ 1 , which is also lower than that predicted from star formation ( L_ 2-10 ^ SF =8.3 10^ 40 erg s^ 1 ), implying that the X-ray emission of this source is dominated by star formation.

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 7591 below the star-formation line, notes its luminous infrared output, and contrasts starburst and shock-powered LINER interpretations.

quotes.quote: NGC 7591 : The log( [ OIII ] /H ) and log( [ NII ] /H ) line ratios, -0.3455 and -0.3224 respectively Moustakas2006 , place it below the star formation line determined by Kauffmann2003 . The infrared luminosity, Log( L_ IR ) = 11.12 L_ , indicates that it is a luminous infrared galaxy (LIRG). Sturm classifies it to be a Starburst galaxy while Veilleux classifies it as a LINER, resulting from shocks and not from AGN accretion activity.

gmw_00003112.name: NGC 1204
gmw_00003112.overview: NGC 1204 is a metal-rich starburst galaxy with a heavily reddened late- or post-starburst nuclear continuum [arXiv:1501.02022]. Its emission line ratios place it in the transition zone between star-forming and LINER regions, suggesting that shocks may power its emission [arXiv:0806.3470, arXiv:1501.02022].

sources.summary: The paper places NGC 1204 in the LINER region, weighs shock and star-forming interpretations, and notes a possible Seyfert/LINER classification with limited AGN contribution.

quotes.quote: NGC 1204 : The (de-reddened) log( [ OIII ] /H ) and log( [ NII ] /H ) line ratios for NGC 1204, -0.47 and -0.11 respectively Veilleux , place it between the Kauffmann2003 and IC5169 lines in the LINER region. Veilleux give reasons to believe that the emission lines are a result of shocks instead of accretion. Sturm also classify it as a star forming galaxy. However, Corbett2003 , based on their radio and spectroscopic COLA survey, claim this galaxy lies on the border between Seyfert and LINER and classify it as a Seyfert even though no radio core was detected and the radio continuum is dominated by star formation. They place a limit on the AGN component, of 25 of the total emission of the galaxy.

sources.summary: NGC 1204 is described as a metal-rich starburst with a reddened late or post-starburst continuum, transition-zone line ratios, and a broad nuclear H component.

quotes.quote: NGC 1204: Appears to be a fairly metal-rich starburst. The nuclear spectrum shows a strong, heavily reddened late- or post- starburst continuum. Zaw09 note that the line ratios in this object put it in the Kewley06 transition zone, suggesting that shocks may be important in powering this source. A broad component to H is detected at the nucleus.

gmw_00003113.name: NGC 5244
gmw_00003113.overview: NGC 5244 is classified as an HII galaxy [arXiv:0806.3470]. Its optical spectrum exhibits nitrogen and hydrogen-alpha emission lines, but it lacks apparent oxygen-three and hydrogen-beta lines and cannot be identified as an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:0806.3470].

sources.summary: The paper says NGC 5244 lacks clear [OIII] and H emission, cannot be identified as an optical AGN, and is classified as an HII galaxy.

quotes.quote: NGC 5244 NGC5244 and NGC 7135 NGC7135 have almost no [ OIII ] emission; they cannot be identified as AGNs from their optical spectra.

NGC 5244 : While the optical spectrum for NGC 5244 NGC5244 has [ NII ] and H emission lines, [ OIII ] and H lines are not apparent. NGC5244 classifies it as an HII galaxy and VCV also identifies it as an HII galaxy.

gmw_00003114.name: IC 5169
gmw_00003114.overview: IC 5169 is classified as an H II star-forming galaxy rather than an optical active galactic nucleus [arXiv:0806.3470]. It features a two-sided polar ionization cone emerging from a rapidly rotating circumnuclear starburst ring, which is surrounded by a larger, lower-velocity ring [arXiv:1708.02683].

sources.summary: The paper evaluates IC 5169 as an HII galaxy, finding that additional line-ratio diagnostics confirm it is star-forming rather than an optical AGN.

quotes.quote: The remaining three (NGC 2989, NGC 1204, IC 5169), shown as triangles, fall between the Kewley and Kauffmann lines, in the LINER region of the BPT diagram. IC5169 uses additional information from other emission lines and determines IC 5169 to be purely star forming; NGC 2989 Phillips1983 and NGC 1204 Corbett2003 are predominantly star-forming galaxies but may have limited AGN activity, contributing 25 of the total luminosity.

IC 5169 : IC5169 classifies this as an HII galaxy based on the following line ratios, log( [ OIII ] /H ) = 0.20, log( [ NII ] /H ) = -0.170, log( [ OI ] 6300/H ) = -1.49, log( [ SII ] 6717,31/H ) = -0.55. While it falls between the Kauffmann2003 and IC5169 lines in the log( [ OIII ] /H ) vs. log( [ NII ] /H ) diagram, it falls well within the star formation line in log( [ OIII ] /H ) vs. log( [ SII ] /H ) and log( [ OIII ] /H ) vs. log( [ OI ] /H ) diagrams, confirming that it is not an optical AGN.

sources.summary: The S7 notes describe IC 5169 as having a two-sided polar ionization cone emerging from a rapidly rotating circumnuclear starburst ring, plus a larger lower-velocity HII ring.

quotes.quote: IC 5169: A two-sided ionization cone with opening angle 130^ emerges in the polar direction from a bright, rapidly rotating circum-nuclear \ starburst ring. This is surrounded at larger radius by a second ring of fainter \ regions with lower rotational velocities. The intimate connection of the ENLR with the inner starburst ring explains the ambiguities in the earlier classification of the galaxy - see the discussion by Zaw09 .

gmw_00003115.name: Q 2207+0122
gmw_00003115.overview: Q 2207+0122 is a low-metallicity dwarf galaxy [arXiv:0806.3470]. Although its optical spectrum displays emission lines, it is classified as a star-forming emission-line galaxy rather than an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:0806.3470].

sources.summary: The paper identifies Q 2207+0122 as an emission-line galaxy rather than an AGN, with line ratios in the star-forming region and low dwarf-galaxy metallicity.

quotes.quote: Two of the eleven (NGC 7591 and Q 2207+0122), shown as a triangle and an asterisk, fall below the Kauffmann2003 empirical star formation line and are not AGNs.

Q 2207+0122 (PC 2207+0122) : Although the optical spectrum for Q 2207+0122 (courtesy of D. Schneider) shows emission lines, Q 2207+0122 is just an emission line galaxy and not an AGN (confirmed by D. Schneider, private communications). The log( [ OIII ] /H ) and log( [ NII ] /H ) line ratios (courtesy of J. Moustakas), 0.815 and -1.581 respectively, place in the star formation region Kauffmann2003 . This galaxy is a low metallicity dwarf galaxy (J. Moustakas, private communication).

gmw_00003116.name: WKK 4374
gmw_00003116.overview: WKK 4374 is classified as a narrow-line Seyfert 2 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:0806.3470]. This galaxy correlates spatially with a previously unidentified ultra-high-energy cosmic ray [arXiv:0806.3470].

sources.summary: The paper identifies WKK 4374 as a narrow-line Seyfert 2 correlated with an Auger cosmic ray, giving [OIII] flux, luminosity, bolometric luminosity, and Eddington ratio.

quotes.quote: Only two have published optical line fluxes, WKK 4374 (IGR J14515-5542) and NGC 7626. The former, WKK 4374, is a narrow line Seyfert (S2) with L_ bol = 1.9 10^ 44 erg s^ 1 and _ bol = 0.46 ; intriguingly, it correlates with a previously uncorrelated UHECR (Auger year, day = 2007, 186; E=64 EeV).
The new optically identified AGN, WKK 4374, increases by one the number of UHECR-AGN correlations, because it is within 2.8 ^ of a previously uncorrelated UHECR.

WKK 4374 (IGR J14515-5542) : From optical spectra of INTEGRAL sources, Masetti2006 determined WKK 4374 to be a Seyfert 2 galaxy. They report an absorption-corrected [ OIII ] flux of 6.4 1.6 10^ 14 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 . This gives an [ OIII ] luminosity of 5.4 10^ 40 erg s ^ 1 and implies a bolometric luminosity of 1.9 10^ 44 , within the range found for the correlated AGNs from VCV; _ bol = 0.46 .

gmw_00003117.name: NGC 2907
gmw_00003117.overview: NGC 2907 is an active galactic nucleus candidate that correlates with an ultra-high-energy cosmic ray [arXiv:0806.3470]. Optical spectra of the galaxy reveal narrow LINER-like emission, though published emission line fluxes are currently unavailable [arXiv:0806.3470].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 2907 as an additional AGN candidate correlated with a cosmic ray and reports narrow LINER-like optical emission but no published line fluxes.

quotes.quote: Two of the UHECRs whose VCV-correlated galaxies were found here to not be identifiable AGNs, (2006, 185; 83 EeV) and (2007, 84; 64 EeV), correlate with the new ``MSPC" source candidates NGC 7626 and NGC 2907 respectively, although a spectrum is needed to determine whether the latter meets optical AGN criteria.

Furthermore, two of the additional AGNs, NGC 7626 and NGC 2907, correlate with two CRs, (2006, 185; E=83 EeV) and (2007, 84; E=64 EeV), respectively, whose VCV counterparts were shown in this paper to be non-nuclear dominated emission line galaxies.

NGC 5140 and NGC 2907 : These two galaxies were determined to have narrow LINER-like emission by Mauch2007 from visual inspections of the 6dFGS optical spectra. No emission line fluxes or ratios have been published.

gmw_00003118.name: NGC 5064
gmw_00003118.overview: NGC 5064 is classified as a LINER galaxy [arXiv:0806.3470]. Its low-ionization nuclear emission-line region is determined to be nuclear powered rather than shock powered [arXiv:0806.3470].

sources.summary: The paper uses published line ratios to identify NGC 5064 as a nuclear-powered LINER rather than a shock-powered source.

quotes.quote: NGC 5064 : Bonatto1989 report a [ NII ] /H value of 1.051 and [ SII ] /H value of 0.461. No [ OII ] , H , or [ OI ] line fluxes were reported. This indicates that NGC 5064 is a LINER. The authors determine this LINER to be nuclear powered rather than shock powered.

gmw_00003119.name: IRAS 13028-4909
gmw_00003119.overview: IRAS 13028-4909 is an active galactic nucleus selected from X-ray and infrared observations [arXiv:0806.3470]. The galaxy has a reported [NII]/H ratio of 1.28, though no other line fluxes or ratios have been provided [arXiv:0806.3470].

sources.summary: The paper identifies IRAS 13028-4909 as an X-ray and infrared selected AGN and reports an [NII]/H ratio, while noting no other line ratios were given.

quotes.quote: IRAS 13028-4909 : This galaxy is from a set of X-ray and IR selected AGNs Kirhakos1990 . An [ NII ] /H ratio of 1.28 was reported. No other line fluxes or ratios were given.

gmw_00003120.name: AM 1754-634 NED03
gmw_00003120.overview: AM 1754-634 NED03 is a galaxy characterized by a bright optical nucleus [arXiv:0806.3470]. However, its published optical spectrum casts doubt on the strength of its nuclear activity [arXiv:0806.3470].

sources.summary: The paper notes AM 1754-634 NED03 has a bright optical nucleus, but its published optical spectrum casts doubt on the strength of its nuclear activity.

quotes.quote: AM 1754-634 NED03 : Shows a bright optical nucleus. However Fairall1981 reports that the 5007 and 4954 [ OIII ] lines were of equal strength (instead of in a 3:1 ratio), H was barely detected, and no other emission lines were detected. This casts doubt on the strength of its nuclear activity based on optical data. Data from other wavelengths has not been published.

gmw_00003121.name: NGC 4406B
gmw_00003121.overview: NGC 4406B is a dwarf elliptical galaxy located in the Virgo Cluster [arXiv:2008.01737]. It coincides with an offset globular cluster overdensity near M86, with which it is believed to be interacting [arXiv:2008.01737].

sources.summary: The paper links dwarf elliptical NGC 4406B to the offset globular-cluster overdensity near M86 and notes that tidal stripping could perturb the M86 cluster system.

quotes.quote: It is notable that the dwarf galaxy NGC 4406B (VCC 882) coincides with this over-density. To estimate the number of GCs NGC 4406B might be contributing, we used the data gathered from a survey of dwarf elliptical galaxies by miller07 .

Considering this, it is reasonable to assume that NGC 4406B contributes, at least in part, to the over-density seen in Feature A. We compared the color distribution of GC candidates within this over-density to that of the GC candidates in the surrounding area. If NGC 4406B is contributing a significant number of clusters to this over-density, we might expect the color distribution of the GC candidates within the over-density to be shifted towards slightly bluer colors compared to the GC candidates in the surrounding area.

First, we find the surface density peak of the M86 GC system is offset from the system's center by approximately 0.7 . This over-density coincides with the dwarf elliptical galaxy, NGC 4406B. Although the GC system of NGC 4406B has not been studied, dwarf elliptical galaxies with similar luminosities can host enough GCs to account, at least partially, for this over-density. Moreover, elmegreen00 has found evidence for an interaction between M86 and NGC 4406B, so a perturbation within the M86 GC system caused by NGC 4406B is within the realm of possibility.

gmw_00003122.name: J1044+0353
gmw_00003122.overview: J1044+0353 is a low-mass, extremely metal-poor starburst dwarf galaxy [arXiv:2308.00351, arXiv:2312.08432] characterized by young stellar populations [arXiv:2109.06725] and intense ultraviolet emission lines [arXiv:1905.06434, arXiv:2111.11508]. It features a complex structure composed of multiple blue and white clumps [arXiv:2206.04709], a density-bounded ionization cone [arXiv:2403.11390], and an edge-on galactic outflow [arXiv:2308.00351].

sources.summary: The paper studies J1044+0353 as a low-mass, metal-poor local analog, mapping its starburst history, metallicity structure, velocity gradient, and edge-on outflow with KCWI spectroscopy.

quotes.quote: J1044+0353 is considered a local analog of the young galaxies that ionized the intergalactic medium at high-redshift due to its low mass, low metallicity, high specific star formation rate, and strong high-ionization emission lines. We use integral field spectroscopy to trace the propagation of the starburst across this small galaxy using Balmer emission- and absorption-line equivalent widths and find a post-starburst population ( 15-20 Myr) roughly one kpc east of the much younger, compact starburst ( 3-4 Myr). Using the direct electron temperature method to map the O/H abundance ratio, we find similar metallicity (1 to 3 sigma) between the starburst and post-starburst regions but with a significant dispersion of about 0.3 dex within the latter.

Over scales several times the size of the galaxy, we discover a velocity gradient parallel to the galaxy's minor axis. The steepest gradients ( 30 \ km \ s^ 1 \ kpc^ 1 ) appear to emanate from the oldest stellar association. We identify the velocity gradient as an outflow viewed edge-on based on the increased line width and skew in a biconical region. We discuss how this outflow and the gas inflow necessary to trigger the starburst affect the chemical evolution of J1044+0353.
Similar to most local analogs, J1044+0353 has an exceptionally high [ O III ] 5007 equivalent width ( 1400 ) and a compact, UV-bright starburst.

sources.summary: The paper compares models to J104457, reproducing strong observed luminosities, reporting observed line ratios, and deriving metallicity and ionization parameters close to prior fits.

quotes.quote: Using a slightly different approach, umeda2022 appeal to Markov Chain Monte Carlo techniques to select the combination of a blackbody and power-law ionizing spectra and gas parameters providing the best fits to the emission-line properties of three extremely metal-poor galaxies with strong emission (included in the sample of Section sec:res_sample ): J1631+4426, J104457 and I Zw 18 NW. umeda2022 then show that, for each galaxy, the best-fitting combination of blackbody+power-law ionizing spectra can be approached by a combination of the accretion-disc model spectrum of gierlinski2009 and the spectrum of a single-star SSP with an age between about 2 and 8 Myr (we note that the emission-line properties actually produced by these spectra are not compared to the observations in their paper). The /SFR ratios of these models are 5.0 10^ 40 erg s ^ 1 / yr ^ 1 (J1631+4426), 1.0 10^ 41 erg s ^ 1 / yr ^ 1 (J104457) and 3 10^ 41 erg s ^ 1 / yr ^ 1 (I Zw 18 NW), reaching again up to 11 above the mean observed value in the douna2015 sample of metal-poor star-forming galaxies.
