Remarkably, among the restricted library of models at hand, those shown in Fig. fig:umeda_fit_lines appear to provide reasonable fits to all but one emission line. In particular, the models reproduce the strong observed luminosities of these galaxies, corresponding to / ratios of 0.023 (J1631+4426), 0.018 (J104457) and 0.034 (I Zw 18 NW), i.e., totally out of reach of previous stellar population synthesis models (Fig. fig:diags_oldmodels b). The only line that the limited set of models considered here does not seem to naturally reproduce is , whose observed luminosities in two of the three galaxies are higher than predicted by the best-fitting models, especially in the case of J1631+4426. The observed / ratios are 0.048 (J1631+4426), 0.032 (J104457) and 0.036 (I Zw 18 NW).

For reference, the /SFR ratios of the best-fitting models in Fig. fig:umeda_fit_lines , computed by estimating the SFR from the 1500- \ luminosity by analogy with see footnote foot:umedasfr umeda2022 , span the range 3.6--7.2 10^ 40 erg s ^ 1 / yr ^ 1 , compatible with the observations of lehmer2019 and douna2015 . The best-fitting metallicities and volume-averaged ionization parameters are (0.0005, -2.0) , (0.001, -1.4) and (0.0005, -1.7) for J1631+4426, J104457 and I Zw 18 NW, respectively, close to the values (0.0004, -1.6) , (0.001, -2.0) and (0.0005, -1.7) derived by umeda2022 using blackbody+power-law ionizing spectra. For reference, the best-fitting and values are (0.09,0.1) , (0.44,0.3) and (0.09,0.1) , respectively.

sources.summary: The paper presents J104457 as a nearby extreme UV emission-line galaxy with strong C IV and He II emission, low metallicity, high specific star formation, and ionizing-photon output relevant to reionization analogs.

quotes.quote: Here we present recent high-resolution Hubble Space Telescope spectra of two nearby extreme UV emission-line galaxies, J104457 and J141851. We report the first observations of intense nebular He 2 and double-peaked, resonantly-scattered C 4 emission, a combination that suggests these galaxies both produce and transmit a significant number of very high-energy ionizing photons ( E > 47.89 eV) through relatively low column densities of high-ionization gas. This suggests that, in addition to photons at the H-ionizing edge, the very hard ionizing photons that escape from these galaxies may provide a secondary source of ionization that is currently unconstrained observationally.

Of these, J104457 and J141851, have the largest reported UV C 4 EW , hereafter, B16 berg16 and He 2 EW , hereafter, B19 berg19a , respectively, amongst local star-forming galaxies. Note that the C 4 and He 2 EWs reported in Table 1 are from the higher-resolution spectra presented here and are lower than (but within the errors of) the EWs originally reported in berg16 and berg19a . =-2 Here we present high-resolution HST /COS G160M spectra of two extreme UV emission-line galaxies, J104457 and J141851. These galaxies are shown in Figure 1 and their properties, many of which liken them to reionization-era systems, are listed in Table tbl1 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies J104457 as the strongest local nebular C IV emitter in the sample, with very low stellar and gas metallicity and unusually strong emission relative to similar galaxies.

quotes.quote: In particular, we selected our sample to include the two highest equivalent width nebular emitters in this doublet J104457 at 11 and HS 1442+4250 at 4 ; bergCarbonOxygenAbundances2016,senchynaExtremelyMetalpoorGalaxies2019 ; two of the systems with the highest gas-phase metallicities still powering nebular emission in C 4 SB 2 and 82, at 12+ O/H 7.8 - 7.9 ; senchynaUltravioletSpectraExtreme2017 ; and two metal-poor systems with extremely high equivalent width optical line emission yet relatively modest C 4 equivalent widths <4 J082555 and J120202; bergCarbonOxygenAbundances2016,bergChemicalEvolutionCarbon2019 . These systems were targeted with G160M/1533 spectroscopy with allocations of 5--10 orbits each over the course of two programs (GO:15646 and GO:15881, PI: Stark) which we describe in Section sec:uvobs . The basic properties of this sample of emitters are summarized in Table tab:basicprop , and described in the remainder of this section.

In particular, consider the strongest nebular emitter in our sample and known locally among star-forming galaxies at all, J104457. This galaxy is clearly more metal-poor in both stars (at Z 3.1 ) and gas (at 12+ O/H =7.5 ) than the next-strongest emitter in our sample, HS 1442+4250 ( Z 2.9 , 12+ O/H =7.7 ). This is consistent with this picture in which a harder stellar ionizing radiation field (and potentially hotter gas temperature) serve to boost this emission at lower metallicities. However, the other two lowest-O/H galaxies in our sample J082555 and J120202 present nearly identical O/H and H equivalent widths to J104457 (Table tab:basicprop ), yet power at only 2.1 and 1.5 ; 5 times smaller than J104457.

sources.summary: The paper studies J104457 as a nearby extreme emission-line galaxy with young low-metallicity stars, very strong C IV emission, compact UV light, and ionization requiring an added hot component.

quotes.quote: We use HST /COS and LBT/MODS spectra to study two nearby EELGs, J104457 and J141851. The FUV spectra indicate that these two galaxies contain stellar populations with ages \ 10 Myr and metallicities 0.15 Z _ . We use photoionization modeling to compare emission lines from models of single-age bursts of star-formation to observed emission lines and find that the single-age bursts do not reproduce high-ionization lines including [ O 3 ] or very-high ionization lines like He 2 or [ O 4 ] .
The first of these galaxies is J104457 which has the largest C 4 1548,1550 \ EW (-6.71 \ ,-2.83 \ ) measured in the local universe berg16 .
However, the spatial profile of J104457 is somewhat larger (FWHM =0.7 ), resulting in a degradation of the spectral resolution.

sources.summary: The paper resolves J1044+0353 into blue clumps and an EMPG tail, finding star formation concentrated in the giant clump and redshifted blue clumps with high dispersion.

quotes.quote: 5 J1044+0353 : The deep HSC image shows that J1044+0353 is composed of a western giant blue clump (thick cyan arrow) , an eastern small blue clump (thin cyan arrow) , and 3 white clumps between the 2 blue clumps (white arrow) Using the 300B grism with a wide wavelength coverage, we detect [ O iii ] 4959,5007 lines at z=0.27 from the southern red object (orange arrow) . This means that the red object is a background galaxy and thus not related to J1044+0353. . We regard the 3 white clumps as the EMPG tail. The H map indicates that the major star formation occurs in the giant blue clump. We obtain similar velocity and velocity dispersion maps to Moiseev2010 's result , while we show more clearly that the small blue clump emits H . We find that the 2 blue clumps are redshifted by 20 km s ^ 1 with respect to the EMPG tail, which indicate that the 2 blue clumps and the EMPG tail are not in the same kinematic structure .

sources.summary: The paper uses lower-resolution COS coadds and MUSE spectra for J1044+0353, noting CIII] blending limits plus broad and double-peaked UV emission profiles.

quotes.quote: For the galaxies J1044+0353 and J1418+2102, instead of the MR co-added spectra, we used the so-called Low Resolution (LR: G130M + G160M + G140L or G130M + G160M + G185M + G225M + G140L; R 1500-4000 ) coadds, with a nominal point source resolution of 80.3 m /pixel or 0.498 /resel.

MUSE spectra are also available for the galaxies J0021+0052 (PI: Göran Östlin), J1044+0353 and J1418+2102 (PI: Dawn Erb). We used these data to retrieve emission line-ratios involving faint auroral lines, if undetected ( S/N<3 ) in SDSS spectra. Finally, for the galaxies J0808+3948, J0944-0038, J1148+2546, J1323-0132 and J1545+0808, Multi-Object Double Spectographs (MODS) data from the LBT telescope, presented in arellano-cordova22 , are also available ( Paper V arellano-cordova22 hereafter). Information on each of the optical datasets is provided in the following paragraphs. Concerning the IFU data available for J0021+0052, J0036-3333, J0127-0619, J0337-0502, J0405-3648, J0934+5514, J1044+0353 and J1418+2102, we extracted a spectrum from a 2.5" aperture centred at the same coordinates of COS observations to match the COS aperture (see also berg22 and Paper V arellano-cordova22 ).

However, after a visual inspection we did notice a clear broad profile in the emission lines of J1044+0353 and J1418+2102 (see also berg21a ), J1016+3754, J0337-0502, J1323-0132 and J1545+0858. For these objects we fitted the 1640 and 1661,6 with two components. We tested a two-Gaussian component fitting also on the \ doublet, without finding a significant improvement in our results. This is due to the very small wavelength separation of the \ doublet lines, which results in degenerate line centroids that make it difficult to use multiple components. Interestingly, for J0337-0502, J1044+0353, J1418+2102, J1323-0132 (see Fig. fig:uvfitcarbon ) we also observed a doubled-peak profile in the doublet.

sources.summary: The paper maps J1044+0353 as a rapidly growing, low-metallicity galaxy where feedback, an ionization cone, superbubble features, and photoionized shells shape LyC escape.

quotes.quote: We present integral field spectroscopy toward and around J1044+0353, a rapidly growing, low-metallicity galaxy which produces extreme \ line emission. A new map of the O32 flux ratio reveals a density-bounded ionization cone emerging from the starburst. The interaction of the hydrogen ionizing radiation, produced by the very young starburst, with a cavity previously carved out by a galactic outflow, whose apex lies well outside the starburst region, determines the pathway for global Lyman continuum (LyC) escape. In the region within a few hundred parsecs of the young starburst, we demonstrate that superbubble breakthrough and blowout contribute distinct components to the \ line profile, broad and very-broad emission line wings, respectively. We draw attention to the large \ luminosity of the broad component and argue that this emission comes from photoionized, superbubble shells rather than a galactic wind as is often assumed.

sources.summary: The paper keeps J104457 in its analysis despite a low-S/N [NeV]/[NeIII] exception, while noting it among galaxies with available X-ray constraints.

quotes.quote: Figure match_diagram indicates that the best-fit models reproduce almost all the observed emission lines within the 3 sigma levels with an exception of the [ Ne v ] / [ Ne iii ] of J104457. This exception is probably because J104457 have low S/N ( 1 ) for [ Ne v ] / [ Ne iii ] compared to other galaxies (S/N 3). Hereafter, we conduct the same analysis for J104457 as other galaxies just for the presentation purpose.
We find X-ray observational resutls for Tol 1214, SBS 0335-052E, and J104457 in the litearture and Chandra Data Archive.

gmw_00003123.name: J1418+2102
gmw_00003123.overview: J1418+2102 is a nearby extreme ultraviolet emission-line galaxy [arXiv:1905.06434, arXiv:2109.06725] characterized by a compact ultraviolet extent [arXiv:2109.06725] and double-peaked emission profiles [arXiv:1905.06434, arXiv:2209.09047]. It hosts a young, low-metallicity stellar population [arXiv:2109.06725] that drives intense star formation [arXiv:1905.06434] and produces record-strength local helium emission [arXiv:2109.06725].

sources.summary: The paper presents J141851 as a nearby extreme UV emission-line galaxy with strong He II and C IV, low metallicity, high specific star formation, and ionizing-photon production comparable to reionization-era systems.

quotes.quote: Here we present recent high-resolution Hubble Space Telescope spectra of two nearby extreme UV emission-line galaxies, J104457 and J141851. We report the first observations of intense nebular He 2 and double-peaked, resonantly-scattered C 4 emission, a combination that suggests these galaxies both produce and transmit a significant number of very high-energy ionizing photons ( E > 47.89 eV) through relatively low column densities of high-ionization gas. This suggests that, in addition to photons at the H-ionizing edge, the very hard ionizing photons that escape from these galaxies may provide a secondary source of ionization that is currently unconstrained observationally.

Of these, J104457 and J141851, have the largest reported UV C 4 EW , hereafter, B16 berg16 and He 2 EW , hereafter, B19 berg19a , respectively, amongst local star-forming galaxies. Note that the C 4 and He 2 EWs reported in Table 1 are from the higher-resolution spectra presented here and are lower than (but within the errors of) the EWs originally reported in berg16 and berg19a . =-2 Here we present high-resolution HST /COS G160M spectra of two extreme UV emission-line galaxies, J104457 and J141851. These galaxies are shown in Figure 1 and their properties, many of which liken them to reionization-era systems, are listed in Table tbl1 .

sources.summary: The paper studies J141851 as a nearby extreme emission-line galaxy with young low-metallicity stars, record local He II emission, compact UV extent, and difficult-to-model ionization.

quotes.quote: We use HST /COS and LBT/MODS spectra to study two nearby EELGs, J104457 and J141851. The FUV spectra indicate that these two galaxies contain stellar populations with ages \ 10 Myr and metallicities 0.15 Z _ . We use photoionization modeling to compare emission lines from models of single-age bursts of star-formation to observed emission lines and find that the single-age bursts do not reproduce high-ionization lines including [ O 3 ] or very-high ionization lines like He 2 or [ O 4 ] .
The second galaxy is J141851 which has the largest He 2 1640 \ EW (-2.82 \ ) measured in the local universe berg19a .
J141851 has less extended emission and has a fitted FWHM =0.12 .

sources.summary: The paper uses lower-resolution COS coadds and MUSE spectra for J1418+2102, noting CIII] blending limits plus broad and double-peaked UV emission profiles.

quotes.quote: For the galaxies J1044+0353 and J1418+2102, instead of the MR co-added spectra, we used the so-called Low Resolution (LR: G130M + G160M + G140L or G130M + G160M + G185M + G225M + G140L; R 1500-4000 ) coadds, with a nominal point source resolution of 80.3 m /pixel or 0.498 /resel.

Information on each of the optical datasets is provided in the following paragraphs. Concerning the IFU data available for J0021+0052, J0036-3333, J0127-0619, J0337-0502, J0405-3648, J0934+5514, J1044+0353 and J1418+2102, we extracted a spectrum from a 2.5" aperture centred at the same coordinates of COS observations to match the COS aperture (see also berg22 and Paper V arellano-cordova22 ).

However, after a visual inspection we did notice a clear broad profile in the emission lines of J1044+0353 and J1418+2102 (see also berg21a ), J1016+3754, J0337-0502, J1323-0132 and J1545+0858. For these objects we fitted the 1640 and 1661,6 with two components. We tested a two-Gaussian component fitting also on the \ doublet, without finding a significant improvement in our results. This is due to the very small wavelength separation of the \ doublet lines, which results in degenerate line centroids that make it difficult to use multiple components. Interestingly, for J0337-0502, J1044+0353, J1418+2102, J1323-0132 (see Fig. fig:uvfitcarbon ) we also observed a doubled-peak profile in the doublet.

gmw_00003124.name: J1253-0312
gmw_00003124.overview: J1253-0312 is a blueberry galaxy that exhibits broad emission line features associated with star-forming outflows [arXiv:2405.03391]. It is unique among similar galaxies for its detectable high-frequency radio emission [arXiv:2405.03391] and displays rare N IV] doublet emission lines [arXiv:2209.09047].

sources.summary: The paper treats BB 22 as an unusual Blueberry galaxy: previous AGN classifications may reflect star-formation outflows, yet it alone occupies the AGN diagnostic region and has radio detections.

quotes.quote: Blueberries BB 22 and BB 7 were reported as AGN galaxies by . These sources were most likely classified as AGN because they show slightly broader emission lines. However, visual inspection of their spectra shows that both permitted and forbidden emission lines show broad features which is a characteristic of outflows related to star-forming activity (e.g., supernovae, stellar winds, superbubbles) rather than an AGN.

BB 22 is the only source located in the AGN region of the diagram while the rest of BBs are clearly below the relation. In order to further examine the classification of the proposed BBs, we performed an archival search of their radio footprints. While the majority of the sources were not observed by LOFAR DR2 FIRST,LoTSS-DR2 , which covers part (27 ) of the northern sky in low 120--168 MHz frequencies, it successfully detected BBs 7, 8, 9, 21, 27, and 33 but BB 22 was not observed.

Among all BBs identified by this work, BB 22 is the only source with detectable radio emission in the radio surveys NRAO VLA Sky Survey , Very Large Array Sky Survey VLASS; VLASS , and the Rapid ASKAP Continuum Survey RACS; RACS-low that operate in higher radio frequencies. All other BBs were not detected or were not observed from the aforementioned radio surveys even though some should have been detected based on the general SFR--radio relation.

sources.summary: The paper notes saturated and clipped optical lines in J1253-0312, uses a special fitting range, and reports N IV] detections plus broader He II emission.

quotes.quote: We did not fix the 4959,5007 fluxes because in the KCWI data of J0934+5514 and SDSS data of J1253-0312 the 5007 line is saturated. For these objects we obtained an estimate of the 5007 by applying the fixed line ratio of 3 with respect to 4959 osterbrock89 . We notice that for J1253-0312 also the \ line is clipped, so we discarded its flux.

To understand if the addition of a second component is significant, we calculated the reduced chi-square ^2 of the single and double-component fits in the rest-frame wavelength range 6540--6590 \ covering \ (4950--5010 \ for J1253-0312 and J0934+5514, for which \ is unavailable), and chose the model with more components only if the ^2 was at least 0.1 dex smaller.

1483,87 is observed with S/N>3 in only 6 CLASSY galaxies (both doublet lines are observed only in J1044+0353, J1253-0312 and J1545+0858). This doublet has rarely been seen in emission in star-forming galaxies fosbury03,raiter10,vanzella10,stark14 . These lines are probably due to young stellar populations, and, if the source is not hosting an AGN, they could be a signature of massive and hot stars with an associated nebular emission vanzella10 . This doublet is also a density diagnostic keenan95 , and it traces higher-ionization regions with respect to the \ and \ doublets (see Sec. sec:results-ne and Sec. sec:discussion-ne ). 1640 is detected with S/N>3 in 19 CLASSY galaxies and generally shows a narrow profile (median velocity dispersion of 55 km/s), indicating its nebular nature. However, in the spectra of J0942+3547, J1129+2034, J1200+1343, J1253-0312 and J1314+3452 the line profile looks broader (with up to 200 km/s), which suggests the presence of a stellar component residual despite the removal of the C B best-fit stellar continuum (see e.g., nanayakkara19,senchyna21a ).

gmw_00003125.name: J1323-0132
gmw_00003125.overview: J1323-0132 is an emission line galaxy [arXiv:2209.09047]. Spectroscopic observations of the galaxy reveal a clear broad profile in its ultraviolet emission lines, as well as a double-peaked doublet profile [arXiv:2209.09047].

sources.summary: The paper uses MODS data for J1323-0132 and reports broad UV emission profiles plus a double-peaked doublet profile.

quotes.quote: Finally, for the galaxies J0808+3948, J0944-0038, J1148+2546, J1323-0132 and J1545+0808, Multi-Object Double Spectographs (MODS) data from the LBT telescope, presented in arellano-cordova22 , are also available ( Paper V arellano-cordova22 hereafter).
For the galaxies J0808+3948, J0944-0038, J1148+2546, J1323-0132 and J1545+0808, instead of SDSS, we took advantage of the MODS data from LBT obtained using the G400L and G670L.

However, after a visual inspection we did notice a clear broad profile in the emission lines of J1044+0353 and J1418+2102 (see also berg21a ), J1016+3754, J0337-0502, J1323-0132 and J1545+0858. For these objects we fitted the 1640 and 1661,6 with two components. We tested a two-Gaussian component fitting also on the \ doublet, without finding a significant improvement in our results. This is due to the very small wavelength separation of the \ doublet lines, which results in degenerate line centroids that make it difficult to use multiple components. Interestingly, for J0337-0502, J1044+0353, J1418+2102, J1323-0132 (see Fig. fig:uvfitcarbon ) we also observed a doubled-peak profile in the doublet.

gmw_00003126.name: J1545+0858
gmw_00003126.overview: J1545+0858 is an emission-line galaxy that exhibits a clear broad profile in its emission lines [arXiv:2209.09047]. The galaxy displays both N IV] doublet lines and shows a large offset between its ultraviolet and optical electron densities [arXiv:2209.09047].

sources.summary: The paper reports broad UV emission in J1545+0858, detects both N IV] doublet lines, and finds a larger offset between UV and optical electron densities.

quotes.quote: However, after a visual inspection we did notice a clear broad profile in the emission lines of J1044+0353 and J1418+2102 (see also berg21a ), J1016+3754, J0337-0502, J1323-0132 and J1545+0858.
1483,87 is observed with S/N>3 in only 6 CLASSY galaxies (both doublet lines are observed only in J1044+0353, J1253-0312 and J1545+0858).

We also find a consistent offset between n_e ( ) and the density values from 1883/ 1892 for the three galaxies for which it is available (i.e., J1044+0353, J1253-0312 and J1448-0110) and an even larger offset for the two galaxies with \ densities (i.e., J1253-0312 and J1545+0858).

gmw_00003127.name: PKS B1424-418
gmw_00003127.overview: PKS B1424-418 is a highly variable flat-spectrum radio quasar located at a redshift of z = 1.522 [arXiv:1602.02012, arXiv:2109.08110]. Its optical-UV spectrum shows accretion-disk emission [arXiv:1807.02085], and its major multiwavelength outbursts have been temporally and positionally associated with a PeV-energy IceCube neutrino event [arXiv:1602.02012, arXiv:2204.12242].

sources.summary: The paper uses Mg II data for J1427.3 4204, models its time-averaged FSRQ SED, and notes optical-UV accretion-disk emission in this source.

quotes.quote: We take the Mg ii line luminosity of J0456.9 2323 and J1427.3 4204 from 1993A&AS..100..395S and 1989A&AS...80..103S respectively. By following the empirical relation and line coefficients of 2012ApJ...748...49S , we derive both L_ disk and M_ BH . In particular, L_ disk is computed following the scaling relations of 1991ApJ...373..465F and 1997MNRAS.286..415C and assuming that the BLR reprocesses 10 of L_ disk .

We generate steady-state, i.e. time-averaged, broadband SEDs of all 8 FSRQs following the details outlined in Section sec3 and reproduce them using both leptonic and lepto-hadronic emission scenarios. The leptonic model SEDs are presented in Figure fig_leptonic_sed and the associated SED parameters are given in Table tab:leptonic_sed_param . The results of the lepto-hadronic SED modeling are shown in Figure fig_hadronic_sed and we provide the physical parameters derived from the modeling in Table tab:hadronic_sed_param .
The leptonic modeling reasonably reproduces most of the observed SEDs of 2FHL FSRQs. The accretion disk emission is observed at optical-UV frequencies in J1224.7+2124, J1427.3 4204, and J1512.7 0906.

sources.summary: The review describes PKS 1424-418 as an FSRQ aligned with IceCube IC-35, with a contemporaneous multiwavelength flare and modeling favoring mostly leptonic emission.

quotes.quote: Its arrival direction, although with large uncertainty, coincided with the FSRQ PKS 1424-418 at a redshift of z = 1.522 . Its arrival time coincided with the rising phase of a multi-wavelength flare, including HE rays, X-rays and radio, although Swift-XRT observations only commenced several days after the alert (see Fig. Fig:PKS1424_XRT_LAT ). In particular, found that the radio outburst contemporaneous with the neutrino alert was dominated by an increase of the flux of the radio core, possibly marking the ejection of a new jet component. A phenomenological, calorimetric estimate of the expected neutrino flux by found a very small probability of an IceCube neutrino detection during multi-wavelength flares of this source.

More detailed physical modeling of the SED and neutrino production through photo-hadronic processes was performed by . They demonstrate that neither a photo-pion dominated nor a proton-synchrotron dominated lepto-hadronic model can simultaneously explain the SED and the PeV neutrino event. Instead, a model with primary-electron SSC dominated ray emission and only a subdominant hadronic contribution to the SED can successfully fit the SED and predict up to 0.3 neutrino events detected by IceCube during the outburst phase.

sources.summary: The paper identifies PKS B1424 418 as a flaring FSRQ whose 2012 gamma-ray outburst coincided with IceCube event IC 35 and was energetic enough to plausibly explain the PeV neutrino.

quotes.quote: The dominant blazar in the field is PKS B1424 418 at redshift z=1.522 and classified as an FSRQ. Due to its relatively low ray flux in the first three months of the Fermi mission in 2008, it was not included in the Fermi /LAT Bright Source List . The source showed two ray flares in 2009-2011 and is listed as a bright ray source in all subsequent Fermi /LAT catalogs. Still, Padovani Resconi (2014) discarded it from their list of most-probable counterparts for the 2 PeV IceCube neutrino due to its relatively low ray emission in the 2008-2011 period. In summer 2012, PKS B1424 418 commenced a dramatic rise in ray brightness .

Here, we report that a major outburst of the blazar PKS B1424 418 occurred in temporal and positional coincidence with the third PeV-energy neutrino event (IC 35) detected by IceCube. Based on an analysis of the full sample of ray blazars in the IC 35 field and assuming a photo-hadronic emission model, we show that the long-term average ray emission of blazars as a class is in agreement with both the measured all-sky flux of PeV neutrinos and the spectral slope of the IceCube signal. The outburst of PKS B1424 418 has provided an energy output high enough to explain the observed PeV event, indicative of a direct physical association.

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 1424-418 as the farthest and second-faintest sample blazar, highly variable, with the strongest optical/gamma-ray correlation and a possible optical QPO near the gamma-ray timescale.

quotes.quote: Of the 12 sources, the farthest and the nearest sources are PKS 1424-418 and Mrk 421 located at the corresponding red-shifts of 1.522 and 0.03, respectively. Similarly, the brightest source in the sample is 3C 273 which has a mean magnitude 12.80, and the faintest source AO 0235+164, with a mean magnitude 18.19 and standard deviation of 0.87 magnitude, is highly variable. The second faintest source, PKS 1424-418, with a mean magnitude of 17.15 is also the most variable source as indicated by its standard deviation of magnitudes 1.01.

Of all the sources, FSRQ PKS 1424-418 shows the highest value (0.86) of correlation coefficient with a large significance of 99.90 , followed by BL Lac AO 0235+164 0.68 with the significance of 99.40 and FSRQ CTA 102 0.62 with 99.70 significance.

PKS 1424-418: A ray QPO with 353 d characteristic timescale was reported for the first time in Bhatta2020 . The LSP analysis of the optical observations of the source also revealed a peak at 326 d, which is is coincident with the ray QPO within the range of the associated uncertainty. However, the significance of the possible QPO turns out to be below 90 against the power law noise of the spectral index 1.75.

sources.summary: The paper reports a visually found repeated pattern in J1427.9 4206, connects its first repetition to an IceCube neutrino/outburst, and notes no reported VLBI data.

quotes.quote: Two events, the third repetition of the pattern in J0457.0 2324 and the first repetition in J1427.9 4206, were missed by the automated search and were detected during the visual inspection of the light curves of these sources. For instance, in the case of J1427.9 4206, the overall significance is p- value = 1.8 10^ 7 ( 5.2 ).

A possible association of this particle with J1427.9 4206 (PKS B1424 418) is discussed by . It arrived at MJD = 56265.1 during a major gamma-ray outburst, which is found to be the first repetition of the pattern found in the light curve of this blazar.

For J1427.9-4206, we could not find any VLBI data reported in the literature. For instance, the patterns in J0108.6+0134 and J1427.9-4206 appear to be a superposition of smooth, long-term and strong, sharp flares.

sources.summary: The paper models PKS B1424-418 as a flat-spectrum radio quasar whose burst may coincide with an IceCube PeV neutrino, finding leptonic emission dominates and the temporal correlation is weak.

quotes.quote: An interesting candidate source for this test is the flat-spectrum radio quasar PKS B1424-418, which recently called attention of a potential correlation between an IceCube PeV-neutrino event and its burst phase. We simulate both the multi-waveband photon and the neutrino emission from this source using a self-consistent radiation model. We demonstrate that a simple hadronic model cannot adequately describe the spectral energy distribution for this source, but a lepto-hadronic model with sub-dominant hadronic component can reproduce the multi-waveband photon spectrum observed during various activity phases of the blazar. As a conclusion, up to about 0.3 neutrino events may coincide with the burst, which implies that the leptonic contribution dominates in the relevant energy band. We also demonstrate that the time-wise correlation between the neutrino event and burst phase is weak.

sources.summary: The paper uses PKS B1424-418 as the comparison case of a flaring blazar temporally and positionally coincident with a PeV neutrino event.

quotes.quote: Recently, 2016arXiv160202012K report that a PeV cascade-like neutrino event occurs in positional and temporal coincidence with a giant gamma-ray flare of the blazar PKS B1424-418. Recently 2016arXiv160202012K find that a cascade-like PeV neutrino event occurs in positional and temporal coincidence with a giant gamma-ray flare of the flat spectrum radio quasars (FSRQs) PKS B1424-418, with a chance probability of 5 for such coincidence (i.e., a 2 confidence level correlation). We note that the gamma-ray flare of PKS B1424-418 is extremely strong and lasts for more than one year.

For comparison, the Fermi LAT data analysis for the gamma-ray flaring blazar PKS B1424-418 in a similar time period (centering at the detection time of the neutrino event number 35 ) is also carried out and the result is presented in Table lcdata and Figure lc8Tfig . For the same period of time, PKS B1424-418 shows bright emission with detection significance of TS =57 , and the photon flux is 3.54 0.99 10^ 7 ph cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 (i.e. the corresponding energy flux is 4.05 1.14 10^ 10 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 in 0.2-100 GeV).

sources.summary: The paper finds compact PKS 1424-41 flare regions, inconclusive spectral and cooling tests, and high-energy photon evidence just incompatible with BLR-origin emission.

quotes.quote: PKS 1424-41 The observed variability timescales of the January 2013 flare from PKS 1424-41 indicate a gamma-ray emission region of size (0.77 0.24) 10^ 13 m. The April 2013 flare from this source was found to have a larger emission region of size (2.76 0.66) 10^ 13 m. However, in neither flare is one spectral model favoured over another, and a lack of statistics at high energies made our study of energy dependent cooling inconclusive. Monte Carlo simulations show that the most energetic photon observed with the Fermi LAT, having energy 140.5 10.7 GeV and observed on MJD 56970.42 outside the flare periods we studied, is compatible with a cut-off energy of 12.3 1.2 GeV. This is just incompatible with the energy cut-off expected due to BLR emission of 9.920 0.001 GeV.

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS B1424-418 as a blazar whose outburst coincided with a PeV neutrino, with gamma-ray flux locally suppressed at the event time.

quotes.quote: PKS B1424-418. A temporal and directional coincidence was found between the outburst of blazar PKS B1424-418 and a PeV-energy neutrino event (HESE 35, dubbed as "Big Bird") . In the first panel of Fig. fig:fourpiece we show the gamma-ray light curve of PKS B1424-418 around the time of the neutrino detection . We see that, while there is a longer outburst, at the actual time of the neutrino there is a sudden suppression in the flux of the blazar, which then quickly rises again afterwards. Therefore, we find that the neutrino detection coincided with a local minimum.

sources.summary: The paper says PKS 1424-418 has prior year-scale oscillation claims but its wavelet analysis instead finds a shorter 90-day high-significance QPO candidate.

quotes.quote: PKS 1424-418: and claimed a flux oscillation at period 353 d and 355 d respectively in the light curve of this FSRQ. The wavelet analysis, however, shows that this QPO candidate is compatible with noise, with a post-trial significance at 1 for both monthly and weekly binned light curves. On the other hand, we detect a much shorter QPO candidate centered at 94 25 d ( 90 22 d) in the CWT of the monthly (weekly) binned light curve, at above 5 significance. This month-long QPO candidate emerged from MJD 56100 to MJD 56500 approximately (June 2012 to August 2013), showing five complete oscillations in the fitted light curve.

gmw_00003128.name: BCG of RXCJ1504.1-0248
gmw_00003128.overview: The brightest cluster galaxy of RXCJ1504.1-0248 is an elliptical LINER galaxy [arXiv:1204.3162] that exhibits a complex, disturbed molecular gas morphology [arXiv:1807.04331]. Star formation is concentrated in its core and along a blue, line-emitting filamentary nebula [arXiv:1003.4511, arXiv:1803.03625].

sources.summary: The paper describes SDSS J150407.51-024816.5 as an elliptical LINER central cluster galaxy with two compact radio components, a flat eastern nucleus, and a western component likely associated with a jet.

quotes.quote: SDSS J150407.51-024816.5 (4): This elliptical LINER galaxy is the central galaxy of the compact X-ray luminous galaxy cluster RXC J1504-0248 with one of the most massive nearby cooling cores 2005ApJ...633..148B . The black hole mass, estimated from the [ SII ] line width cf., 2007ApJ...667L..33K , of about 3 10^7 M_ , is somewhat less than those estimated for the brightest cluster central galaxies 10^ 8-9 M_ , e.g., 2006ApJ...652..216R,2004ApJ...612..797F . In our MERLIN 18 cm images we detected two sources (4E [ ast ] and 4W [ est ] ) at the optical position of the galaxy (Fig. fig:maps_evn ).

The eastern component has a relatively flat 18-6-3.5 cm spectrum. The western component is steeper, but flattens towards shorter wavelengths. The flat nuclear spectrum might be the result of a set of closely spaced homogeneous synchrotron components or optically thin bremsstrahlung in a disk wind cf., blundell_origin_2007 . The SDSS image of the host galaxy is regular and shows no signs of a recent merger. Together with the steep spectral index, the western component does not appear to be a companion nucleus, but rather a jet component.

sources.summary: The paper presents ALMA CO observations of the RXCJ1504.1-0248 brightest cluster galaxy, describing dense molecular gas, disturbed morphology, extreme cooling, and links to AGN feedback.

quotes.quote: We present recent ALMA observations of the CO(1-0) and CO(3-2) emission lines in the brightest cluster galaxy of RXCJ1504.1 0248, which is one of the most extreme cool core clusters known. The central galaxy contains 1.9 10^ 10 M_ of molecular gas. The molecular gas morphology is complex and disturbed, showing no evidence for a rotationally-supported structure in equilibrium. 80 of the gas is situated within the central 5 kpc of the galactic center, while the remaining gas is located in a 20 kpc long filament. The cold gas has likely condensed out of the hot atmosphere.

RXCJ1504 has one of the most extreme cooling cores ever discovered. Its hot atmosphere is condensing at a rate of 80 Ogrean10 , which can supply its 1.9 10 of molecular gas in 2.4 8 . This is well-matched to both the central cooling time, 2.3 8 , and the timescale to be consumed by star formation, 1.4 8 (see Section sec:SF ).

sources.summary: The paper analyzes the brightest cluster galaxy in RXC J1504-0248, focusing on its emission-line filaments and arguing old stars are not the major ionization source while shocks contribute.

quotes.quote: This cluster presents a prominent filamentary structure associated to its BCG, located mainly along its major axis and aligned with the X-ray emission. A combined study of three emission line diagnostic diagrams has shown that the filament emission falls in the so-called transition region of these diagrams. Consequently, several ionizing sources should be playing an meaningful role. We have argued that old stars, often invoked to explain LINER emission, should not be the major source of ionization. We have noticed that most of the filamentary emission has line ratios consistent with the shock excitation limits obtained from shock models.

These numbers allow us to obtain a crude estimate of the expected H luminosity due to shocks, 10^ 42 erg s ^ 1 , which corresponds to 10 of the observed H luminosity, 2.12 10^ 43 erg s ^ 1 . This estimate is strongly dependent of the shock parameters, but our result nevertheless suggests that indeed part of the filament emission can be due to shocks.

sources.summary: The paper describes the BCG as a blue, line-emitting central galaxy whose core and 42 kpc filament host most star formation, surrounded by an exceptionally luminous nebula needing additional heating.

quotes.quote: Although there are several galaxies within 100 kpc of the cluster core, only the brightest cluster galaxy (BCG), which lies at the peak of the X-ray emission, has blue colours and strong line-emission. Approximately 80 \ of intracluster gas is cooling below X-ray emitting temperatures, similar to the observed UV star formation rate of 140 . Most star formation occurs in the core of the BCG and in a 42 kpc long filament of blue continuum, line emission, and X-ray emission, that extends southwest of the galaxy. The surrounding filamentary nebula is the most luminous around any observed BCG. The number of ionizing stars in the BCG is barely sufficient to ionize and heat the nebula, and the line ratios indicate an additional heat source is needed.

sources.summary: The paper finds RXC J1504 may be better fitted by continuous star formation, prefers supersolar old-population metallicity, and has a single-burst best-fit model.

quotes.quote: Similar investigations for RXC J1504 (YSPs with 1 Myr separation although with a limited number of bursts) showed that it might be better-fitted by a continuous star formation model with 1 .
Our results show that RXC J1504, on the contrary, seems to clearly prefer a supersolar metallicity for the OSP.
The best-fit model for RXC J1504 comprises a single YSP burst.

gmw_00003129.name: NGC 2369
gmw_00003129.overview: NGC 2369 is an isolated, highly inclined barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:2104.06790, arXiv:1811.11386] classified as a luminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:1011.3435, arXiv:1811.11386]. Its structure features a bright, clumpy nucleus [arXiv:2104.06790, arXiv:2304.00515], two spiral arms that wind into an outer ring-like structure [arXiv:2304.00515, arXiv:1811.11386], and an ultraluminous X-ray source candidate located to the north [arXiv:1011.3435].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2369 as a point-like X-ray LIRG with a northern ULX candidate detected in UV and fit by an absorbed power-law spectrum.

quotes.quote: Some of them are dominated by the nuclear emission and appear as point like sources at the XMM-Newton spatial resolution (5 arcsec), e.g. NGC 2369 (Figure fig1 top panels) whereas others are more extended, e.g. IC 5179 (Figure fig1 bottom panels). The extended X-ray emission of IC 5179 is accompanied by extended UV emission. In these two galaxies there are extranuclear X-ray bright sources that might be ultraluminous X-ray sources (ULXs). The ULX candidate in NGC 2369 is located 25 arcsec ( 5 kpc) to the north of the nucleus, the candidate in IC 5179 is located 19 arcsec ( 4 kpc) to the south-west of the nucleus. Both sources also appear clearly detected in the UV images (Figure fig1 middle panels) but not in the 3.6 m images (Figure fig1 right panels).

We extracted the spectrum of the NGC 2369 ULX candidate. An absorbed power law model reproduces well the observed spectrum ( ^2 dof =1.02 ) and implies an intrinsic X-ray luminosity ( L^ int _ 0.5-8 keV = 3.1 0.4 10^ 40 erg s ^ 1 ) one order of magnitude higher than the ULX luminosity threshold ( L^ int _ 0.5-8 keV > 10^ 39 erg s ^ 1 ) and comparable to the luminosities measured for other ULXs (Swartz et al. 2004).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2369 as nucleus-dominated in the mid-infrared, with extended molecular hydrogen along the disk, a southern region in line-ratio maps, and a relatively deep nuclear silicate feature.

quotes.quote: NGC 2369 The MIR emission of NGC 2369 is dominated by the resolved nucleus. The molecular hydrogen ( 1 and 2 ) emission appears more extended along the galaxy disk than the \ emission. This causes a relatively large 1 \ ratio in the regions around the nucleus. The \ ratio map reveals an \ region 17 arcsec ( 3.4 kpc projected) away from the nucleus to the south. The nuclear silicate strength, S_ Si 0.9, is slightly larger than the average silicate strength of our LIRGs sample, probably because this galaxy is seen almost edge-on.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2369 as a highly inclined barred galaxy with clumpy nuclear structure, extinction, structured velocity residuals, and double-component gas kinematics explained by spiral arms.

quotes.quote: NGC 2369 : This highly inclined SB(s)a galaxy presents a complex morphology with bright clumps dominating the nuclear regions along with faint arms at larger scales. Similarly to IRAS F17138-1017, this galaxy shows aligned ( 170 ) star-forming regions at its inner-most regions that complicate the measurement of the photometric parameters. Moreover, NGC 2369 also presents an intense extinction filament along the star-forming regions. During the kinematic analysis, the rotating-disc modelling of the velocity maps revealed highly structured residuals (i.e. >40 km s ^ 1 ). A double-Gaussian profile fit of the Br line (see Sect. subsec:gas_kin ) was performed to shed some light on the kinematics of this target.

sources.summary: The paper says NGC 2369 has a bright nucleus, outer ring-like spiral arms, HI near the inner nucleus, and HI following outer arms despite high inclination.

quotes.quote: NGC 2369: From the composite optical image shown in Fig. fig:mosaic3 , it is not clear if this galaxy has bar, however it does show a bright nucleus. Two spiral arms wind around to almost for an outer ring-like structure. Due to the high degree of inclination it is hard to comment on the exact nature of the 1 distribution in this galaxy, but we do observe an 1 ring co-located at the outskirts of the inner nucleus. The 1 gas is also observed to trace the outer spiral arms, but due to the near edge-on view, it is hard to say if the 1 gas traces this into a ring-like structure.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2369 as an isolated barred luminous infrared galaxy with two prominent arms and no diffuse atomic gas, recent tidal interaction, or ram-pressure stripping.

quotes.quote: NGC 2369 is a Luminous Infrared Galaxy (LIG) and classified as a SB(s)a. The optical image of the galaxy shows the presence of a bar and two prominent arms. It is an isolated galaxy with the nearest group (LDCE 0505) at a projected distance of 0.7 Mpc. We find no diffuse and extended 1 gas surrounding this galaxy thereby ruling out any recent tidal interactions, and the low densities of the IGM will not support ram pressure stripping.

gmw_00003130.name: IC 5179
gmw_00003130.overview: IC 5179 is a face-on spiral starburst galaxy [arXiv:2104.06790, arXiv:1004.1364] that exhibits extended X-ray, ultraviolet, and polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbon emission [arXiv:1011.3435, arXiv:1004.1364]. Its complex gas and stellar kinematics are accompanied by a bright nucleus that lacks active galactic nucleus activity [arXiv:1207.3186, arXiv:2104.06790].

sources.summary: The paper uses an XMM-Newton observation of IC 5179 to estimate its 2-10 keV flux and finds its X-ray-to-optical ratio far below the AGN range.

quotes.quote: We also analyzed the XMM-Newton observation of IC 5179. Using the source imaging PPS processing provided by XMM, we took the count rate on the EPIC PN-CCD camera (0.5-12 keV) and used webPIMMS to estimate the corresponding 2-10 keV flux of the source.

The X-ray to optical ratios are far below what is expected from AGNs, log(f_x/f_R) > -1 , for ten of the ambiguous or indeterminate galaxies: the nine we observed with Chandra and IC 5179, for which we used an XMM-Newton observation. This holds even when the larger of the two X-ray flux determinations is used to calculate the X-ray to optical flux ratios.

The accuracies of the X-ray flux determinations are limited by shot noise, which at these low counts produces relative uncertainties of order one. Since our measure of nuclear activity depends on log_ 10 (f_x) , this corresponds to an uncertainty of less than 0.3 in log(f_x/f_R) . The observed ratios are far from the AGN domain, so our conclusion is robust even with these large uncertainties. We conclude that none of these galaxies have active nuclei.

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 5179 as a starburst spiral with bright nucleus and arms, centre offsets from arm asymmetry, inner pseudo-bulge-like velocity curve, and complex gas and stellar kinematics.

quotes.quote: IC 5179 : This SA(rs)bc galaxy, classified as starburst Veilleux+95 , presents intense H II regions distributed along its spiral arms Alonso-Herrero+06 . The photometric analysis displayed a bright elongated nucleus of 0.2 kpc and two bright inner arms. The bright difference between the SW and NE arms induced an offset of almost 4.5 at apertures of 6 kpc in the centre determination (see Fig. fig:morph_5179 ). The velocity curve shows a steeper profile in the innermost regions (i.e. <0.3 kpc) which is compatible with the presence of a pseudo-bulge.

The gas kinematics maps see PiquerasLopez+12 of this object show a complex structure in all the phases, which is also observed in the stellar kinematics in this work (panels `a' and `', Fig. fig:kin_2369 ). These structures could not be explained by the motions expected in a rotating disc or an outflow. Indeed, during the DiskFit modelling, these structures could not be reproduced by adding a bar-like velocity component, and its impact can be observed in the highly structured residuals (panels `i' and `k'), and in the difference between the modelled and observed velocity curves (panels `j' and `l').

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 5179 as an extended X-ray and UV LIRG with a southwest ULX candidate, though its candidate spectrum could not be extracted.

quotes.quote: Some of them are dominated by the nuclear emission and appear as point like sources at the XMM-Newton spatial resolution (5 arcsec), e.g. NGC 2369 (Figure fig1 top panels) whereas others are more extended, e.g. IC 5179 (Figure fig1 bottom panels). The extended X-ray emission of IC 5179 is accompanied by extended UV emission. In these two galaxies there are extranuclear X-ray bright sources that might be ultraluminous X-ray sources (ULXs). The ULX candidate in NGC 2369 is located 25 arcsec ( 5 kpc) to the north of the nucleus, the candidate in IC 5179 is located 19 arcsec ( 4 kpc) to the south-west of the nucleus. Both sources also appear clearly detected in the UV images (Figure fig1 middle panels) but not in the 3.6 m images (Figure fig1 right panels).
We could not extract the spectrum of the ULX candidate in IC 5179 due to the lower number of counts of this source and the underlying extended X-ray emission.

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 5179 as a face-on spiral with numerous unresolved regions, extended PAH and atomic-line emission across 8 kpc, and continuum concentrated toward the nucleus.

quotes.quote: IC 5179 This is a face-on spiral galaxy classified as like from optical spectroscopy, and it shows numerous \ regions distributed in the spiral arms AAH06s . These \ regions are not resolved in the \ maps. They appear as a relatively uniform extended emission (PAH features and atomic lines) over 8 kpc. The continuum emission is slightly more concentrated towards the nucleus. The patterns in the ratio maps are possibly related to the intricate distribution of the \ regions.

gmw_00003131.name: Mrk 359
gmw_00003131.overview: Mrk 359 is a nearby narrow-line Seyfert 1 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2006.09005, arXiv:2308.08528] powered by a low-mass central black hole [arXiv:astro-ph/0309242, arXiv:2308.08528]. The galaxy exhibits high and low X-ray flux states [arXiv:2006.09005], a soft-excess below 2 keV [arXiv:2308.08528], and a very weak X-ray absorber [arXiv:0808.3904].

sources.summary: The paper models Mrk 359 from UV to hard X-rays with XMM-Newton/NuSTAR data, finds high and low flux states, and favors a two-corona explanation for its spectrum.

quotes.quote: In this work, we present the timing and spectral properties of five simultaneous XMM-Newton and NuSTAR observations of the Narrow Line Seyfert 1 galaxy Mrk 359. We aim to provide the first broadband spectral modeling of Mrk 359 describing its emission spectrum from the UV up to the hard X-rays. We performed temporal and spectral data analysis, characterising the amplitude and spectral changes of the Mrk 359 time series and computing the 2-10 keV normalised excess variance.

The spectral broadband modelling assumes the standard hot Comptonising corona and reflection component, while for the soft excess we tested two different models: a warm, optically thick Comptonising corona (the two-corona model) and a reflection model in which the soft-excess is the result of a blurred reflected continuum and line emission (the reflection model). High and low flux states were observed during the campaign. The former state has a softer spectral shape, while the latter shows a harder one.
The present analysis suggests the two-corona model as the best scenario for the optical-UV to X-ray emission spectrum of Mrk 359.

sources.summary: The paper treats Mrk 359 as a Narrow-line Seyfert 1 benchmark, correcting its optical flux, comparing its X-ray slope with Ton S180, and applying the model to it as a low-mass AGN.

quotes.quote: As examples, we include in the same plot the broad-band spectrum of quasar PG1211+143 (Janiuk et al. 2001) and two Narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxies - Ton S180 ( et al. 2003) and Mrk 359 (Mathur et al. 2001; O'Brien et al. 2001). The bolometric luminosity corresponding to those spectra is 1.0 10^ 46 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 , 9.0 10^ 45 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 , 2.5 10^ 45 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 and 9.7 10^ 43 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 , assuming a Hubble constant equal to 50 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 . The optical flux for Mrk 359 has been corrected for starlight contribution (factor 2.5) on the basis of the ratio of the observed value of the H_ flux in the starlight-free source Ton S180 (46 ) and Mrk 359 (18 ) (V eron-Cetty et al. 2001). The sources covering two orders of magnitude in bolometric luminosity, and therefore possibly two orders of magnitude in mass, show nevertheless a surprisingly similar broad-band shape. The description of the X-ray spectra of Ton S180 and Mrk 359 may seem different (the X-ray photon index of Ton S180 is given as 2.44, Turner et al. 2001 and of Mrk 359 as 1.85, O'Brien et al. 2001).

We now apply this model, without any specific adjustments of the parameters, both to the low mass objects (Mrk 359, M = 10^6 M_ ) and to the high mass composite spectrum ( M = 10^8 M_ ). The result is shown in Fig.  fig:best , together with the data points representative for the low and the high mass objects.

sources.summary: The paper describes Mrk 359 as a local active galaxy in its bare-AGN sample and analyzes XMM-Newton/Swift X-ray spectra, including continuum, soft-excess, and spectral-fitting behavior.

quotes.quote: Mrk 359 is a nearby (0.0174) narrow line Seyfert 1 AGN where the width of the broad emission line was reported to be smaller than 2000 km/s Elvis1992 . From the X-ray data analysis, OBrien2001 first reported the presence of prominent soft-excess below 2 keV without any significant neutral/warm intrinsic absorption along the line of sight. Besides this, a neutral Fe line of equivalent width 200 eV was also reported via X-ray data analysis. From the X-ray variability study, Middei2020 estimated the mass of the central object of this source as 3.6 10^6 M _ . Mrk 359 have been observed with XMM-Newton and Swift /XRT multiple times between 2000 to 2019 (Table table:obs_log ).

sources.summary: The paper identifies Mrk 359 as the lone outlier in the sample, with [O III] velocity dispersion lower than stellar dispersion, possibly due to gas in a cold rotating disk.

quotes.quote: In fact all targets except one (Mrk 359) occupy the upper half of the _*- _ [ O III ] plane, and are not grouped around the 1:1 separation line. I n other words, this is the first indication that using _ O III in place of _* could be wrong in NLS1s, which seem to have a < _*> 84 14 . In this picture, Mrk 359 is an outlier. It is the only case among our data having _ [ O III ] < _* . A justification was given by JB00 , who suggested that such cases could be caused by gas settled in a cold rotating disc.

sources.summary: The paper compares Markarian 359 to Arakelian 564 as an NLS1 with a very weak X-ray absorber, weak iron UTA evidence, and narrow emission lines.

quotes.quote: Markarian 359 is a NLS1 which, like Arakelian 564, has a very weak X-ray absorber. obrien01 detect evidence for a weak Iron M-shell UTA, but no other significant absorption features. Also identified in the RGS spectrum, are three narrow emission lines at 13.6, 22.0 and 18.9 , corresponding to the Ne IX and O VII triplets and the O VIII Ly emission line. The signal-to-noise of the spectrum is such that accurate velocity values cannot be well constrained.

sources.summary: The paper notes subtle H-profile polarization differences for Mrk 359, wavelength-dependent polarization in V and R bands, and failure of the adopted model to recover spin and angle.

quotes.quote: We also note that for a number of objects -- Mrk 957, Mrk 359 and Mrk 142 -- in the H profiles, we observe slight differences in polarization from the neighbouring continuum.
A similar dependence is observed in the spectrum of Mrk 359: P(V)=(3.4 0.4) , (V)=(125 2)^ and P(R)= (2.1 0.4) , (R)=(130 4)^ .
Note that for the object Mrk 359, the model used did not give a result for any of the values of the spin and angle.

gmw_00003132.name: NGC 5940
gmw_00003132.overview: NGC 5940 is an unobscured Seyfert 1 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2004.01273, arXiv:2205.11612] that exhibits a strong soft X-ray excess below 2 keV [arXiv:2205.11612]. The galaxy displays correlated continuum and line variability [arXiv:1304.4643], with its broad-line region showing a strong contribution from iron emission [arXiv:1809.05113, arXiv:1909.01395].

sources.summary: The paper reports that Mrk 1511 line shape and flux variability are well fit, while template-dependent profile differences broaden uncertainties in BLR structure.

quotes.quote: Mrk 1511 Both the \ emission-line shape and the variability in the integrated \ flux are very well fit by the models for . However, the posterior PDFs in Figure fig_post_mrk1511 indicate that the \ spectra produced using the three \ templates give somewhat different modeling results. The right panels of Figure fig:decomps show that there is a discrepancy between the three \ profiles that is on the order of the size of the flux uncertainty. This is likely a result of the strong \ contribution to the AGN spectrum, shown by the green line in the left panel. Since the \ template is made up of five individual components whose strengths are given by five free parameters, it has more flexibility to fit asymmetries in the \ emission than the other templates.

This can result in asymmetries in the resulting \ spectrum. Since the model results are still consistent with each other, we choose to combine them, the result of which is larger parameter uncertainties. The emitting BLR in \ has a radial profile that is between Gaussian and exponential ( = ) and is shifted from the central ionizing source by r_ min = \ light days.

sources.summary: The paper reports that most Mrk 1511 BLR parameters are not constrained, likely because Fe II residuals and host-flux assumptions affect the single-epoch spectrum.

quotes.quote: Mrk 1511 Our model is not able to constrain most of the parameters for Mrk 1511 (Fig. posterior_mrk1511 ). The only exception is = mrk1511_rmin light-days which is constrained and agrees within the uncertainties with what was found for the full light-curve. The model marginally constrains = mrk1511_opening degrees. This parameter is one of the few cases where the single-epoch estimate is not consistent within the uncertainties (68 confidence range) with what was found from the full light-curve modelling. Since we used the total (AGN + host galaxy) flux to calculate the mean time delay from the R L relation, we assume a mean value for the prior on _ mean which is significantly higher than what was found in the full light-curve modelling. This results in the time delay parameters ( _ mean , _ median , r_ mean and r_ median ) to be overestimated.

The inferred M _ BH from single-epoch modelling agrees with the full light-curve result. This indicates that independent information on the H line profile helps to constrain M _ BH beyond the approximate value suggested by the R L relation. As observed by , it is likely that Fe II has a strong contribution to the spectra of Mrk 1511. found that due to the strong Fe II contribution, different spectral decompositions resulted in different values for the BLR parameters. A residual Fe II contribution may be one of the reasons why the single-epoch spectrum of Mrk 1511 does not provide meaningful constraints.

sources.summary: The paper compares NGC 5940 with Arecibo and EDD measurements, finding most values consistent and attributing a W20 offset to lower resolution.

quotes.quote: NGC 5940: All previous measurements for NGC 5940 originate from observations with the Arecibo telescope, including a reprocessed measurement for inclusion in the EDD. The measurements conducted by contain consistent W _ 50 and V _ R values with our values, however their W _ 20 value of 240 km s ^ 1 is slightly higher than ours of 204.5 ^ +5.9 _ 6.5 km s ^ 1 . The lower resolution of their spectrum ( 8.2 km s ^ 1 compared to ours of 1.3 km s ^ 1 ) contributes to some of the discrepancy between the measurements, because we measure W _ 20 =214 km s ^ 1 when we smooth our spectrum to match their resolution. However, we expect that the lower S/N in their spectrum also contributes to the difference in W _ 20 . The remaining measurements from , , , , and are consistent with our measurements within the uncertainties.

sources.summary: The paper reports Fe reverberation in Mrk 1511, measuring correlated continuum and line variability, five-to-seven-day Fe lags, and an Fe-to-Hbeta size ratio of about 1.5.

quotes.quote: Here we report the detection of \ reverberation in two Seyfert 1 galaxies, NGC 4593 and Mrk 1511, based on data from a program carried out at Lick Observatory in Spring 2011.

For spectral decompositions done using the \ template of veron2004 , we find ( )/ ( ) = 1.9 0.6 in NGC 4593 and 1.5 0.3 in Mrk 1511. The detection of highly correlated variations between \ and continuum emission demonstrates that the \ emission in these galaxies originates in photoionized gas, located predominantly in the outer portion of the broad-line region. galaxies: active --- galaxies: individual (NGC 4593, Mrk 1511) --- galaxies: nuclei Blends of \ emission lines are often among the most prominent broad emission features in the ultraviolet (UV) and optical spectra of broad-lined active galactic nuclei (AGNs), and the integrated flux of \ emission in quasars can be greater than that of any other single emission line, including Ly wills1985 .
The \ lags measured relative to the V band light curves are in the range of 3 -4 days for NGC 4593 and 5 -7 days for Mrk 1511.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC5940 with a fixed photon index plus soft-X-ray mekal component and classifies the source as an unobscured AGN.

quotes.quote: Therefore, for 2MASXJ08384815+0407340, IC-588, ESO439-G009, and NGC5940 we fixed photon index to =1.7 or =1.8 .
For IC-588, ESO439-G009 and NGC5940 we used the additional mekal model Mewe1985 to model the soft X-rays below 1 keV.
As a result of our analysis we classify 2MASXJ08384815+0407340, IC-588, 2MASXJ16510578-0129258, MCG+00-58-028 as obscured AGN and ESO439-G009, NGC5940, ESO450-16, CGCG081-001 as unobscured AGN.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 5940 as a Seyfert 1 AGN with strong soft X-ray excess in XMM-Newton/Swift data and favors thermal Comptonisation for that excess.

quotes.quote: NGC 5940 is a Seyfert 1 AGN which reveals strong, prominent soft X-ray excess below 2 keV as seen in both its XMM-Newton and Swift observations. Model fit to the data revealed that this feature could be equally well explained by the ionised partial covering, the thermal Comptonisation and the blurred reflection models. Although the other models cannot be decisively ruled out with the data at hand, the lack of significant broad iron K_ as well as any significant emission/absorption line features in the reflection grating spectrometer (RGS) data tend to favour the thermal Comptonisation origin for the soft X-ray excess in NGC 5940 .

gmw_00003133.name: W2 1925+50
gmw_00003133.overview: W2 1925+50 is a Seyfert galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.067 [arXiv:2405.02584]. It hosts an active galactic nucleus with a supermassive black hole estimated at approximately 10^8.04 solar masses [arXiv:2405.02584].

sources.summary: The paper uses W2 1925+50 as a supermassive Seyfert comparison with a high-mass black hole, stitched years-long light curve, long baseline, global SF breaks, and fitted slopes.

quotes.quote: Among them, the Seyfert galaxy Zw 229-15 (at z = 0.0275 and with M_ BH 10^7 M_ ; Barth2011ApJ...732..121B ) has been observed in four quarters, within which changes from 1.48 to 1.65 ; the Seyfert galaxy W2R 1904+37 (at z = 0.089 with M_ BH 10^ 7.66 M_ ; Smith2018ApJ...857..141S ) has been observed in three quarters, within which changes from 1.30 to 1.38 ; the Seyfert galaxy W2 1925+50 (at z = 0.067 with M_ BH 10^ 8.04 M_ ; Smith2018ApJ...857..141S ) has been observed in three quarters, within which changes from 1.37 to 1.48 ; and the Seyfert galaxy W2R 1858+48 has been observed in two quarters, within which transitioned from 1.44 to 1.49 . Except the last one, the other three AGNs, i.e., Zw 229-15, W2R 1904+37, and W2 1925+50, have years-long light curves stitched by The light curves of W2R 1858+48 can not be stitched according to .

In the top panels of Figures fig:sf_dis to fig:sf_dis_more_5548 , we show the observed SFs (crosses) for Zw 229-15, W2 1925+50, W2R 1904+37, and NGC 5548, respectively. For the two AGNs with the longest baselines, i.e., Zw 229-15 over 3.3 yrs and W2 1925+50 over 2.8 yrs, their SFs intuitively have at least two breaks near 10^ 5-6 s and 10^ 6-7 s, indicating that a single could be not enough for describing their global SFs. Using a single power-law (SPL) function fitting to the SF in three distinct timescale ranges, we find that with increasing timescales changes from 1.8 , through 1.3 , to 0.7 for both Zw 229-15 and W2 1925+50 (see Table tab:smbh for the selected timescale ranges and the corresponding best-fit ). Note that owing to the significance of the noise contribution within the smallest timescale range, a noise term has been included in the fit, that is, the nominated _n + SPL fit (cf. the dotted curves in Figures fig:sf_dis and fig:sf_dis_more_kepler2 for Zw 229-15 and W2 1925+50, respectively).

sources.summary: The paper flags KIC 12158940 as the main uncertain CBV-correction case, with altered light curves and a possible but visually downweighted characteristic timescale.

quotes.quote: The major concern is with KIC 12158940. The nearest star is too far away to be reliably used as a calibrator, and in any case does not vary coincidentally with the source, and the light curves are significantly different before and after CBV application both in terms of characteristic timescale and high-frequency slope. It is possible that there is a characteristic timescale in this object's high-frequency variations, and perhaps it would be a coincidence for this to match reasonably well with the mass and the expected orbital timescale of this object (see Figure  fig:mbhtchar and Figure  fig:tcharrkep ), but it is possible that this is not a real break. Accordingly, a hollow symbol is used in plots involving _ char   so that it can be discarded visually if desired.

gmw_00003134.name: W2R 1904+37
gmw_00003134.overview: W2R 1904+37 is a Seyfert galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.089 [arXiv:2405.02584]. The active galactic nucleus exhibits rapid short-term variability [arXiv:1803.06436] and has an estimated black hole mass of approximately 10^7.66 solar masses [arXiv:2405.02584].

sources.summary: The paper discusses W2R 1904+37 as a supermassive Seyfert comparison whose observed quarters, stitched light curve, SF fit, large break timescale, and inferred underestimated black-hole mass affect the scaling analysis.

quotes.quote: Among them, the Seyfert galaxy Zw 229-15 (at z = 0.0275 and with M_ BH 10^7 M_ ; Barth2011ApJ...732..121B ) has been observed in four quarters, within which changes from 1.48 to 1.65 ; the Seyfert galaxy W2R 1904+37 (at z = 0.089 with M_ BH 10^ 7.66 M_ ; Smith2018ApJ...857..141S ) has been observed in three quarters, within which changes from 1.30 to 1.38 ; the Seyfert galaxy W2 1925+50 (at z = 0.067 with M_ BH 10^ 8.04 M_ ; Smith2018ApJ...857..141S ) has been observed in three quarters, within which changes from 1.37 to 1.48 ; and the Seyfert galaxy W2R 1858+48 has been observed in two quarters, within which transitioned from 1.44 to 1.49 . Except the last one, the other three AGNs, i.e., Zw 229-15, W2R 1904+37, and W2 1925+50, have years-long light curves stitched by The light curves of W2R 1858+48 can not be stitched according to .

Similarly, we have performed the _n + SPL fit to the observed SFs of the other two AGNs at the supermassive scale, i.e., W2R 1904+37 (Figure fig:sf_dis_more_kepler3 ) and NGC 5548 (Figure fig:sf_dis_more_5548 ). However, likely owing to their relatively short baselines, only a single short-term slope for each of them can be determined.

Note that the reported BH mass of W2R 1904+37 is similar to that of NGC 5548 and smaller than that of W2 1925+50. However, the break timescale of the SF of W2R 1904+37 is clearly much larger than that of NGC 5548 and likely even larger than that of W2 1925+50. This suggests that the reported BH mass of W2R 1904+37 may have been underestimated.

sources.summary: The paper uses KIC 2694186 as a case where CBV processing removes clear systematics; despite rapid short-term variability and a steep high-frequency slope, it lacks a significant break.

quotes.quote: Because of the abundance of large figures elsewhere, the sizes are condensed, but the purpose of this figure is merely to indicate that sometimes there are massive, clear systematics in the pre-CBV light curves which are removed (e.g., KIC 2694186), sometimes there is behavior that may or may not be intrinsic that is inadvertently removed (e.g., 9215110), and sometimes the CBV treatment has almost no effect at all (e.g, KIC 8946433).
However, such comparisons allow us to see for certain that many trends that the CBV correction has removed (like the periodicity in KIC 2694186) are definitively systematic.

In KIC 2694186, there is rapid short-term variability and a steep high-frequency slope. The removal of a large amount of (spurious) power at low frequencies flattens the power spectrum, but the object does not pass our test for the significance of a power spectral break.

gmw_00003135.name: Mrk 478
gmw_00003135.overview: Mrk 478 is a bright, radio-quiet Type-1 quasar [arXiv:2105.13460] and narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy [arXiv:1909.01897, arXiv:0810.2272] hosted within a barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:2105.13460]. It harbors a compact starburst region concentrated within its central kiloparsec [arXiv:astro-ph/0302327, arXiv:1901.02158] and displays X-ray spectral variability [arXiv:1909.01897, arXiv:2209.08969].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes multi-epoch X-ray spectra of the NLS1 AGN Mrk 478, favoring blurred reflection variability from coronal flux changes and finding ionized iron emission.

quotes.quote: A multi-epoch X-ray spectral and variability analysis is conducted for the narrow-line Seyfert 1 (NLS1) active galactic nucleus (AGN) Mrk 478. All available X-ray data from \ and \ satellites, spanning from 2001 to 2017, are modelled with a variety of physical models including partial covering, soft-Comptonisation, and blurred reflection, to explain the observed spectral shape and variability over the 16 years. All models are a similar statistical fit to the data sets, though the analysis of the variability between data sets favours the blurred reflection model.

The X-ray nature of Mrk 478 The spectra of Mrk 478 at all epochs are similar, characterised by a strong, smooth soft excess and excess residuals in the 5-7 band, attributable to some form of iron emission. The variation between epochs is primarily due to normalisation changes, and little evidence for shape changes are found. The data also do not require any additional emission or absorption features produced in a warm absorber.
All models support the existence of a narrow 6.7 emission line, which is attributable to \ emission.

sources.summary: PG 1440+356 is a bright radio-quiet quasar in a barred spiral host with possible shells; NOEMA finds complex CO kinematics including a double-peaked disk and NE-SW extended emission.

quotes.quote: PG 1440+356 (Mrk 478) is a classic, bright Type-1 radio-quiet quasar schmidt83,meurs84,green86,miller93 . The spectral energy distribution of this source is extremely well observed at radio through X-ray wavelengths e.g., shang05,shang11 and is very far-infrared bright schweitzer06 . The host galaxy is a barred spiral with an elliptical disk veilleux09a and potentially also faint evidence for shells surace01 . A warm molecular outflow may be present based on OH  119  m seen in emission at 150   (whereas OH absorption would have constituted an unambiguous outflow detection) veilleux13b . Single-dish CO observations do not resolve any spatial structure at 4 \ resolution, but the \ line profile has z=0.078 and is obviously complex evans01 .

A visual inspection of the data cube shows complex velocity and spatial structure. There is a double-peaked line profile that is indicative of an emitting disk beckwith93 with the highest velocity components distributed approximately East-West, as seen in Figure  fig:smaps . In addition, the \ emission is spatially extended along the NE-SW direction (Figure  fig:cmaps at e.g., 75   ), with this emission corresponding to the most red and blueshifted gas in the spectral maps beyond the double-peaked line profile.

sources.summary: The paper finds 3.3 micron PAH emission in Mrk 478 and argues that its luminosity, molecular gas, and compact location imply a hidden vigorous starburst.

quotes.quote: The 3.3 m PAH feature is clearly present in the QSO Mrk 478 and in the two Seyfert 1 galaxies Mrk 766 and NGC 3227. For Mrk 478 and Mrk 766 it is the first time that the 3.3 m PAH emission is detected. The peak of the PAH feature as determined from the Gaussian fit was 3.29 m for Mrk 478 and 3.30 m for NGC 3227 and Mrk 766, in agreement with the values reported in the literature for starburst and Seyfert 2 galaxies ( mi ; ID00; IM02). The FWHM of the 3.3 m PAH emission are rather similar: 550 , 460 \ and 450 \ for Mrk 478, Mrk 766 and NGC 3227, respectively.

The L _ 3.3 /L _ IR ratio for this source reaches 0.56 10 ^ 3 , more than half of the typical value found for starburst dominated galaxies (L _ 3.3 /L _ IR 1 10 ^ 3 ; mo90 ). The above means that Mrk 478 harbors a powerful hidden compact starburst region, not detected by optical and/or UV spectroscopy. Mrk 478 is among the objects showing millimetre CO (1 0) emission. According to b4 results, this QSO shows one of the largest CO luminosities and a very high H _ 2 molecular mass (8.0 10 ^ 9 M ).

sources.summary: The paper presents XMM-Newton spectra of Mrk 478, finding a reflection-dominated NLS1 with hidden continuum emission, compact inner-disk source region, and constant RMS/hardness behavior.

quotes.quote: Multi-epoch XMM-Newton spectra of the two Narrow Line Seyfert 1 (NLS1), Mrk 478 and EXO 1346.2+2645, are presented. The data were fitted with different models, including the relativistically-blurred reflection model reflionx , which was found to give a good description of both spectra in all epochs. The two sources are reflection dominated with the illuminating continuum hidden from view. This can be explained either in terms of a corrugated disc or strong gravitational light bending effects.

The emission from these two sources comes from a very small region (few gravitational radii), very close to a rapidly spinning black hole. Spectral variability analysis show that both sources have constant RMS spectra, and constant hardness ratios in all epochs, in agreement with a single spectral component. The constancy the spectrum between high and low states rules out alternative models such as partial covering.

sources.summary: The paper treats Mrk 478 as ambiguous because several nearby velocity peaks could be blended with neighboring galaxies before assigning a likely dual-horn profile.

quotes.quote: Mrk 478: There are multiple emission peaks near the expected location of HI emission from Mrk 478 (see Fig. \ batmen2 ), which is between 22484 23700 km s ^ 1 richards2009,dv1991 from recessional velocities measured from optical emission lines. The systemic velocity of Mrk 478 is not well constrained, and we do not detect emission at or near the low end of its range of optical velocities. We fit independent Gaussians to the three peaks present in our spectrum to compare to the systemic velocities of galaxies in the NASA/IPAC Extragalactic Database (NED) in a 9 1 diameter neighbor search (equal to the GBT L-Band beam size). We measure the following velocities for each peak (from left to right): 23540 11, 23800 15, and 23980 4 km s ^ 1 .

From the present information, it is unclear whether Mrk 478 exhibits a dual-horned shape, and it is also unclear as to which of the peaks represent emission from Mrk 478. Based on the similarity of the center and right peak's shape with that of the measured shapes of low S/N dual-horned profiles, as seen with other objects in our sample (SBS1116+583A, Mrk 817, Mrk 290), we have assumed the center and right peaks belong to the emission profile of Mrk 478. For analysis with BusyFit , we fixed the slope of the right flank ( b_ 2 ) at 0.26, the half-width parameter ( w ) at 23, and the polynomial degree ( n ) at 3 in order to achieve a characteristic dual-horned fit.

sources.summary: The paper treats PG 1440+356 as an exceptional polarized, time-variable HI absorber whose redshifted feature may reflect underestimated systemic velocity or infalling HI.

quotes.quote: Time Variability The most dramatic examples of polarized spectra are seen in PG 1211+143 and PG 1440+356. Not only are these spectra polarized, but they are also time variable on timescales of 30 minutes (Figures fig:pg1211var and fig:pg1440var ).

PG 1440+356 The profile of the HI absorption feature depends slightly on the polarization. Exceptionally, the feature is redshifted by 300 km s ^ 1 with respect to systemic velocity rather than being blueshifted. A possible explanation is that the systemic velocity taken from NED and based on optical emission lines underestimates the actual systemic velocity because the line-emitting gas arises in part from outflowing material. Another possibility is that we may be detecting infalling HI.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes Mkn 478 as a reflection-dominated narrow-line Seyfert 1, finding correlated flux, spectral-index, and reflection-fraction behavior from hours to years using XMM-Newton, AstroSat, and Swift data.

quotes.quote: The X-ray spectrum of Mkn 478 is known to be dominated by a strong soft excess which can be described using relativistic blurred reflection. Using observations from XMM-Newton , AstroSat and Swift , we show that for the long-term ( years) and intermediate-term (days to months) variability, the reflection fraction is anti-correlated with the flux and spectral index, which implies that the variability is due to the hard X-ray producing corona moving closer to and further from the black hole. Using flux-resolved spectroscopy of the XMM-Newton data, we show that the reflection fraction has the same behaviour with flux and index on short time-scales of hours.
The multi-epoch XMM-Newton observations have revealed that the X-ray spectra of the NLS1 Mkn 478 is dominated by the blurred reflection component Zoghbi2008 .

We performed spectral analysis of Mkn 478 using XMM-Newton , AstroSat and Swift observations using a blurred relativistic reflection model and as was reported earlier Zoghbi2008,Waddell2019 , we find that the source is reflection dominated. We report that the reflection fraction inversely correlates with the flux and power-law index over time-scales of years and using flux-resolved spectroscopy for the XMM-Newton observations, we find that a similar correlation is obtained for time-scales of hours.

sources.summary: The paper detrends PG 1440 + 356 light curves before measuring a roughly 79-day lag, finds consistency with other lags, and rates it A.

quotes.quote: We had to detrend the light curves of PG 1440 + 356 before measuring the time lag since the light curve showed a monotonic increase in the light curve. Without detrending, the CCF values at any lag were higher than 0.4, rendering the lag and its uncertainty measurements unreliable. After detrending, we measured 79 68 29 days of time lag.
