This value is consistent with and lags. We note that both ICCF and are skewed toward higher lag values, and this is reflected in the measurements. We assess the lag of this object as rating A.

sources.summary: The paper identifies PG 1440+356 (MRK 478) as the strongest PAH-detected QSO in the high-resolution sample, with at least half of its PAH-derived SFR inside 0.7 kpc.

quotes.quote: PG 1440+356 (MRK 478) is the only QSO exhibiting strong 11.3 m PAH emission ( EW_ 11.3 m 0.04 m) allowing a clear detection of this feature in the CC/GTC spectrum with a S/N =8 .

Indeed, the emission of the PAH at 11.3 m is strong (S/N =8 ) in the high angular resolution spectrum of PG 1440+356 (MRK 478) on scales of 0.7 kpc. We estimate the inner-aperture (CC and VISIR) SFR of the 13 QSOs in our sample with high angular resolution spectra (see Table tab:_indv_pah ), the SFR of the stacked spectra of groups 1 and 2, and of the stack of all QSOs (see Table tab:SFR_inner ). Note that PG 1440+356 (MRK 478) is one of the objects in group 2. Removing this QSO from group 2 does not change our estimation of the SFR within the uncertainties. Using the same PAH and technique, we calculate the larger aperture SFRs from the IRS/ Spitzer spectrum of each QSO and from the stacked spectra of groups 1 and 2. Comparing the inner (few hundred pc) and larger aperture (few kpc) SFRs we find that they are similar (see right panel in Figure SFR_irs ). Therefore, it is likely that the star formation activity detected on larger scales is mainly concentrated within the central kpc. Additionally, we find that at least half of the SFR estimated from the IRS/Spitzer spectrum of PG 1440+356 (MRK 478) arises from an inner region of 0.7 kpc (see Figure ind_nuc and tab:SFR_inner ).

sources.summary: The paper singles out PG1440+356 as the brightest quasar in the sample and estimates its X-ray variability power spectrum, finding a rough 1/f law and flattening.

quotes.quote: We evaluated the power spectrum of PG1440+356 (the brigthest quasar in our sample) between 2 10^ 7 and 10^ 3 Hz, where it goes into the noise. The power spectrum is roughly consistent with a 1/f law between 10^ 3 and 2 10^ 6 Hz. Below this frequency it flattens significantly.

In particular for PG1440+356 the power spectrum roughly follows a 1/f law between 10^ 3 and 10^ 6 Hz, and below this frequency flattens significantly indicating a break in the power spectrum. The frequency of the break is however ill defined, because of the insufficient sampling on timescales between 1 week and several months.

sources.summary: The paper uses Mrk 478 as an observed AGN whose strong EUV/soft-excess continuum cannot be matched by a blackbody but can by thermal Comptonisation.

quotes.quote: For example, the SED for Mrk 478 may be 1-3 times brighter in L_ at E 120 than it is at 1300 ( 10 ) gondhalekar94, marshall96 . We find that a blackbody soft excess cannot satisfy such a condition even with a relatively high normalisation of = 3.0 (top panel, BbSeSpectraScurves ). We want to investigate if the alternative description of the soft excess component, namely the soft excess due to thermal Comptonisation, can account for a SED similar to that seen in Mrk 478, and to see what are its effect on the WA.
The continuum with _c = 2.0 satisfies the conditions required by observations of Mrk 478 as mentioned by korista97 .

sources.summary: The atlas treats Mrk 478 as an individually observed active or starburst galaxy, summarizing its prior classification and near-infrared continuum, emission-line, and absorption properties.

quotes.quote: Mrk 478 . Mrk 478 is an NLS1 with a steep soft X-ray spectrum gon94 . Imaging work of sur01 detected evidence of faint shells or arms attributed to its host galaxy. per88 reports Ca ii lines with FWHM of 2500 \ and H with an FWHM of 1250 . Later, gru04 measured an FWHM of 1630 \ on this same line. It is also a strong Fe ii emitter with optical Fe ii /H 0.97 gru04 . rudy01 report the 1 m Fe ii lines as well as emission from H i , He i , Ca ii and O i .

sources.summary: For PG 1440+356, the paper finds three methods broadly consistent despite scatter, with simultaneous SRM comparison favoring reliable ICCF-Cut broadband PRM results.

quotes.quote: For PG 1440+356, three methods give consistent results despite the large scatters of lag distributions. But by comparing our H lags with simultaneous SRM H 2021ApJS..253...20H , the high correlation coefficient of ICCF-Cut in Figure 13 panel (e) as well as the close-to-zero ICCF lag in panel (d) still make the results reliable. The large scatters of the JAVELIN and ^2 methods probably mean that the ICCF-Cut process is more suitable for the broadband PRM.

gmw_00003136.name: GOODSN06
gmw_00003136.overview: GOODSN06 is a high-redshift, dust-obscured ultraluminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:1312.0902]. It was identified as a marginal submillimeter galaxy, and its high infrared luminosity is primarily driven by star formation [arXiv:1312.0902].

sources.summary: GOODSN06 is a high-redshift dust-obscured ULIRG; it was a marginal SMG and has six possible contributors within the Herschel beam.

quotes.quote: We found that in most cases the single Herschel sources are made of multiple components that are not necessarily at the same redshifts. Our decomposition succeeded in identifying and extracting their major contributors. We show that these are all ULIRGs at z 1 -2 whose high L_ IR is mainly due to dust-obscured star formation.

GOODSN06 (J123634.3+621241) This source is in the SMG sample of Wang et al. (2004; their GOODS 850-19), however it was detected at <4 level ( S_ 850 =3.26 0.85 mJy) and was not included in the SMA observations of Barger et al. (2012). Morphologies and Potential Components Fig. 2 shows this source in various bands from 250 m to H_ 160 . Within 18 radius, there are 74 objects in H_ 160 that have S/N 5 (measured in the MAG AUTO aperture). By inspecting the FIR and the 24 m images, it is obvious that only six of these objects could possibly contribute to GOODSN06.

gmw_00003137.name: GOODSN63
gmw_00003137.overview: GOODSN63 is a high-redshift, dust-obscured ultraluminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:1312.0902] that was previously identified as a submillimeter galaxy [arXiv:1312.0902]. While detected as a single source at 250 microns, it resolves into two blended components at shorter wavelengths [arXiv:1312.0902].

sources.summary: GOODSN63 is a high-redshift dust-obscured ULIRG and known SMG, single at 250 microns but split into two blended PACS components.

quotes.quote: We found that in most cases the single Herschel sources are made of multiple components that are not necessarily at the same redshifts. Our decomposition succeeded in identifying and extracting their major contributors. We show that these are all ULIRGs at z 1 -2 whose high L_ IR is mainly due to dust-obscured star formation.

GOODSN63 (GOODSN-J123730.9+621259) This source has a multi-wavelength dataset nearly as rich as GOODSN06 does except that it lacks 70 m image. While being a single source in 250 m, it breaks up into two sources (but still blended) in 160 and 100 m. According to the PEP DR1 catalog, these two PACS components have S_ 160 =22.8 1.4 and 9.5 1.2 mJy, and S_ 100 =4.9 0.4 and 3.6 0.4 mJy, respectively. This source is a previously discovered SMG (Wang et al. 2004, their GOODS 850-6), whose position has been precisely determined by the SMA observations to be RA=12^ h 37^ m 30.80^ s , DEC=62 ^o 12^ ' 59.00^ '' (J2000), and has S_ 850 =14.9 0.9 mJy (Barger et al. 2012).

sources.summary: The paper includes GOODS 850-6 in its clean SMA sample, identifies it as the one source with only a millimetric redshift, and fits its dust SED.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: Submillimeter Properties of the SMA Sample: 850-6 & & 189.378326 & 62.216389

quotes.quote: This leaves us with a clean sample of 5 sources, 4 of which have spectroscopic redshifts and 1 (GOODS 850-6) of which has only a millimetric redshift.

In Figure fig8:sed (a), we normalized the modified blackbody fit to GOODS 850-6, the one source with only a millimetric redshift, which is why the radio point for this source lies directly on the synchrotron relation.
If we instead fix =1.5 , then we find T_d=36.2 , 46.1, 39.1, 43.1, 37.0 K for GOODS 850-3 (GN05), GOODS 850 5 (GN10), GOODS 850-6, GOODS 850-7 (GN04), and GOODS 850-9 (GN19), respectively.

gmw_00003138.name: UDS01
gmw_00003138.overview: UDS01 is a high-redshift, dust-obscured ultraluminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:1312.0902]. Its high infrared luminosity is primarily driven by star formation, and its Herschel beam contains nine potential far-infrared contributors [arXiv:1312.0902].

sources.summary: UDS01 is a high-redshift dust-obscured ULIRG with WFC3-based morphology; its Herschel beam contains nine possible FIR contributors and severe mid-IR blending.

quotes.quote: We found that in most cases the single Herschel sources are made of multiple components that are not necessarily at the same redshifts. Our decomposition succeeded in identifying and extracting their major contributors. We show that these are all ULIRGs at z 1 -2 whose high L_ IR is mainly due to dust-obscured star formation.

UDS01 (UDS-J021806.0-051247) This source does not have data in the ACS, and we relied on the WFC3 IR data for their morphologies. The optical data from the CFHTLS-Wide were used in their optical-to-NIR SED analysis. Morphologies and Potential Components Fig. 12 shows the images of UDS01. Within 18 radius, of the 250 m position, there are 35 objects in H_ 160 with S/N 5 . By comparing the 250 m and the 24 m images, we found that only nine of these objects could be significant contributors to the FIR flux.

gmw_00003139.name: UDS04
gmw_00003139.overview: UDS04 is a high-redshift ultraluminous infrared galaxy located at a redshift of 1.267 [arXiv:1312.0902]. Its high infrared luminosity is primarily driven by dust-obscured star formation rather than an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1312.0902].

sources.summary: UDS04 is a high-redshift dust-obscured ULIRG whose FIR emission is attributed to star formation rather than an SXDS X-ray source.

quotes.quote: We found that in most cases the single Herschel sources are made of multiple components that are not necessarily at the same redshifts. Our decomposition succeeded in identifying and extracting their major contributors. We show that these are all ULIRGs at z 1 -2 whose high L_ IR is mainly due to dust-obscured star formation.

UDS04 is not detected in the SXDS X-ray data (Ueda et al. 2008). At z=1.267 , the sensitivity limit of this catalog corresponds to an upper limit of 5.8 10^ 42 erg/s in restframe 1.1--4.5 keV. Based on the same argument as in 5.1.4, we take it that the FIR emission of UDS04, which is all from A , is all due to star formation.

gmw_00003140.name: EGS07
gmw_00003140.overview: EGS07 is a dust-obscured ultraluminous infrared galaxy at a redshift of z=1.497 [arXiv:1312.0902]. It exhibits a highly disturbed morphology indicative of a strong interaction [arXiv:1312.0902], and its high infrared luminosity is primarily driven by star formation [arXiv:1312.0902].

sources.summary: EGS07 is a high-redshift dust-obscured ULIRG; its central source has z=1.497 and disturbed morphology, suggesting dominant FIR contribution.

quotes.quote: We found that in most cases the single Herschel sources are made of multiple components that are not necessarily at the same redshifts. Our decomposition succeeded in identifying and extracting their major contributors. We show that these are all ULIRGs at z 1 -2 whose high L_ IR is mainly due to dust-obscured star formation.
EGS07 (EGS-J141900.3+524948) Morphologies and Potential Components Fig. 20 shows the images of EGS07.

The central source, 1 , has spectroscopic redshift of z=1.497 from the DEEP2 galaxy redshift survey (Newman et al. 2013). In the ACS images, this source shows a very disturbed morphology that is indicative of strong interaction. This suggests that it could be the dominant contributor.

sources.summary: The paper identifies ID30186 as a Type I galaxy with an X-ray AGN, compact-SMG-candidate core, high core SFR, and a major merger in a dense group environment.

quotes.quote: 4 galaxies hosts X-ray AGNs that do not dominate the FIR emissions; 1 is a Type I galaxy (ID30186), 2 are Type II galaxies (ID13098 and ID13776) and the last one is a Type III galaxy (ID23205) ( nandra_aegis-x_2015 ). A total of 5 galaxies have either a 3 detection in SCUBA2/ 850 m or a prediction > 1.1 mJy at (ID13776 and ID21190 from the Type II class and ID16544, ID29608 and ID30186 from the Type I class). They correspond to the 5 galaxies measured with a SFR 100 M _ yr ^ 1 in their core.

The circular marker showing a weakly lopsided galaxy in a high density environment is ID30186. This galaxy is the brightest galaxy of a group of 16 members at z_ spec = 1.85 , is undergoing a major merger and is surrounded by quiescent intra-halo light ( coogan_z185_2023 ).

gmw_00003141.name: EGS14
gmw_00003141.overview: EGS14 is a high-redshift, dust-obscured ultraluminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:1312.0902]. Its high infrared luminosity is primarily driven by dust-obscured star formation, and it contains ten identified potential contributors to its 250-micron flux [arXiv:1312.0902].

sources.summary: EGS14 is a high-redshift dust-obscured ULIRG source with ten inspected possible contributors to its 250 micron flux.

quotes.quote: We found that in most cases the single Herschel sources are made of multiple components that are not necessarily at the same redshifts. Our decomposition succeeded in identifying and extracting their major contributors. We show that these are all ULIRGs at z 1 -2 whose high L_ IR is mainly due to dust-obscured star formation.

EGS14 (EGS-J142025.9+525935) Morphologies and Potential Components Fig. 25 shows the images of EGS14. Within 18 of the 250 m source centroid, there are 58 objects detected in i that have S/N 5 . Based on inspection, we identified 10 objects as potential contributors to the 250 m flux, which we label as `` 1 '' to `` 10 ''.

gmw_00003142.name: EGS19
gmw_00003142.overview: EGS19 is a high-redshift, dust-obscured ultraluminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:1312.0902]. Its high infrared luminosity is primarily driven by dust-obscured star formation, and it features three distinct components at its 250-micron source location [arXiv:1312.0902].

sources.summary: EGS19 is a high-redshift dust-obscured ULIRG source outside CANDELS WFC3, with three PEP components at its 250 micron source location.

quotes.quote: We found that in most cases the single Herschel sources are made of multiple components that are not necessarily at the same redshifts. Our decomposition succeeded in identifying and extracting their major contributors. We show that these are all ULIRGs at z 1 -2 whose high L_ IR is mainly due to dust-obscured star formation.

EGS19 (EGS-J141943.4+525857) This source is different from the previous two in that it is outside of the CANDELS WFC3 area. At its 250 m source location, the PEP catalog reports three sources. From north to south, their flux densities are 6.4 1.2 , 9.9 1.2 and 6.0 1.2 mJy in 100 m, respectively, and 19.7 3.3 , 22.0 3.4 and 17.1 2.9 mJy, respectively.

gmw_00003143.name: NGC 2712
gmw_00003143.overview: NGC 2712 is an isolated barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:1106.5976, arXiv:1504.04013] that exhibits very strong star formation in its disk [arXiv:1601.04706]. Its inner structure harbors an oval distortion, while the outer regions show a modest warp and asymmetric neutral hydrogen gas [arXiv:1106.5976].

sources.summary: The paper maps CIG 292 in H I, finding an isolated spiral with asymmetric gas, no detected gas-rich companions or tidal tails, and evidence for an oval distortion plus modest warp.

quotes.quote: We present Expanded Very Large Array (EVLA) D-array observations in the 21-cm line of neutral hydrogen ( ) of CIG 292, an isolated SA(s)b galaxy at a distance of 24.3 Mpc. From previous \ single dish observations the galaxy was known to have a mildly asymmetric \ profile (A _ flux =1.23 0.3 ). Our EVLA observations show there is 12 more \ projected South of the optical centre (approaching velocities) than in the North (receding velocities), despite the \ extending 16 further to the North than the South. The \ projected within the optical disk must have been perturbed within the \ relaxation time ( 10 ^8 yr ) which implies that this can not have been caused by any of the three nearest companions, as their distance ( 0.5 Mpc) is too large. Neither -rich companions nor tidal tails were found within our field of view and velocity range covered.

Our kinematical data suggest that the inner part harbors an oval distortion whereas the outer regions show signs of a modest warp. The mild asymmetry in the \ global profile thus actually masks stronger asymmetries in the two--dimensional distributions of gas and star forming regions in this galaxy. Since the galaxy is isolated, this must predominantly be due to processes related to its formation and secular evolution.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 2712 as an inclined barred-galaxy test case, showing star-formation-desert line emission shares disk rotation.

quotes.quote: We selected the galaxy NGC 2712 (shown in Fig. fig:N2712R_Ha ) for this test as it is one of the more significantly inclined galaxies in the present study, and the slit angle differs by only 8 ^ from the galaxy major axis, with both factors maximising the rotation signal. For this test, five velocities were calculated from different regions of our long slit spectrum, corresponding to the nucleus, to the star formation desert regions to the north and south of the nucleus (SFD1 and SFD2 respectively), and to star formation regions lying just beyond each of the desert regions. Velocities were calculated from the centroids of gaussian profiles fitted to the H lines from each of the above regions. The resulting velocities are shown in Table tab:rot_vel .

This analysis makes clear that the line emission from the two star formation desert regions shares fully in the rotational velocity of the disk of NGC 2712. In one case, SFD1 to the north of the nucleus, the rotation is 20 km s ^ 1 slower than that of the HII region lying just outside it; but in SFD2, south of the nucleus, the pattern is reversed, and in any case most rotation curves show fluctuations of at least 20 km s ^ 1 . So it is clear that whatever drives the SFD line emission, it is linked to the rotating disk component.

sources.summary: NGC 2712 lacks the GALEX SFD confirmation available for the other galaxies, but its SFD indicates recent SF truncation and it is an SBb with very strong disk star formation.

quotes.quote: This characteristic SF pattern is confirmed by other indicators, e.g. three of the galaxies studied here (all except for NGC 2712) have GALEX ultra-violet light profiles presented by gild07 , all of which show distinctive peaks or plateaus of red far-UV -- near-UV colours in the radial range corresponding to the SFD.

However, significant differences are evident in the ages revealed by our spectroscopic analysis, with the SFD regions in two galaxies (NGC 3185 and NGC 3351) having very old populations, while NGC 2543 and NGC 2712 have evidence for more recent truncation of SF, within the last 2 Gyr.
The second youngest is found in NGC 2712, another SBb with very strong disk SF, that is morphologically very similar to NGC 2543.

gmw_00003144.name: R.A. 01^h 32^m 55 2 and DEC. +59^ 32^ 14 7
gmw_00003144.overview: The source at R.A. 01^h 32^m 55 2 and DEC. +59^ 32^ 14 7 is a variable X-ray source [arXiv:1611.05609]. It is identified as a likely low-power active galactic nucleus of the BL Lac type [arXiv:1611.05609].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes a quadruply lensed quasar system at the stated J2000 position, using Keck AO and HST imaging to model its lens galaxy and predict time delays.

quotes.quote: The \ system (J2000: 09 ^ h 24 ^ m 55.87, 02 19 24 9) is a quadruply-lensed quasar discovered by InadaEtal03 . The main lensing galaxy is at a redshift of z_ d = 0.394 0.001 EigenbrodEtal06 , and the source redshift is z_ s = 1.524 0.001 InadaEtal03 . The analysis in this paper is based on new Keck AO and archival \ observations of . We describe the data acquisition and analysis in sec:HSTreduction and sec:AOreduction . We show the data from three HST bands and one Keck AO K ^ band in Figure fig:aoimages .

When we marginalized over different source resolutions of the power-law model, the power-law model predicts t_ BA h _ v ^ 2 =6.75 +0.78 0.68 days, t_ CA h _ v ^ 2 =10.2 +1.2 1.0 days, and t_ DA h _ v ^ 2 =7.31 +0.86 0.74 days. When we marginalized over different source resolutions of the composite model, the composite model predicts t_ BA h _ v ^ 2 =6.99 +0.81 0.71 days, t_ CA h _ v ^ 2 =11.6 +1.4 1.2 days, and t_ DA h _ v ^ 2 =8.10 +0.96 0.82 days. When we marginalized power-law and composite model, we obtain t_ BA h _ v ^ 2 =6.89 +0.78 0.74 days, t_ CA h _ v ^ 2 =10.7 +1.6 1.2 days, and t_ DA h _ v ^ 2 =7.70 +0.97 0.91 days.

sources.summary: The paper identifies the coordinate-selected Swift/infrared/radio source as a likely low-power AGN at z=0.1143; a BL Lac SED could explain Fermi emission.

quotes.quote: In parallel, the Swift telescope data clearly revealed a different X-ray source at the J2000.0 position R.A. 01^h 32^m 55 2 and DEC. +59^ 32^ 14 7 with a 90 confidence error circle of 15 arcsecond. Hereafter, we refer to it as \ and this is one of the plotted objects in the panels of Fig. ellipses . Its count rate amounts to 0.020 0.003 count s ^ 1 corrected for point-spread-function (PSF) effects, thus providing a 7.7 detection.

The other peculiar object inside the Fermi confidence ellipse is the infrared source , found to be coincident with the variable X-ray source \ and also with \ as a likely radio counterpart. The 2-10 keV luminosity of the X-ray source is 1.7 10^ 43 erg s ^ 1 . Combining the X-ray properties, the optical spectrum and the multi-wavelength SED (see Fig. sed2 ), an active galactic nucleus (AGN) of BL Lac type can offer a natural explanation for both X-ray and gamma-ray emission detected by Swift and Fermi , respectively. The SED would be a sum of blazar emission and stellar light of the host galaxy dominating in the near-infrared to optical band.

gmw_00003145.name: ESO 325-G004
gmw_00003145.overview: ESO 325-G004 is a nearby giant elliptical galaxy [arXiv:1306.4983, arXiv:2304.11210] with a boxy structure [arXiv:astro-ph/0504453]. It acts as a strong gravitational lens [arXiv:1306.4983, arXiv:1806.08300], producing an Einstein ring [arXiv:1806.08300] and hosting a large population of globular clusters [arXiv:2304.11210].

sources.summary: The paper studies ESO325--G004 as the closest-known strong-lensing giant elliptical, measuring the lensed source redshift, Einstein-radius mass, dark-matter correction, and stellar mass-to-light ratio.

quotes.quote: We present new observations of the closest-known strong-lensing galaxy, the 330 \ giant elliptical ESO325--G004, made with the ESO Very Large Telescope. The low redshift of the lens ( z_ l = 0.035) results in arcs being formed at a small fraction of the effective radius, ( R_ Ein = 2.85 arcsec R_ eff /4 ). At such small radii, stars dominate the lensing mass, so that lensing provides a direct probe of the stellar mass-to-light ratio, with only small corrections needed for dark matter. However, the redshift of the galaxy lensed by \ was unknown until now, so the lensing mass was not securely determined. Using X-SHOOTER, we have detected multiple spectral lines, from two bright parts of the arc system, and measured a source redshift of z_ s = 2.141.

Combined with lens modelling constraints, this yields a total mass inside the Einstein radius of 1.50 0.06 10^ 11 M_ . We estimate the range of possible contribution of dark matter to the lensing mass, using halo profile statistics from cosmological N-body simulations. Subtracting this component yields a stellar mass-to-light ratio for the lens of M_*/L_ F814W = 3.14^ +0.24 _ 0.42 (M/L)_ , F814W .

sources.summary: The paper identifies ESO325-G004 as a nearby boxy elliptical strong lens whose ACS images show arcs; SIE modeling matches its light distribution and arc geometry.

quotes.quote: ESO325-G004 was observed with ACS in January 2005, as part of our program measuring surface-brightness fluctuations in the Shapley Foreground region. The total integration times were 18882 seconds in F814W (22 individual exposures) and 1101 seconds (three exposures) in F475W. The frames were combined using multidrizzle in stsdas to yield the stacks used in this study (Figure fig:image ). Inspection of the images revealed two long, narrow arcs at 3 arcsec separation from the galaxy center (Figure fig:lensmod ). A third object, less obviously distorted, is present at similar radius.

Modeling the lensing potential with a singular isothermal ellipse (SIE), we find close agreement with the light distribution. The best fit SIE model reproduces the ellipticity of the lens galaxy to 10 , and its position angle within 1 ^ . The model predicts the broad features of the arc geometry as observed; the unlensed magnitude of the source is estimated at I_c 23.75 .

sources.summary: The paper uses ESO 325-G004 as its worked example, deriving its globular-cluster population from the radial density profile and estimating a total GCS mass of 3.49e9 solar masses.

quotes.quote: Figure fig:lineardense shows the GC radial density distribution for ESO 325-G004, and Table tab:fits shows the powerlaw fits and uncertainties to the GC radial distributions for the entire galaxy sample as well.

In this section we will illustrate a sample calculation of the mass of the GCS for one of the galaxies in the sample: ESO 325-G004. The values used in this sample calculation can be found in tables tab:Names , tab:fits , and tab:lum-vals . First, we determine the number of GCs brighter than the limiting magnitude by integrating the fit to the GC density as a function of radius, out to 0.1R_ vir .

The final result for the total out to 0.2 R_ vir is N_ tot = 8593 1210 . Now, the average mass of a GC in the galaxy can be determined from equation eq:avg_GC using the galaxy's total visual magnitude, shown in Table tab:Names : M_ GC &= 5.698 + 0.1294 M_V^T + 0.0054 (M_V^T)^2 M_ GC &= 4.05 10^5 M_ Finally, the total mass M_ GCS for ESO 325-G004 is then M_ GCS &= N_ GC,tot M_ GC &= (3.49 0.49) 10^9 M_

sources.summary: The paper uses nearby strong-lens ESO 325-G004 to test gravity, modeling HST arcs and MUSE kinematics; it finds a central mass-to-light rise and gamma consistent with GR.

quotes.quote: The nearby ( z_l=0.035 ) galaxy ESO 325-G004 (hereafter ) is located at right ascension 13:43:33.2, declination 38:10:34 (J2000 equinox). Hubble Space Telescope (HST) imaging of serendipitously revealed the presence of an Einstein ring with 2.95 arcsecond radius around the centre of the galaxy , shown in Figure fig:colourimage . Follow-up observations have revealed that the source is an extended star-forming galaxy at z_s =2.1 . provides a laboratory for testing GR on kiloparsec scales as the small distance to the lens enables spatially resolved measurements of the stellar kinematics; this places tight constraints on the 3-dimensional mass structure of the lens. also has an extended arc system, which provides tight constraints on the 2-dimensional surface mass profile of the lens.

Within the constraints of our fiducial model, we infer that it is only possible to simultaneously reconstruct the lensing and kinematics if the stellar mass-to-light ratio of the lens increases within the central kiloparsec, with an observed F814W-band stellar mass-to-light ratio at large radius of 2.8 0.1 M_ /L_ in units of solar mass over solar luminosity in this band (consistent with a Milky Way-like stellar initial mass function [ IMF ] ), rising to 6.6 0.1 M_ /L_ in the centre (consistent with an excess of mass in low luminosity stars relative to the Milky Way).
In our fiducial model, we find = 0.978^ +0.010 0.015 at 68 confidence, with the uncertainties estimated from a Markov Chain Monte Carlo analysis.

sources.summary: The paper uses ESO325-G004 as a gravitational-lensing example, citing the observed Einstein-ring radius, lens/source redshifts, and mass parameter in a rough numerical estimate for its dark-matter lensing model.

quotes.quote: In this section, we try to use the data of galaxy ESO325-G004 to estimate the order of magnitude of to illustrate our discussion at the end of Section section5 . All the data below are from and its references, and for the purpose of our discussion we only do very rough estimations without error analysis. The observed value of the Einstein ring around the center of the galaxy ESO325-G004 is _E^ obs =2.85'' . The lens redshift is z_l=0.035 ; and the source (the background galaxy) redshift is z_s=1.141 .
According to , the mass parameter M for the galaxy ESO325-G004 is about 10^ 14 m , and thus M/b 10^ 5 , where b=D_L _E^ obs 10^ 19 m .

gmw_00003146.name: MS0735
aliases_external: MS0735 central radio source in MS 0735.6+7421
gmw_00003146.overview: MS0735 is a central radio galaxy located within the cool-core galaxy cluster MS 0735.6+7421 [arXiv:2104.08294]. The active galactic nucleus features a defined core, outer lobes, and has experienced at least three distinct phases of jet activity [arXiv:2104.08294].

sources.summary: The paper treats MS0735 as the central radio source in MS 0735.6+7421, identified with 4C +74.13, and uses radio and X-ray data to reconstruct three jet-activity phases.

quotes.quote: MS 0735.6+7421 One of the most famous examples of AGN mechanical feedback is represented by the radio source located at the centre of the cool-core galaxy cluster MS 0735.6+7421 (hereafter MS0735). The source (RA = 07:41:40.3, DEC = +74:14:58.0) has a redshift of z = 0.216 , corresponding to a scale of 1 = 3.53 kpc (assuming a CDM cosmological model with _ = 0.7 , _m = 0.3, and H_0 = 70 \ km \ s^ 1 \ Mpc^ 1 ).
Based on data obtained with the Very Large Array (VLA), the central radio source (4C +74.13) was identified as a radio galaxy with a clearly defined core and outer lobes Cohen2005 .

We have studied the peculiar radio source at the centre of the cool-core cluster MS0735. A detailed study of the source in the radio band, together with a re-inspection of the X-ray data, revealed that the source was subjected to at least three different phases of jet activity. From the radio spectrum we derived estimates of the radiative age of the source under different hypotheses in order to reconstruct its duty cycle and to provide an independent comparison with previously established age estimates from the cavities detected in X-rays.

gmw_00003147.name: ZTF18aajyzfv
gmw_00003147.overview: ZTF18aajyzfv is a galaxy system that has undergone a recent merger, as revealed by its tidal arms [arXiv:2405.11026]. It hosts a bright, blue active galactic nucleus that is spatially offset from the redder host galaxy by 6.7 kiloparsecs [arXiv:2405.11026].

sources.summary: The paper selects ZTF18aajyzfv for detailed follow-up as a blue offset AGN in a recent merger system, measuring a 6.7 kpc separation from the galaxy light.

quotes.quote: We identified ZTF18aajyzfv as a runaway AGN candidate worthy of further follow-up due to its clearly offset blue AGN, with no apparent extended emission centered on the AGN. This is the candidate that we will focus on for further detailed study in this paper.

ZTF18aajyzfv has a bright blue AGN clearly offset from a redder galaxy, seen clearly in the top panel of Figure Fig:Master . The system's tidal arms, seen in Subaru imaging in Figure Fig:TidalArms , reveal that this system has undergone a recent merger. As noted earlier, recoiling and slingshot AGNs form after merger events and can remain visible before tidal arms dissipate Blecha .

We use the Scarlet Pan-STARRS scene model to measure a spatial offset of 1.8 0.2 " between the best-fit sub-pixel position of the point source and the position of the brightest pixel in the galaxy morphology model. This corresponds to 6.7 0.2 kpc at z=0.224 .

gmw_00003148.overview: The object 103913.81+094003.0 is a candidate runaway active galactic nucleus that exhibits a statistical spatial offset from its putative host galaxy [arXiv:2405.11026]. Its spectrum displays a blue-shifted peak in the broad hydrogen emission line, which may indicate a velocity offset or a double-peaked accretion disk profile [arXiv:2405.11026].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 103913.81+094003.0 as a runaway AGN candidate with statistical spatial offset, possible broad-line velocity offset, and a foreground/background coincidence needing spectroscopy.

quotes.quote: 103913.81+094003.0 (ZTF19aavwybq) consists of a bright AGN with a sufficiently small spatial offset from its putative host galaxy that it is not visible by eye, but it is statistically offset based on astrometric jitter and chromatic offset. Its SDSS spectrum in the second panel of Figure Fig:SDSSOtherCands shows a blue-shifted peak in the broad H emission line, which could be due to the velocity offset of broad-line gas in a runaway AGN's accretion disk. However, the shape of the broad-line profile is also consistent with double-peaked emission from the Keplerian motion of gas in an accretion disk, where Doppler boosting results in a brighter blue peak compared to the red peak see for a review Eracleous2009 . Double-peaked accretion disk profiles are observed in 2-10 of broad-line AGN, but were identified in the majority of the offset AGN identified in ZTF by Ward , perhaps suggesting a connection between recent merger activity and the occurrence of disk-dominated broad-line emission. In summary, due to multiple confirmations of a statistical spatial offset in this work and in Ward , and a possible broad-line velocity offset, 103913.81+094003.0 is a prime candidate for further follow-up with integral-field spectroscopy and high-resolution imaging in order to confirm or rule out this object as a runaway SMBH system.

In unpublished data, Ward visually identified 103913.81+094003.0 as a possible coincidence between a foreground/background AGN and a background/foreground galaxy. Follow-up spectroscopy would be needed to confirm whether or not the AGN and galaxy are at the same redshift.

gmw_00003149.name: J0321--455N
gmw_00003149.overview: J0321--455N is a tailed radio galaxy classified as a Fanaroff-Riley class I source [arXiv:astro-ph/0402574]. Its radio tail contains collimated jets that undergo numerous sharp bends, forming a loop-like projected structure and a possible plume [arXiv:astro-ph/0402574].

sources.summary: The paper describes J0321--455N as a northern tailed radio galaxy with a long FR I radio tail, sharply bending jets, loop-like projected structure, and a possible plume.

quotes.quote: The images at the different frequencies and resolutions show complex structure in the radio tails of both J0321--455N and J0321--455S. The tails of both radio sources appear as collimated jets that undergo numerous sharp bends and wiggles as they trail away from the host galaxy. At 1.4 GHz, the radio tails have been detected out to projected linear distances of 200 kpc and 150 kpc respectively in J0321--455N and J0321--455S.

The jets in J0321--455N appear, in projection, to bend and almost form a closed loop and then bend sharply in opposite directions; the overall structure in the inner parts is shaped as seen clearly in the 4.8 GHz image. The South-East jet in J0321--455N appears to end in a plume of higher surface brightness: this structure might simply be a collimated tail viewed close to the line of sight.

gmw_00003150.name: J0321--455S
gmw_00003150.overview: J0321--455S is a southern tailed radio galaxy that displays a wide-angle tail morphology [arXiv:astro-ph/0402574]. Its inner structure exhibits a C-shaped morphology with a compact, unresolved core, and its radio tails extend to a projected linear distance of 150 kpc [arXiv:astro-ph/0402574].

sources.summary: The paper describes J0321--455S as a southern tailed radio galaxy with WAT morphology, a C-shaped inner source, compact unresolved core, and spectral steepening along its radio tail.

quotes.quote: The images at the different frequencies and resolutions show complex structure in the radio tails of both J0321--455N and J0321--455S. The tails of both radio sources appear as collimated jets that undergo numerous sharp bends and wiggles as they trail away from the host galaxy. At 1.4 GHz, the radio tails have been detected out to projected linear distances of 200 kpc and 150 kpc respectively in J0321--455N and J0321--455S.

J0321--455S, on the other hand, displays more typical WAT morphology. The jets from this source bend to the same side and the inner part of this source, as seen in the 4.8 GHz image, has a C-shaped morphology.

J0321--455S has a compact core that is unresolved in our highest resolution observations and the spectral index is also the flattest towards this core, implying it may still be active. Both galaxies also show a steepening in the two-point spectral index away from their centres and along their radio tails.

gmw_00003151.name: central object of RX J1552.2+2013
gmw_00003151.overview: The central object of RX J1552.2+2013 is a highly luminous cD galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0509884]. Photometric analysis reveals a light excess over the de-Vaucouleurs profile, which indicates the presence of an envelope [arXiv:astro-ph/0509884].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes surface photometry of the central galaxy of RX J1552.2+2013, finding a profile excess interpreted as an envelope and arguing its luminosity and envelope identify it as a cD galaxy.

quotes.quote: In the upper panel of Fig. phot , the azimutally averaged photometric profile of the central galaxy of RX J1552.2+2013 is shown. The surface photometry was performed using the task ELLIPSE in STSDAS/IRAF , which fits ellipses to extended object isophotes. We allowed the ellipticity and position angle of the successive ellipses to change but the center remained fixed. The ellipse fitting was performed only in the deeper ^ image.

We have fitted a ^ 1/4 law to the bright end of the galaxy profile, from well outside the seeing disk (3.0 ) to a radius corresponding to _ ^ =23.0 mag arcsec ^ 2 ( 8.3 ). In the lower panel of Fig. phot , the residuals (data -- ^ 1/4 law model) for the ^ band data are shown. The light excess over the de-Vaucoleurs profile, which is clearly detected in all three filters, is interpreted here as due to an envelope. The presence of this light envelope, in addition to the high luminosity of the galaxy, strongly suggests that the central object of RX J1552.2+2013 is a cD galaxy.

gmw_00003152.name: Sculptor dwarf spheroidal galaxy
aliases_external: NAME Sculptor Dwarf Galaxy / NAME Sculptor Dwarf Spheroidal
Sculptor Dwarf Galaxy / Sculptor dwarf spheroidal
gmw_00003152.overview: The Sculptor dwarf spheroidal galaxy is a satellite of the Milky Way [arXiv:0909.3092] that is dominated by an old, metal-poor stellar population [arXiv:1201.2408]. It experienced an extended history of star formation [arXiv:0909.3092, arXiv:1201.2408] and is chemically characterized by a metallicity gradient [arXiv:1605.02768, arXiv:2304.11980].

sources.summary: The paper measures element abundances for Sculptor member stars, finding a metal-poor tail, many very metal-poor stars, declining alpha abundance with metallicity, and evidence for extended star formation.

quotes.quote: We present measurements of Fe, Mg, Si, Ca, and Ti abundances for \ radial velocity member stars in the Sculptor dwarf spheroidal galaxy (dSph), a satellite of the Milky Way. This is the largest sample of individual element (Mg, Si, Ca, Ti) abundance measurements in any single dSph. The measurements are made from Keck/DEIMOS medium-resolution spectra (6400--9000 , R 6500 ). Based on comparisons to published high-resolution ( R 20000 ) spectroscopic measurements, our measurements have uncertainties of = and = . The Sculptor \ distribution has a mean = and is asymmetric with a long, metal-poor tail, indicative of a history of extended star formation.

Sculptor has a larger fraction of stars with < -2 than the Milky Way halo. We have discovered one star with = , which is the most metal-poor star known anywhere except the Milky Way halo, but high-resolution spectroscopy is needed to measure this star's detailed abundances. As has been previously reported based on high-resolution spectroscopy, \ in Sculptor falls as \ increases.

sources.summary: The paper measures neutron-capture abundances in Sculptor red giants, finding r-process enrichment throughout its chemical history and s-process dominance in barium only at higher metallicity.

quotes.quote: To study these processes in an environment different from the Milky Way, we targeted the n capture elements in red giant branch stars in the Sculptor dwarf spheroidal galaxy. Using ESO VLT/FLAMES spectra, we measured the chemical abundances of Y, Ba, La, Nd, and Eu in 98 stars covering the metalliticy range 2.4< [ Fe/H ] <-0.9 . This is the first paper in a series about the n capture elements in dwarf galaxies, and here we focus on the relative and absolute timescales of the slow ( s )- and rapid ( )-processes in Sculptor. From the abundances of the s process element Ba and the process element Eu, it is clear that the process enrichment occurred throughout the entire chemical evolution history of Sculptor. Furthermore, there is no evidence for the process to be significantly delayed in time relative to core-collapse supernovae.

Neutron star mergers are therefore unlikely the dominant (or only) nucleosynthetic site of the process. However, the products of the s process only become apparent at [ Fe/H ] 2 in Sculptor, and the s process becomes the dominant source of Ba at [ Fe/H ] 2 . We tested the use of [ Y/Mg ] and [ Ba/Mg ] as chemical clocks in Sculptor.

sources.summary: The paper derives Sculptor dSph's RR Lyrae metallicity distribution and gradient, finding broad old-population enrichment and evidence for a more metal-rich central population without RR Lyrae counterparts.

quotes.quote: We present the metallicity distribution of a sample of 471 RR Lyrae (RRL) stars in the Sculptor dSph, obtained from the I band Period-Luminosity relation. It is the first time that the early chemical evolution of a dwarf galaxy is characterized in such a detailed and quantitative way, using photometric data alone. We find a broad metallicity distribution (FWHM=0.8 dex) that is peaked at [ Fe/H ] -1.90 dex, in excellent agreement with literature values obtained from spectroscopic data. Moreover, we are able to directly trace the metallicity gradient out to a radius of 55 .

However, in the central part of Sculptor we do not observe the latter gradients. This suggests that there is a more metal-rich and/or younger population in Sculptor that does not produce RRLs. This scenario is strengthened by the observation of a metal-rich peak in the metallicity distribution of RG stars by other authors, which is not present in the metallicity distribution of the RRLs within the same central area.

sources.summary: The paper models Sculptor's dark-matter halo and velocity dispersion, finding larger Sommerfeld-related boosts for cuspy profiles and concentrated halos.

quotes.quote: In figure fig:corner_draco_sculpt we show the outcome of our Bayesian fit on Sculptor and Draco datasets, describing the underlying DM halo with the NFW (red triangle plot) or with the Burkert (blue triangle plot) profile. We report the one-dimensional marginalized posterior p.d.f. for each of the estimated parameters, highlighting the 16- th , 50- th , 84- th percentiles, and their joint probability distribution with the 68 and 95 highest probability density (h.p.d.) contours. While a strong correlation between the DM halo parameters emerges in all our fits, we can observe for the case of Draco and Carina that the NFW scenario is sensitive to the physically motivated upper-limit assigned on r_ s . As a general trend, we find in NFW fits a non-negligible correlation among DM halo parameters and _ 0, , while for the fits with Burkert profile the correlation of the halo parameters with inner trend of the stellar anisotropy gets essentially milder. Heliocentric distance and half-light angle show overall mild correlations in the bi-dimensional joint distributions with the rest of the fitted parameters. Finally, to provide a concrete picture of the goodness of our fits, we show in figure fig:slos_draco_sculpt the 68 and 95 h.p.d. interval for the predicted l.o.s. velocity dispersion profile in Draco and Sculptor, together with their binned data points.

For both, Draco and Sculptor, we see larger net enhancement for cuspy density profiles (i.e. the NFW and our sample non-parametric case), since they typically imply deeper potential wells and therefore these halos host colder particle populations at their centers. At the same time, the effect one finds for a given dwarf cannot be rigidly applied to another object, since details of the enhancement depend on the preferred region in the parameter space. In general the larger the halo concentration, the larger the flux increase: e.g., in Draco we found that the fit in case of the NFW profile points to significantly larger r_s and lower _s than for Sculptor, see figure fig:corner_draco_sculpt , while for the Burkert profile the preferred regions in parameter space are closer one to the other; correspondingly we find a smaller relative boost in the NFW versus Burkert comparison for Draco, while it is appreciably larger for Sculptor. As we wish to illustrate now, the choice of phase-space modelling has also a significant impact on the extrapolated enhancements, both when considering an approximation to the isotropic case, such as for the MB model introduced above, and even more drastically when one allows for anisotropic velocity distributions. In figure fig:J_integrand we consider the ratio S (v_ rel ) () / S (v_ rel ) _ E () , comparing the velocity averaged enhancement of a given PSDF to the Eddington's case at the radial distance from the center of halo. The angular and line-of-sight integrals appearing in the definition of J factor, see eq. ( eq:j_factor ), when pointing towards the center of a spherical object, can be reduced to an integral on , while performing the other integrals analytically, see . After rewriting J as J = _0^ r_b dr dJ/dr() , where r_b is the outer truncation radius, the appropriately normalized ratio dJ/dr over J -- assuming the Eddington's PSDF and the Coulomb regime for the Sommerfeld-enhancement --- is plotted in figure fig:J_integrand with thin black lines (with corresponding vertical axis scale at the .h.s. \ of the plots; note that this scale is logarithmic). The three lines in each plot are for three different apertures of the instrument acceptance cone . The curves for the NFW profile peak all at =0 , while for the Burkert profile the largest contribution to J shifts towards r_s ; analogous trends --- but sharper --- would appear also in the resonant regime of the Sommerfeld enhancement. This check provides a visual guidance to the plots, indicating the intervals in over which there is a significant impact on the expected annihilation flux, would a different DM velocity distribution provide a significantly different S (v_ rel ) () . In figure fig:J_integrand thick lines show this quantity as computed for the MB, OM, _c PSDF over the result for the Eddington's case (now the reference vertical scale is on the l.h.s. \ of the plots). From the plot one can see that, for the NFW profile, the Maxwell-Boltzmann approximation gives a systematic (but numerical-friendly) underestimate of the true result in the isotropic case, since the corresponding curve is smaller than 1 at the peak of the contribution to J_1 (and even more so for J_2 ), while it gets marginally above 1 in the radial range less relevant for the J factor; the MB approximation causes instead a much smaller error for the Burkert profile. In the same way one sees that even the very small bias on circular velocity introduced by the model with _ () = -1/2 , impacting on the abundance of slow particles at the center of the systems, is going to significantly boost J_ 1,2- _c for both density profiles. Finally, for what regards Osipkov-Merritt model, J_ 1,2 tend to be smaller then in the isotropic case, which however becomes significant only when the suppression in S (v_ rel ) () , which is maximized at slightly below r_a , gets within the radial range relevant for the computation of J_ 1,2 , namely if we consider r_a close to r_s for Burkert and r_a r_s for NFW profile. With the general trends delineated in figure fig:J_integrand , we can now quantify the Sommerfeld effect for Milky Way classical dSphs. From the samples obtained in the MCMC analysis described in section sec:MCMC , we can actually compute the J factor posterior distributions. Note that evaluation requires the non-trivial computational task of performing multidimensional integration for each of the MCMC events collected. In order to make the demanding numerics feasible, we can resort to the scaling relations explained in greater detail in appendix app:J-factor_scaling . In figure fig:J_histogram we show the J factor p.d.f. for our benchmark galaxies, Draco and Sculptor.

In this respect, the prime targets for detection among the eight classical dSphs remain essentially the same as in the non-enhanced case, with slight improvement for Sculptor and Sextans, which we find to have more concentrated DM halos.

sources.summary: The paper models Sculptor as a rapidly formed dSph with strong outflow; its Mg and Mn abundance trends require sub-Chandrasekhar enrichment and an elevated SN Iax contribution.

quotes.quote: The models for Sculptor and Sextans are very similar; both are formed by a rapid infall and star formation with a strong outflow. Since the star formation efficiency in Sextans is lower than in Sculptor, the average [ Fe/H ] of the MDF is 0.3 dex lower, which is probably due to the mass difference of the systems.

Also in Figure fig:sculptor for Sculptor, the observed [ Mg/Fe ] decreases at [ Fe/H ] 2 , which cannot be reproduced without sub-Ch-mass SNe Ia. The observed [ Mn/Fe ] ratios are highest around [ Fe/H ] 1.5 , and then sharply decreases until [ Fe/H ] 1 . This is not reproduced with the model including all three SN Ia channels (red solid lines). Obviously, without Ch-mass SNe Ia, [ Mn/Fe ] monotonically decreases (green dotted lines). The SN Iax is a potentially good source to reproduce this [ Mn/Fe ] evolution but a higher rate is required.

sources.summary: The paper builds a large VLT/FLAMES and Gaia survey of Sculptor, deriving velocities, metallicities, membership, a metallicity gradient, and distinct metal-poor stellar kinematics.

quotes.quote: We present a new homogeneous survey of VLT/FLAMES LR8 line-of-sight radial velocities ( ) for 1604 resolved red giant branch stars in the Sculptor dwarf spheroidal galaxy. In addition, we provide reliable Ca II triplet metallicities, , for 1339 of these stars. From this combination of new observations (2257 individual spectra) with ESO archival data (2389 spectra), we obtain the largest and most complete sample of \ and \ measurements for individual stars in any dwarf galaxy.

Our spectroscopic velocities are combined with \ DR3 proper motions and parallax measurements for a new and more precise membership analysis. We look again at the global characteristics of Sculptor, deriving a mean metallicity of = -1.82 0.45 and a mean line-of-sight velocity of = +111.2 0.25 km/s. There is a clear metallicity gradient in Sculptor, 0.7 deg/dex, with the most metal-rich population being the most centrally concentrated. Furthermore, the most metal-poor population in Sculptor, < -2.5 , appears to show kinematic properties distinct from the rest of the stellar population.

sources.summary: The paper derives Sculptor's detailed star-formation and chemical-enrichment history, finding an old metal-poor dominant population, younger central metal-rich stars, and 7.8 million solar masses formed between 14 and 7 Gyr ago.

quotes.quote: We have combined deep photometry in the B,V and I bands from CTIO/MOSAIC of the Sculptor dwarf spheroidal galaxy, going down to the oldest Main Sequence Turn-Offs, with spectroscopic metallicity distributions of Red Giant Branch stars. This allows us to obtain the most detailed and complete Star Formation History to date, as well as an accurate timescale for chemical enrichment. The Star Formation History shows that Sculptor is dominated by old ( > 10 Gyr), metal-poor stars, but that younger, more metal-rich populations are also present.

Using Star Formation Histories determined at different radii from the centre we show that Sculptor formed stars with an increasing central concentration with time. The old, metal-poor populations are present at all radii, while more metal-rich, younger stars are more centrally concentrated. We find that within an elliptical radius of 1 degree, or 1.5 kpc from the centre, a total mass in stars of 7.8 10 ^ 6 M _ was formed, between 14 and 7 Gyr ago, with a peak at 13 14 Gyr ago.

sources.summary: For Sculptor, the dispersion-kurtosis Jeans analysis suggests the data are incompatible with a steep profile and favor a cored dark-matter profile.

quotes.quote: For Sculptor however we find that the technique suggests that the data is incompatible with a steep profile and a cored profile seems more consistent. Data Stellar position and velocity data for Fornax and Sculptor is taken from the Magellan survey walkdat and after using the spectral metallicity data provided in to remove potential interlopers with probability of membership less than 0.95 one retains a sample of 2304 and 1351 stars for Fornax and Sculptor respectively. To ensure that there are at least two hundred stars in each bin which is a minimum for a meaningful measurement of the fourth moment Amorisco , the Fornax data is split into nine radial annuli of 230 stars plus an outer annulus of 234 stars and in the same manner the Sculptor data is split into five annuli of 225 plus one of 226.

The inclusion of the kurtosis for sculptor is rather more dramatic than for Fornax. Here we see that though the anisotropy at the galactic centre is obscured the joint analysis makes a clear prediction for radial anisotropy. More interesting still, the cored solution, in excellent agreement with the prediction from multiple stellar populations, is favoured over the NFW one to high significance up to and beyond the half-light radius breaking the degeneracy.

sources.summary: Sculptor is analyzed as a cored dSph in the seven-object Fermi study, with flattened axisymmetric modeling producing more substantial differences than cusped NFW assumptions.

quotes.quote: We analyse about seven years of PASS8 Fermi LAT data for seven classical dSphs, namely Draco, Leo I and II, Sextans, Carina, Sculptor, and Fornax. These dSphs are selected as the overlapping part of the samples considered by Ref. and Ref. . We fit each dSph both with NFW and axisymmetric profiles, and compare their cross section upper limits. We underline, in particular, that Sextans, Carina, Sculptor and Fornax are characterised by cored axisymmetric profiles rather than cusped, and their results can differ significantly from those of the NFW profiles.

While for the NFW profiles the differences are insignificant, with maxima of about 1 and 2.5 for Draco and Sculptor, respectively, the differences in the case of the axisymmetric mass models are much more severe in most cases, except for Leo I and II. The analyses of the dSphs best described by a cored profile (Sextans, Sculptor, Carina and Fornax) result in a more substantial difference between the two adopted profiles.

sources.summary: The paper uses Sculptor stellar S and Zn abundances to test DLA abundance proxies, finding Sculptor differs from DLAs and the Milky Way in chemical history.

quotes.quote: Abundances of the volatile elements S and Zn have now been measured in around 80 individual stars in the Sculptor dwarf spheroidal galaxy, covering the metallicity range 2.4 [ Fe/H ] 0.9 . These two elements are of particular interest as they are not depleted onto dust in gas, and their ratio, [ S/Zn ] , has thus commonly been used as a proxy for [ /Fe ] in Damped Lyman- systems (DLAs). The S abundances in Sculptor are similar to other elements in this galaxy, consistent with S being mainly created in core-collapse supernovae, but also having some contribution from type Ia supernovae.

In particular, the Sculptor dSph stellar sample is from the center of this galaxy which is younger and more metal-rich compared to other regions. From the star formation history it is estimated that over 80 of Sculptor's stellar population was formed more than 10 Gyr ago deBoer2012 , while the data are spread over a larger range of ages, from 6 to 12 Gyr.

From these data it is evident that the Sculptor dSph, the Milky Way, and DLAs all show clear differences in their [ S/Fe ] and [ Zn/Fe ] ratios, both as a function of [ Fe/H ] and [ Zn/H ] . In addition, Sculptor stars cover a lower [ Zn/H ] range compared to DLAs.

sources.summary: The paper studies Sculptor's metal-poor stellar population, using carbon and metallicity measurements to constrain its chemical evolution and compare its CEMP population with the halo.

quotes.quote: Here we present metallicity and carbon abundance measurements of 100 stars in Sculptor from medium-resolution ( R 2000) spectra taken with the Magellan/Michigan Fiber System mounted on the Magellan-Clay 6.5m telescope at Las Campanas Observatory. We identify 24 extremely metal-poor star candidates ( [ Fe/H ] < 3.0) and 21 carbon-enhanced metal-poor (CEMP) star candidates. Eight carbon-enhanced stars are classified with at least 2 confidence and five are confirmed as such with follow-up R 6000 observations using the Magellan Echellette Spectrograph on the Magellan-Baade 6.5m telescope.

Chemically characterizing members of the Sculptor dSph galaxy has provided insights on its chemical evolution and formation using high-resolution spectroscopy of red giant stars svt+03, tvs+03, gsw+05 . tih+04 found evidence for two stellar components in Sculptor, as also seen in other dSphs. Additional modeling of Sculptor by dts+12 showed evidence for extended star formation, and further modeling by rs+13 suggested the importance of dilution and metal-removal in chemical evolution scenarios.

sources.summary: The paper uses high-resolution abundances of red giants to characterize Sculptor as a textbook dwarf spheroidal with simple chemical evolution, early Type Ia enrichment, efficient mixing, and no unusual IMF evidence.

quotes.quote: Astrofisica, E-38206 La Laguna, Tenerife, Spain Department of Physics, University of Surrey, Guildford, GU2 7XH, UK European Southern Observatory, Alonso de Cordova 3107, Vitacura, Casilla 19001, Santiago, Chile Centre for Space Research, North-West University, Potchefstroom, 2520, South Africa Leibniz Institute for Astrophyics Potsdam (AIP), An der Sternwarte 16, D-14482 Potsdam, Germany We present detailed chemical abundances for 99 red-giant branch stars in the centre of the Sculptor dwarf spheroidal galaxy, which have been obtained from high-resolution VLT/FLAMES spectroscopy.

The s process products from low-mass AGB stars also participate in the chemical evolution of Sculptor on a timescale comparable to that of supernovae type Ia. However, the process is consistent with having no time delay relative to core-collapse supernovae, at least at the later stages of the chemical evolution in Sculptor.

We see only a very small or no contribution to O, Mg, Ni, and Zn from supernovae type Ia in Sculptor. The observed chemical abundances in Sculptor show no evidence of a significantly different initial mass function, compared to that of the Milky Way. With the exception of neutron-capture elements at low [ Fe/H ] , the scatter around mean trends in Sculptor for [ Fe/H ] >-2.3 is extremely low, and compatible with observational errors.

sources.summary: The paper measures sulphur in Sculptor stars, finding NLTE-corrected sulphur trends like other elements: a low-metallicity plateau, decline with metallicity, and Type Ia enrichment signatures.

quotes.quote: Here, we use high-resolution ESO VLT/FLAMES/GIRAFFE spectra to determine sulphur abundances for 85 stars in the Sculptor dwarf spheroidal galaxy, covering the metallicity range 2.5 [ Fe/H ] 0.8 . The abundances are derived from the S I triplet at 9213, 9228, and 9238 . These lines have been shown to be sensitive to departure from local thermodynamic equilibrium, i.e. NLTE effects. Therefore, we present new NLTE corrections for a grid of stellar parameters covering those of the target stars. The NLTE-corrected sulphur abundances in Sculptor show the same behaviour as other elements in that galaxy (such as Mg, Si, and Ca). At lower metallicities ( [ Fe/H ] 2 ) the abundances are consistent with a plateau at [ S/Fe ] +0.16 , similar to what is observed in the Galactic halo, [ S/Fe ] +0.2 . With increasing [ Fe/H ] , the [ S/Fe ] ratio declines, reaching negative values at [ Fe/H ] 1.5 . The sample also shows an increase in [ S/Mg ] with [ Fe/H ] , most probably because of enrichment from Type Ia supernovae.

sources.summary: The paper cites Sculptor as a dwarf-galaxy comparison where alpha trends decline with metallicity, possible Mn scatter correlates with alpha abundance, and distant giants have large abundance errors.

quotes.quote: In the Sculptor dSph galaxy, for example, \ shows a declining metallicity trend from 0.4 at = -1.8 to 0.2 at = -1.0 . Interestingly, skuladottir17 find indications of a cosmic scatter in \ of the Sculptor stars, i.e. from about 0.6 dex to +0.4 dex and a positive correlation between \ and . At a distance of 85 kpc, the giants observed in Sculptor are, however, faint ( 17.0 < V < 18.5 ), and the errors of \ are in the order of 0.3 dex. It will require more precise \ and \ values and also improved precision of \ north12 to verify if stars with enhanced Mn/Fe, Ni/Fe, and Zn/Fe ratios are present in Sculptor.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes Sculptor with published stellar kinematics, finding its kinematic sample highly constraining and its total J value the best constrained in the dwarf sample.

quotes.quote: We use the stellar-kinematic data published by 2008ApJ...675..201M for Leo I, and by walker09a for Carina, Fornax, Sculptor and Sextans.

However, Sculptor and, to a lesser extent, Fornax and Carina have the most constraining kinematic sample, giving a very small range of allowed values for J and thus the smallest uncertainties (in agreement with the analysis of 2011MNRAS.418.1526C ).
Of all the dwarf galaxies in this sample, Sculptor has the smallest uncertainty in its total J value (about 15 ).

sources.summary: The paper highlights Sculptor as an example of heterogeneous dwarf-galaxy distances, noting many literature measurements and a sizable spread even among recent TRGB estimates.

quotes.quote: As one example, NED reports 35 literature distances to Sculptor, a list that is likely incomplete. Even when limited to the modern era (i.e., CCD detectors after the year 2000), the distance to Sculptor spans nearly a full magnitude in distance modulus Here and in the rest of this work, we use ``distance modulus'' to refer to the quantity = 5 d/ 10 pc , where d is distance. (e.g., kovacs2001, huxor2015 ), as illustrated in Figure fig:sculptor_distances . Narrowing this comparison to the same distance indicator (e.g., TRGB) and even more contemporary data (i.e., post-2010), the distance modulus to Sculptor varies from =19.46 to 19.70 (Figure fig:sculptor_distances ; e.g., menzies2011, gorski2011, stringer_identifying_2021 ).

gmw_00003153.name: cosmos330
gmw_00003153.overview: Located at a redshift of z = 0.044, cosmos330 is an isolated, barred spiral galaxy featuring an inner ring and a substantial bulge [arXiv:1511.04185]. It hosts a bright, heavily obscured Compton-thick active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1511.04185, arXiv:2311.04832] that exhibits an Fe K emission line [arXiv:1511.04185].

sources.summary: The paper identifies Source ID 330 as a local Compton-thick AGN in a barred spiral galaxy, using NuSTAR hard X-ray data to reveal heavy absorption and Fe K emission.

quotes.quote: In section 6, we present in detail the spectral analysis of source ID 330 at z =0.044, with the most extreme hardness ratio in the whole sample. Source ID 330 is a new CT AGN in the local Universe.

The spectrum of ID 330, shown in Fig. id330 (top) along with the best-fit model and residuals, shows heavy absorption, a prominent Fe K line and a significant scattered component at energies < 4 keV. The Cstat/dof obtained with the baseline model is significantly better than the one reported for the first model (91.3/99 versus 176.4/98), indicating a better fit. According to the baseline model, this source would be classified as CT with N_ H =1.6 10^ 24 cm ^ 2 and = 2.1 .

In the optical, this source is a barred, isolated, spiral galaxy with an inner ring (Figure id330 , bottom panel) and a substantial bulge as classified by Hernandez-Toledo et al. (2010). Contrary to what was found for the obscured ECDFS source J033202-274650 (see also Civano et al. 2005), the X/O of ID 330 is very low, as already discussed in Section xo , using both 3-8 keV \ and/or 2-10 keV \ fluxes (star symbol in Figure xo ). Overall, having a low X/O , source ID 330 could then be classified as an XBONG at least using fluxes below 10 keV, while using higher energies fluxes could have been recognized as an AGN, being at the edge of the AGN locus in the 8-24 keV band (star symbol in Figure xo , bottom panel).

sources.summary: The paper identifies cnuid330 as a bright Compton-thick AGN and uses HEX-P wide-survey simulations to test how CTK-cover affects its hard-band spectra and posterior constraints.

quotes.quote: As presented by , two of the three sources (nuid117 and cnuid330) are classified as CT, and one (cnuid272) is obscured but Compton-thin. Using the best-fit uxclumpy models found by BXA, we simulated HEX-P spectra for both the LET and HET. For the bright CT source (cnuid330), we simulated exposure times consistent with the proposed wide field survey (50, 60, and 100 ks; see Figure areaexpo , salmon curves) which is likely to detect similarly bright CT sources.

The effects of different values of CTK-cover for these X-ray faint obscured AGN (nuid117 and cnuid330) are clearly observable in HEX-P spectra. The corresponding constraints on CTK-cover are plotted against N_ H in Figure fig:CT_constraint for simulations of nuid117 and cnuid330 using the 500 ks and 100 ks exposures. For cnuid330 in the wide survey (100 ks), the constraints are 0.06 ^ + 0.496 _ 0.064 (true CTK-cover =0.0 ), 0.37 ^ + 0.23 _ 0.36 (true CTK-cover =0.3 ), and 0.59 ^ + 0.01 _ 0.57 (true CTK-cover =0.6 ).

sources.summary: The paper models cosmos330 while accounting for a nearby contaminant, recovers parameters for lid1791, and states that the source remains classified as Compton-thick.

quotes.quote: cosmos330 : The \ extraction radius includes two sources separated by 26 arcsec: lid1791 (the identified low-energy counterpart) and lid1792. In the 3-8 keV band the counterpart is a factor 2-2.5 (a factor of 2 in the \ collected net counts) brighter than the latter contaminant source. We fit the cosmos330 spectra jointly with the \ spectrum of lid1792 to account for its contamination and recover the intrinsic spectral parameters for lid1791. We find that the spectral parameters of cosmos330 do not appreciably vary and that the source is still classified as CT.

gmw_00003154.name: ecdfs5
gmw_00003154.overview: ecdfs5 is an active galactic nucleus that is detected as an ambiguous NuSTAR source [arXiv:1801.04280]. It is modeled with two possible unabsorbed counterparts, including a low-redshift counterpart at z=0.141 that dominates the hard-band flux [arXiv:1801.04280].

sources.summary: The paper treats ecdfs5 as an ambiguous NuSTAR source with two possible counterparts, both unabsorbed, and adopts low-redshift ID 103 because it dominates the hard-band flux.

quotes.quote: ecdfs5 : This source does not have unique counterparts in M15. There is one at low redshift ( z=0.141 ; \ ID 103) and one at high-redshift ( z=1.957 ; \ ID 100). Their separation is 22 arcsec which is smaller than the \ spectral extraction radius. We therefore tried a joint modeling of these two sources with the \ data.

Both sources are unabsorbed with 2.1 . The \ spectra in the common 3-8 keV band have normalizations which are higher than the \ spectra by factors of 2 and 5.4 for ID 103 and ID 100, respectively. In the 8-24 keV band, ID 103 has a flux 3.5 times larger than ID 100. We therefore assume that ID 103 is the correct low-energy counterpart and used it in our analysis.

gmw_00003155.name: egs26
gmw_00003155.overview: The active galactic nucleus egs26 exhibits a flat, unabsorbed spectrum under simple power-law fitting [arXiv:1801.04280]. However, a dual-absorber model provides a better fit, indicating a second absorption component from an inhomogeneous cold medium at larger scales [arXiv:1801.04280].

sources.summary: The paper finds egs26 has a flat unabsorbed spectrum under simple fitting, but a dual-absorber model gives more reasonable reflection and absorption parameters.

quotes.quote: egs26 : The spectrum of this source is flat and unabsorbed. A fit with an absorbed power-law returns a best-fit =0.9 with negligible column density for which we place an upper limit at 20.3 with Wstat/dof=472.31/433 . Our baseline parametrization returns an apparently better fit with Wstat/dof=442.9/431 with =2.37 , 21.3 and R 67 . The reflection parameter value is extremely high and unphysical.

We therefore tried to add a dual-absorber modeling e.g., C1999,D2004 to the primary power-law, i.e., a further absorption component given by an inhomogenous cold medium at larger scales by employing the model zpcfabs . With this parametrization, we obtained an even better fit ( Wstat/dof=424.4/429 ) with more reasonable parameters (as reported in Table specparambase ): =1.56 , 21.1 and R<0.18 . For the second absorber we find =23.40_ 0.02 ^ +0.05 and a covering fraction f_c=0.73 0.03 .

gmw_00003156.name: ser243
gmw_00003156.overview: The active galactic nucleus ser243 exhibits a significantly flat X-ray spectrum [arXiv:1801.04280]. It appears to require a scattered component, which becomes steeper if the primary photon index is frozen to its canonical value [arXiv:1801.04280].

sources.summary: The paper reports ser243 has a significantly flat spectrum, appears to need a scattered component, and yields higher reflection when the photon index is fixed.

quotes.quote: ser243 : This source has flatter than the canonical value at high significance (see Table specparambase ). It also seems to require a scattered component (in \ data at energies below 0.5 keV) with _ sc 2.4 . Freezing the primary photon index to the canonical =1.8 makes the scattered component steeper, _ sc 3 . In this case the reflection strength raises from R<0.74 to R=1.1 .

gmw_00003157.name: ser37
gmw_00003157.overview: The active galactic nucleus ser37 exhibits an X-ray spectrum that appears very flat and unabsorbed in simple power-law fits [arXiv:1801.04280]. However, acceptable modeling of this source requires the inclusion of a warm, partially ionized partial-covering absorber [arXiv:1801.04280].

sources.summary: The paper finds ser37 is very flat and unabsorbed in simple fits, requires additional warm partial-covering absorption, and otherwise gives unphysical reflection parameters.

quotes.quote: ser37 : The spectrum of this source is very flat and unabsorbed. A fit with an absorbed power-law returns =0.2 and negligible column density. However the modeling is not acceptable, with strong residuals across the broad-band and fit statistics of Wstat/dof=345.04/218 . The baseline modeling yields a much better parametrization with Wstat/dof=225.77/217 .

The best-fit parameters are 2.5 , 19.9 and R=10.3 . Both and R are too high. Therefore, as done with egs26 , we tried a dual absorber model modifying the primary power-law with cold and partially ionized partial covering absorbers ( zpcfabs and zxipcf , respectively). The model which gives the better parametrization in terms of fit statistics and reasonable parameters (see Table specparambase ) is obtained using the warm ionized absorption model and imposing =1.8 . For the absorber we obtained the following parameters: =22.80_ 0.03 ^ +0.02 , f_c=0.969_ 0.006 ^ +0.003 and a ionization parameter ( / erg cm s^ 1 )=-0.55_ 0.19 ^ +0.10 . Spectra and best-fit model are reported in Fig. spectra_addabs .

gmw_00003158.name: ser267
gmw_00003158.overview: The active galactic nucleus ser267 is located at a redshift of z=0.131 [arXiv:1801.04280]. Although its optical spectrum displays broad lines indicating a Type 1 classification, X-ray observations show a potential Fe K residual that may suggest the presence of an obscured active nucleus [arXiv:1801.04280].

sources.summary: The paper treats ser267 as usually unabsorbed but notes a possible Fe K residual, broad optical lines, and the need for better data to assess obscuration.

quotes.quote: On the other hand, in the \ spectra (i.e., in both focal plane modules) we find evidence of a strong residual at an energy of 5.7 keV (10 net-counts in both focal plane modules). The significance of this feature if modelled with a Gaussian line is at the 2 level (based on ^2 confidence contours on line energy and normalization). The line can be modelled by a 6.4 keV Fe K at the redshift z=0.131 of the source.

In our best-fit baseline parametrization the line has an observed EW 1.4 keV. The low quality \ spectrum is consistent with this best-fit line solution. This may be an indication that the source hosts an obscured AGN. We mention however that the optical spectrum from SDSS shows broad lines pointing to a Type 1 classification for this source.

gmw_00003159.name: ser148
gmw_00003159.overview: The active galactic nucleus ser148 exhibits a flat X-ray spectrum with strong residuals at low energies [arXiv:1801.04280]. Its spectrum does not require cold absorption or reflection, but is well represented by the addition of a warm absorber [arXiv:1801.04280].

sources.summary: The paper finds ser148 has a flat simple power-law spectrum with soft residuals, no required cold absorption, and a better warm-absorption representation.

quotes.quote: ser148 : A simple power-law model shows a flat spectrum with =1.4 with strong residuals at low energies ( Wstat/dof=3143.79/1261 ). A simple cold absorption is not required. The baseline model does not improve the modeling. Reflection is not required as the large residuals are at soft energies ( <1-2 keV). We therefore further tried additional warm absorption ( zxipcf ) on the primary component and obtained a good representation of the spectrum (see Table specparambase ) with the following warm absorber parameters: =22.76 0.01 , f_c=1.0 and a ionization parameter ( / erg cm s^ 1 )=1.39 0.05 . Spectra and best-fit model are reported in Fig. spectra_addabs .

gmw_00003160.name: ser261
gmw_00003160.overview: The object ser261 is a faint, high-redshift active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2311.04832] that is heavily obscured [arXiv:2311.04832] or absorbed [arXiv:1801.04280]. Its optical counterpart in SDSS shows evidence of broad emission lines [arXiv:1801.04280].

sources.summary: The paper treats nuid117 as a faint, high-redshift obscured AGN whose CTK-cover is unconstrained in previous data but whose HEX-P simulations show observable hard-band spectral effects.

quotes.quote: For the faint and higher redshift AGN (nuid117 and cnuid272), CTK-cover is not constrained with previous data. HEX-P 's effective area beyond 20 keV will allow us to discern the subtle effects (e.g., a general sharpening of the Compton hump at > 10 keV) that CTK-cover has on the hard-band spectra of these AGN. To show this, we have simulated obscured HEX-P spectra with CTK-cover = 0.0, 0.3, and 0.6 .

The effects of different values of CTK-cover for these X-ray faint obscured AGN (nuid117 and cnuid330) are clearly observable in HEX-P spectra. The corresponding constraints on CTK-cover are plotted against N_ H in Figure fig:CT_constraint for simulations of nuid117 and cnuid330 using the 500 ks and 100 ks exposures.

sources.summary: The paper says ser261 lacks low-energy XMM or Chandra data, may be a heavily absorbed source if the Swift counterpart is correct, and has broad optical lines.

quotes.quote: ser261 : No observations at low energy are available for this sources from either \ or . There are short observations ( <10 ks ) from Swift XRT in which a source is barely detected 10-20 arcsec from the \ position. If this is the right low-energy counterpart it is difficult to model the joint Swift XRT- \ spectrum. It results in a heavily absorbed source (from \ data) with a large scattered component (from Swift ). We mention that the SDSS spectrum of the optical counterpart shows evidence of broad emission lines.

gmw_00003161.name: LBQS 1203+1530
gmw_00003161.overview: LBQS 1203+1530 is a high-ionization broad-absorption line quasar that is weakly detected in the hard X-ray band [arXiv:1804.05074]. The source exhibits a soft spectral shape and hard-band flux weakness, making it a candidate for an intrinsically weak active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1804.05074].

sources.summary: The paper identifies LBQS 1203+1530 as a HiBAL quasar with soft X-ray shape, hard-band weakness by about 14.7, weak hard-band detection, and intrinsic X-ray weakness candidacy.

quotes.quote: In particular, two sources ( LBQS 1203+1530 and LBQS 1442-0011 , magenta points in Figures fig-aox-l2500 and fig-daox ) lie outside the shaded regions and close to the slanted line, showing significant hard-band flux weakness and soft spectral shapes. For LBQS 1203+1530 ( LBQS 1442-0011 ), the effective power-law photon index is _ eff = 2.2^ +0.9 _ 0.9 ( _ eff =1.9_ 0.8 ^ +0.9 ), and the factor of hard-band flux weakness ( f_ weak ) is 14.7 (11.9). Therefore, we suggest that these two sources are good candidates for intrinsically \ weak AGNs.

Based on the current \ observational data, LBQS 1203+1530 and LBQS 1442-0011 are only weakly detected in the hard band. In both cases, there are only two counts in the source apertures, and the binomial no-source probabilities are P_ B 0.033 and 0.019 (corresponding to 2.1 and 2.3 detections) for LBQS 1203+1530 and LBQS 1442-0011 , respectively. Thus, there are considerable uncertainties on the effective power-law photon indices ( _ eff ) and the factors of hard-band \ weakness ( f_ weak ), as shown in Figure fig-daox , Table tbl-xbasic , and Table tbl-aox . Additional \ or XMM Newton observations are required in order to improve the detection significance, better constrain the spectral shapes and hard-band flux weakness, and confirm the nature of these two objects as intrinsically \ weak AGNs.

gmw_00003162.name: LBQS 1442-0011
