gmw_00003162.overview: LBQS 1442-0011 is a broad-absorption line quasar that is only weakly detected in the hard X-ray band [arXiv:1804.05074]. It exhibits a soft spectral shape and hard-band flux weakness, making it a candidate for an intrinsically weak active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1804.05074].

sources.summary: The paper identifies LBQS 1442-0011 as a HiBAL quasar with soft X-ray shape, hard-band weakness by about 11.9, weak hard-band detection, and intrinsic X-ray weakness candidacy.

quotes.quote: In particular, two sources ( LBQS 1203+1530 and LBQS 1442-0011 , magenta points in Figures fig-aox-l2500 and fig-daox ) lie outside the shaded regions and close to the slanted line, showing significant hard-band flux weakness and soft spectral shapes. For LBQS 1203+1530 ( LBQS 1442-0011 ), the effective power-law photon index is _ eff = 2.2^ +0.9 _ 0.9 ( _ eff =1.9_ 0.8 ^ +0.9 ), and the factor of hard-band flux weakness ( f_ weak ) is 14.7 (11.9). Therefore, we suggest that these two sources are good candidates for intrinsically \ weak AGNs.

Based on the current \ observational data, LBQS 1203+1530 and LBQS 1442-0011 are only weakly detected in the hard band. In both cases, there are only two counts in the source apertures, and the binomial no-source probabilities are P_ B 0.033 and 0.019 (corresponding to 2.1 and 2.3 detections) for LBQS 1203+1530 and LBQS 1442-0011 , respectively. Thus, there are considerable uncertainties on the effective power-law photon indices ( _ eff ) and the factors of hard-band \ weakness ( f_ weak ), as shown in Figure fig-daox , Table tbl-xbasic , and Table tbl-aox . Additional \ or XMM Newton observations are required in order to improve the detection significance, better constrain the spectral shapes and hard-band flux weakness, and confirm the nature of these two objects as intrinsically \ weak AGNs.

gmw_00003163.name: LBQS 2201-1834
gmw_00003163.overview: LBQS 2201-1834 is a broad-absorption line quasar and active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1804.05074]. The source remains undetected in both soft and hard X-ray bands, suggesting it may be an intrinsically weak active nucleus [arXiv:1804.05074].

sources.summary: The paper reports LBQS 2201-1834 remains undetected in soft and hard bands, lacks a spectral fit, may be intrinsically X-ray weak, and needs deeper observations.

quotes.quote: The other target ( LBQS 2201-1834 ) is undetected in both the soft and hard bands.

Only spectra with more than 4 counts are useful, and thus we did not fit the spectrum of LBQS 2201-1834 . The total numbers of counts in the combined spectra of the other targets span a range of 7--34. The best-fit _ XSPEC values and their 1 errors are listed in column 6 of Table tbl-xbasic . These _ XSPEC values are in general consistent with the _ eff values, considering their large uncertainties. In the following analyses and discussions, we adopted the _ eff values; using _ XSPEC instead would not change our results. We converted the observed source count rates to fluxes using a power-law spectrum model with a photon index of _ eff and the spectral response files. The errors on the fluxes or flux densities were propagated from the errors on the net counts. We used the measured _ eff value and the soft-band flux to derive a 2 keV flux density ( f_ 2keV,soft ), and computed the soft-band derived optical-to-X-ray slope ( _ OX,soft ). Additionally, we used =2 and the hard-band flux to calculate a hard-band derived 2 keV flux density ( f_ 2keV,hard ), and computed the hard-band derived _ OX,hard (see Footnote 10). The flux density at rest-frame 2500 ( f_ 2500 ) and the 2500 monochromatic luminosity ( l_ 2500 ) used to compute _ OX,soft and _ OX,hard were adopted from . The errors of _ OX,soft ( _ OX,hard ) were propagated from the errors of f_ 2keV,soft ( f_ 2keV,hard ). For the one undetected source ( LBQS 2201-1834 ), we assumed _ eff =1 to calculate the upper limits on fluxes and f_ 2keV,soft (and subsequently _ OX,soft ) because of its BAL-quasar nature, which suggests a hard spectrum due to absorption . We used =2 and the hard-band flux upper limit to calculate the upper limit on f_ 2keV,hard (and subsequently _ OX,hard ).
Moreover, LBQS 2201-1834 is still undetected in the soft and hard bands. A deeper \ observation is required to constrain its \ spectral shape and determine if it is an intrinsically \ weak AGN.

gmw_00003164.name: PHL 1811
gmw_00003164.overview: PHL 1811 is a nearby, radio-quiet, narrow-line type 1 quasar [arXiv:0808.2603, arXiv:1503.02085] that is intrinsically X-ray weak [arXiv:0808.2603, arXiv:1804.05074] and exhibits a steep X-ray continuum [arXiv:0902.1366, arXiv:1804.05074]. Its UV spectrum features exceptionally weak high-ionization emission lines [arXiv:0902.1366, arXiv:1503.02085] and two associated absorption-line systems [arXiv:1307.6799].

sources.summary: PHL 1811 is described as a nearby narrow-line type 1 QSO that lacks BAL and strong X-ray absorption, remains persistently X-ray weak, and has an unusually blue, weak-line UV spectrum.

quotes.quote: The nearby ( z = 0.192 ), narrow-line type 1 QSO PHL 1811 shows no evidence of BALs in its UV spectrum. With an upper limit for a neutral X-ray absorbing column of N_H < 8.7 10^ 20 cm ^ 2 , the source is not strongly X-ray absorbed lhjgcp07 . It has been consistently X-ray weak since it was first observed almost 20 years ago with ROSAT , although the X-ray flux has varied by a factor 5. This variation (small compared to the degree of X-ray weakness) suggested to lhjgcp07 that the X-ray emission was not scattered into view, as the medium responsible for the scattering would need to be implausibly small. PHL 1811 therefore appears to be an intrinsically weak X-ray emitter.

The optical/UV spectrum of PHL 1811 is also unusual lhjc07 . It is very blue and shows no forbidden or semi-forbidden line emission. The C 4 1549 line emission is weak by a factor of 5 compared to the composite spectrum of fhfcwm91 .

sources.summary: The paper compares PHL 1811 with high-redshift WLQs, noting similar weak high-ionization emission, an exceptionally weak C IV line, proposed high accretion rate, and steep X-ray continuum.

quotes.quote: Figure  templates shows a comparison between the UV spectrum of one of these low-redshift WLQs, PHL 1811, and the mean UV spectrum of ten of our high-redshift WLQs (i.e., excluding the Keck spectrum of SDSS J1335 + 3533) alongside the mean UV spectrum of `typical' SDSS quasars. Overall, the mean spectrum of our high-redshift WLQs appears similar to the spectrum of PHL 1811, and the high-ionization emission lines in both spectra are significantly weaker compared to the mean SDSS spectrum. The Ly + N 5 complex in PHL 1811 has EW =24 , which is somewhat larger than our definition of WLQs (EW <10 ), but the C 4 emission line in that source is exceptionally weak with EW =6.6 \ (Leighly 2007b).

A high accretion rate has also been proposed for at least one of the low-redshift WLQs, PHL 1811, to explain the absence of the high-ionization emission lines (Leighly 2007b). If WLQs are AGNs that constitute the high-end tail of the \ distribution, then they may also be expected to exhibit steep hard- \ continua (see, e.g., Brandt 1997; Shemmer 2008). For example, high-quality \ spectra of two of the low-redshift WLQs, PG 1407 + 265 and PHL 1811, indeed show relatively steep \ continua at rest-frame energies 2 keV, with photon indices of =2.19 0.07 (Piconcelli 2005) and =2.28^ +0.12 _ 0.11 (Leighly 2007a), respectively.

sources.summary: The paper uses PHL 1811 as a benchmark intrinsically X-ray weak type 1 quasar and reports its recent NuSTAR hard-band weakness lacks strong Compton reflection.

quotes.quote: One well-studied example is PHL 1811, which is a bright nearby ( z=0.192 ) type 1 quasar with a B band magnitude of 13.9. Its observed \ emission is weaker than the expectation from the _ OX - L_ 2500 relation by a factor of 30-100 , and its soft \ spectrum (with a power-law photon index 2.3 ) and short-term \ variability argue against an absorption scenario . Thus it is believed to be intrinsically \ weak.

We have also analyzed a recent 55 ks \ observation (observation ID 60101004002) of PHL 1811 following the basic procedure in . There is a weak detection of this source in the 3--8 keV band, but no (or at most a marginal) detection in the 8--24 keV band, indicating that there is no strong Compton-reflection component emerging in its hard \ spectrum.

sources.summary: PHL 1811 has two associated absorption-line systems, including a blended system where Ly reaches zero flux and another doublet-detected system with additional absorber context.

quotes.quote: PHL 1811 ( =0.1765,0.1919) -- We detect two associated absorption-line systems in the STIS spectrum of this quasar, which were previously reported by tripp08 . The =0.1765 \ absorption-line system, previously reported by jenkins03 from Lyman series, , and absorption from a FUSE spectrum, is detected in Ly \ and Ly , and the weaker member of the 1031.926, 1037.617 doublet (Figure fig:phl1811a ). The stronger member is wiped out by the Galactic Ly \ line, while the weaker member sits on the red damping wing. The blending precludes testing for partial coverage. However, we note that Ly \ reaches zero flux, indicating full coverage in that ion.

sources.summary: The paper treats PHL 1811 as the prototype intrinsically X-ray weak radio-quiet quasar, with unusual UV emission and soft ionizing continuum motivating the PHL 1811 analog sample.

quotes.quote: The best-studied intrinsically X-ray weak AGN is the type 1 quasar PHL 1811, a very bright ( B=13.9 ) radio-quiet quasar at z=0.19 e.g., Leighly2007b,Leighly2007 . It is X-ray weak by a factor of 30 -100 relative to expectations from the _ OX - L_ 2500 relation, and its X-ray weakness is likely intrinsic instead of being due to absorption given its canonical X-ray spectrum (power-law photon index =2.3 0.1 ), lack of detectable photoelectric X-ray absorption, and short timescale X-ray variability Leighly2007 . Interestingly, PHL 1811 also has an unusual UV spectrum Leighly2007b , which is dominated by strong Fe ii and Fe iii emission with very weak high-ionization lines.

Based on photoionization modeling, Leighly2007b suggested that many of the unusual emission-line properties of PHL 1811 are a result of the soft optical-to-X-ray ionizing continuum caused by the intrinsic X-ray weakness. The intriguing possible connection between the extreme emission-line and X-ray properties of PHL 1811 prompted a search for more such X-ray weak quasars using UV emission-line selection criteria ( Wu2011 , hereafter W11).

sources.summary: The paper presents PHL 1811 as the best-studied intrinsically X-ray weak narrow-line quasar, with weak X-rays, steep spectrum, little absorption evidence, and unusual UV line emission.

quotes.quote: The nearby ( z=0.192 ) narrow-line type 1 quasar PHL 1811 is the best-studied example of this class Leighly2007b . The X-ray luminosity of PHL 1811 is a factor of 30--100 times weaker than that expected from the _ OX - L_ 2500 relation. The steep X-ray spectrum (with photon index =2.3 0.1 ), lack of evidence for intrinsic X-ray and UV absorption, and short-term X-ray flux variability by a factor of 5 strongly suggest that PHL 1811 is intrinsically X-ray weak Leighly2007b . The optical/UV line emission of PHL 1811 is also unusual (e.g., no forbidden or semiforbidden lines, very strong Fe ii and Fe iii , unusual very low-ionization lines, and very weak high-ionization lines; see Leighly2007a ). Its C iv emission line is blueshifted and asymmetric, and it has a rest-frame equivalent width (REW) of only 6.6 , about 5 times smaller than that in the composite quasar spectrum Vanden Berk2001 .

sources.summary: The paper describes PHL 1811 as a radio-quiet high-luminosity NLS1 quasar whose seasonal variability can rival 3C 273 and which shows day-scale optical variations weaker than PDS 456.

quotes.quote: All three have comparable absolute magnitudes, but PDS 456 and PHL 1811 are radio quiet. PDS 456 is a broad-line object, but PHL 1811 could be classified as a high-luminosity Narrow-Line Seyfert 1 (NLS1). Both of the radio-quiet quasars show significant variability on a timescale of a few days. The seasonal rms V-band variability amplitudes of 3C 273 and PDS 456 are indistinguishable, and the seasonal rms variability amplitude of PHL 1811 was only exceeded by 3C 273 once in 30 years of monitoring.
PHL 1811 also shows significant variations over a day or two, although the amplitude of short-term variability is not as great for PHL 1811 as for PDS 456.

gmw_00003165.name: central Brightest Cluster Galaxy (BCG)
gmw_00003165.overview: The central Brightest Cluster Galaxy (BCG) is a radio galaxy located at the X-ray luminous core of its cluster [arXiv:2001.02365]. It is surrounded by diffuse, steep-spectrum radio emission that may represent an intermediate radio halo transitioning into a mini-halo [arXiv:2001.02365].

sources.summary: The paper identifies the SPT2031 central BCG as the radio/optical counterpart at the X-ray core, surrounded by a steep-spectrum diffuse halo possibly linked to mini-halo evolution.

quotes.quote: The GMRT 325 MHz radio contours reveal the diffuse radio emission surrounding the central radio galaxy, typical of radio mini-halos, but the emission has similar east-west stretch as in the X-ray, roughly tracing the possible merger axis like giant radio halos. The position of this radio galaxy coincides with the X-ray luminous core, and the Hubble Space Telescope (HST) optical image clearly shows the BCG as the optical counterpart. The BCG emission was also detected at the 150 MHz TIFR GMRT Sky Survey (TGSS; Intema2017 ) with a flux density of S_ 150 = 232.6 24.3 mJy and at the 843 MHz Sydney University Molonglo Sky Survey Bock1999,Mauch2003 with a flux density of S_ 843 = 42.1 1.8 mJy.

Since the cool core is not destroyed and the radio BCG is coincident with cool core, the late stage of this merger event can trigger sloshing motion in the ICM, resulting in a mini-halo emission. Consequently, the diffuse emission in SPT2031 of 0.7 Mpc size surrounding the BCG in a WCC system can be classified as a steep spectrum intermediate \ radio halo vanWeeren2019 which is in transition into a possible future mini-halo. Also, the SPT2031 cluster is massive enough to host a radio mini-halo ( >6 10^ 14 M_ ; Giacintucci2017 ) and the radio BCG coincides with the X-ray brightness peak, suggesting that this cluster may have harboured radio mini-halo in the past, but it is not possible to verify this possibility with the current observational data.

gmw_00003166.name: GHOSTS I
gmw_00003166.overview: GHOSTS I is a very isolated dwarf irregular galaxy candidate located approximately 12 Mpc away [arXiv:1312.0602]. It is a low-luminosity, low-metallicity system with a half-light radius of 226 pc that appears to be actively forming stars [arXiv:1312.0602].

sources.summary: The paper reports GHOSTS I as a newly discovered, very isolated faint dwarf irregular candidate at 12 Mpc, with resolved stars, low luminosity, low metallicity, and a 226 pc half-light radius.

quotes.quote: We report the discovery of a new faint dwarf galaxy, GHOSTS I, using HST/ACS data from one of our GHOSTS (Galaxy Halos, Outer disks, Substructure, Thick disk, and Star clusters) fields. Its detected individual stars populate an approximately one magnitude range of its luminosity function (LF). Using synthetic color-magnitude diagrams (CMDs) to compare with the galaxy's CMD, we find that the colors and magnitudes of GHOSTS I's individual stars are most consistent with being young helium-burning and asymptotic giant branch stars at a distance of 12 2 Mpc. Morphologically, GHOSTS I appears to be actively forming stars, so we tentatively classify it as a dwarf irregular (dIrr) galaxy, although future HST observations deep enough to resolve a larger magnitude range in its LF are required to make a more secure classification.

GHOSTS I's absolute magnitude is M_ V 9.85^ +0.40 _ 0.33 , making it one of the least luminous dIrr galaxies known, and its metallicity is lower than [ Fe/H ] = -1.5 dex. The half-light radius of GHOSTS I is 226 38 pc and its ellipticity is 0.47 0.07 , similar to Milky Way and M31 dwarf satellites at comparable luminosity. There are no luminous massive galaxies or galaxy clusters within 4 Mpc from GHOSTS I that could be considered as its host, making it a very isolated dwarf galaxy in the Local Universe.

gmw_00003167.name: J0945+1737
gmw_00003167.overview: J0945+1737 is a quasar host galaxy that features a bent, 2.1 kpc radio jet-like structure [arXiv:2311.03453]. Its molecular CO emission is confined to a central, contiguous region [arXiv:2311.03453], while a 10 kpc ionized bubble is impacted by the radio source [arXiv:2103.06805].

sources.summary: The paper studies J0945+1737 as a quasar host with a bent 2.1 kpc jet-like structure; its CO emission is confined to the central region.

quotes.quote: For J0945+1737, we also make use of the 1.5 GHz e-MERLIN image from , which has a beam size of 0.2 - 0.3 arcsec. This image highlights the 2.1 kpc bent jet-like structure in this source.
In two of the galaxies, J0945+1737 (panel a1) and J1430+1339 (panel d1), we see that the CO emission is only observed in a central, contiguous region.

Using this systematic approach, for J0945+1737 and J1430+1339, we do not identify any extended molecular gas structures away from the central emission. This confirms the observation made from the total CO emission-line images shown in Figure fig2: all4targets . These two quasars are hence not included in our analysis pertaining to the extended CO gas structures.

sources.summary: J0945+17 shows a compact-scale jet or lobe candidate, a radio component 11 kpc from the AGN, and a 10 kpc ionised bubble impacted by the radio source.

quotes.quote: The jet sizes are in the range 1-few kpc (J0945+17, J1000+12, J1356+10, the Teacup), 10 kpc (J1517+33), several tens of kpc (J0841+01, J0853+38, J0907+46). Additional extended AGN related radio structures (e.g. lobes or radio bubbles, plumes, hot spots) are detected in J0853+38 (472 kpc), J0841+01 (40 kpc), J1000+12 (43 kpc), J1356+10 (160 kpc), the Teacup (19 kpc). The radio component at 11 kpc from the AGN in J0945+17 may be a jet or a lobe.

We have identified a 10 kpc edge-brightened bubble on the W side of J0945+17 and, possibly, a counter-bubble to the E (Appendix results-objects2 , Fig. fig0945 ). The radio source is currently impacting on the western bubble and is enhancing the optical line emission at the location of the ``cloud'' identified by . A past radio activity episode or a wide angle wind could have inflated the bubble(s).

gmw_00003168.name: J1000+1242
gmw_00003168.overview: J1000+1242 is an obscured, radio-quiet quasar [arXiv:2403.01258, arXiv:2311.03453] undergoing a merger event indicated by elongated tidal tails [arXiv:2403.01258] and two distinct nuclear emission sources [arXiv:2403.01258]. The galaxy features a kiloparsec-scale radio jet [arXiv:2103.06805], five extended molecular gas filaments [arXiv:2311.03453], and an ionized gas outflow [arXiv:2403.01258].

sources.summary: The paper describes J1000+1242 as an obscured quasar in a merger, with tidal tails, possible dual nuclei, and a radio-aligned OIII outflow tied to nuclear radio emission.

quotes.quote: J1000+1242 is an obscured quasar at z 0.148 (1 '' 3.5 kpc). The ionized gas morphology shows unambiguous evidence of a merger event, as indicated by the elongated tidal tails (Fig. MOM012_J1000-1242 ). In addition, data from HST /WC3 F621M (Fig. HST ) reveal two distinct nuclear emission sources separated by approximately 1.5 kpc ( Jarvis2019 ). These sources could either represent the double AGN resulting from the merger or the two opposite sides of the NLR obscured by a dusty circumnuclear ring. However, they are not spatially resolved in our MUSE data.

The [ OIII ] broad component shows a velocity gradient of 350 km/s that extends north-south for 8-10 kpc. It is oriented like the radio jet and consistent with the slight [ OIII ] line asymmetry seen in . We interpret the presence of the gradient of the [ OIII ] broad component, whose direction is consistent with the radio jet, as an outflow launched by the nuclear radio emission, which might demonstrating a direct connection between the radio jet and the outflows.

sources.summary: The paper finds the radio-quiet quasar host J1000+1242 has molecular CO structures beyond its central region, identifying five filaments extending 5-11 kpc at 100-190 km/s.

quotes.quote: As shown in Figure fig1: paramplot , 4/9 sources with ALMA data meet this criteria; namely, J0945+1439, J1000+1242, J1010+1413, and J1430+1339. Table tab1: litprops lists the basic properties of these four targets. The CO maps and radio images are shown for these four targets in Figure fig2: all4targets (center-panel; see Section sec: datared_alma and Section sec: datared_rad for details). All four targets are bright AGN with high, quasar-like bolometric luminosities of (L _ bol /erg s ^ 1 ) = 45.3 - 46.2 (from the fitting of the spectral energy distributions; jarvis19 ). These four targets are all classified as `radio quiet' based on the L _ 1.4GHz versus L _ O III criteria of .
However, for the other two galaxies, J1000+1242 (panel b1) and J1010+1413 (panel c1), in addition to the central molecular gas, we see molecular CO structures outside of the central regions.
For J1000+1242, across the 5 filaments, there is a radial extent range of R _ fil = 5 - 11 kpc and velocities of V _ fil = | 100 - 190 | km s ^ 1 .

sources.summary: J1000+12 is reported to have a few-kiloparsec jet, 43 kpc AGN-related radio structure, ionised tidal features, and kinematic evidence for a giant ionised bubble.

quotes.quote: The jet sizes are in the range 1-few kpc (J0945+17, J1000+12, J1356+10, the Teacup), 10 kpc (J1517+33), several tens of kpc (J0841+01, J0853+38, J0907+46). Additional extended AGN related radio structures (e.g. lobes or radio bubbles, plumes, hot spots) are detected in J0853+38 (472 kpc), J0841+01 (40 kpc), J1000+12 (43 kpc), J1356+10 (160 kpc), the Teacup (19 kpc).

Many of the large scale ( 10 kpc) ionised features identified in our sample are remnants of the interaction/process. In some cases, they trace entire or partial tidal tails, bridges or shells (e.g. J0841+01, J0907+46, J1000+12, J1316+44 and J1356+10; see also Villar2010,Villar2017 ). The extended ionised gas frequently overlaps partially with wide large and amorphous scale continuum halos clearly associated with a merging or interaction event (e.g. J0802+25, J1437+30, J1356+10, the Teacup). Five QSO2 in our sample present firm or tentative evidence for giant bubbles of ionised gas: J0841+01, J0945+17, J1000+06, J1356+10 and the Teacup. The sizes 10 2 kpc are similar in all of them. The Teacup ( Keel2012 ), with clear morphological evidence, and J1356+10 and J1000+12, based on kinematic evidence, have been discussed by other authors ( Greene2012,Jarvis2019 ).

sources.summary: J1000+1242 stands out among the modeled detections for different temperature-density properties, the lowest line ratios, and r21 and r32 below one.

quotes.quote: J1000+1242 is the only target showing different properties of the temperature and density.
This is significant as J1000+1242 has the lowest line ratios amongst the 5 and is the only one with r_ 21 < 1.
On the other hand, for J1000+1242 with both r_ 21 and r_ 32 < 1, this was reduced to 25K.

gmw_00003169.name: J1010+1413
gmw_00003169.overview: J1010+1413 is a late-stage merger remnant [arXiv:1907.03757] hosting a highly luminous, radio-quiet quasar [arXiv:2311.03453, arXiv:2403.01258] and a candidate dual active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1907.03757, arXiv:2403.01258]. Beyond its central region, the galaxy features three molecular carbon monoxide filaments [arXiv:2311.03453] and complex merger-driven morphology [arXiv:2403.01258].

sources.summary: The paper finds the radio-quiet quasar host J1010+1413 has molecular CO structures beyond its central region, identifying three filaments with 12-13 kpc extents and 220-340 km/s velocities.

quotes.quote: As shown in Figure fig1: paramplot , 4/9 sources with ALMA data meet this criteria; namely, J0945+1439, J1000+1242, J1010+1413, and J1430+1339. Table tab1: litprops lists the basic properties of these four targets. The CO maps and radio images are shown for these four targets in Figure fig2: all4targets (center-panel; see Section sec: datared_alma and Section sec: datared_rad for details). All four targets are bright AGN with high, quasar-like bolometric luminosities of (L _ bol /erg s ^ 1 ) = 45.3 - 46.2 (from the fitting of the spectral energy distributions; jarvis19 ). These four targets are all classified as `radio quiet' based on the L _ 1.4GHz versus L _ O III criteria of .
However, for the other two galaxies, J1000+1242 (panel b1) and J1010+1413 (panel c1), in addition to the central molecular gas, we see molecular CO structures outside of the central regions.
In case of the 3 filaments in J1010+1413, we see a radial extent range of 12 - 13 kpc with comparatively higher velocities of 220 - 340 km s ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper reports SDSSJ101022.96+141300.9 as a z~0.2 late-stage merger remnant hosting two close quasar cores and a candidate bound supermassive black-hole binary.

quotes.quote: SDSSJ101022.96+141300.9 (hereafter, J1010+1413) is a late-stage merging galaxy at z 0.198 (angular scale of 3.27kpc/ '' ) with high equivalent-width emission lines Mullaney13 in the Sloan Digital Sky Survey SDSS; York2000 . At mid-infrared wavelengths (22 m ), it is one of the most luminous systems ( L_ AGN 6 10^ 46 erg s ^ 1 ) at z 0.2 identified with the Wide-field Infrared Survey Explorer (WISE). Using high spatial resolution (0.03 '' /pixel) multi-band imaging from HST's Wide Field Camera 3 (WFC3) instrument, we identified two distinct quasar-produced [ OIII ] 5007-emitting regions that are spatially co-incident with two nuclear stellar cores buried close to the center.

Using HST/WFC3 images, we have discovered a z 0.2 quasar hosted in a merger remnant with two closely separated (0.13 '' or 430pc) continuum cores at the heart of the galaxy SDSSJ1010+1413. The two cores are spatially coincident with two powerful [ OIII ] emitting point sources with quasar-like luminosities ( L_ AGN 5 10^ 46 erg s ^ 1 ), suggesting the presence of a bound SMBH system, each with M_ BH > 4 10^8 . We place an upper limit on the merging timescale of the SMBH pair of 2.5 billion years, roughly the Universe lookback time at z 0.2 .

sources.summary: The paper presents J1010+1413 as a highly luminous quasar and dual-AGN candidate with complex merger-driven morphology, nuclear OIII emission, radio-lobe hotspots, and high-dispersion ionized gas.

quotes.quote: J1010+1413 ( z 0.1992 ) is one of the most luminous quasars at z 0.1–0.2 in [ OIII ] ( L_ [ OIII ] 1.69 10^ 43 erg/s). The HST /WFC3 F621M observation revealed a promising dual AGN candidate with the presence of two distinct [ OIII ] emitting point sources with a projected separation of 430 pc ( Goulding2019 ). This cannot be probed with our MUSE observations (which have a resolution FWHM of 2 kpc), as in the case of J1000+1242.

The morphology and kinematics of J1010+1413 are really complex, as is shown also in the continuum image from HST /WFC3 F689M in Fig. HST . This is probably due to a recent merger ( Goulding2019, Jarvis2021 ). [ OIII ] shows strong emission around the nucleus and two lower-luminous [ OIII ] (as well as in H , which we do not show) hotspots cospatial with the radio lobes (see Figs. MOM012_J1010-1413 and HST ).

gmw_00003170.name: BR 1202 0725
aliases_local: DLA system in the QSO BRI 1202-0725
gmw_00003170.overview: BR 1202 0725 is an interacting system at redshift 4.7 consisting of an optically luminous quasar, a dusty submillimeter starburst galaxy, and two Lyman-alpha emitting galaxies [arXiv:1401.5076, arXiv:1706.03018]. The highly star-bursting system contains massive reservoirs of molecular gas and dust [arXiv:2102.09219, arXiv:astro-ph/0408015], with the quasar host itself exhibiting a strong broad-line region outflow [arXiv:2105.13498].

sources.summary: The paper observes the z=4.69 BR 1202-0725 system with SINFONI, finding no [O II] emission, tight SFR limits, high extinction, and interacting SMG/QSO/LAE components.

quotes.quote: We present deep SINFONI K band integral field spectra of two submillimeter (SMG) galaxy systems: BR 1202-0725 and J1000+0234, at z=4.69 and 4.55 respectively. Spectra extracted for each object in the two systems do not show any signature of the [ O ii ] 3726,29 emission-lines, placing upper flux limits of 3.9 and 2.5 10^ 18 for BR 1202-0725 and J1000+0234, respectively. Using the relation between the star formation rate (SFR) and the luminosity of the [ O ii ] doublet from kennicutt98 , we estimate unobscured SFR upper limits of 10-15 and 30-40 for the objects of the two systems, respectively. For the SMGs, these values are at least two orders of magnitude lower than those derived from SED and IR luminosities. The differences on the SFR values would correspond to internal extinction of, at least, 3.4 - 4.9 and 2.1 - 3.6 mag in the visual for BR 1202-0725 and J1000+0234 SMGs, respectively.

BR 1202-0725 ( z = 4.69 ) is a system composed by a dusty, luminous starburst (the SMG itself), an optically luminous QSO and two Ly emitting extented regions, hereafter Ly 1 and Ly 2 omont96,carilli02 . The QSO and the SMG are separated by 3 8 , with Ly 1 between them ( 2 3 north-west of the QSO), a clear signature of interaction, while Ly 2 lies 2 7 south-west of the QSO (see left panel of Figure hst ). Recent observations of the system using ALMA detects narrow [ C ii ] 158 m emission in all four sources wagg12,carilli13 .

sources.original_name: DLA system in the QSO BRI 1202-0725
aliases_in_paper: Damped (DLA) absorber at redshift z_ abs =4.383 observed toward QSO BRI 1202-0725
sources.summary: The paper studies the high-redshift DLA toward BRI 1202-0725, deriving C, N, O, and Si abundances and arguing its nitrogen pattern implies an older, low-SFR protogalaxy with star formation before z > 6.

quotes.quote: In this paper we present VLT-UVES observations of the QSO BRI 1202-0725 = 4.69, mcmahon whose spectrum shows a DLA at =4.383, detected for the first time by giallo94 , which is one of the few highest-redshift DLA known song:cowie . The paper structure is the following: Section 2 gives details about the observations and the reduction process; in Section 3 we present the analysis of the spectrum and the column densities derived for the metal ions associated with the DLA system. Section 4 is dedicated to the obtained abundance ratios, in particular of carbon, oxygen and silicon, and how they relate to observations in other DLAs. In Section 5 we focus on nitrogen and we discuss its abundance in the framework of the present production models.

The highest redshift system is also the youngest, according to the interpretation of the N abundance, but it shows O abundance similar to our DLA. The observed pattern suggests that the system under study is older but had a lower star formation rate (SFR) generating a smaller amount of oxygen. Then nitrogen might be considered a more reliable age indicator than oxygen, since the production of the latter is more dependent on the SFR. If, according to the chemical evolution models of henry00 with low SFR, the time to attain the upper plateau level is of the order of 500 Myrs, the onset of star formation in the studied protogalaxy is placed at redshift higher than 6.

sources.summary: The paper observes BR 1202 0725 with Herschel/PACS, describing its submillimeter, millimeter, CO, and FIR detections plus a nearby same-redshift companion contributing infrared flux.

quotes.quote: The z=4.69 quasar BR 1202 0725 hereafter 1202 0725 mcm94 was observed in chop-nod mode in the blue (70 m, FWHM 5 5) and red band of the PACS instrument for a total of 2550 sec (AOR length) with an on-source time of 1980 sec. BR 1202 0725 BR 1202 0725 is a well studied quasar at z=4.69 with strong detections at sub-mm and mm wavelengths isa94,omo96b,ben99,ion06 and in CO line emission oht96,omo96a,car02 . At 4 distance to the northwest of the quasar this source shows a secondary component in the dust continuum and in CO.

We detected BR 1202 0725 at 70 m and 160 m with fluxes of 15.0 2.0 mJy and 39.8 3.7 mJy, respectively (Fig. pic_br1202 ), which is consistent with the upper limits from ISO lee01 . In Fig. sed_j1148 we show the spectral flux and energy distributions of J1148+5251 and 1202 0725. The SEDs of both high-redshift QSOs appear very similar in shape, and the strong emission in all infrared bands shows dust at a wide range of temperatures.

For 1202 0725 this may be understood to be due to the inclusion of the companion (which is at the same redshift as the quasar; omo96a , car02 ) and which contributes roughly half of the flux in many infrared and (sub)mm bands e.g. omo96a,hin06,ion06 .

sources.summary: The SE galaxy is the quasar host in BR1202--0725; the paper resolves its CO emission, measures a narrower line than the NW galaxy, and identifies two CO peaks plus a tail.

quotes.quote: Figures 2, 3, and 5 show the CO(5--4) line data from the SE and NW galaxies in detail not previously seen. The most striking result is how dissimilar the spectra are. The CO profile of the SE galaxy, centered at redshift z= 4.6952 , has a FWHM linewidth of V = 360 40 km s ^ 1 (Table 2), comparable to those of other high- z quasar host galaxies detected in CO.

SE galaxy (Fig. 4, left) shows two main CO peaks separated by 0.4 '' to 0.5 '' in position, and by 180 \ in velocity , that may indicate rotation of the molecular disk in the inner = 2 kpc of the quasar host galaxy. There is also evidence of a weaker CO source or streamer extending 3 '' southwest of the SE galaxy. This tail is blueshifted by --100 \ relative to zero velocity in Fig. 4, left, and it has a relatively narrow linewidth (100 to 200 ). This weak CO tail to the southwest does not coincide, however, with the 0.9 mm continuum source reported by Wagg et al. \ (2012).

sources.summary: The paper presents ALMA imaging of the interacting z=4.7 BRI 1202-0725 system, resolving its QSO and SMG components and deriving dust, star-formation, and gas-depletion properties.

quotes.quote: We present the results from Atacama Large Millimeter/submillimeter Array (ALMA) imaging in the \ 205 \ fine-structure line (hereafter ) and the underlying continuum of BRI 1202-0725, an interacting galaxy system at z = 4.7, consisting of an optical QSO, a sub-millimeter galaxy (SMG) and two Lyman- emitters (LAEs), all within 25 kpc of the QSO. We detect the QSO and SMG in both \ and continuum. At the 1 \ (or 6.6 kpc) resolution, both QSO and SMG are resolved in , with the de-convolved major axes of 9 and 14 kpc, respectively. In contrast, their continuum emissions are much more compact and unresolved even at an enhanced resolution of 0.7 .

Our best estimated T_ dust equals 43 \ ( 2) K for both galaxies (assuming an emissivity index = 1.8 ). The resulting L_ CO(7-6) / \ ratios are statistically consistent with that of local luminous infrared galaxies, confirming that L_ CO(7-6) traces the star formation (SF) rate (SFR) in these galaxies. We estimate that the on-going SF of the QSO (SMG) has a SFR of 5.1 (6.9) 10^3 M_ yr ^ 1 ( 30 ) assuming Chabrier initial mass function, takes place within a diameter (at half maximum) of 1.3 (1.5) kpc, and shall consume the existing 5 (5) 10^ 11 M_ of molecular gas in 10 (7) 10^7 years.

sources.summary: The paper finds BR1202-0725 has large gas, stellar, and halo masses, the brightest extended Ly nebula in the sample, and quasar luminosity sufficient to power it.

quotes.quote: We find M_ gas =8 10^ 10 for BR1202-0725, and M_ gas =6.3 10^ 10 and M_ gas =1.9 10^ 10 for G09-0902+0101 and G15-1444-0044, respectively. We find stellar masses of M_ * =6.7^ +5.9 _ 2.1 10^ 10 for BR1202-0725, and M_ * =8.0^ +10.9 _ 2.8 10^ 10 and M_ * =2.3^ +2.7 _ 0.8 10^ 10 for G09-0902+0101 and G15-1444-0044, respectively. Therefore, the expected DM halo masses using the halo mass-stellar mass relation by Moster2018 for the targeted sources are: log( M_ DM / [ M_ ] ) 12.3 for BR1202-0725, log( M_ DM / [ M_ ] ) 12.5 and log( M_ DM / [ M_ ] ) 11.9 for G09-0902+0101 and G15-1444-0044, and log( M_ DM / [ M_ ] ) 11.9 and log( M_ DM / [ M_ ] ) 12.0 for ALESS61.1, and ALESS65.1, respectively.
From these profiles it is evident that the QSO+SMG system BR1202-0725 has a more extended and bright nebula compared to the two SMGs hosting a QSO.

We find that for each quasar system these photons would be able to power the observed nebulae, with the quasar luminosities exceeding by 6.4, 4.3 and 4.0 the nebular luminosities (computed in the same range; Table table:SFRv2 ) for BR1202 0725 , G09 0902 + 0101 and G15 1444 0044 , respectively. Interestingly, the luminosity of the quasar in the BR1202-0725 system is 14 greater than G09 0902 + 0101 and G15 1444 0044 . This calculation aligns well with the observation that the nebula around BR1202-0725 is 9 more luminous than G09 0902 + 0101 and G15 1444 0044 .

sources.summary: The paper describes BR1202--0725 as a z=4.7 quasar complex whose resolved CO, carbon, and dust emission indicates a young, interacting group of highly active starburst galaxies.

quotes.quote: The radio-quiet quasar BR1202--0725 ( z=4.695 ) is a remarkable source with a bright Northwest (NW) companion detected at submm and radio wavelengths but invisible in the optical. In the absence of amplification by gravitational lensing, BR1202--0725 would be the most luminous binary CO and far-IR source in the Universe. In this paper, we report observations with the IRAM Plateau de Bure interferometer of BR1202--0725 in the redshifted emission of the CO(5--4) and (7--6) lines, the [ C I ] ( ^3 P _2-^3 P _1 ) line, a high angular resolution ( 0.3'' 0.8'' ) 1.3 mm map of the rest-frame, far-IR dust continuum, and a search for the CO(11--10) line.

The difference in linewidths and center velocities, and the absence of any lens candidate or arc-like structure in the field, at any wavelength, show that the obscured NW galaxy and the SE quasar host galaxy cannot be lensed images of the same object. Instead, we find morphological and kinematic evidence for sub-structures in both the NW and SE sources. We interpret these results as strong indications that the BR1202--0725 complex is a group of young, interacting, and highly active starburst galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper treats BR1202-0725 as a z=4.7 interacting system containing a QSO, SMG, and LAEs, using fine-structure lines to probe diverse ISM and star-formation conditions.

quotes.quote: We study the properties of the interstellar medium in the interacting system BR1202-0725 at z=4.7 via its and fine--structure line emission. This system consists of a QSO, a sub-mm galaxy (SMG), and two Ly- emitters (LAEs). Such a diversity in galaxy properties makes BR1202-0725 a unique laboratory of star formation and galaxy evolution at high redshift.

In this Letter, we present first observations in BR1202-0725, a system of interacting galaxies at z=4.695 . This system is of particular interest because it encompasses a QSO (South--East), a sub-mm galaxy (SMG, North--West), and two Ly- emitters (LAEs, one located between the QSO and the SMG, the other located South-West of the QSO), all accommodated within 10'' on sky ( 65 kpc at z=4.695 ).
Given the diversity of galaxy types in such a small region of the sky, BR1202-0725 represents a unique laboratory of star formation and galaxy assembly only 1.2 Gyr after the Big Bang.

sources.summary: The paper treats the BRI1202--0725 QSO host as a luminous optical quasar in an extreme starburst galaxy, with FIR dust emission, a massive black hole, and less ordered gas kinematics.

quotes.quote: The system includes two HyLIRGs separated by 4'' : a prototypical, highly dust obscured SMG, and a luminous optical quasar (QSO) in an extreme starburst galaxy. We will refer to the QSO host galaxy as the QSO in what follows. Two Ly- emitting galaxies (LAEs) are also seen in the system ( omonA96,hu96 ). The 340 GHz emission of cari13 and the HST /ACS F775W filter image of deca14 are shown in an overview of the field (Fig. finder ). SMG QSO The first detected component of the system, the QSO, was originally discovered as the highest redshift source in a color-selected (UKST B_J , R, and I plates) survey for high redshift QSOs irwi91 using the Automated Plate Measuring (APM) machine. This discovery in the optical was followed with a detection of FIR emission from thermal dust mcma94 with a 12'' beam. Optical spectra show that it features a central black hole with mass 10^9 M _ , as estimated from the MgII linewidth carn13 .
The velocity field of the QSO is less ordered, but [ CII ] observations suggest a small-scale outflow wagg12,cari13,carn13 .

sources.summary: The paper describes the BRI 1202-0725 QSO as an optically selected high-redshift quasar with extreme FIR luminosity, [NII] detections, broader 122 micron emission, and higher electron density.

quotes.quote: It consists of an optically selected QSO, optically faint SMG, which is located 4'' ( 26 kpc) northwest of the quasar ( Omont1996, Hu1996 ), and two Lyman- selected galaxies in their very vicinity ( Hu1996, Fontana1996, Ohta2000, Salome2012, Carilli2013b, Carniani2013 ) 1 - one between QSO and SMG and the other at 2'' northwest of the QSO . Extremely high FIR luminosities of QSO and SMG ( Omont1996, Iono2006, Yun2000 ) ( 10^ 13 L_ ) imply vigorous star forming activity of 1000 M_ yr ^ 1 .

The derived electron densities are 26^ +12 _ 11 and 134^ +50 _ 39 cm ^ 3 for the SMG and the QSO, respectively. The electron density of the SMG is similar to that of the Galactic Plane and to the average of the local spirals. Higher electron densities by up to a factor of three could however be possible for systematic uncertainties of the line flux estimates. The electron density of the QSO is comparable to high- z star-forming galaxies at z=1.5-2.3 , obtained using rest-frame optical lines and with the lower limits suggested from stacking analysis on lensed starbursts at z=1-3.6 using the same tracer of .

Therefore, we conclude that the different line widths between 122 and 205 for the QSO are likely real. This may indicate higher electron densities at higher velocities for the QSO that we will discuss in the following section.

sources.summary: The paper describes B1202-0725 as a high-redshift ULIRG/QSO system with CO detections, double millimeter and CO morphology, possible interaction, starburst activity, and high far-infrared luminosity.

quotes.quote: B1202-0725 ( z=4.7 ): At z = 4.7 , BR 1202-0725 is observed at a cosmic time of 1.3 Gyr after the Big Bang. It was detected in multiple CO transitions and recently also in CO(1--0) using the GBT (see Riechers et al. 2006, also this volume). This source has the curious property that the optical QSO is a single source, but the mm continuum and CO line observations show a double source with a separation of about 4 '' (Omont et al. \ 1996; Guilloteau et al. \ 1999; Carilli et al. \ 2002).

This is illustrated in the CO imaging shown in Fig. 2. This double morphology may indicate a pair of interacting objects separated by about only 20 kpc (Yun et al. \ 2000) or a QSO exhibiting a massive starburst (southern source) with a dust--obscured, Ly-- emitting companion (northern source, Hu et al. \ 1996) ionized by the strong QSO, which also contains a starburst. The total far-IR luminosity of the ULIRG BR 1202-0725 is L_ FIR = 4.2 10^ 13 L_ , leading to a L_ FIR /L'_ CO(1-0) ratio of 350.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes the BR1202 0725 QSO [C II] and CO line profiles, finding the CO line broader and estimating redshift offsets and F/F values.

quotes.quote: The [ C II ] and CO (J = 5 4 ) spectra for the BR1202 QSO are shown in Fig. figure:1202QSOprofiles . The [ C II ] emission line is best modeled by a single Gaussian with a FWHM of 290 4 km s ^ 1 , whilst the CO (J = 5 4 ) line is described by a single gaussian with a FWHM of 390 30 km s ^ 1 . As such the two profiles are not consistent with one another within their errors, with the CO emission line being 30 broader than the [ C II ] line. Performing a joint fit cc & cc & The difference in the line widths between the [ C II ] and CO (J = 5 4 ) emission lines is demonstrated in the difference in the evidence values when comparing the joint fit to independent models, with a difference of approximately 0.5, corresponding to a 62 probability that the two lines do not share the same line profile.

For the BR1202 0725 QSO our value for F/F of ( 5 15) 10^ 5 results from a velocity offset of 15 45 km s^ 1 , which is consistent with our estimates of the intrinsic velocity dispersion of the two species.

sources.summary: BR1202-QSO is described as a highly star-bursting z=4.7 quasar galaxy with warm, dense neutral gas, high far-infrared luminosity, and detected 145 micron and CO(12--11) line emission.

quotes.quote: The high / ratios and the joint analysis with previous detection of lines for both of the QSO and the SMG suggest the presence of warm and dense neutral gas in these highly star-forming galaxies. This is further supported by new CO (12--11) line detections and a comparison with cosmological simulations.
The BR1202-QSO and BR1202-SMG are highly star-bursting galaxies with L_ FIR of 10^ 13 L_ (corresponding to SFR of 1000 M_ yr ^ 1 ; e.g., Omont1996, Yun2000, Iono2006 ).
In this following paper, we report _ 145 line detections from both the QSO and the SMG. By adding these two detections, the total number of the _ 145 detection at z>4 has now reached five.

sources.summary: The paper reports a high-resolution spectrum of the z 4.7 quasar BR 1202-0725, finding strong blueshifted BLR outflow signatures and multiple C IV and Si IV absorbing components.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we report a strong broad line region (BLR) outflow from the z 4.7 quasar BR 1202-0725 based on the high-resolution optical spectrum taken with the Magellan Inamori Kyocera Echelle (MIKE) spectrograph installed on the 6.5m Magellan/Clay telescope, obtained from the ``Probing the He II re-Ionization ERa via Absorbing C IV Historical Yield'' (HIERACHY) project. This rest-frame ultraviolet (UV) spectrum is characterized by a few significantly blueshifted broad emission lines from high ions; the most significant one is the C IV line at a velocity of 6500  km s^ 1 relative to the H emission line, which is among the highest velocity BLR outflows in observed quasars at z>4 . The measured properties of UV emission lines from different ions, except for O I and Ly , also follow a clear trend that higher ions tend to be broader and outflow at higher average velocities. There are multiple C IV and Si IV absorbing components identified on the blue wings of the corresponding emission lines, which may be produced by either the outflow or the intervening absorbers.

sources.summary: The paper identifies 1202 0727 as a z = 4.69 high-redshift quasar with near-infrared emission far above the low-redshift median, cool dust, and excess warm/hot dust whose heating source is uncertain.

quotes.quote: The highest redshift sources: 1202 0727 ( z = 4.69 ); 1508+5714 ( z = 4.3 ); and 1413+1143 ( z = 2.551 ), are factors of 10--100 brighter in the near-IR than the low- z median SED (Figure fg:hizseds ).

Both sources have large excesses over the median SED in a spectral region that corresponds to warm and hot dust emission. This emission cannot yet be conclusively attributed to a thermal source, but that is a strong possibility, since cool dust is clearly present in 1202 0727 and both objects show a sharp cutoff at wavelengths corresponding to the Wien region of dust near the sublimation temperature. The difficulty with a dust interpretation is the energy source to heat the material; the observed rest-frame optical and near-UV luminosity is clearly too low in both sources.

sources.summary: The paper describes BR 1202 0725 as an optically bright z=4.695 quasar with massive molecular gas and dust; its radio properties and inferred jet are linked to triggered star formation toward 1202B.

quotes.quote: At z=4.695 , BR 1202 0725 is an optically bright ( M_B=-28.5 ) quasar with broad emission lines sto96 and the first high redshift quasar confirmed to host massive reservoirs of molecular gas and dust omo96a,oht96 . Its 1.4 GHz luminosity ( 10^ 25  W Hz ^ 1 ; car02b ; hereafter C02) is consistent with either massive star formation ( 2800   year ^ 1 ; Equation 21 of con92 ) or a Fanaroff-Riley type I radio galaxy fan74 .

However, we find evidence to suggest that there is a relativistic jet emanating from BR 1202 0725 along a PA of about 35 ^ . If correct, a portion of the radio emission could be due to synchrotron radiation from the relativistic electrons in the jet and the companion itself the result of jet-induced star formation; hereafter we refer to the quasar as 1202A and the star forming companion as 1202B.

gmw_00003171.name: 1202B
gmw_00003171.overview: 1202B is an optically faint, submillimeter-bright starburst galaxy [arXiv:1909.02284] that acts as a companion to a nearby quasar [arXiv:astro-ph/0408015, arXiv:1607.06755]. The system exhibits extreme star formation [arXiv:1909.02284, arXiv:2102.09219], resolved carbon monoxide emission [arXiv:1207.6722, arXiv:astro-ph/0408015], and kinematics that suggest it may consist of two merging galaxies [arXiv:1207.6722].

sources.summary: The NW galaxy in BR1202--0725 is resolved in CO and dust, has an exceptionally broad CO profile, and is interpreted as an obscured submillimeter merger containing two galaxies.

quotes.quote: Figure 1 shows two different CO(5--4) integrated intensity maps, made separately to best show the SE and NW galaxies, with a limited velocity range for the SE quasar host galaxy, and a much wider velocity range to show the total emission from the NW galaxy. These maps indicate that the CO in the NW galaxy is resolved to the north or northwest, in the direction of the beam minor axis (HPBW 0.7'' ). They also indicate a probable extension or tail somewhat south or slightly southwest of the SE galaxy.

In contrast, the NW galaxy has an extraordinarily broad profile with V_ FWHM 1000 km s ^ 1 and V_ FWZP 1700 km s ^ 1 (Fig. 4, right, and Table 2). These values exceed the CO linewidths normally observed in quasars or galaxies, with some notable exceptions like the 1500 \ wide CO line in the NGC 6240 merger (e.g., Feruglio et al. \ 2012), the high- z quasar-and-multiple-galaxy merger in SMM J02399-0136 (see Ivison et al. \ 2010 and references therein), and the z = 3.8 radio-galaxy merger in 4C41.17 (De Breuck et al. \ 2005).
These two spectral features at 200 and +400 \ suggest that the obscured NW submm object is in reality two merging galaxies, separated by about 0.6 '' (4.0 kpc).

sources.summary: The paper treats the BR1202 0725 SMG as a high-redshift companion galaxy with perturbed [C II], broader CO emission, and a best-fit velocity offset.

quotes.quote: The [ C II ] and CO (J = 5 4 ) spectra for the BR1202 0725 SMG are shown in Fig. figure:1202SMGprofiles . Whilst the previous line profiles have all been roughly Gaussian in nature, the [ C II ] emission line has a much more perturbed shape, with n_ max of 5 required to adequately describe it with FWHM of 700 21 km s ^ 1 . The CO line however is simply described by a single Gaussian with FWHM 970 90 km s ^ 1 , such that as with the QSO the CO ( 5 4 ) line is approximately 30 broader than the [ C II ] . Performing a joint fit cc & cc & The evidence heavily favors two independent line shapes, with a difference in the log evidence of 20, corresponding to a probability of > 99.99 that the emission lines do not share the same profile. Due to the non-Gaussian nature of their shape, comparing the line centroids for independent models makes little sense, as the centers are at that stage largely arbitrary, however forcing a single profile upon the pair we find a best fit described by n_ max of 3, with FWHM of 700 26 km s ^ 1 .
Finally for the BR1202 0725 SMG the best fit model we found a velocity offset of 120 60 km s ^ 1 , whilst the marginalised posterior had multiple peaks between a range of 375 and +30 km s ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies the SMG in BRI 1202-0725 as an optically faint submillimeter-bright galaxy with detected [NII] lines, low electron density, compact emission, and extreme star formation.

quotes.quote: It consists of an optically selected QSO, optically faint SMG, which is located 4'' ( 26 kpc) northwest of the quasar ( Omont1996, Hu1996 ), and two Lyman- selected galaxies in their very vicinity ( Hu1996, Fontana1996, Ohta2000, Salome2012, Carilli2013b, Carniani2013 ) 1 - one between QSO and SMG and the other at 2'' northwest of the QSO . Extremely high FIR luminosities of QSO and SMG ( Omont1996, Iono2006, Yun2000 ) ( 10^ 13 L_ ) imply vigorous star forming activity of 1000 M_ yr ^ 1 .

The derived electron densities are 26^ +12 _ 11 and 134^ +50 _ 39 cm ^ 3 for the SMG and the QSO, respectively. The electron density of the SMG is similar to that of the Galactic Plane and to the average of the local spirals. Higher electron densities by up to a factor of three could however be possible for systematic uncertainties of the line flux estimates.
We could constrain the size only for the QSO, which is 0''.43( 0''.15) 0''.23 ( 0''.18) and obtain the upper limit for the SMG, which is 0''.38 0''.23 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies 1202B as a dusty star-forming companion north of BR 1202 0725, with strong radio-inferred star formation, resolved CO emission, and a broad CO profile consistent with jet-triggered collapse.

quotes.quote: The northern source, almost twice as luminous as the quasar at 1.4 GHz, coincides with a dusty, star forming companion (C02) which is undetected at optical wavelengths. If the radio emission from the companion is dominated by star formation, the corresponding star formation rate is a titanic 4400   year ^ 1 ; more than three times the rate implied from the submm emission (Equation 26 of con92 ). However, we find evidence to suggest that there is a relativistic jet emanating from BR 1202 0725 along a PA of about 35 ^ . If correct, a portion of the radio emission could be due to synchrotron radiation from the relativistic electrons in the jet and the companion itself the result of jet-induced star formation; hereafter we refer to the quasar as 1202A and the star forming companion as 1202B.

VLA imaging (C02) with 2.6'' 1.8'' resolution reveals unresolved CO(2-1) emission at both 1202A and 1202B, whereas higher resolution observations ( 0.3'' 0.21'' ) only detect 1202A confidently, suggesting 1202B has been resolved. The CO line profile at 1202A is both stronger and narrower than at 1202B.

sources.summary: The paper describes the BRI1202--0725 SMG as a distinct HyLIRG companion to the QSO, with mm/CO detections, extreme FIR luminosity, and [CII] kinematics suggesting rotation.

quotes.quote: The SMG companion was first discovered in the 1.25 mm observations of omonN96 . These 2'' resolution images revealed mm continuum and CO( J=5 4 ) emission in the SMG and QSO, showing that they are a pair of distinct galaxies cari02 . Spectral energy distribution (SED) fits of iono06 yield FIR luminosities of 1.2 10^ 13 L _ and 3.8 10^ 13 L _ for the SMG and QSO, respectively, indicating that both galaxies are HyLIRGS independently.

The [ CII ] imaging in particular shows an ordered velocity gradient across the SMG, suggesting rotation on scales of a few kpc cari13 . If this is the case, the dynamical mass is M _ dyn 4 10^ 11 M _ .

sources.summary: BR1202-SMG is described as a highly star-bursting z=4.7 submillimeter galaxy with warm, dense neutral gas, high far-infrared luminosity, and detected 145 micron and CO(12--11) line emission.

quotes.quote: The high / ratios and the joint analysis with previous detection of lines for both of the QSO and the SMG suggest the presence of warm and dense neutral gas in these highly star-forming galaxies. This is further supported by new CO (12--11) line detections and a comparison with cosmological simulations.
The BR1202-QSO and BR1202-SMG are highly star-bursting galaxies with L_ FIR of 10^ 13 L_ (corresponding to SFR of 1000 M_ yr ^ 1 ; e.g., Omont1996, Yun2000, Iono2006 ).
In this following paper, we report _ 145 line detections from both the QSO and the SMG. By adding these two detections, the total number of the _ 145 detection at z>4 has now reached five.

sources.summary: The paper treats J1205 0742 as unusual: its concave spectrum is attributed to nuclear starburst radio emission rather than a radio-loud AGN.

quotes.quote: J1205 0742 The spectrum of J1205 0742 (Fig. fig:J1205 0742 ) is concave, with evidence of variability at 1.4 GHz. Using its spectral index between 1.4 and 350 GHz, morphology, brightness temperature and linear size, MCP2005(V) showed that the radio emission from J1205 0742 is from a nuclear starburst, and that the source does not have a radio-loud AGN. This explains why J1205 0742 has a concave spectrum. At _ o <100 GHz, the negative spectral index is caused by starburst-driven radio synchrotron emission, while at _ o 100 GHz _ 570 GHz, the increase in flux density is the result of thermal dust emission e.g. 1994MNRAS.267L...9M,2000ApJ...528..171Y,2005AJ....129.1809M,2011A&A...536A...7P .

gmw_00003172.name: NGC 7715
gmw_00003172.overview: NGC 7715 is a lower-mass companion galaxy in an interacting pair with NGC 7714 [arXiv:2209.08147, arXiv:0908.2619]. The galaxy features a faint eastern tidal tail [arXiv:1406.6345, arXiv:0908.2619] and is in a post-starburst phase with no ongoing massive star formation in its nucleus [arXiv:1406.6345, arXiv:0908.2619].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 7715 as NGC 7714's companion: prior work found a bridge, postburst phase and tidal tail, and the authors link NGC 7714's stellar populations to this interaction.

quotes.quote: This galaxy is in interaction/merger with its companion NGC 7715, and such interaction could be responsible for the star formation bursts kinney93 . In fact, lancon01 show the bridge between NGC 7714 and its companion NGC 7715 (see their Fig. 2).

bern93 has presented an evolutionary model for the pair NGC 7714 and NGC 7715 and stated that NGC 7715 is in the postburst phase, starting its star formation around 10 ^8 yr ago, similar to our results. A tidal tail between these two galaxies can bee seen in smwa92 , and we suggest that the SP distribution northward (increasing intermediate age SP component) can be related to this poststarburst population in NGC 7715. In fact, such an age is consistent with the scenario proposed by rosa99 , where the presence of red super giants is associated with the Ca ii triplet, present in the galaxy spectrum. Besides, this result is further supported by the fact that this is the side in which NGC 7714 interacted with NGC 7715 in the past ss97,ss03 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 7715 as the smaller companion in the NGC 7714 interacting pair and applies the pair to constraints on powerful extraterrestrial transmitters.

quotes.quote: The field BL J233623.1+020609.0 provides a good example of an interacting pair of galaxies - NGC 7714 at z = 0.009333 and NGC 7715 at z = 0.009243 . NGC7714 (also known as Arp 284) is a prototypical starburst galaxy that is interacting with its smaller neighbour NGC 7715 - the average star formation rate is 1 M _ y^ 1 and its properties are comparable to M82 (see Lancon2001 and references therein).
Fig. f2 shows the field BL J233623.1+020609.0, containing NGC 7714 and NGC 7715.

For example, in the case of NGC 7714 and 7715, no evidence is found for transmitters with an EIRP in excess of 7.5 10^ 23 W. We define the prevalence of extraterrestrial transmitters as the fraction of stars in this interacting system that host a powerful ( EIRP > 7.5 10^ 23 W) long-duty cycle transmitter at the time the observations were made. For this particular system, the prevalence is < 10^ 12 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7715 as Arp 284's lower-mass companion; its nucleus lacks current massive star formation, while models connect its eastern tail to bridge and NGC 7714 tail material.

quotes.quote: The peculiar morphology of the Arp 284 system results from an off-centre collision between two disc galaxies, with the primary (NGC 7714) having roughly three times the mass of the companion (NGC 7715).

Nucleus of NGC 7715 The lack of significant H emission in the NGC 7715 nucleus indicates that there is no ongoing massive star formation gon95,smi97 . Emission from the 21 cm radio line is also minimal, and offset to the north of the optical galaxy. The nucleus is comparatively bright in the red continuum, suggesting an aged stellar population smi97 . Spectral studies of the nucleus show a post-starburst spectrum, again confirming the lack of ongoing star formation ber93 . Eastern Tail of NGC 7715 * (Feature 6) The eastern tail of NGC 7715 is faintly seen in optical images. Radio maps show an counterpart, offset slightly to the north b23,smi97 . Massive star formation is thought to be weak based on the lack of H emission. This feature has not been studied in detail, and it falls outside the field of the HST images. This poor coverage is unfortunate, because the models suggest that this tail curves behind the NGC 7715 disc, passes behind the bridge, and comes back into view as the faint outer southern tail of NGC 7714 str03 . In this scenario, the tail contains a significant fraction of the original mass from the NGC 7715 disc str03 .

gmw_00003173.name: SDSSJ134420.86+663717.8
gmw_00003173.overview: SDSSJ134420.86+663717.8 is an ongoing major merger of two nearly orthogonal spiral galaxies of approximately equal masses [arXiv:2005.12888]. The system features an extended tidal tail [arXiv:1204.3162], a star-forming nucleus [arXiv:2005.12888], and a second nucleus with Seyfert active galactic nucleus properties [arXiv:2005.12888, arXiv:1204.3162].

sources.summary: The paper concludes SDSSJ134420.86+663717.8 is an ongoing merger of two nearly orthogonal spiral galaxies; spectroscopy finds nucleus A AGN-like, nucleus B star-forming, and simulation reproduces the system.

quotes.quote: The striking similarities between the model and the actual galaxies show that we are on the right path, and SDSS J134420.86+663717.8 could indeed be a product of an orthogonal interaction between two spiral galaxies. We present the optical long-slit spectroscopic data of SDSS J134420.86+663717.8, a pair of interacting galaxies. We study their optical spectra and analyse the nature of the two galaxies that form the object. Using diagnostic diagrams and based on the presence of broad Hydrogen recombination lines, we conclude that the mechanism responsible for emission in the host of nucleus A is AGN-like, with Seyfert 1 properties, whereas nucleus B appears to be star-forming in nature.

Additionally, we use a simple N-body simulation to understand the possible initial conditions of SDSS J134420.86+663717.8. With initial parameters of R_p=22.5  kpc , i_1=-85^ , _1=-10^ , i_2=10^ , and _2=0^ , the outcome of the simulation resembles our object rather closely. We thus conclude that SDSS J134420.86+663717.8 is an ongoing merger of two almost orthogonal spiral galaxies with approximately equal masses.

sources.summary: The paper describes SDSS J134420.87+663717.6 as a Seyfert 1.5 major merger with an extended tidal tail, possible double nucleus, and IR-luminous star-formation-dominated radio emission.

quotes.quote: SDSS J134420.87+663717.6 (13): IRAS 13428+6652 has been identified as Seyfert 1.5 by . It appears to be a major merger with an extended tidal tail and possibly two nuclei. It is suitable for AO assisted studies ( SDSS J134427.10+663705.2 ). The source has been detected at 20 cm by NVSS. However, we could not detect any compact flux density at 18 cm with MERLIN.
Since it is an IR luminous galaxy ( L_ FIR 5 10^ 11 L_ ), the radio emission is most likely dominated by extended star formation.

gmw_00003174.name: Antlia 2
gmw_00003174.overview: Antlia 2 is a giant Milky Way satellite galaxy located at a distance of 124.1 kpc [arXiv:2112.08467]. It contains hundreds of RR Lyrae stars and anomalous Cepheids, which reveal a clear distance gradient along its semi-major axis likely caused by ongoing tidal disruption [arXiv:2112.08467].

sources.summary: The paper discusses Antlia 2 as a giant Milky Way satellite with hundreds of RR Lyrae and anomalous Cepheids, large spatial extent, Oosterhoff-intermediate variables, a 124.1 kpc distance, and tidal elongation.

quotes.quote: deg.) covering the Antlia 2 satellite galaxy. The sample of variables includes 318 RR Lyrae stars and eight anomalous Cepheids in the galaxy.

We found RR Lyrae stars over the full search area, suggesting that the galaxy is very large and likely extends beyond our observed area. The mean period of the in Antlia 2 is 0.599 days, while the have a mean period of 0.368 days, indicating the galaxy is an Oosterhoff-intermediate system. The distance to Antlia 2 based on the RR Lyrae stars is 124.1 kpc ( _0=20.47 ) with a dispersion of 5.4 kpc. We measured a clear distance gradient along the semi-major axis of the galaxy, with the South-East side of Antlia 2 being 13 kpc farther away from the North-West side. This elongation along the line of sight is likely due to the ongoing tidal disruption of Ant 2.

gmw_00003175.name: NGC 1371
gmw_00003175.overview: NGC 1371 is a large barred spiral galaxy in the Eridanus group [arXiv:astro-ph/0503542] that features a short bar, inner and outer rings, and an extended neutral hydrogen disk with a central hole [arXiv:2303.00626, arXiv:2304.00515]. It hosts a low-luminosity active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2303.00626] that powers linear, kpc-scale radio jets [arXiv:astro-ph/0503542, arXiv:2303.00626].

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 1371 as a large SABa Eridanus galaxy with radio excess, bulge-dominated optical light, HII-region emission, a nuclear source, and jet-like radio structure indicating an AGN.

quotes.quote: NGC 1371 (SABa) NGC 1371 has more than 5 times excess radio emission compared to that expected from the radio-FIR correlation. It is among one of the largest (D _ B25 38 kpc) galaxies in the Eridanus group. The optical luminosity ( L_ B = 2 10^ 10 L _ ) is dominated by the bulge.

The H image shows emission from HII regions in the spiral arms, weak diffuse emission in the bulge, a nuclear source and a ring-like structure surrounding the nucleus. The GMRT radio continuum morphology (Fig. fig:atlas1 ) shows kpc-scale linear jet like structure and a bright radio source coincident with the H nuclear source. No H emission is seen corresponding to the extended radio emission. Therefore, it is unlikely that the extended radio emission is due to the star formation activities, and could be due to a radio jet. The radio morphology, the presence of a nuclear point source in radio and in H and a significant radio excess indicate an AGN in this galaxy.

sources.summary: NGC 1367 is a mixed-bar spiral with extended HI and double radio lobes/jets; its radio jets are offset from the bar, coincide with an HI hole, and accompany a low-luminosity AGN.

quotes.quote: NGC 1367 is classified as a slightly inclined mixed-bar spiral galaxy with a mixed inner ring and star forming outer ring. The HI deficiency parameter with respect to its expected HI mass for this galaxy in not measured due to R band contamination as well as missing a large amount of extended HI flux. Murugeshan2021 however do find this galaxy to be slightly HI deficient with respect to its integrated HI disk stability. NGC 1367 is a large galaxy with an extremely extended HI diameter of 22.3 arcmin as observed by the 9.1' observations by the Green Bank Telescope Sorgho2019 , however WALLABY only recovers an HI diameter of 9.9' and 83 of the total HI mass. We observe double radio lobes and jets in this galaxy.
The radio jets of this galaxy have half the extent of the inner stellar disk, lie slightly offset from the bar, and coincide with the HI hole.

NGC 1367 also appears in the Chandra X-ray survey and contains a nuclear source with surrounding diffuse emission which is analysed in detail see NGC 1371; Hughes2007 with the conclusion that NGC 1367 contains a low-luminosity AGN similar to other nearby spiral galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1371 as a short-barred ring galaxy with extended HI, a central HI hole, mild warping, and HI/SFR enhancement near resonances.

quotes.quote: NGC 1371: Classified as a SAB(rs)a, this galaxy is observed to possess a short bar, inner and outer spiral arms. The 1 gas is much more extended than the stellar disc, but a prominent central 1 depression/hole is observed and the 1 is observed to trace the outer spiral arms as well. The 1 velocity map shows ordered motion with slight warping observed towards the outskirts of the 1 disc (see bottom left panels in Fig. fig:mosaic2 ). Only the oUHR and OLR locations can be derived for this galaxy based on the Lindblad curves. We find that an inner ring-like structure corresponds to the location of the oUHR (pink ellipse) and an outer ring-like spiral structure is observed just outside the predicted OLR location (orange ellipse). We also observe the co-location of an 1 ring just outside the OLR radius and an enhancement in both _ 1 and _ SFR .

gmw_00003176.name: IC 1953
gmw_00003176.overview: IC 1953 is a face-on barred spiral galaxy with a bar, grand spiral arms, and a mixed inner ring [arXiv:2303.00626, arXiv:astro-ph/0503542]. The galaxy exhibits star formation in its core and arms, with a nuclear radio excess that suggests a recent nuclear starburst [arXiv:2303.00626, arXiv:astro-ph/0503542].

sources.summary: IC 1953 is described as a face-on barred spiral with HI deficiency, an HI/W3PAH extension toward ESO 548-G036, core and arm star formation, and little q12 asymmetry.

quotes.quote: IC 1953 IC 1953 is classified as a face-on barred spiral galaxy with a mixed inner ring. It is determined to be HI deficient in comparison to both its expected HI mass and integrated HI disk stability. As shown in Fig. fig:Murphy1 there is an HI extension towards ESO 548-G036 that lies to the south-west. This is suggestive of interaction between these galaxies, since Wangs2022 measure this galaxy is only weakly perturbed by the interaction. The radio continuum emission is primarily produced in the core of the galaxy but we do also see localised emission in the arms of the galaxy both of which coincide with increased HI density suggesting SF is occurring in these areas.

An extension towards the south-west is also seen in the W3PAH emission in the direction of ESO 548-G036 tracing the HI extension. The q_ 12 map for this galaxy does not show any evidence of environmental effects or asymmetries.

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 1953 as an SBd galaxy with grand spiral arms and a bar; its disk radio emission matches infrared emission while the nuclear radio excess suggests a recent nuclear starburst.

quotes.quote: IC 1953 (SBd) IC 1953 has grand spiral arms and a prominent bar. The radio continuum morphology consists of diffuse emission from the disk and a nuclear point source (Fig. fig:atlas3 ). Using high resolution (kpc-scale) mid-infrared images from ISO (Infrared Space Observatory) observations and high resolution radio continuum images from the VLA observations, Roussel et al. (2003) showed that while the disk radio continuum emission in IC 1953 is consistent with the disk infrared emission, the nuclear radio emission is not. According to them, it is suggestive of a recent nuclear star-burst.

gmw_00003177.name: CGCG 137-068
gmw_00003177.overview: CGCG 137-068 is a face-on, barred dwarf spiral galaxy [arXiv:1808.00969, arXiv:2308.07381] located approximately 60 to 63 Mpc away [arXiv:1810.10720, arXiv:2210.01144]. It is a normal star-forming galaxy [arXiv:1903.00477, arXiv:1906.05446] featuring flocculent spiral arms [arXiv:2210.01144], a possible weak active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1808.00969], and a high-density gas ring that may indicate a recent interaction [arXiv:1903.00477, arXiv:2308.07381].

sources.summary: CGCG137-068 is examined as AT 2018cow's host; its atomic gas is regular, not concentrated near the transient, with low gas mass for its SFR and bar-associated star formation.

quotes.quote: We provide resolved atomic gas observations of the host galaxy, CGCG137-068, of the unusual, poorly-understood transient searching for clues to understand its nature. We test whether it is consistent with a recent inflow of atomic gas from the intergalactic medium, as suggested for host galaxies of gamma-ray bursts (GRBs) and some supernovae (SNe). We observed the hyperfine structure line of the host with the Giant Metrewave Radio Telescope. There is no unusual atomic gas concentration near the position of . The gas distribution is much more regular than those of GRB/SN hosts.

The host has an atomic gas mass lower by 0.24 dex than predicted from its star formation rate (SFR) and is at the lower edge of the galaxy main sequence. In the continuum we detected the emission of and of a star-forming region in the north-eastern part of the bar (away from ). This region hosts a third of the galaxy's SFR.

sources.summary: CGCG 137-068 is the AT2018cow host at z=0.01406 and 63 Mpc; prior ALMA work and this study describe molecular gas, active star formation, flocculent arms, and mapped ionized/stellar components.

quotes.quote: For AT2018cow, M2019 used data from the Atacama Large Millimeter/submillimeter Array (ALMA) to show that its host galaxy is rich in molecular gas and has active star formation.

In this paper, we carry out a comprehensive environmental analysis of AT2018cow. We use a combined dataset from ALMA, VLT/MUSE and HST/WFC3 to probe the molecular gas, ionized gas and stellar populations in its environment and, with a variety of techniques, to derive their accurate parameters. We also try to identify the relationship between AT2018cow and the environmental components. Our aims are to constrain the properties of AT2018cow's progenitor and to explore the nature of its mysterious late-time brightness. Throughout this paper, we adopt a redshift of 0.01406 and a distance of 63 Mpc for the host galaxy, CGCG 137-068, and a Galactic extinction of A_V^ mw = 0.24 mag galebv.ref and an explosion date of MJD = 58284.79 for AT2018cow (all consistent with Xiang2021 ).

The galaxy has a bright nuclei and very flocculent spiral arms. The arms are only marginally depicted by the young stars and H ii regions, and can hardly be seen in the spatial distribution of molecular gas.

sources.summary: The paper maps CGCG 137 068 in H I, finding normal dwarf-galaxy global properties but a high-column-density asymmetric ring suggestive of interaction-driven star formation.

quotes.quote: We report Giant Metrewave Radio Telescope (GMRT) H i 21 cm imaging of CGCG 137 068, the host galaxy of the fast-evolving luminous transient (FELT) AT2018cow. This is the first study of the gas properties of a FELT host galaxy. We obtain a total H i mass of (6.6 0.9) 10^8 M _ for the host galaxy, which implies an atomic gas depletion time of 3 Gyr and a gas-to-stellar mass ratio of 0.47 , consistent with values in normal star-forming dwarf galaxies.

At spatial resolutions of 6 kpc, the H i of CGCG 137 068 appears to be distributed in a disk, in mostly regular rotation. However, at spatial resolutions of 2 kpc, the highest column density H i is found to lie in an asymmetric ring around the central regions, with H i column densities 10^ 21 cm ^ 2 ; AT2018cow lies within this high column density ring. This H i ring could be suggestive of an interaction between CGCG 137 068 and a companion galaxy.
Overall, both the global stellar and properties of CGCG 137 068 are consistent with its being a normal star-forming dwarf galaxy, on the main sequence.

sources.summary: The paper studies the molecular gas and star-formation properties of the AT2018cow host, finding a normal star-forming dwarf galaxy with detected CO, substantial molecular gas, and high metallicity.

quotes.quote: Molecular gas of CGCG 137-068 Morokuma-Matsui et al. ALMA observations of molecular gas in the host galaxy of Kana Morokuma-Matsui kanamoro@ioa.s.u-tokyo.ac.jp 0000-0003-3932-0952 Kana Morokuma-Matsui JSPS Fellow Institute of Astronomy, Graduate School of Science, The University of Tokyo, 2-21-1 Osawa, Mitaka, Tokyo 181-0015, Japan 0000-0001-7449-4814 Tomoki Morokuma Institute of Astronomy, Graduate School of Science, The University of Tokyo, 2-21-1 Osawa, Mitaka, Tokyo 181-0015, Japan 0000-0001-8537-3153 Nozomu Tominaga Department of Physics, Faculty of Science and Engineering, Konan University, 8-9-1 Okamoto, Kobe, Hyogo 658-8501, Japan 0000-0001-6469-8725 Bunyo Hatsukade Institute of Astronomy, Graduate School of Science, The University of Tokyo, 2-21-1 Osawa, Mitaka, Tokyo 181-0015, Japan 0000-0002-9321-7406 Masao Hayashi National Astronomical Observatory of Japan, 2-21-1 Osawa, Mitaka, Tokyo 181-8588, Japan 0000-0003-4807-8117 Yoichi Tamura Department of Physics, Nagoya University, Furo-cho, Chikusa-ku, Nagoya 464-8602, Japan Yuichi Matsuda National Astronomical Observatory of Japan, 2-21-1 Osawa, Mitaka, Tokyo 181-8588, Japan Graduate University for Advanced Studies (SOKENDAI), Osawa 2-21-1, Mitaka, Tokyo 181-8588 Kazuhito Motogi Graduate School of Sciences and Technology for Innovation, Yamaguchi University, Yoshida 1677-1, Yamaguchi, Yamaguchi 753-8512, Japan 0000-0002-8169-3579 Kotaro Niinuma Graduate School of Sciences and Technology for Innovation, Yamaguchi University, Yoshida 1677-1, Yamaguchi, Yamaguchi 753-8512, Japan Masahiro Konishi Institute of Astronomy, Graduate School of Science, The University of Tokyo, 2-21-1 Osawa, Mitaka, Tokyo 181-0015, Japan We investigate the molecular gas in, and star-formation properties of, the host galaxy ( ) of a mysterious transient, , at kpc and larger scales, using archival band-3 data from the Atacama Large Millimeter/submillimeter Array (ALMA).

CO( J =1-0) emission is successfully detected. The total molecular gas mass inferred from the CO data is (1.85 0.04) 10^8 M _ with the Milky Way CO-to-H _2 conversion factor. The H _2 column density at the site is estimated to be 8.6 10^ 20 cm ^ 2 .

The ALMA data reveal that (1) is a normal star-forming (SF) dwarf galaxy in terms of its molecular gas and star-formation properties and (2) is located between a CO peak and a blue star cluster. These properties suggest on-going star formation and favor the explosion of a massive star as the progenitor of . We also find that has a solar or super-solar metallicity.

sources.summary: The paper describes CGCG 137-068 as AT2018cow's nearby face-on spiral host, with close star-forming regions and debated signs of interaction: a gas ring and tidal tail versus internally produced gas structure.

quotes.quote: The host galaxy CGCG137 068 has a luminosity distance of 63.0 4.4 Mpc (redshift z=0.01404 0.00002) (SDSS DR6; SDSS2008 .
