The host galaxy of \ appears to be a face on spiral system, and there are several (at least two) star-forming regions that lie close to (within 170 parsec) the (projected) position of . Assuming \ lies in the plane of the host galaxy and not above or below it, this provides suggestive evidence for a link between massive star evolutionary processes and \ Lyman2020, MM2019 .

There is evidence of a recent interaction between CGCG 137-068 and a companion galaxy from a ring of high column density gas as well as from a faint tidal tail . If the host galaxy underwent a recent (minor) merger it is conceivable that an IMBH or SMBH, with its nuclear star cluster, is in the process of falling into the center of CGCG 137-068. This, means that a TDE origin of \ remains a viable possibility. However, attributes the presence of the ring of high column density gas reported by to internal processes instead of a galaxy merger/interaction.

sources.summary: The paper identifies CGCG 137-068 as the dwarf star-forming host of AT 2018cow, adopts its redshift and distance, and accounts for host-galaxy contamination in photometry.

quotes.quote: AT 2018cow was discovered on June 16, 2018 by the ATLAS survey as a rapidly evolving transient located within a spiral arm of the dwarf star-forming galaxy CGCG 137-068 at 60 Mpc ( Smartt+2018a,Prentice+2018 ). AT 2018cow is located in the host galaxy CGCG 137-068 ( z=0.0141 ) and we adopt a distance of 60 Mpc as in .

We used a 3 \ source region of extraction to minimize the effects of the contamination from the underlying host-galaxy flux and we manually corrected for imperfections of the astrometric solution of the automatic UVOT pipeline re-aligning the frames. In the absence of template images, we estimated the host galaxy contribution by measuring the host galaxy emission at a similar distance from the nucleus.

Finally, any engine model needs to be naturally compatible with the location of AT 2018cow within a faint spiral arm of the star-forming dwarf spiral galaxy CGCG 137-068 (Figure Fig:Optical ; Perley18 ).

sources.summary: The paper identifies CGCG 137-068 as AT 2018cow's star-forming dwarf host, notes debated local star formation around the transient, and subtracts host-galaxy background for photometry.

quotes.quote: The focus of this study is the first luminous FBOT -- AT 2018cow -- discovered on 2018-06-16 at 10:35:02 UTC (or MJD 58285.441) by the Asteroid Terrestrial-impact Last Alert System 2018ATel11727....1S,2018ApJ...865L...3P , located in the star-forming dwarf galaxy CGCG 137-068 ( z=0.0141 ).

Host galaxy studies have also found ongoing star-formation activities around AT 2018cow Roychowdhury2019,Morokuma-Matsui2019,Lyman2020,Sun2022new , favoring massive star progenitors. (Although Michalowski2019 have argued against associating the star formation with AT 2018cow.)

To account for local background contamination, we applied the same source and background apertures to UVOT images of the host galaxy taken in MJD 58761 (or t 467 days ) and measured the background emission at the location of AT 2018cow. We then subtracted the measured background flux from the fluxes of AT 2018cow obtained in the previous step.

sources.summary: The paper describes CGCG 137-068 as the nearby dwarf barred spiral coincident with AT 2018cow, noting a likely weak AGN, an HII-region source, and the transient's off-nuclear position.

quotes.quote: The first detection of the transient was an image taken at 2018-06-16 10:35:02 UT (MJD 58285.441), appearing as a strikingly bright (14.7 0.1 mag in the ATLAS o band) optical source coincident with the galaxy CGCG 137-068 ( z = 0.0141, d = 60 Mpc We assume h = 0.7, _M = 0.3, _ = 0.7. ; Abolfathi+2018 ).

The transient lies on the sky coincident with the catalogued galaxy CGCG 137-068, an unremarkable dwarf spiral galaxy showing a faint bar and spiral arms (Figure fig:image ). Two sources are present within the SDSS and PS1 pre-imaging: a reddish point source at the galaxy nucleus (likely a weak AGN) and a compact, but not truly pointlike, source approximately 1.9 east-southeast of the transient (probably an HII region). AT 2018cow is located far from the centre of the galaxy (5.9 or 1.7 kpc from the nucleus), and no point or pointlike source is visible at the location of the transient itself.

gmw_00003178.name: NGC 3672
gmw_00003178.overview: NGC 3672 is a barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:1402.2291]. Its spiral arms are located inside their corotation resonance and rotate at a lower pattern speed than the galaxy's bar [arXiv:1402.2291].

sources.summary: The paper compares simulations with NGC 3672, arguing its CO velocity and density patterns match a slow spiral-arm model with arms inside corotation and below the bar pattern speed.

quotes.quote: reg02 presented a velocity map of CO emission from the barred-spiral galaxy NGC 3672, derived from Gaussian fits to the line profiles. Their Figure 6 shows that the observed distributions of in the outer regions, especially the loci of =0 , match those in Model S10G better than in Model F10G presented in Figure fig-vlosmod , suggesting that the spiral arms in NGC 3672 are located inside the their CR. A close inspection of Figure 4a of reg02 (see also Figure 1c of che03 ) reveals that the density profiles of the gaseous arms along a radial cut have a steeper gradient at the inner edge than the outer edge, which also hints that the spiral arms rotate slow. Since the CR of a strong bar is placed just outside the bar end, these all indicate that the spiral arms in NGC 3672 have a lower pattern speed than the bar.

gmw_00003179.name: 3C 210
gmw_00003179.overview: 3C 210 is a radio galaxy located in a high-redshift galaxy cluster characterized by a red sequence [arXiv:1605.03161, arXiv:1605.03196]. The host galaxy is undergoing major interactions with nearby companions and exhibits rest-frame ultraviolet emission from star-forming regions [arXiv:1605.03196].

sources.summary: 3C 210 is described as a radio galaxy in a cluster, with merger activity, UV star-forming blobs, diffuse UV emission, and nearby companion structure.

quotes.quote: 3C 210 (z=1.169) 3C 210 is the eponymous member of the 3C 210 galaxy cluster stanford2002 . The cluster appears in our IR image as an overdense region of red galaxies. The host of 3C210 is clearly undergoing major interactions with nearby companions marco15 . Rest-frame UV emission, in the form of blobs most likely associated with star forming regions, is strongest in the northeastern-most part of the host galaxy. The galaxy immediately to the south of the target also shows a fainter compact UV blob.

sources.summary: The paper uses 3C 210 as a known high-redshift cluster case, finding both its red sequence and source overdensity are recovered by the two cluster-candidate methods.

quotes.quote: One of our targets (namely 3C 210) is known to reside in a well studied high-redshift cluster characterized by the presence of a red sequence stanford02 .

As for the red sequence method in Sect. redsequencemodeling , we test our overdensity method against the two confirmed clusters in our sample, which are associated with 3C 186 and 3C 210. Our second method correctly identifies both fields as cluster candidates because of the presence of a significant overdensity of objects around the radio source.

gmw_00003180.name: 3C68.1
gmw_00003180.overview: 3C68.1 is a bright, compact radio-loud quasar at a redshift of z=1.238 [arXiv:1605.03161, arXiv:1605.03196]. Its field is identified as a cluster candidate, containing a dense concentration of red objects and early-type galaxies [arXiv:1605.03161].

sources.summary: The paper identifies the 3C68.1 field as a cluster candidate by both red-sequence and overdensity methods, with five of nine early-type galaxies lying near the red-sequence models.

quotes.quote: In four cases (2 QSOs and 2 RGs, namely 3C68.1, 3C186, 3C210, 3C300.1) both methods return a positive result.

For the remaining two objects for which both of our methods return a positive result, there is no information in the literature about any association with a cluster of galaxies, to the best of our knowledge. However, we point out that the presence of a RS is very clear from their CMDs in Figs. CMD_QSO and CMD_RG . This is particularly evident in the case of 3C68.1 in which 5 out of the 9 ETGs lie close to the RS models.

In the top left panel the field of 3C68.1 is classified as a cluster by both the RS and the over density methods. The presence of a large number of red objects in this dense field is apparent from visual inspection.

sources.summary: 3C 68.1 is a compact QSO with two nearby eastern sources, radio core and lobes, and unusually red photometry attributed to inclination and dust.

quotes.quote: 3C 68.1 (z=1.238) This QSO appears in our IR image as a bright compact object. There are only two other sources within 8 of the target. These are both smaller, dimmer sources 3.75 and 5.5 to the east. Neither of these extra sources is visible in the corresponding optical image. The radio map of the area shows emission in the core of the quasar, as well as two lobes roughly 10 to the north and 15 to the south.

gmw_00003181.name: 3C230
gmw_00003181.overview: 3C230 is a high-redshift Fanaroff-Riley II radio galaxy [arXiv:1806.11125] classified as a high-excitation radio galaxy [arXiv:1806.11125] with a biconical narrow-line region [arXiv:1605.03196]. It is a double-lobed source [arXiv:1605.03196, arXiv:1806.11125] that may reside in a small galaxy group or an underdetected cluster [arXiv:1605.03161].

sources.summary: The paper classifies 3C230 as a red-sequence cluster candidate without a significant overdensity, suggesting either an underdetected cluster at z 1.5 or a smaller galaxy group.

quotes.quote: Three RGs (3C230, 3C255, 3C257) are identified as cluster candidates by the RS method but they do not show an over density of objects. Even if at least half of the ETGs fall within the area spanned by the models, a RS is not clearly identified in 3C230, since they show no clear linear relationship.
It is possible that these 3 radio sources reside in small groups that include a small population of ETGs, but the evidence for the presence of a cluster is not convincing.

In the top right panel, the field of 3C230 is classified as a cluster according to a RS method, but there is not a significant over density of objects. In this case, it is possible that a cluster is present, but since the object is located at z 1.5 a significant number of the cluster galaxies are below our detection threshold. Alternatively, this object could be located in a group that includes a smaller number of galaxies with respect to rich clusters.

sources.summary: 3C 230 is described as a wide double-lobed radio galaxy whose optical and IR morphology resembles a biconical narrow-line region.

quotes.quote: 3C 230 (z=1.487) This object appears as a wide, double lobed radio galaxy. The very bright object in the center of the field is a foreground star. Just east of this star, a large number of small objects are visible in the IR image within a radius of 10 from our target. The morphology in our optical and IR observations appears quite similar to that shown in the emission line images of s11 , and is reminiscent of the biconical structure observed in the narrow line region of many Type II AGN. The size of the NLR as estimated from our images is 24 kpc.

sources.summary: The paper presents 3CR 230 as a high-redshift FR II HERG with double hotspots, uncertain core registration, optical/radio asymmetry, star-formation structures, and possible cluster candidacy.

quotes.quote: 3CR 230 is an FR II radio galaxy at z =1.487 with a double hotspot at the end of the northern lobe hewitt91 . It is optically classified as a HERG jackson97 . The radio image at 8.4 GHz shows two knots along the southern jet: it was claimed that the radio core position is coincident with the northern one, at R.A. = 9^ h 51^ m 58^ s .90 and = 00^ 01'27''.87 (J2000.0) steinbring11 . However, the comparison with the HST hilbert16 and WISE wright10 images of the sources shows that the optical nucleus is 0 .5 North of this position.

gmw_00003182.name: PS J0125 1012
gmw_00003182.overview: PS J0125 1012 is an unclassified quasar pair consisting of two quasars at a redshift of z=1.22 [arXiv:2307.13729]. The system is modeled as two point sources with an image separation of 1.12 arcseconds, which may represent a dual quasar with a physical separation of 9.3 kiloparsecs [arXiv:2307.13729].

sources.summary: The paper classifies PS J0125 1012 as an unclassified quasar pair, favoring a dual-quasar interpretation after spectra show similar quasars and imaging lacks a significant lens galaxy.

quotes.quote: PS J0125 1012 The NTT/EFOSC2 resolved spectrum shows two quasars at redshift z=1.22 with very similar continua and broad emission lines. We jointly fit the iz Legacy Survey and rzy Pan-STARRS bands. The data is well modelled with only two point sources, and the resulting image separation is 1.12 , compatible with the Gaia measurement. Although the reduced ^2 of the fit can be slightly improved by allowing for an additional Sersic profile, the improvement is not significant and the point sources are pushed further apart, breaking the compatibility with the Gaia separation.

If this system turns out to be a dual quasar, the physical separation would be 9.3 kpc. We suggest this system is a dual quasar, but designate this system as an Unclassified Quasar Pair (UQP) until deeper high-resolution imaging can be obtained.

gmw_00003183.name: PS J0557 2959
gmw_00003183.overview: PS J0557 2959 is a gravitationally lensed quasar system at a redshift of z=2.04 [arXiv:2307.13729]. The system resolved into two quasar images with identical continua and broad emission lines, separated by a lensing galaxy [arXiv:2307.13729].

sources.summary: The paper confirms PS J0557 2959 as a lensed quasar after matching quasar spectra, a Legacy Survey residual, improved Sersic-profile fitting, and Gaia-consistent separation support a lensing galaxy.

quotes.quote: PS J0557 2959 The NTT/EFOSC2 resolved spectrum shows two quasars at redshift z=2.04 with identical continua and broad emission lines. Even though our shallower band 2p2 data is well fitted with two PSFs, the same fit of Legacy Survey data shows a clear sign of a lensing galaxy in the z band data. This residual subsequently disappears when including a Sersic profile, which falls between the quasars. We also note that the PSF separation is 0.783 when fitting without a galaxy, and 0.808 when fitting with a galaxy, the latter of which agrees better with the Gaia separation of 0.810 , providing more evidence for the existence of the lensing galaxy. We therefore conclude that this system is a lensed quasar.

gmw_00003184.name: PS J0909 0749
gmw_00003184.overview: PS J0909 0749 is an unclassified quasar pair at a redshift of z=1.08 [arXiv:2307.13729]. The system is suggested to be a dual quasar with a physical separation of 6.6 kpc, as its spectra show distinct differences and imaging reveals no evidence of a lensing galaxy [arXiv:2307.13729].

sources.summary: The paper designates PS J0909 0749 as an unclassified quasar pair, favoring a dual quasar because spectra differ slightly and imaging finds no lens galaxy.

quotes.quote: PS J0909 0749 The NTT/EFOSC2 resolved spectrum shows two quasars at redshift z=1.08 with distinct differences in the ratio of the continuum and broad lines. There is no evidence for a lensing galaxy in the WFI 2p2 imaging. The simultaneous fit of Legacy Survey iz and Pan-STARRS grizy data with two point sources is good, and does not significantly improve when adding a Sersic profile. The Gaia separation is also recovered without the need for a model containing a galaxy.

Given the slight differences in spectra and lack of lensing galaxy, we suggest this system is a dual quasar with physical separation 6.6 kpc. However we conservatively designate this system as an UQP, needing deeper high-resolution imaging.

gmw_00003185.name: PS J1019 1322
gmw_00003185.overview: PS J1019 1322 is an unclassified quasar pair at a redshift of z=2.32 [arXiv:2307.13729]. The system is suggested to be a gravitationally lensed quasar due to its highly similar emission line profiles, though a lensing galaxy has not yet been detected [arXiv:2307.13729].

sources.summary: The paper lists PS J1019 1322 as an unclassified quasar pair that may be lensed, based on similar quasar spectra but no detected lensing galaxy in available imaging.

quotes.quote: PS J1019 1322 The NTT/EFOSC2 resolved spectrum shows two quasars at redshift z=2.32 with very similar emission line profile shapes. The flux ratio across wavelength is 2.2, with some small variations around the emission lines. There is no evidence for a lensing galaxy in the WFI 2p2 imaging. Similarly, the simultaneous fit of the Pan-STARRS grizy and Legacy Survey griz data with two point sources does not leave significant residuals, and the Gaia separation is recovered as well.
We suggest this system is a lensed quasar given the similarity of the spectra, however a deep image detecting the lensing galaxy is still needed. We designate this system as an UQP.

gmw_00003186.name: PS J1037+0018
gmw_00003186.overview: PS J1037+0018 is a confirmed strong gravitational lensing system containing two highly similar quasars at a redshift of z=2.46 [arXiv:2307.13729]. Both quasar spectra exhibit a damped Lyman-alpha system, and subtracting the point sources in imaging reveals the lensing galaxy located near the fainter quasar image [arXiv:2307.13729].

sources.summary: The paper confirms PS J1037+0018 as a lens, with two similar z=2.46 quasars, a constant flux ratio, a damped Lyman-alpha system, and HSC residuals revealing a lens galaxy.

quotes.quote: PS J1037+0018 The NTT/EFOSC2 resolved spectrum shows two very similar quasars at redshift z=2.46 . The flux ratio is approximately 5.5 across all wavelengths, and both spectra show a damped Lyman-alpha system. The available HSC images are poorly fit with two point sources, but the subsequent introduction of a Sersic profile produces much cleaner residuals. Subtracting the two point sources then reveals the lensing galaxy close to the fainter point source. We designate this system as a lens.

gmw_00003187.name: PS J1225+4831
gmw_00003187.overview: PS J1225+4831 is an unclassified quasar pair consisting of two quasars at a redshift of z=3.09 [arXiv:2307.13729]. If the system is a dual quasar, it has a physical separation of 6.87 kpc, though no lensing galaxy has been detected [arXiv:2307.13729].

sources.summary: The paper keeps PS J1225+4831 as an unclassified quasar pair, noting two z=3.09 quasars, no secure lens galaxy, and a possible 6.87 kpc dual-quasar separation.

quotes.quote: PS J1225+4831 This system has also been discovered by Minghao2023 , who obtained Large Binocular Telescope LUCI1 imaging with an effective PSF of 0.35 \ (FWHM). The images show no evidence for a lensing galaxy, and they further note a possible flux ratio difference between the H and [ Oiii ] lines. We present resolved NTT/EFOSC2 spectra of the system, which show two quasars at redshift z=3.09 with a reasonably constant flux ratio of 3 across wavelength.

One possible explanation of these observations is that the lensing galaxy falls very close to one of the images and is being fit by the PSF subtraction of Minghao2023 , and the fainter component's spectrum is too noisy to conclude incompatibility with the brighter object's spectrum. Additionally, jointly fitting the available Pan-STARRS and Legacy Survey data produces slightly prominent residuals with a 2-PSFs model, but the color of the said residuals matches that of the point sources. We thus attribute the poor fit to a defect in our PSF model, and thereby cannot find evidence for a lensing galaxy either. If this system were a dual quasar, its physical separation would be 6.87 kpc. We designate this system as an UQP, needing deeper imaging to confirm the presence or absence of the lensing galaxy.

gmw_00003188.name: PS J1800+0146
gmw_00003188.overview: PS J1800+0146 is a strong gravitational lensing system [arXiv:2307.13729]. The system consists of a bright lens galaxy and two similar quasars at a redshift of z=1.53 [arXiv:2307.13729].

sources.summary: The paper classifies PS J1800+0146 as a lens after spectra show two similar z=1.53 quasars and imaging/modeling recovers a bright lens galaxy and Gaia-consistent separation.

quotes.quote: PS J1800+0146 The NOT/ALFOSC resolved spectrum shows two very similar quasars at redshift z=1.53 . The flux ratio is roughly one across wavelength. Here the 116 similar OM10 mocks suggest a lensing galaxy as faint as i 21.6 , which is relatively bright and was blindly picked up by starlets in the 2p2 data. The Gaia separation of 1.12 \ is also recovered by the fit. We designate this system as a lens.

gmw_00003189.name: PS J2122 1621
gmw_00003189.overview: PS J2122 1621 is a strong gravitational lensing system [arXiv:2307.13729]. The system consists of two very similar lensed quasars at a redshift of z=1.47, which are separated by 1.20 arcseconds [arXiv:2307.13729].

sources.summary: The paper classifies PS J2122 1621 as a lens after resolving two similar z=1.47 quasars and fitting a lensing galaxy while recovering the Gaia separation.

quotes.quote: PS J2122 1621 The NTT/EFOSC2 resolved spectrum shows two very similar quasars at redshift z=1.47 , with a flux ratio of 1.2 across wavelength. Just like in the case of PS J1800+0146, the lensing galaxy could be fitted blindly with STARRED in the 2p2 data. The Gaia separation of 1.20 was recovered as well. We designate this system as a lens.

gmw_00003190.name: DDO 87
gmw_00003190.overview: DDO 87 is a low-luminosity dwarf galaxy associated with the M81 group [arXiv:astro-ph/0203133]. Its low-column-density neutral hydrogen disk has a radius of 7.1 kiloparsecs [arXiv:2006.01735, arXiv:astro-ph/0203133], exhibiting low-amplitude gas flaring [arXiv:2010.07948] and 18 distinct interstellar holes [arXiv:2006.01735].

sources.summary: The paper reports that DDO 87 has 18 distinct H I holes of all three types, including five Type 1 holes larger than one kiloparsec.

quotes.quote: DDO 87 DDO 87 (PGC 32405, UGC 5918) has 18 distinct \ holes in its ISM. It contains all three types of the holes almost in equal proportions. The Type 1 holes of the galaxy are larger than the others and are found in the outer part of the disk. Among them, the diameters of five of the holes are larger than one kiloparsec. The scale height of DDO 87 is estimated to be about 460 pc and the \ disk radius is estimated as 7.1 kpc.

sources.summary: The paper says DDO 87 has low-amplitude gas flaring, appears slightly detached from the VSF law but remains error-compatible, and only has upper-limit 24 micron flux.

quotes.quote: The gas distribution flares of a factor ranging from a minimum of 1.1-1.6 (but with large uncertainty) for DDO 101 and DDO 87 to a maximum of 4-4.5 (e.g. DDO 52, DDO 47).

From a thorough inspection of Fig. fig:vsf , we can see that a few points belonging to three galaxies, DDO 87, DDO 168 and WLM, seem slightly detached from the VSF law. We note that DDO 168 and DDO 87 are actually compatible within the errors with the best-fit relation.
NGC 2366 and DDO 101 have the highest 24 m fluxes, while only upper limits are available for DDO 52 and DDO 87.

sources.summary: DDO 87 is described as a low-column-density HI disk with unresolved dense regions, a fragmented low-S/N velocity field, a nearly flat outer rotation curve, and low luminosity inferred from M81-group association.

quotes.quote: DDO 87: The HI is distributed over a low-column-density disk with a number of small high column density regions, unresolved at 13'' \ resolution. The fragmented appearance of the velocity field is the result of low signal-to-noise ratios. The XV map shows a nearly flat rotation curve in the outer regions. The low luminosity of DDO 87 is inferred from its association with the M81 group (Huchtmeier Skillman 1998).

sources.summary: The paper uses DDO 87 to show that MOND rotation-curve predictions remain similar with or without the stellar disk, though including stars improves agreement with observations.

quotes.quote: In Fig. 3 we see the calculated rotation curves for one case where the fraction of stellar mass is fairly typical, DDO 87. The lower dashed curve is the Newtonian rotation curve of baryons excluding the estimated contribution of the visible disk and the upper dashed curve is the same but including the stellar disk with a contribution of 17.5 of the total baryonic mass as given in table 1; the upper and lower solid curves are the same but for the MOND rotation curves. While it is obvious that the the contribution of the stellar disk provides better agreement with the observations, it is also clear that, with no stellar contribution at all, the rotation curve is qualitatively similar: The overall dependence of the rotation curve shape is quite independent of the mass-to-light ratio of the stellar disk and the final asymptotic rotation velocity given by MOND is essentially identical with and without stars.

gmw_00003191.name: NGC 7259
gmw_00003191.overview: NGC 7259 is a spiral galaxy that hosted the supernova SN 2009ip [arXiv:1607.01056]. While the inner spiral arms of the galaxy contain dozens of active H II regions, its outer regions beyond 3 to 4 kiloparsecs lack massive recent star formation [arXiv:1607.01056].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7259 as a spiral host whose inner regions contain H ii regions, while the outer area around SN 2009ip lacks massive recent star formation.

quotes.quote: The intent of these observations was to search for evidence of recent star formation in the local ( 1kpc; 10 ) environment around SN 2009ip, in the remote outskirts of its host spiral galaxy NGC 7259. We can rule out the presence of any massive star-forming complexes like 30 Dor or the Carina Nebula at the SN site or within a few kpc.

Indeed, the F657N image reveals dozens of H ii regions with a wide range of surface brightness in the interior few kpc of the host galaxy nucleus, but there is a marked absence of any H ii emission in the outer regions of the galaxy beyond 3--4 kpc from the center. The new F657N image of SN 2009ip and its surroundings is shown in Figure fig:carina . This image reveals no detectable H emission from any young H ii regions in the surrounding 1 kpc around SN 2009ip, which is found about 5 kpc from the galaxy center.

It is evident that the eastern side of NGC 7259 has no extreme mini-starbursts comparable to 30 Dor, although there are a few detected H sources that have a scale in between 30 Dor and Carina, and several comparable to Carina in the inner spiral arms of the galaxy. There are a few dozen smaller H ii regions comparable to Orion scattered around NGC 7259, but none in the vicinity of SN 2009ip.

gmw_00003192.name: A-Z06/A-B20
gmw_00003192.overview: A-Z06/A-B20, also known as Aerith A, is a massive companion galaxy located at a redshift of z=5.856 [arXiv:2111.05446]. It is exceptionally bright in Spitzer/IRAC 3.6-micron observations and is estimated to have a stellar mass of approximately 10^10.34 solar masses [arXiv:2111.05446].

sources.summary: The paper identifies A-Z06/A-B20 as Aerith A, a z=5.856 companion that is Spitzer-bright, very massive, likely in a massive halo, and possibly nearing the end of active star formation.

quotes.quote: Redshifts were obtained for three objects: ``Aerith A" at z=5.856 0.003 (A-B20 which is identical to A-Z06), ``Aerith B" at z=5.793 0.003 (B-B20 has no counterpart in zheng06 because of its relatively small - \ color) and ``Aerith C" at z=5.726 0.003 (C-B20 which is identical to B-Z06). Object A-Z06/A-B20 is also detected in the NB816 narrowband, which is interpreted as continuum emission blueward of \ bosman20 .

Massive companion galaxy A overzier09_irac presented Spitzer/IRAC observations of the J0836 structure, and showed that object A-Z06/A-B20 is very bright at 3.6 m with m_ 3.6 =23.78 0.09 mag. Assuming that its redshift was indeed z 6 , they concluded that it is among the brightest and most massive dropout galaxies. Here we will revisit the analysis of A-Z06/A-B20. The \ and 3.6 m images are shown in Fig. fig:irac . Using the redshift firmly established by bosman20 , we can now make a much more secure determination of the properties of A-Z06/A-B20 based on the available photometry.

Assuming a constant star formation history and attenuation by dust according to the calzetti00 law, we find a stellar mass log _ 10 ( M _ /M_ )=10.34_ 0.16 ^ +0.30 with a mass-weighted age of 0.27_ 0.14 ^ +0.28 Gyr ( z_f 7.5 ), a metallicity log _ 10 (Z/Z_ )=0.86_ 0.45 ^ +0.86 , and A_V=0.7_ 0.1 ^ +0.3 mag (see Fig. fig:afit ).

gmw_00003193.name: B-B20
gmw_00003193.overview: B-B20, also known as Aerith B, is a bright Lyman-break galaxy at a redshift of approximately 5.79 [arXiv:2111.05446, arXiv:2004.14496]. It is a star-forming companion to a nearby quasar [arXiv:2111.05446] and exhibits a double-peaked Lyman-alpha emission profile [arXiv:2006.03232].

sources.summary: The paper describes B-B20/Aerith B as a spectroscopically confirmed companion near the quasar redshift, undetected in NB816, and likely a typical star-forming galaxy of a few billion solar masses.

quotes.quote: Redshifts were obtained for three objects: ``Aerith A" at z=5.856 0.003 (A-B20 which is identical to A-Z06), ``Aerith B" at z=5.793 0.003 (B-B20 has no counterpart in zheng06 because of its relatively small - \ color) and ``Aerith C" at z=5.726 0.003 (C-B20 which is identical to B-Z06). Object A-Z06/A-B20 is also detected in the NB816 narrowband, which is interpreted as continuum emission blueward of \ bosman20 . Object B-B20 is not detected in the NB816.

The proper distances along and perpendicular to the line of sight (l.o.s.) to the quasar ( R_ , R_ ) are (3.64,0.28) pMpc for A-Z06/A-B20, (--0.67,0.33) pMpc for B-B20 and (--5.03,0.44) pMpc for B-Z06/C-B20, where a minus sign indicates objects in the foreground of the quasar.

Other companions We checked the IRAC image for the other confirmed companions, but object B-B20 was not detected and object B-Z06/C-B20 (and thus also B ^ A06) lies too close to a very bright foreground object to attempt a flux measurement. A direct stellar mass estimate can thus not be made. However, assuming that, unlike object A-Z06/A-B20, these objects do follow the log _ 10 ( M_ /M_ )-M_ UV relation of stefanon21 (reddening by dust can be neglected at these magnitudes, bouwens07 ), the stellar masses would be a few times 10^9 M_ for B-B20 and B-Z06/C-B20 and a few times 10^8 M_ for B ^ A06.

sources.summary: The paper describes Aerith B as a bright z=5.79 Lyman-break galaxy with strong Ly-alpha emission, quasar-enhanced UV irradiation, and a large red-blue peak velocity separation.

quotes.quote: Lastly, `Aerith B' is a bright ( M_ AB = -21.0_ 0.2 ^ +0.3 ) Lyman-break galaxy at z 5.79 displaying strong emission ( EW_ rest = 64^ +39 _ 24 ). Aerith B is situated within the ionisation cone of a neighbourhing bright quasar at a distance d 750 proper kpc, with an estimated resulting UV intensity at its location of J_ 21 = 406 40 : a factor 100 higher than the cosmic peak of the UVB. Unlike COLA1 and NEPLA4, the velocity separation between the red and blue peak of the line is large in Aerith B ( v = (580 80) ), indicating the galaxy is unlikely a significant Lyman continuum leaker 2016ApJ...828...71D,2016MNRAS.461.3683I .

sources.summary: The paper cites Aerith B as a z=5.8 double-peaked Lyman-alpha emitter whose blue peak challenges homogeneous reionization prescriptions and illustrates patchy reionization near a quasar.

quotes.quote: These include Aerith B at z=5.8 Bosman.2020 , NEPLA4 at z=6.5 Songaila.2018 and COLA1 at z=6.6 Hu.2016 , which all show impressive \ double-peaks, strongly resemblant of the z 0 compact starbursts Henry.2015,Jaskot.2017,Izotov.2020 . According to simplified, homogeneous prescriptions for the neutral fraction of the universe at these redshifts, these profiles should not exist, and even in modern radiation hydrodynamic simulations of the EoR, blue peaks at z 6 are rare Gronke.2020 . Clearly the reionization progression is patchy, and Aerith B is a perfect example, being discovered inside the proximity zone of a luminous quasar.

gmw_00003194.name: B-Z06/C-B20
gmw_00003194.overview: B-Z06/C-B20 is a high-redshift Lyman-alpha galaxy located at a redshift of z=5.726 [arXiv:2111.05446]. It is a companion galaxy with an estimated stellar mass of a few billion solar masses [arXiv:2111.05446].

sources.summary: The paper treats B-Z06/C-B20 as Aerith C, a z=5.726 companion whose Ly emission is offset from B ^ A06 and whose stellar mass is estimated at a few billion solar masses.

quotes.quote: Redshifts were obtained for three objects: ``Aerith A" at z=5.856 0.003 (A-B20 which is identical to A-Z06), ``Aerith B" at z=5.793 0.003 (B-B20 has no counterpart in zheng06 because of its relatively small - \ color) and ``Aerith C" at z=5.726 0.003 (C-B20 which is identical to B-Z06).

The redshift of B-Z06/C-B20 places the wavelength of its \ emission in the NB816. A relatively bright object (B ^ A06) has indeed been detected, as first reported by ajiki06 . However, as explained in bosman20 , the redshift of B-Z06/C-B20 was obtained at the location of its z^ band continuum, which is offset from the NB816 source B ^ A06 by about 6.3 kpc. It is thus possible that there are two galaxies at this location having approximately the same redshift.

Other companions We checked the IRAC image for the other confirmed companions, but object B-B20 was not detected and object B-Z06/C-B20 (and thus also B ^ A06) lies too close to a very bright foreground object to attempt a flux measurement. A direct stellar mass estimate can thus not be made. However, assuming that, unlike object A-Z06/A-B20, these objects do follow the log _ 10 ( M_ /M_ )-M_ UV relation of stefanon21 (reddening by dust can be neglected at these magnitudes, bouwens07 ), the stellar masses would be a few times 10^9 M_ for B-B20 and B-Z06/C-B20 and a few times 10^8 M_ for B ^ A06.

gmw_00003195.name: B ^ A06
gmw_00003195.overview: B ^ A06 is a high-redshift Lyman-alpha emitting galaxy candidate [arXiv:2111.05446]. It is located approximately 6.3 kiloparsecs from the continuum source B-Z06, suggesting the two objects may be distinct neighboring galaxies [arXiv:2111.05446].

sources.summary: The paper describes B ^ A06 as a strong Ly-emitting candidate near B-Z06, offset from that continuum source, possibly a second galaxy, and likely less massive than the confirmed companions.

quotes.quote: They found one strong \ emitting candidate at z 5.7 about 1 \ or 6 kpc from object B-Z06. We will refer to this object as B ^ A06.

A relatively bright object (B ^ A06) has indeed been detected, as first reported by ajiki06 . However, as explained in bosman20 , the redshift of B-Z06/C-B20 was obtained at the location of its z^ band continuum, which is offset from the NB816 source B ^ A06 by about 6.3 kpc. It is thus possible that there are two galaxies at this location having approximately the same redshift.

Other companions We checked the IRAC image for the other confirmed companions, but object B-B20 was not detected and object B-Z06/C-B20 (and thus also B ^ A06) lies too close to a very bright foreground object to attempt a flux measurement. A direct stellar mass estimate can thus not be made. However, assuming that, unlike object A-Z06/A-B20, these objects do follow the log _ 10 ( M_ /M_ )-M_ UV relation of stefanon21 (reddening by dust can be neglected at these magnitudes, bouwens07 ), the stellar masses would be a few times 10^9 M_ for B-B20 and B-Z06/C-B20 and a few times 10^8 M_ for B ^ A06.

sources.summary: The paper singles out E+A 6 as the sample's blue-core object and only slow rotator, with simulated zero-rotation fields reaching 53 percent of its measured angular-momentum proxy.

quotes.quote: Two galaxies have irregular color structure in their centres (E+A 1,E+A 5) and two little structure at all (E+A 4, E+A 9) whilst one galaxy in our sample shows a clear blue core (E+A 6).

Comparison of the _ R values found for our E+A galaxies with the tracks for the SAURON sample shows that 7 out of 8 of the E+As are consistent with being fast rotators and 1 out of 8, E+A 6, sits on the slow rotator tracks. We note, without implication, that E+A 6 is also the sole blue core object in our sample.

The values of _ R produced in this manner were, on average, between 10 and 20 per cent of the values measured from the real data, being as small as 2 per cent for the high signal-to-noise data obtained for E+A 3 and as high as 53 per cent for the slow rotator E+A 6.

gmw_00003196.name: NGC 1125
gmw_00003196.overview: NGC 1125 is a Seyfert 2 galaxy [arXiv:1501.02022] that exhibits a complex star formation history with young and intermediate-age ring structures [arXiv:2203.07484]. Its nucleus features a dynamically quiescent extended narrow-line region [arXiv:1501.02022] and a central core with non-nuclear millimeter-wave components [arXiv:2309.02776].

sources.summary: NGC 1125 contains a Seyfert 2 nucleus, a double-paraboloidal quiescent ENLR aligned with a larger radio structure, and bright H II regions in its disk.

quotes.quote: NGC 1125: This Seyfert 2 galaxy contains a superb ionisation cone extending not quite at right angles from the disk which is seen nearly edge on. The ENLR appears as a double paraboloidal structure, and is dynamically quiescent, co-rotating with the disk gas. The orientation of the ENLR is well aligned with the much larger 12 kpc double-lobe structure seen at 8.4GHz Thean00 . There are a number of bright \ regions in the disk. An HST F606W continuum image has been published by Malkan98 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1125 as having complex star formation, young and intermediate-age ring structures, older components toward the north, and sub-Gyr populations in the center.

quotes.quote: NGC 1125: This galaxy presents a complex star formation history (SFH). Locations 100 pc from the centre towards the NW are dominated by a xy SPC, reaching values up to 50 percent. A ring-like structure is observed in the xyo map starting at 100 pc from the centre with a fraction of 25 percent. This ring structure surrounds a ring of intermediate age stars ( xiy ), which dominates the light in the central region of this galaxy. Fractions of 25 percent for the two older components are also observed, being located in regions from the centre towards the North.

sources.summary: The paper reports central and non-nuclear ALMA components in NGC 1125, comparing them with VLA radio lobes and noting mismatches in the counterpart structure.

quotes.quote: 12: NGC 1125 This source was observed at a resolution of 0 1 using ALMA and the image shows central core emission ( a ) and two non-nuclear components to the southwest (SW) and the southeast (SE), labeled b and , respectively. A moderate resolution ( 0 3) image at 8.4 GHz was obtained with VLA by and revealed a linear structure, which extends at a PA of 120 ^ and has two peaks in the SE and northwest (NW) regions, looking like radio lobes. The brightest radio component in the SE direction appears to coincide with the mm-wave blob of . No mm-wave counterpart is however found for the fainter NW radio component. This could be because mm-wave emission is also fainter and thus cannot be detected at the achieved sensitivity.

gmw_00003197.name: NGC 5899
gmw_00003197.overview: NGC 5899 is an inclined SAB(rs) spiral galaxy that hosts a Seyfert 2 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1902.06790, arXiv:2103.08736]. Its central region features a rotating molecular gas disk [arXiv:1902.06790, arXiv:2103.08736], a partial ring of low stellar velocity dispersion [arXiv:2103.08736], and a compact north-south ionized gas outflow [arXiv:1902.06790, arXiv:2107.07635].

sources.summary: The paper maps NGC 5899, describing an inclined paired Seyfert 2 galaxy with high nuclear reddening, shock-associated line ratios, similar gas-density profiles, and north-south outflow signs.

quotes.quote: firstpage - lastpage We have used the Gemini Near-Infrared Integral Field Spectrograph (NIFS) in the J and K bands to map the distribution, excitation and kinematics of the ionized H ii and warm molecular gas H _2 , in the inner few 100 pc of 6 nearby active galaxies: NGC 788, Mrk 607, NGC 3227, NGC 3516, NGC 5506, NGC 5899.

The E(B-V) values are mostly in the range 1--3, reaching the highest values at the nucleus of NGC 5506 ( E(B-V) 5 ) and NGC 5899 ( E(B-V) 3 ) while for the other galaxies the highest values are observed outwards. The [ Fe ii ] 1.2570 m/Pa ratio maps can be used to investigate the excitation mechanism of [ Fe ii ] e.g. ardila05,riffel08b,rogerio09,storchi2009 , with typical values for Seyfert galaxies ranging from 0.6 to 2. Most values are observed within this range, usually increasing from the centre outwards. In the case of NGC 3227 its value increases to 4 in a region with the shape of an off-centered ring surrounding the nucleus in which an enhanced velocity dispersion in the [ Fe ii ] emission lines is observed. Higher values than 2 are also observed in NGC 3516 and NGC 5899, also in association with regions of higher velocity dispersion than the surroundings, suggesting the contribution of shocks for the gas emission. The [ Fe ii ] 1.2570 m/ [ P ii ] 1.8861 m line ratio can also be an indicator of shocks storchi2009 , if its value becomes larger than 2. The corresponding maps are compact, due to the small extent of the [ P ii ] emission, with typical values of 5 at the nucleus and increasing to 10 at 0 5 , thus supporting the presence of shocks. In the cases of NGC 3227 and NGC 5899, the off-nuclear values reach values larger than 10, also in association with high [ Fe ii ] values, supporting even stronger contribution from shocks. The H _2 2.1218 m/Br , used to investigate the origin of the H _2 excitation, is usually in the range 0.6 to 2 for Seyfert galaxies ardila05,riffel08b,rogerio09,storchi2009 . Most measured values are indeed in this range, with a general behavior of showing the lowest ratios at the nucleus and increasing outwards, that we attribute to the destruction of the H _2 molecule by the strong radiation field close to the nucleus. The lowest values -- lower than 2 -- are observed for NGC 788 and NGC 5506, while the highest, reaching values in the range 8--10, are seen again in NGC 3227, NGC 3516 and NGC 5899, at similar locations as those where an enhancement is seen in [ Fe ii ] /Pa and [ Fe ii ] values, supporting again a contribution from shocks. Line-of-sight Velocity Fields The line-of-sight velocity (V _ LOS ) fields are shown in the second line of Figs. ngc788 - ngc5899 , after the subtraction of the systemic velocity obtained through the fit of a rotating disk model to the H _2 velocity field. Again, here we just describe the general features of these velocity fields, as a proper discussion is deferred to Paper B. The most common characteristic of the velocity fields is the rotation pattern, that is clearer in the H _2 velocity maps (presenting the typical ``spider diagram'' structure gal_dynamics-book ). The H _2 velocity amplitudes is similar to those also seen in the [ Fe ii ] and Pa velocity fields, except in the case of NGC 788, for which the H _2 rotation amplitude is smaller. The rotation pattern can also be seen in the [ Fe ii ] and Pa velocity fields, but in these cases it is usually disturbed -- more in the case of [ Fe ii ] than in the case of Pa - indicating the presence of additional kinematic components, usually associated with enhanced values. Velocity dispersion maps These maps are shown in the third line of Figs. ngc788 - ngc5899 . The [ Fe ii ] maps usually show the highest values, reaching up to 150--300 km s ^ 1 , while the H _2 maps show the lowest values, in the range 40--90 km s ^ 1 . The Pa maps are similar to those of [ Fe ii ] although reaching somewhat overall lower values, in the range 40 km s ^ 1 to 250 km s ^ 1 . An exception is the case of NGC 5506 that shows regions of enhanced to 350 km s ^ 1 ) for all emission lines, extended perpendicularly to the major axis for [ Fe ii ] and Pa and in patches parallel to the major axis in H _2 ; such patches are also seen in [ Fe ii ] . h_3 and h_4 Gauss-Hermite moments The h_3 and h_4 maps show values ranging from --0.3 to 0.3 for all emission lines. An inverse correlation between the h_3 map and the velocity fields is observed for all emission-lines: positive values of h_3 are seen at the locations where negative velocities are observed in the velocity fields, while negative values are seen where positive velocities are observed. This means that red wings are observed in blueshifted emission lines, while blue wings are observed in redshifted emission lines. We also observe in some cases (e.g. for the three emission lines in NGC 3227 and some of the lines in the other galaxies) an inverse correlation between the h_4 and the velocity dispersion maps. Positive values and highest values of h_4 are seen at the locations showing the lowest velocity dispersion values, while values zero or most negative ones are seen in the regions with the highest velocity dispersion. Total gas mass and surface mass density distributions The measurement of gas masses and surface mass densities within the inner few hundred pc of the host galaxies of AGN can be used to evaluate the gas reservoir available to trigger and maintain the nuclear activity, as well as the formation of new stars in the circumnuclear region. The presence of recent star formation in the circumnuclear region of active galaxies has been evidenced via the observation of low stellar velocity dispersion structures (Riffel et al. 2017) and their association with young stars e.g. storchi2012 . We can also use gas mass estimates in the vicinity of AGN to look for a possible correlation between the amount of available gas and the power of the AGN. We have calculated the ionized gas masses, in units of solar masses (M _ ) using the following expression e.g. scoville,riffel08,storchi2009,astor2014,astor2017 : M_ HII 5.1 10^ 16 F_ Pa erg s^ 1 cm^ 2 D Mpc ^2 [ M_ ] , where F_ Pa is the flux in the Pa line and D is the distance to the galaxy. This equation was obtained from scoville assuming an electron temperature of T=10^4 K, electron density of N_e=100 cm ^ 3 and case B recombination osterbrock , values applicable to the inner kiloparsec of active galaxies. The flux of Br used in the original equation was replaced by the stronger Pa one assuming the theoretical ratio F_ Pa / F_ Br =5.88 osterbrock . For NGC 5506, we did use the flux of Br emission line instead of Pa , as the signal to noise in the J-band spectrum is worse than in the K-band one, particularly in the extranuclear regions. The mass of the warm molecular gas (in M _ ) was obtained using scoville : M_ H_2 5.0776 10^ 13 F_ H_ 2 2.1218 erg s^ 1 cm^ 2 D Mpc ^2 [ M_ ] , where F_ H_ 2 2.1218 is the flux for the corresponding emission line. In the derivation of the above equation by scoville it was assumed that the vibrational temperature is T=2000 K, indicating a thermalized gas which is valid for n_ H_2 >10^ 4.5 cm ^ 3 . Previous studies by members of our group have found that the assumption of thermal equilibrium is indeed applicable for nearby AGNs and the derived temperatures are very close to the above value storchi2009,diniz2015 . Uncertainties in the derived mass values were obtained considering that the mass derived from both Eq. mhii and mh2 are directly proportional to the line fluxes. All the mass uncertainties are thus lower than 30 , as we have masked out regions with flux uncertainties larger than this value. The actual uncertainties may be somewhat higher due to further uncertainties in the absolute flux calibration. In addition, we cannot derive precise physical parameters such as gas density and temperature, thus, we have to rely on the above assumptions even though they are just ``educated estimates" based on our previous similar studies. We show the surface mass density distributions of ionized and warm molecular gas -- _ HII and _ H_2 , respectively -- in the first two columns of panels of Fig. mass , and the corresponding azymuthally averaged profiles in the third column, in units of solar masses per parsec square. The surface mass densities were obtained by calculating the gas masses in each spaxel using the equations above and dividing them by the surface area of each spaxel, taking into account projection effects via the use of the disk inclinations quoted in riffel2018 . The resulting ionized gas surface mass density distributions are in some cases more compact when compared with that of the warm molecular gas, that seems to extend farther and more uniformly from the nucleus than the ionized gas. The azimuthaly-averaged profiles of _ HII and _ H_2 , shown in the last column of Fig. mass , reveal that typical ratios between the two surface mass densities within the inner 100 pc are of the order of 10 ^3 and that gradients are steeper for the ionized than for the molecular gas. In Table tabmass we show the integrated gas mass values (for the whole FOV) as well as the average surface mass densities (in units of M _ pc ^ 2 ) obtained as the ratio between the integrated masses and the area over which they are distributed, listed also in the Table. We also include in Table tabmass an estimate of the cold molecular gas mass. A number of studies have derived the ratio between the cold and warm H _2 gas masses by comparing the masses obtained using the cold CO molecular lines observed in millimetric wavelenghts with that of the warm H _2 observed in the near-IR. dale05 obtained ratios in the range 10^5 -10 ^7 , while ms2006 , using a larger sample of 16 luminous and ultra-luminous infrared galaxies, derived a ratio M _ cold /M _ warm = 1 - 5 10^6 . More recently, ma2013 compiled from the literature values of M _ cold derived from CO observations and H _2 (2.12 m) luminosities for a larger number of galaxies, covering a wider range of luminosities, morphological and nuclear activity types. From those data, they propose that an estimate of the cold H _2 gas mass can be obtained from the flux of the warm H _2 2.1218 line as: M_ H_2 cold 1174 L_ H_2 2.1218 L_ , where L_ H_2 2.1218 is the luminosity of the H _2 (2.12 m) line. The error associated to the factor = 1174 is 35 , according to ma2013 . Table tabmass shows that the integrated mass of warm H _2 gas (within typically 100 -- 400 pc from the nucleus) ranges from 106 M _ for Mrk 607 to 820 M _ for NGC 5506, while the estimated mass of cold molecular gas ranges from 8 10 ^7 M _ for Mrk 607 to 6 10 ^ 8 M _ for NGC 5506. The masses of ionized gas range from 1.8 10 ^5 M _ for NGC 3516 to 1.9 10 ^7 M _ for NGC 5506. We point out again that the uncertainties in the calculated masses, presented in Tab. tabmass , were obtained only by propagating the line flux uncertainties and are thus lower limits, considering that there are additional uncertainties in adopted physical properties -- e.g. on the M_ H_2 cold /M_ H_2 ratio, H _2 vibrational temperatures and electron densities. In this section we discuss individually each galaxy presenting a brief review of previous results from the literature, trying to relate them with ours . We also discuss and analyze the masses of ionized and molecular gas, as well as their surface mass density distributions and the global properties of the gas kinematics, leaving the kinematic modelling and the analysis of the gas kinematics and excitation to Paper B . NGC 788 NGC 788 (Fig. ngc788 ) was identified as a Seyfert galaxy by huchra1982 , and has been observed in the optical hamuy1987,wagner1988,kay1994,cruz1994 , radio ulvestad1989 , and millimetre heckman1989 wavebands. This galaxy is an early-type spiral with faint arms visible up to 30 from the nucleus evans1996 , most conspicuous to the north-west of the nucleus with a string of bright compact H ii regions, while a complex of fainter H ii regions is associated with a southern arm evans1996 . The kinematics major axis PA 125 ^ was determined from the V_ LOS fields (Fig. ngc788 ), with a value close to that obtained by riffel2017 for the stellar kinematics of 130 ^ . The velocity amplitude observed for [ Fe ii ] and Pa , of 150 km s ^ 1 is much higher than that for H _2 , of only 50 km s ^ 1 . These higher velocities are associated with a more collimated emission and higher velocity dispersions suggesting the presence of a bipolar outflow along the east-west direction, as suggested from the morphology of the gas emission in the [ Fe ii ] flux map. The E(B-V) map from Fig. ngc788 has the highest values to southwest of the nucleus, which coincide with a dust lane seen in the HST - F606W optical continuum image from . Mrk 607 Mrk 607 (Fig. mrk607 ) is an Sa galaxy hosting a Seyfert 2 nucleus. A continuum image from ferruit2000 shows a high ellipticity of 0.60, yielding an inclination i 67 ^ ), with major axis PA of 43^ , which agrees with our estimate of the kinematic major axis orientation of 38^ from the H _2 velocity field (Paper B). Our data reveal that the three velocity fields -- [ Fe ii ] , Pa and H _2 - show what seems to be a rotation pattern, very steep in the center in H _2 and less steep in the first two lines. Spiral dust lanes are clearly visible in the HST F547 continuum image of the inner 8 ^ (1.4 kpc) ferruit2000 . The high inclination of the galaxy may explain the large velocity dispersions observed. In the central 5 ^ (875 pc) it presents weak radio emission extended along PA 180 ^ colbert1996,nagar1999 which is not aligned with the axis of the most extended gas emission, which is observed along the galaxy major axis. The E(B-V) map from Fig. mrk607 has high values ( 5) to southeast/west of the nucleus, which coincide with a dust lane seen in the HST - F606W optical continuum image from . NGC 3227 NGC 3227 (Fig. ngc3227 ) is a well studied barred galaxy, with a Seyfert 1.5 nucleus ho1997 and in interaction with the dwarf elliptical galaxy NGC 3226 mundell2004 . The galactic disc has an inclination of 56 ^ , with an outer photometric major-axis at a position angle PA = 158 ^ mundell1995 . This value agrees with the PA 155 ^ we have obtained from the fit of the H _2 V _ LOS velocity field (Paper B), and with the value of 156 ^ found by riffel2017 from the stellar kinematics. The three emission-line velocity fields show a rotation pattern, that is nevertheless distorted due to the presence of additional components, mainly to the north-east, east and south-east. These components are most conspicuous in [ Fe ii ] and Pa velocity maps and are associated with regions of enhanced velocity dispersion. The central region has been mapped in ^ 12 CO(1-0) and ^ 12 CO(2-1) by schinnerer2000 , who detected molecular gas very close to the nucleus (within 13 pc), in agreement with our results that show a large molecular gas concentration towards the centre (see the surface mass density profile in the third line of Fig. mass ). In addition, schinnerer2000 found an asymmetric nuclear ring with a diameter of about 3 0 (220 pc), which seems to correspond to the structure delineated in the line ratio maps [ Fe ii ] /Pa and H _2 /Br of Fig. ngc3227 . The inner kiloparsec hosts a radio jet at PA 10^ and a conical NLR outflow at PA 15 ^ mundell1995 , while arribas1994 report an H outflow at PA 50 ^ . The extent of the [ Fe ii ] emission to the north, as well as its enhanced velocity dispersion may be related to the radio emission, while the outflows observed at PAs between 15 ^ and 50 ^ can explain the deviation from rotation observed in the velocity fields of [ Fe ii ] and Pa to the north-east. Signatures of gas outflows in NGC 3227 have also been observed by davies2014 in the inner 1^ 2^ using near-IR IFS observations with the instrument SINFONI at the VLT. A V-H colour map shows dust lanes to the south-west of the nucleus martini2003,davies2014 , which are co-spatial with the highest E(B-V) values seen in Fig. ngc3227 . NGC 3516 The Seyfert 1 nucleus of this SB0 galaxy (Fig. ngc3516 ) shows variable ultraviolet absorption lines voit1987,kriss1996 as well as variable broad emission lines and continuum koratkar1996 . Spectroscopic studies of the gaseous kinematics using either long-slit ulrich1980,goad1987,goad1988,mulchaey1992 or integral field spectroscopy veilleux1993,aoki1994,arribas1994 have outlined multiple spectral components displaying strong deviations from ``normal'' galaxy rotation. Two general models have been proposed to explain the morphology and the kinematics of the emission-line gas that presents a Z-shaped structure covering the inner 5 0: a bent bipolar mass outflow, first suggested by goad1987 and further developed by mulchaey1992 and veilleux1993 , and a precessing twin-jet model by veilleux1993 . The stellar rotation curve obtained by arribas1997 yields a systemic velocity of 2593 15 km s ^ 1 , close to that derived by riffel2017 of 2631 km s ^ 1 . The kinematic major axis obtained by arribas1997 has PA= 53 ^ 5^ and is consistent with our results for the stellar kinematics riffel2017 and also with the orientation of the kinematic major axis of the molecular gas of 60 ^ . NGC 5506 The nucleus of NGC 5506 (Fig. ngc5506 ) is classified as a Sy1.9 based on the detection of broad wings in the Pa profile blanco1990 . However, more recently, nagar2002 presented evidence that NGC 5506 is an obscured narrow-line Sy1, via the detection of the permitted O i 1.129 m line, together with a broad pedestal of Pa and rapid X-ray variability. The galaxy nucleus is very compact in the mid-IR, with an apparent optical depth of ^ app _ 10 m 1.4 roche1991,roche2007,siebenmorgen2004 , although NGC 5506 also shows variations in its silicate absorption depth on parsec scales roche2007 . The optical spectrum has strong [ O iii ] and [ N ii ] narrow lines zaw2009 . The log( [ O iii ] /H ) and log( [ N ii ] /H ) line ratios, 0.88 and 0.09 respectively kewley2001 , place it firmly in the Seyfert region of the BPT diagram. NGC 5506 is close to edge-on, with an inclination of 75 ^ , above and below which ionized gas is in outflow within cones with an opening angle of 80 ^ maiolino1994 . These outflows are consistent with the distribution of enhanced sigma values we observe in the ionized gas in a vertical structure crossing the galaxy plane and opening to the north and south of it (Fig. ngc5506 ). The outflow to the north is also well traced by the [ Fe ii ] flux distribution (its highest emission shows an approximately conical shape), while the H _2 flux distribution seems to better trace the gas in the galaxy plane, as it is oriented along this direction. As observed in Fig. espectros , the nucleus of NGC 5506 shows a steep red continuum over the 2.1-2.4 m spectral range that can be attributed to a blackbody source with temperature 1000 K. Although large scale dust is observed in the central region of this galaxy, possibly due to its high inclination and as seen in the V-H dust map from martini2003 , the origin of the K-band continuum is most probably hot dust emission from the AGN torus, as already observed for other active galaxies e.g. riffel09-4151,burtscher15,diniz18 . NGC 5899 This inclined SAB(rs) galaxy (Fig. ngc5899 ) is reported to be in a pair and presents an optical Seyfert 2 spectrum devaucouleurs91 . The stellar kinematics derived by riffel2017 gives a PA 24 ^ , that is somewhat distinct from that obtained by us for the molecular gas (Paper B), of 4 ^ . The kinematics of the ionized gas is very different from that of the molecular gas (Fig. ngc5899 ): while the later seems to be dominated by rotation in the inclined galaxy plane, with blueshifts to the north and redshifts to the south, the ionized gas kinematics shows velocities that are opposite to that observed in the H _2 velocity field. A possible interpretation is that the ionized gas is tracing an outflow at PA 0 ^ , as supported also by the north-south elongation observed mostly in the [ Fe ii ] flux map and by the enhanced velocity dispersion observed at these locations. Global properties Surface mass density distributions Fig. mass shows that the molecular gas seems to be more evenly distributed over the field of view than the ionized gas, which is more concentrated towards the nucleus and shows a more patchy and sometimes collimated mass distribution. The more peaked distribution of the ionized gas is clearly seen in the average surface mass density profiles shown in the third column of Fig. mass for the galaxies NGC 788, Mrk 607, NGC 3227 and NGC 5506. In the cases of NGC 3516 and NGC 5899, the average profiles of the molecular and ionized gas surface mass densities are similar to each other.

Regarding the presence of outflows, we have found signatures of them in most galaxies of our sample, most clearly observed in the [ Fe ii ] kinematics: (1) in NGC 788, along the east-west direction; (2) in NGC 3227, towards the northeast, as suggested by distortions in the velocity field and patterns in h_3 and h_4 ; (3) in NGC 3516, from the south-east to the north-west, as suggested by the disturbed velocity field and enhanced velocity dispersion; (4) in NGC 5506, along north-south, as suggested by the enhanced velocity dispersions; (5) and in NGC 5899, towards north and south, as indicated by the opposite velocity field between the ionized and molecular gas.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5899 as a Seyfert 2 galaxy with a low-dispersion stellar ring, molecular gas following disc rotation, and ionized gas displaced into likely outflow structures.

quotes.quote: NGC 5899 NGC 5899 is located at 39 Mpc, classified as SAB(rs) ann15 and hosts a Sy 2 nucleus devaucouleurs91 . This galaxy presents a partial ring of low stellar velocity dispersion ( 50-80 km s ^ 1 ) with radius of 200 pc centred at the galaxy nucleus likely produced by young/intermediate age stellar populations rogemar_stellar . astor19 found that the H _2 2.1218 m emission is extended along the galaxy major axis ( PA=25^ ) and shows a rotation pattern with small velocity dispersion values over the whole field of view. On the other hand, the strongest 1.2570 m and Pa emissions are seen along the north-south direction, as well as the highest gradient in the velocity fields.

The velocity dispersion of the \ reaches values of 300 km s ^ 1 \ reinforcing the hypothesis that the ionised gas traces an outflow, while the molecular gas follows the rotation of the stars astor19 . To the best of our knowledge, no extended radio emission is detected in NGC 5899. The H _2 emission in NGC 5899 (Fig. fig:N5899 ) is more extended along the orientation of the galaxy's disc 24^ 3^ ; rogemar_stellar , while the ionised gas is displaced by 23 ^ from it, and the emission peak of the ionised gas emission is observed at 0.3 arcsec south of the nucleus.

sources.summary: The paper reports that NGC 5899 has a rotating disk pattern, an S-shaped zero-velocity line suggesting a nuclear bar or spiral arms, and low-sigma patches near 200 pc.

quotes.quote: A rotating disk pattern is recognized in the LOS velocity field for all galaxies, with a straight zero velocity line for most of them. For two galaxies -- Mrk 1066 m1066_kin and NGC 5899 (Fig. n5899 ) -- an S shape zero velocity line is observed, which is a signature of the presence of a nuclear bar or spiral arms in these galaxies e.g. combes95,emsellem06 . For most galaxies, the maximum amplitude of the rotation curve is expected to be observed outside of the NIFS FoV. The rotation disk signature is clearly seen in the one one-dimensional plots shown in Fig. vcuts , obtained by averaging the velocity and values within a pseudo-slit with width of 0 25 oriented along the major axis of the galaxy. In addition, distinct structures of low- values ( 50-80 ) are seen in the the maps: low rings or partial rings are seen for Mrk 1066, Mrk 1157, NGC 5929 and NGC 788.

The velocity dispersion maps show low- ( 50-80 ) rings for 4 galaxies (Mrk 1066, Mrk 1157, NGC 5929 and NGC 788) or ``patches" of low- structures (for Mrk 607, NGC 2110, NGC 3516, NGC 4051, NGC 4235 and NGC 5899) at typical distances of 200 pc, interpreted as being originated in young/intermediate age stellar populations. Centrally peaked maps are observed for three galaxies (NGC 1052, NGC 3227 and NGC 4388). The h_ 3 moment is anti-correlated with the velocity field for 8 galaxies (NGC 1052, NGC 2110, NGC 3227, NGC 3516, NGC 4051, NGC 5506, Mrk 607 and Mrk 1066) -- positive h_ 3 values seen at locations where the velocity field shows blueshifts and h_ 3 <0 for locations where the velocity field shows redshifts. The presence of these wings are attributed to the contribution of stars from the galaxy bulge that present lower rotation velocities. The observed velocity fields are well reproduced by a rotating disk model, with deprojected velocity amplitudes in the range 60--340 .

sources.summary: NGC 5899 is an SABc Seyfert 2 galaxy dominated by old populations, with possible young and intermediate-age edge contributions, weak inner featureless continuum, nearly constant reddening and metallicity, and rotating disk kinematics.

quotes.quote: NGC 5899 NGC 5899 is an SABc galaxy, hosting a Seyfert 2 AGN. This galaxy is dominated by an old SP, with ages between 5 and 7 Gyr. At the edges of our FoV, we detected some contribution of young and intermediate-age SPs, but these results may be affected by border effects.

Traces of a FC were detected in the inner 1 0, contributing with up to 10 at some spaxels. Except for statistical uncertainties, the dust reddening, stellar and metallicity appear to be nearly constant within our FoV, without any discernible pattern. Lastly, we did not detect any deviation from a rotating disk in the V map.

sources.summary: The paper reports NGC 5899 has small young components, dominant intermediate-age populations, a flat old component across the field, weak nuclear hot dust/FC, and a mild age gradient.

quotes.quote: NGC 5899: The two youngest components show a small contribution over the FoV, with an enhancement to the West/NW side ( 75 percent for > 100 pc in this side). The intermediate age components are clearly the dominant ones with xiy being enhanced on the East side of the FoV (where it reaches a 75 percent contribution). The xio SPC is well distributed over the FoV with a mean value of 40 percent in the inner 100 pc radius. For the old SPC we can see a flat distribution over the entire FoV, with a contribution of 20 percent. Very small fractions of HD and FC are required in the central region and the mean age shows a small gradient with values dropping from 1.3 Gyr to 0.7 Gyr in the outer regions.

sources.summary: The paper maps NGC 5899's central ionized gas, describing its strong-emission region, velocity field, and signs of nuclear outflow, rotation, density structure, or inflow.

quotes.quote: This Sy 2 galaxy shows the SER elongated in the direction N-S, tilted by 20 ^ relative to the PMA. A hint of a rotation pattern with the KMA similar to the PMA is observed towards the borders of the FoV, but opposite blueshifts and redshifts are observed internal to the these regions (within the inner 200 pc radius) due to a compact outflow, with blueshifts to the south and redshifts to the north, in agreement with near-IR studies of the same galaxy schoenell2019 . Farther out, blueshifts and redshifts are also observed perpendicularly to the major axis, where enhanced \ is observed.

gmw_00003198.name: NGC 5929
gmw_00003198.overview: NGC 5929 is a spiral Seyfert 2 galaxy [arXiv:1311.6142, arXiv:1505.04052] that forms an interacting pair with NGC 5930 [arXiv:0905.3349, arXiv:1311.6142]. The galaxy hosts a bipolar radio jet [arXiv:1311.6142, arXiv:1505.04052] and exhibits intrinsic X-ray flux variability [arXiv:2007.02616].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5929 as an interacting Seyfert with radio lobes, conical optical emission, jet-gas interaction constraints, misaligned stellar and gas velocities, and split line profiles.

quotes.quote: NGC 5929 NGC 5929 forms an interacting pair with NGC 5930 e.g., Bower94 . The galaxy hosts a linear radio source with two lobes separated by 1 along PA =60 Whit86,Ulve84 , close to the galaxy major axis at PA =45 Schm97 . Optical emission in [ O iii ] and H + [ N ii ] is in the form of cones co-aligned with the radio source, characterised by the opening angle of 80 and an extent of 150-175 pc, and separated by a perpendicular dust lane Bower94,Schm96 . Ferr5929 performed IFU observations in H , [ N ii ] , and [ S ii ] with the TIGER spectrograph, and tested several models to account for the optical and radio emission.

Our map of stellar velocities showed strong misalignment with the gas velocities: the stellar kinematic axis ( PA 30 ) was almost perpendicular to the symmetry axis of the gas. Moreover, the polarity of the stellar velocity field was reversed with respect to gas: in the north-east region, stars had receding motion, while the gas was approaching. The LOS velocity dispersion maps show a stripe of maximum dispersion at PA 160 through the optical nucleus, approximately perpendicular to the photometric axis of H (Fig. NGC5929maps ). The large-FWHM region is associated with asymmetric line profiles (Fig. NGC5929maps ), with a separation of 900 km s ^ 1 between the line components.

sources.summary: The paper studies the Seyfert 2 galaxy NGC 5929, finding a bipolar radio jet, interaction with NGC 5930, and inner gas kinematics including a perpendicular two-component strip.

quotes.quote: NGC 5929 is a spiral galaxy harboring a Seyfert 2 nucleus, located at a distance 35.9 Mpc as quoted in NASA/IPAC EXTRAGALACTIC DATABASE --http://ned.ipac.caltech.edu/ , for which 1 arcsec corresponds to 175 pc at the galaxy. It presents a well defined bi-polar radio jet oriented along the position angle PA 60 ^ , showing a triple structure with two bright hot spots, one located at 0 5 north-east from the nucleus and the other at 0 6 south-west from it. The third and fainter radio structure is observed at the nucleus of the galaxy ulvestad84,wilson89,su96 . Besides this main jet, weak extended emission is observed at 400 MHz, perpendicularly to the main radio jet su96 , and approximately along the minor axis of the galaxy.

We used near-IR integral field spectroscopy of the inner 250 pc of NGC 5929 at a spatial resolution of 20 pc and report the discovery of a 50 pc wide strip crossing the nucleus perpendicularly to the radio jet (PA =-30^ , the SE-NW strip) and extending by 300 pc where the emission lines show two velocity components, one at 150 \ and the other at +150 . We also show that the extended gas emission perpendicular to the SE-NW strip shows only one component, observed in blueshift to the north-east and redshift to the south-west, except around the region of the hotspots, where they are again double. This apparent rotation pattern is opposite to that of the stellar velocity field. We have fitted a rotation model to the one component gas and found a steep rotation curve, and residuals as high as 100 , evidencing deviations from simple rotation, which we attribute to the effect of the companion galaxy NGC 5930.

sources.summary: The paper describes J1526+4140 as a merging radio galaxy also called NGC 5929 in Arp 090, with detected 21-cm absorption, radio lobes, and shocked ionized gas.

quotes.quote: The \ 21-cm absorption towards J1526+4140 (see bottom panel of Fig. fig:det_hi_spec ) is best fit with a single Gaussian component and has a velocity width of 99 . The location of the peak optical depth is blueshifted by 124 \ from the systemic redshift, and is aligned with the weaker of the two \ emission peaks. The total optical depth leads to \ 3 10^ 21 \ for \ = 100 K and \ = 1.

This radio galaxy, also known as NGC 5929, is part of an interacting pair called as Arp 090. Based on high-resolution (25 pc) MERLIN 1.4 GHz observations, the radio source consists of a weak core and two lobes cole1998 . \ 21-cm absorption is detected towards the north-eastern radio lobe with \ of 6 10^ 21 , while no absorption is detected towards the core ( \ <3 10^ 21 ) or the other lobe ( \ <0.2 10^ 21 ). rosario2010a have reported detection of shocked ionized gas due to radio-jet interaction in this AGN based on Hubble Space Telescope spectra and 5 GHz radio image. The \ absorption could be tracing either neutral gas outflow, disturbed gas in the circumnuclear region due to the merger occurring in this system, or the large-scale patchy \ gas in the host galaxy that is detected against one of the radio lobes.

sources.summary: The paper maps NGC 5929 as a Seyfert 2 spiral whose radio jet, shocks, X-ray heating, gas kinematics, and ionized/molecular gas masses shape the inner 520 pc.

quotes.quote: NGC 5929 is a spiral galaxy with a Seyfert 2 nucleus located at a distance of 35.9 Mpc as quoted in NASA/IPAC EXTRAGALACTIC DATABASE --http://ned.ipac.caltech.edu/ . NGC 5929 has a companion, NGC 5930 at 20 ^ northeast from it page52 . It presents a well defined bi-polar radio jet oriented along the position angle PA 60 ^ , showing a triple structure with two bright hot spots, one located at 0 5 north-east from the nucleus and the other at 0 6 south-west from it.

We conclude that the main excitation mechanism of the \ emission in NGC 5929 are shocks due to the radio jet, with some contribution from X-ray heating at locations distant from the radio structures. The highest velocity gas is associated to the interaction of the radio jet with the ISM and thus we conclude that its \ emission is produced mainly by shocks, while X-ray excitation is more important for the rotating gas at lower velocities.

We used Gemini integral field J- and K _ l band spectroscopy of the inner 250 pc radius of the Seyfert 2 galaxy NGC 5929, at spatial resolution 20 pc and velocity resolution 40 , to map the stellar and gas kinematics and the flux distributions of emission lines from the ionized and warm molecular gas. Our main conclusions are: The flux distributions for the 2, H recombination and forbidden emission lines are extended to up to 250 pc, with the highest flux levels oriented along PA=60/240^ (the direction of the radio jet), and well correlated with the radio emission.

sources.summary: The review describes NGC 5929 IFU observations as showing extended [Fe II], enhanced dispersion near radio hot spots, rotation, and an equatorial AGN outflow.

quotes.quote: Let's consider the case of NGC 5929 riffel14b . Our NIFS observations of the NLR of this Seyfert 2 galaxy allow us to resolve 0 1 = 18 pc. The left panel of Fig. n5929 shows the flux distribution in the [ Fe II ] 1.64 m emission line over the inner 300 pc, that is most extended along the direction connecting two radio hot spots shown in green in the figure. The central panel shows a ``stripe" of high velocity dispersion values running perpendicularly to the radio jet. Enhanced velocity dispersion is observed also at the loci of the radio hot spots, where it can be attributed to interaction between the radio jet and the ambient gas.

This 3D rendering of the velocity field shows that most of the gas is rotating with a major axis approximately coincident with the orientation of the radio axis, but there is also an outflow along the equatorial plane of the AGN.

sources.summary: The paper reports rotating stellar kinematics for NGC 5929, low-sigma rings or partial rings near 150-250 pc, and a weaker h3-velocity anti-correlation trend.

quotes.quote: A rotating disk pattern is recognized in the LOS velocity field for all galaxies, with a straight zero velocity line for most of them. For two galaxies -- Mrk 1066 m1066_kin and NGC 5899 (Fig. n5899 ) -- an S shape zero velocity line is observed, which is a signature of the presence of a nuclear bar or spiral arms in these galaxies e.g. combes95,emsellem06 . For most galaxies, the maximum amplitude of the rotation curve is expected to be observed outside of the NIFS FoV. The rotation disk signature is clearly seen in the one one-dimensional plots shown in Fig. vcuts , obtained by averaging the velocity and values within a pseudo-slit with width of 0 25 oriented along the major axis of the galaxy. In addition, distinct structures of low- values ( 50-80 ) are seen in the the maps: low rings or partial rings are seen for Mrk 1066, Mrk 1157, NGC 5929 and NGC 788.
A similar trend (but not so clear) is observed for other four galaxies (NGC 4235, NGC 4388, NGC 5929 and Mrk 1157).

sources.summary: The paper identifies shocked gas in NGC 5929 from a localized radio-jet interaction, linking disturbed low-ionization gas, broad emission, and the dense merger environment to Seyfert feedback.

quotes.quote: We report the discovery of kinematic shock signatures associated with a localized radio jet interaction in the merging Seyfert galaxy NGC 5929. We explore the velocity-dependent ionization structure of the gas and find that low ionization gas at the interaction site is significantly more disturbed than high ionization gas, which we attribute to a local enhancement of shock ionization due to the influence of the jet. The characteristic width of the broad low-ionization emission is consistent with shock velocities predicted from the ionization conditions of the gas. We interpret the relative prominence of shocks to the high density of gas in nuclear environment of the galaxy and place some constraints of their importance as feedback mechanisms in Seyferts.

sources.summary: The paper fits NGC 5929 with XMM-Newton and NuSTAR spectra, finding Compton-thin absorption and intrinsic-flux variability between observations.

quotes.quote: NGC 5929: We fit the \ and \ FPMA/FPMB spectra for this source. All four models used in this analysis confirm a Cth column density during this observation. We note that variability in the intrinsic flux can be seen between the two observations. Letting the column density vary between the observations resulted in consistent results.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 5929 has little very-young stellar contribution, central emission dominated by intermediate and older populations, no hot dust/FC, and a decreasing age gradient.

quotes.quote: NGC 5929: A very small fraction of xy SPC is observed SE of the nucleus ( > 110 pc) of NGC 5929, while no signs of the xyo component are seen. The dominant emission of the central region is due to the xiy SPC reaching 75 percent in most locations of the FoV, and presenting a value of 25 percent in the unresolved nucleus ( < 25 pc), which is dominated by the xio population (up to 50 percent). No hot dust and FC emission are detected. The mean age shows a decreasing gradient with ages between 1.5 Gyr in the nucleus and 0.6 Gyr in the outermost locations ( 100 pc).

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 5929's triple radio structure, identifying a flatter-spectrum central component near the optical nucleus and detecting compact VLBI emission with weak northeast extensions.

quotes.quote: NGC 5929 (1524+418) Y : The central component of the triple structure visible in the 8.4 GHz VLA A array image of Su96 cannot be distinguished in our lower-resolution image. The spectral indices of the north-east and south-west components are steep, with _ 1.6 GHz ^ 15 GHz = 0.9 and 0.8, respectively.
