It is not clear if the two radio components are both part of SDSS J1458+459 or if the eastern radio feature is an independent source, possibly associated with the un-cataloged optical object, although there is a 1 arcsecond discrepancy between the radio and optical positions of the secondary feature.

gmw_00003235.name: ESO534-G001
gmw_00003235.overview: ESO534-G001 is a nearby, low-mass E+A galaxy with a stellar mass of approximately 1.26 billion solar masses [arXiv:1611.07672]. Observations confirm that the galaxy is rich in neutral hydrogen gas, which exhibits substantial substructure [arXiv:1611.07672].

sources.summary: The paper presents ESO534-G001 (GalA) as a low-mass E+A galaxy and confirms it is HI-rich, with atomic star-formation scatter tied to incomplete sampling of HI clouds and star-forming regions.

quotes.quote: In this study, we present high spatial resolution follow-up observations of two HI-rich nearby low-mass E+A galaxies: ESO534-G001 (hereafter referred to as GalA) and 2dFGRS S833Z022 (hereafter referred to as GalB) from zwaan2013cold . These galaxies were identified as E+A galaxies based on their central optical spectra. The stellar masses of these galaxies are log( M_ /M _ ) = 9.1 and log( M_ /M _ ) = 9.3 , respectively zwaan2013cold . These stellar masses are lower than or comparable to that of the Large Magellanic Cloud, which has a stellar mass of log( M_ /M _ ) = 9.3 skibba2012spatial .

We confirm the HI-richness of ESO534-G001 and 2dFRS S833Z022, and find that the scale dependence of the atomic SF relation in these galaxies is consistent with the predicted increase in the scatter towards small size scales. This is due to the incomplete sampling of independent HI clouds and SF regions. This finding adds to the existing literature, which has reported a scale dependence of the molecular SF relation, showing that the atomic and molecular phases are both susceptible to the evolutionary cycling of individual regions. This suggests that the atomic gas reservoirs host substantial substructure, which should be observable with future high-resolution observations.

gmw_00003236.name: 2dFGRS S833Z022
gmw_00003236.overview: 2dFGRS S833Z022 is a nearby, low-mass E+A galaxy with a stellar mass of approximately 2 billion solar masses [arXiv:1611.07672]. High-resolution observations confirm that the galaxy is rich in neutral hydrogen gas, which exhibits a scale-dependent atomic star-formation relation [arXiv:1611.07672].

sources.summary: The paper presents 2dFGRS S833Z022 (GalB) as a low-mass E+A galaxy and confirms it is HI-rich, with atomic star-formation scatter tied to incomplete sampling of HI clouds and star-forming regions.

quotes.quote: In this study, we present high spatial resolution follow-up observations of two HI-rich nearby low-mass E+A galaxies: ESO534-G001 (hereafter referred to as GalA) and 2dFGRS S833Z022 (hereafter referred to as GalB) from zwaan2013cold . These galaxies were identified as E+A galaxies based on their central optical spectra. The stellar masses of these galaxies are log( M_ /M _ ) = 9.1 and log( M_ /M _ ) = 9.3 , respectively zwaan2013cold . These stellar masses are lower than or comparable to that of the Large Magellanic Cloud, which has a stellar mass of log( M_ /M _ ) = 9.3 skibba2012spatial .

We confirm the HI-richness of ESO534-G001 and 2dFRS S833Z022, and find that the scale dependence of the atomic SF relation in these galaxies is consistent with the predicted increase in the scatter towards small size scales. This is due to the incomplete sampling of independent HI clouds and SF regions. This finding adds to the existing literature, which has reported a scale dependence of the molecular SF relation, showing that the atomic and molecular phases are both susceptible to the evolutionary cycling of individual regions. This suggests that the atomic gas reservoirs host substantial substructure, which should be observable with future high-resolution observations.

gmw_00003237.overview: 1406 076 is a blazar located at a redshift of z=1.49 [arXiv:astro-ph/0511664, arXiv:astro-ph/0603268]. The galaxy features a relativistic jet with rapidly fading components [arXiv:astro-ph/0511664], and its X-ray emission is well-fitted by a simple unabsorbed power law [arXiv:astro-ph/0603268].

sources.summary: The paper reports that 1406 076 has a jet similar to earlier observations, rapidly fading components, non-radial motion, and a marginally confident peak apparent speed of 28.2.

quotes.quote: 1406 076.---( z=1.49 ) We find a similar morphology to J01. The jet dims rapidly with distance from the core, and the components become faint and difficult to follow at 22 GHz. Non-radial motion of a component toward the south was seen by J01 for their component B3, similar to the path followed by our C2. However, the fitted speeds of those components are different at the 2 level: J01 measure 43.8 3.5 for their B3, we measure 28.2 6.6 for our C2. Note also that the very high apparent speed we measure for C2 is in part due to the low value of the radial position at the first epoch (see Figure 5, without this first point the fitted apparent speed is a somewhat lower 20.4 7.4 ), although this position seems well-determined (see Figure 4).

sources.summary: The paper reports that PKS 1406-076 had not previously been studied in X-rays, was observed twice by XMM-Newton, and is fitted by a simple unabsorbed power law.

quotes.quote: This source was never studied in X-rays: ROSAT observation resulted only in an upper limit ( 2 ) with F_ 0.1-2.4 keV <2.35 10^ 13 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 (Siebert et al. 1998). XMM-Newton observed this blazar twice, with the second observation about one month after the first one, and the fluxes in the ROSAT energy band were 4.2 and 3.9 10^ 13 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 , respectively. In both observations, the source was best fitted with a simple power law with 1.6 and no additional absorption.

gmw_00003238.name: ULASJ1216-0313
gmw_00003238.overview: ULASJ1216-0313 is a heavily reddened quasar located at a high redshift [arXiv:1910.00602, arXiv:2001.05507]. The galaxy hosts an active galactic nucleus detected in the X-ray band [arXiv:1910.00602] and exhibits prolific, obscured star formation [arXiv:2001.05507].

sources.summary: The paper detects ULASJ1216-0313 at 850 m and infers prolific obscured star formation, with conservative quasar-contamination modelling still giving an SFR near 1570 solar masses per year.

quotes.quote: Of the 19 quasars, only three sources are detected at > 3 confidence. These are ULASJ1216-0313, ULASJ2200+0056 and ULASJ2315+0143 and the corresponding 850 m maps can be seen in Fig. fig:detect .

When accounting for the quasar contamination estimated by the model, we derive SFR = 1570 820 (1440 750) and 690 730 (630 670) M _ yr ^ 1 for ULASJ1216-0313 and ULASJ2200+0056 respectively, based on the parameters in and beelen06 , whilst all the 850 m flux observed in ULASJ2315+0143 can be accounted for by the model. Despite deriving SFRs a factor of > 2.5 lower than those in Section sec:sfrs , in which all cool dust is attributed to star formation, both ULASJ1216-0313 and ULASJ2200+0056 continue to show evidence for prolific star formation.

sources.summary: The paper reports J1216 as an off-axis detected z>2 heavily reddened quasar with spectral analysis over 0.5-10 keV and only an upper-limit fit constraint.

quotes.quote: J0144 was covered as part of the Stripe 82 X-ray survey ( LaMassa16a ), J1216 was detected off-axis in an archival observation (PI Ponman), and J1539 was targetted (PI Zappacosta) and published in Martocchia17 .

For subsequent analyses of these sources, we limit to the energy ranges where there is significant signal above the background: 0.2 - 10 keV for J1234, J2200, and J2315; 0.2 - 4.5 keV for J2243; 0.5 - 10 keV for J1216 and J1539; and 0.5 - 4.5 keV for J2314.
For one source (J1216), the fitting only places an upper limit on .

gmw_00003239.name: ULASJ2200+0056
gmw_00003239.overview: ULASJ2200+0056 is a heavily reddened quasar [arXiv:1910.00602] that exhibits strong blueshifted wings in its emission line profiles [arXiv:1910.00602]. The host galaxy is a strongly star-forming system [arXiv:2001.05507] with a submillimeter emission peak that is spatially offset from the quasar centroid [arXiv:2001.05507].

sources.summary: The paper detects ULASJ2200+0056 with a spatially offset 850 m peak and finds UV and submillimetre evidence for a dusty, strongly star-forming quasar host.

quotes.quote: Of the 19 quasars, only three sources are detected at > 3 confidence. These are ULASJ1216-0313, ULASJ2200+0056 and ULASJ2315+0143 and the corresponding 850 m maps can be seen in Fig. fig:detect . We note that the peak sub-mm flux for ULASJ2200+0056 appears to be spatially offset from the quasar centroid. Nevertheless we class this source as a detection as the peak sub-mm flux lies within one SCUBA-2 beam from the quasar position.

Two of the three quasars, ULASJ2200+0056 and ULASJ2315+0143 also showed evidence for residing in star-forming host galaxies based on our rest-frame ultraviolet detections of the quasar hosts in the Dark Energy Survey wethers18 . The ultraviolet-derived star formation rates for these two quasars are among the highest in our sample - SFR _ UV 200-400 M _ yr ^ 1 . Nevertheless, the sub-mm derived star formation rates are significantly higher, implying that a large fraction of the star formation in heavily reddened quasar hosts is obscured by dust.

sources.summary: ULASJ2200+0056 is modeled with a reddened-quasar plus Lyman component, but multi-band UV excess rules out pure scattered Lyman emission; scattered light remains possible.

quotes.quote: We model the remaining six quasars - ULASJ2200+0056, VHSJ2227-5203, VHSJ2306-5447, ULASJ2315+0143, VHSJ2332-5240 and VHSJ2355-0011 - with a two-component model consisting of a reddened quasar template and a Lyman- emission line.

Although both ULASJ2200+0056 and VHSJ2306-5447 return ^ 2 _ red,ly 2, we note that excess rest-frame UV emission relative to the reddened quasar model is detected in multiple bands in ULASJ2200+0056, further ruling out the scattered Lyman- scenario for this quasar.

Although we cannot rule out the scattered light scenario in explaining the UV excess in ULASJ2200+0056 or ULASJ2224-0015, where we see no evidence for extended emission, we likely require an alternative explanation for the remainder of our quasar sample.

sources.summary: The paper identifies J2200 as a newly observed heavily reddened quasar targeted for luminous line emission and showing strong blueshifted line-profile wings.

quotes.quote: For five of these sources (J1122, J2200, J2205, J2243, and J2314), the quasars were targetted as part of a new program (PI Lansbury; program ID 082409), which targetted sources with luminous line detections ( L_ OIII >10^ 43 ) from Temple19 .

For subsequent analyses of these sources, we limit to the energy ranges where there is significant signal above the background: 0.2 - 10 keV for J1234, J2200, and J2315; 0.2 - 4.5 keV for J2243; 0.5 - 10 keV for J1216 and J1539; and 0.5 - 4.5 keV for J2314.
For four HRQs the 80 velocity width is large, with a range of w_ 80 =1710 - 2572 km \ s^ 1 , and in two cases (J2200 and J2243) strong blueshifted wings are clearly observed in the line profiles.

gmw_00003240.name: JADES-GS-z10-0
gmw_00003240.overview: JADES-GS-z10-0 is a spectroscopically confirmed high-redshift galaxy located in the GOODS-S region [arXiv:2306.02468, arXiv:2212.12804]. It is a compact, star-forming system characterized by a young stellar population and a stellar mass of approximately 10^8 solar masses [arXiv:2212.12804].

sources.summary: JADES-GS-z10-0 is a GOODS-S spectroscopically confirmed high-redshift galaxy; EAZY gives z_a=10.84, slightly high but within 2 sigma, with a comparable Prospector offset.

quotes.quote: In the GOODS-S region, this redshift range includes two of the spectroscopically-confirmed galaxies from robertson2022 and curtislake2022 , JADES-GS-z10-0 ( z_ spec = 10.38^ +0.07 _ 0.06 ) and JADES-GS-z11-0 ( z_ spec = 11.58^ +0.05 _ 0.05 ). The EAZY photometric redshifts for these targets are z_a = 10.84 for JADES-GS-z10-0, and z_a = 12.31 for JADES-GS-z11-0. Both photometric redshifts are higher than the measured spectroscopic redshift, but considering the P(z) uncertainty in both measurements, the measurements are within 2 of the true values. Indeed, the ^2 between the minimum value corresponding to z_a and the value at z_ spec is 10.25 for JADES-GS-z10-0 and 1.75 for JADES-GS-z11-0. In robertson2022 , the authors estimate photometric redshifts for these sources using the Bayesian stellar population synthesis fitting code Prospector johnson2021 and recover P(z) surfaces that are similarly offset to higher values than the spectroscopic redshifts.

As described in robertson2022 , both JADES-GS-z10-0 (JADES-GS-53.15883-27.7735) and JADES-GS-z11-0 (JADES-GS-53.16476-27.77463) were previously identified in bouwens2011 , as UDFj-38116243 and UDFj-39546284, respectively. Both of these galaxies are in our z > 8 sample, as JADES-GS-53.15883-27.7735 and JADES-GS-53.16476-27.77463.

sources.summary: The paper treats JADES-GS-z10-0 as one of four spectroscopically confirmed z>10 JADES galaxies, with measured stellar mass and star formation properties compared against cosmological simulations.

quotes.quote: Spectroscopic confirmation has now arrived with the discovery in the JADES survey of 4 galaxies with z_ spec >10 and M_* 10^8 Curtis-Lake2022,Robertson2022 . By observing 65 arcmin ^2 of the GOODS-S field with JWST NIRCam and NIRSpec, JADES has confirmed the four earliest detected galaxies: JADES-GS-z10-0 at z=10.38^ +0.07 _ 0.06 , JADES-GS-z11-0 at z=11.58^ +0.05 _ 0.05 , JADES-GS-z12-0 at z=12.63^ +0.24 _ 0.08 , and JADES-GS-z13-0 at z=13.2^ +0.04 _ 0.07 . Curtis-Lake2022,Robertson2022 have measured stellar masses and star formation rates for these galaxies using the Prospector SED-fitting code Johnson2021 , finding them to be compact, star forming galaxies with young stellar populations and relatively high star formation surface densities. In this letter, we compare the observed JADES galaxies to predictions from an array of large cosmological hydrodynamical simulations.

gmw_00003241.name: JADES-GS-z11-0
gmw_00003241.overview: JADES-GS-z11-0 is a compact, star-forming galaxy [arXiv:2212.12804] with a spectroscopically confirmed redshift of z = 11.122 [arXiv:2404.04325]. It exhibits weak emission lines [arXiv:2404.04325] and is potentially associated with a nearby, compact companion galaxy located 0.3 arcseconds to its south [arXiv:2404.04325].

sources.summary: The paper reports deep MOSFIRE spectroscopy of UDFj-39546284, finding a tentative line signal but concluding that current data cannot distinguish a low-redshift line emitter from a z~12 galaxy candidate.

quotes.quote: We report the results of deep (4.6h) H band spectroscopy of the well studied z 12 H-band dropout galaxy candidate UDFj-39546284 with MOSFIRE on Keck-I. These data reach a sensitivity of 5-10 10^ 19 per 4.4 \ resolution element between sky lines. Previous papers have argued this source could either be a large equivalent width line emitting galaxy at 2<z<3.5 or a luminous galaxy at z 12 . We find a 2.2 peak associated with a line candidate in deep Hubble-Space-Telescope Wide-Field-Camera 3 Infrared grism observations, but at a lower flux than what was expected. After considering several possibilities we conclude these data can not conclusively confirm or reject the previous line detection, and significantly deeper spectroscopic observations are required.

Using the Keck-I MOSFIRE infrared spectrograph we recover the 2.7 line detection reported in 2013ApJ...765L...2B at the 2.2 level. Combining these results we find 3.5 line a 15985.5 4.4 with an estimated flux of 1.8 0.5 10^ 18 , but the likelihood that this is a chance coincidence of noise peaks is non-negligible, so further observations are needed. If confirmed, the detection indicates UDFj-39546284 is actually a low redshift line emitter at z 2.19 or 3.29, so both H and K band spectra should be obtained to uniquely determine its redshift.

sources.summary: The paper remeasures UDF-22980 as a spectroscopically confirmed z~11.6 galaxy candidate, finding a strong high-redshift photometric solution and revisiting earlier low-redshift interpretations.

quotes.quote: Out of these 6 sources, one is UDFj-39546284 (UDF-22980 in this study) which is the previously-detected source at z 12 e.g. bouwens2011,ellis2013 with recent spectroscopic confirmation at z_ spec =11.58 curtislake2022 . Our best-fitting photo-z for this galaxy from EAZY is in excellent agreement with this, yielding z = 11.65^ +0.17 _ 0.21 , with a ^2=40 between the primary solution and the low-redshift secondary solution ( z=2.9^ +0.1 _ 0.1 ), indicating a clear preference for the primary (higher) redshift. This is consistent with the photo-z from ellis2013,mclure2013 which at the time was based on only a F160W detection (combined with non-detections through other HST and Spitzer filters).

After UDF-22980 was proposed to lie at z=11.9 ellis2013,mclure2013 , there followed much discussion on the nature of this object, in part informed by a series of follow-up observations. brammer2013 used HST GRISM spectroscopy and capak2013 used Keck MOSFIRE to search for emission lines. No robust emission lines were detected but tentative ( 2 ) detections were reported at _ obs 1.6 m. It was concluded that this could be consistent with an [ OIII ] 4959,5007 detection at z=2.19 or [ OII ] 3727,3729 at z=3.29 . Now, however, with the JWST medium band imaging at _ obs >1.6 m we can robustly constrain the shape of the galaxy SED.

sources.summary: The paper presents JADES-GS-z11-0 as a compact star-forming z=11.58 galaxy whose stellar mass is challenging for several simulations, though Simba gives a plausible abundance range.

quotes.quote: Spectroscopic confirmation has now arrived with the discovery in the JADES survey of 4 galaxies with z_ spec >10 and M_* 10^8 Curtis-Lake2022,Robertson2022 . By observing 65 arcmin ^2 of the GOODS-S field with JWST NIRCam and NIRSpec, JADES has confirmed the four earliest detected galaxies: JADES-GS-z10-0 at z=10.38^ +0.07 _ 0.06 , JADES-GS-z11-0 at z=11.58^ +0.05 _ 0.05 , JADES-GS-z12-0 at z=12.63^ +0.24 _ 0.08 , and JADES-GS-z13-0 at z=13.2^ +0.04 _ 0.07 . Curtis-Lake2022,Robertson2022 have measured stellar masses and star formation rates for these galaxies using the Prospector SED-fitting code Johnson2021 , finding them to be compact, star forming galaxies with young stellar populations and relatively high star formation surface densities. In this letter, we compare the observed JADES galaxies to predictions from an array of large cosmological hydrodynamical simulations.

As we move to higher redshift, only Simba and OBELISK produce even a single galaxy above the best-fit stellar masses of JADES-GS-z11-0, JADES-GS-z12-0, and JADES-GS-z13-0. Illustris and TNG100 are unable to produce galaxies at z 11.5-12 which reach even the lower estimate for the stellar mass of JADES-GS-z11-0.

Even for the most extreme case, JADES-GS-z11-0, the probability of finding a galaxy with stellar mass above M_*=10^ 8.9 at z 11 , in a random volume of 2.7 10^5 ^3 in Simba, is 8 . If we instead take the lower estimate for the stellar masses from JADES-GS-z11-0 ( M_*=10^ 8.5 ), this probability rises to 92 .

sources.summary: HUDFj-39546284 is the single plausible z~10 candidate, detected in H160 but not other bands or IRAC, with photometric redshift z=10.4.

quotes.quote: The z 10 Galaxy Candidate 0 pt 7 ID & & & H_ 160 & J_ 125 H_ 160 & S/N _ H160 HUDFj-39546284 & 03:32:39.54 & -27:46:28.4 & 28.8 0.2 & >2.02 & 6.3 tbp All galaxies passing the above selection criteria, using both the ACS and WFC3/IR data, are retained and analyzed individually. These total to 17 sources with H_ 160,AB in the range 23.6-28.8 mag; one source in the HUDF, none in the parallel HUDF09 fields, three in the ERS and 8 and 5 in the CANDELS Deep and Wide, respectively (see Tables tab:phot and tab:photContamin ). Interestingly, only one source (the previously reported galaxy with H_ 160 29 mag from Bouwens et al. 2011) did pass our selection in the three deep HUDF09 fields, while the shallower CANDELS and ERS fields contribute a total of 16 sources (all with H_ 160,AB 26 mag).

All sources with IRAC detections are thus removed from our sample of potential z 10 galaxies, reducing the sample to one single candidate in the HUDF, previously reported in see Figure fig:stampz10 and Table tab:phot Bouwens11 . The 16 removed sources are shown in the appendix in Figure fig:stampsContamin and listed in Table tab:photContamin . The z 10 Galaxy Candidate Images of the only possible z 10 galaxy candidate are shown in Figure fig:stampz10 . The source is detected at 6.3 in H_ 160 (

As can be seen, the source is not significantly detected in any other band. Its value of ^2_ opt =2.77 is very close, but just below the limit of ^2_ cut =2.8 . This is mainly due to a 1.5 flux excess in i_ 775 , which appears to be due to an extended structure in the background of that image. When adopting smaller apertures, the ^2_ opt value is found to be reduced, indicating also that this excess of flux is not associated with the source itself. The Spitzer IRAC 3.6 m data shows some flux from a nearby source. However, after subtraction of all the neighboring sources in IRAC, the candidate is undetected ( 0.09 ), with a 2 upper limit on its IRAC [ 3.6 ] magnitude of >27.2 mag AB (Gonzalez et al., in prep.). This thus corresponds to H_ 160 [ 3.6 ] <1.6 at 2 , which is much bluer (by >0.4 mag) than the typical low-redshift contaminants that we culled from our sample (see also next section). After adding the newly acquired IRAC data from the IUDF10 program (Spitzer proposal 70145, PI: Labbe) to the GOODS IRAC data and removing neighboring sources, the z 10 candidate is also undetected at [ 4.5 ] ( S/N=0.3 ) providing added weight to the likelihood of it being at high rather than low reshift. We derive the photometric redshift of the candidate using the code ZEBRA Feldmann06,Oesch10c with synthetic stellar population models from Bruzual03 to which we added nebular continuum and line emission following, e.g., Schaerer09 . Using the full 11 band fluxes and flux errors, we derive a photometric redshift for this source of z_ phot = 10.4^ +0.5 _ 0.4 , with a likelihood of a low redshift solution at z<8 of <6 .

sources.summary: JADES-GS-z11-0 is a GOODS-S spectroscopically confirmed high-redshift galaxy; EAZY gives z_a=12.31, higher than spectroscopy but within 2 sigma, with similar Prospector behavior.

quotes.quote: In the GOODS-S region, this redshift range includes two of the spectroscopically-confirmed galaxies from robertson2022 and curtislake2022 , JADES-GS-z10-0 ( z_ spec = 10.38^ +0.07 _ 0.06 ) and JADES-GS-z11-0 ( z_ spec = 11.58^ +0.05 _ 0.05 ). The EAZY photometric redshifts for these targets are z_a = 10.84 for JADES-GS-z10-0, and z_a = 12.31 for JADES-GS-z11-0. Both photometric redshifts are higher than the measured spectroscopic redshift, but considering the P(z) uncertainty in both measurements, the measurements are within 2 of the true values. Indeed, the ^2 between the minimum value corresponding to z_a and the value at z_ spec is 10.25 for JADES-GS-z10-0 and 1.75 for JADES-GS-z11-0. In robertson2022 , the authors estimate photometric redshifts for these sources using the Bayesian stellar population synthesis fitting code Prospector johnson2021 and recover P(z) surfaces that are similarly offset to higher values than the spectroscopic redshifts.

As described in robertson2022 , both JADES-GS-z10-0 (JADES-GS-53.15883-27.7735) and JADES-GS-z11-0 (JADES-GS-53.16476-27.77463) were previously identified in bouwens2011 , as UDFj-38116243 and UDFj-39546284, respectively. Both of these galaxies are in our z > 8 sample, as JADES-GS-53.15883-27.7735 and JADES-GS-53.16476-27.77463.

sources.summary: The paper identifies JADES-GS+53.16474-27.77471 as a possible high-redshift companion to JADES-GS-z11-0, with dropout photometry, compact size, and a stellar mass estimate.

quotes.quote: JADES-GS+53.16474-27.77471 Our updated, deeper NIRCam imaging provides stronger evidence of a secondary source 0.3^ south ( 1.2 kpc at z = 11.39 ) of JADES-GS-z11-0, which can be seen in the thumbnail in Figure fig:2d_1d_plots . This object, JADES-GS+53.16474-27.77471, appears to be an F150W dropout potentially associated with JADES-GS-z11-0, although it did not fall onto the NIRSpec MSA. In Figure fig:JADES-GS-z11b , we plot the SED and thumbnails for this source, where we show both the 0.2 ^ circular aperture and ForcePho photometry. We fit both sets of photometry with EAZY following the procedure in Section sec:nircam-photometry , and the minimum ^2 redshift is z_a = 12.41 for the fit to the circular aperture photometry, and z_a = 12.31 for the fit to the ForcePho photometry. While this redshift is potentially biased high for the same reasons as are described in Section sec:dla-fits , there is some probability of the source being at the spectroscopic redshift of JADES-GS-z11-0 as shown in the P(z) plot inset of the figure.

From the ForcePho fit to the source, we calculate a half-light radii of only 0.03^ 0.01^ for JADES-GS+53.16474-27.77471, which is 109 pc at z_a = 12.41 (116 pc at the redshift of JADES-GS-z11-0), a similar size to JADES-GS-z11-0 given the uncertainties. We fit the ForcePho photometry for this source with Prospector to estimate the stellar mass of this potential satellite, and calculate a stellar mass of (M_*/M_ ) = 8.0^ +0.4 _ 0.6 .

sources.summary: GS-z11-0 is a z=11.58 JADES galaxy whose mass is marginally above the dn/dz=1 line and remains difficult for hydrodynamic simulations to match perfectly.

quotes.quote: The JADES survey Bunker_2019 has provided measurements on five galaxies with spectroscopically confirmed redshifts at z>10 . Three of these galaxies are the most distant yet detected. , and have constrained physical properties of these five galaxies, finding that the galaxies lie at (mean) spectroscopic redshifts of z = 10.38 (GS-z10-0), z = 11.58 (GS-z11-0), z = 12.63 (GS-z12-0), z = 13.20 (GS-z13-0) and z = 10.60 (GN-z11).

All of the galaxies are consistent with the dn/dz = 1 line except the GS-z11-0 galaxy whose error bars are marginally above the dn/dz = 1 line. This means that we should expect to observe at least one galaxy of that stellar mass in the field of view of NIRCam.
Both GN-z11 and GS-z11-0 are unusual galaxies and detailed hydrodynamic simulations are clearly struggling to match their luminosities and stellar masses perfectly.

sources.summary: The paper recovers UDFj-39546284 as the lone earlier z>8.5 candidate, revises it toward z=11.9 from F140W non-detection, and notes an exotic emission-line interloper remains possible.

quotes.quote: Bouwens \ initially located 3 promising J band dropouts at z 10 but, on re-examining the completed dataset, presented only a single candidate, UDFj-39546284 at a photometric redshift of z =10.3, not drawn from the original three.

Although we recover the candidate UDFj-39546284 (previously proposed at z =10.3), it is undetected in the newly added F140W image, implying it lies at z =11.9 or is an intense emission line galaxy at z 2.4 . Although no physically-plausible model can explain the required line intensity given the lack of Lyman or broad-band UV signal, without an infrared spectrum we cannot rule out an exotic interloper.

In summary, only one object claimed to be at z>8.5 from the earlier UDF09 analysis remains and that is the final J band dropout presented by bouwens2011a at z =10.3, UDFj-39546284 ( UDF12-3954-6384 in Table 1). However, its non-detection in the UDF12 F140W data indicates a yet higher redshift of z =11.9 (Figure 2).

sources.summary: The paper presents ultra-deep JWST spectra of JADES-GS-z11-0, measuring weak emission lines, a refined spectroscopic redshift, and a damped Lyman-alpha absorber fit to its break.

quotes.quote: We describe new ultra-deep James Webb Space Telescope (JWST) NIRSpec PRISM and grating spectra for the galaxies JADES-GS-z11-0 ( z_ spec = 11.122^ +0.005 _ 0.003 ) and JADES-GS-z13-0 ( z_ spec = 13.20^ +0.03 _ 0.04 ), the most distant spectroscopically-confirmed galaxy discovered in the first year of JWST observations. The extraordinary depth of these observations (75 hours and 56 hours, respectively) provides a unique opportunity to explore the redshifts, stellar properties, UV magnitudes, and slopes for these two sources. For JADES-GS-z11-0, we find evidence for multiple emission lines, including [ O 2 ] 3726,3729 and [ Ne 3 ] 3869 , resulting in a spectroscopic redshift we determine with 94 confidence. At this spectroscopic redshift, the Lyman- break in JADES-GS-z11-0 can be fit with a damped Lyman- absorber with (N_ HI / cm ^ 2 ) = 22.42^ +0.093 _ 0.120 .

sources.summary: The paper treats the single B11 galaxy candidate as the lone z~10 J-dropout in HUDF09, saying it weakly constrains L_10 and may be spurious or a low-redshift interloper.

quotes.quote: For example, B11 found one candidate J-dropout above their detection limit in the two-year Hubble Ultra Deep Field 09 dataset, yet nine were expected assuming no evolution in the LF from z=8 .
The single galaxy candidate observed at z 10 poorly constrains L_ 10 from below.
This result would remain unchanged if the single B11 galaxy candidate were actually a spurious detection or a low-redshift interloper (B11 estimate a 20 total probability for such an event).

sources.summary: The paper treats UDFj-39546284 as a real high-redshift candidate, noting its F140W non-detection and ambiguity between an extreme z~2 emission-line galaxy and a z~12 source.

quotes.quote: Ellis13 also re-analyzed our previously detected z 10 candidate UDFj-39546284 Bouwens11a,Oesch12a . From the three years of WFC3/IR H_ 160 data, it is completely clear now that XDFjh-39546284 is a real source as it is significantly detected in all three major sets of data taken in 2009, 2010 and 2012 Bouwens12c . Very surprisingly, however, UDFj-39546284 appears not to be detected in the new F140W image, indicating that this source is either a very extreme emission line galaxy at z 2 or that it lies at z 12 with the spectral break of the galaxy at 1.6 see also Brammer13 .

gmw_00003242.name: SDSS J1111+1437
gmw_00003242.overview: SDSS J1111+1437 is a mini-broad-absorption line quasar located at a redshift of z=2.138 [arXiv:1805.01545]. The galaxy hosts an active galactic nucleus with an ionized gas outflow located approximately 880 parsecs from the central source [arXiv:1805.01545].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes the mini-BAL quasar SDSS J1111+1437 at z=2.138, deriving column density, ionization, electron density, and an outflow distance of about 880 pc from VLT/X-shooter absorption troughs.

quotes.quote: We determine the physical conditions and location of the outflow material seen in the mini-BAL quasar SDSS J1111+1437 (z = 2.138). These results are based on the analysis of a high S/N, medium-resolution VLT/X-shooter spectrum. The main outflow component spans the velocity range 1500 to 3000 km s ^ 1 and has detected absorption troughs from both high-ionization species: , , , , , and ; and low-ionization species: , , , , , , and . Measurements of these troughs allow us to derive an accurate photoionization solution for this absorption component: a hydrogen column density, log( )=21.47^ +0.21 _ 0.27 cm ^ 2 and ionization parameter, log( )=-1.23^ +0.20 _ 0.25 . Troughs produced from the ground and excited states of \ combined with the derived \ value allow us to determine an electron number density of log( ) = 3.62^ +0.09 _ 0.11 cm ^ 3 ; and to obtain the distance of the ionized gas from the central source: R=880^ +210 _ 260 pc .

gmw_00003243.name: CLM 1
gmw_00003243.overview: CLM 1 is a high-redshift Lyman-break galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 6.17 to 6.2 [arXiv:1504.05875, arXiv:1611.06654]. It is a UV-bright galaxy that exhibits spatially extended line emission [arXiv:1504.05875] and a tentative dust continuum detection [arXiv:1611.06654].

sources.summary: The paper presents CLM 1 as a z=6.17 UV-bright galaxy with significant, spatially extended ALMA line emission in its observed interstellar medium.

quotes.quote: The galaxy which we name here as CLM 1 was discovered by Cuby:2003 at a redshift of z=6.17 . The other galaxy, WMH 5 at z=6.07 , was discovered in Willott:2013a . Both galaxies have near-IR continuum magnitudes AB 24 making them among the brightest rest-frame UV galaxies known at this epoch.

In Figure fig:mapsclm1 we present the ALMA and NIR imaging of the galaxy CLM 1. The \ map is made from a sum over all channels showing significant line emission. The \ emission is spatially extended along a similar direction as the beam extension. A Gaussian fit to the source gives an observed size of 0 73 0 42 at position angle east of north (PA) = 42, compared with a beam size of 0 52 0 44 at PA = 55.

sources.summary: The paper treats CLM1 as a z=6.2 Lyman-break galaxy with tentative ALMA dust continuum, requiring efficient dust accretion unless metallicity or stellar dust production is high.

quotes.quote: So far, A1689-zD1 Watson:2015aa,Knudsen:2016aa and CLM1 Willott:2015aa satisfy this criterion. The dust continuum of CLM1 at z=6.2 was tentatively detected at the 2 level by ALMA. Because of the rareness of the sample, we use this flux as detected, keeping in mind that this might overestimate the dust emission (or the efficiency of dust enrichment) in our model.

The upper and lower panel of Fig. \ fig:constraint-2 show the results for CLM1. Comparing Figs. \ fig:constraint and fig:constraint-2 , we observe that the condition for dust formation and SN destruction is similar for both objects. That is, for small dust condensation efficiency in stellar ejecta ( f_ in 0.1 ), both object need very efficient dust accretion ( _ acc,0 0.004 ) under the metallicity constraint Z<0.1 Z _ .

gmw_00003244.name: WMH 5
gmw_00003244.overview: WMH 5 is a highly luminous Lyman-break galaxy at a redshift of approximately 6.07 [arXiv:1504.05875, arXiv:1202.5330]. The system appears to be an ongoing merger [arXiv:1504.05875] featuring a compact main galaxy and a tail of emission containing two distinct sub-galaxies [arXiv:1706.09968].

sources.summary: The paper describes WMH 5 as a z=6.07 UV-luminous system with extended ALMA line and continuum emission, spatial UV-FIR offsets, and evidence for an ongoing merger.

quotes.quote: The ALMA and NIR images for WMH 5 are shown in Figure fig:mapswmh5 . There is a much more firm 1.2 mm continuum detection in this source at significance 6 . The beam is more circular for WMH 5 than for CLM 1 and both the \ and 1.2 mm continuum are clearly spatially extended at PA 80 . The \ emission centroid shows a significant offset from the NIR emission of 0 4 .

However, in this system we know that there is a spatial offset between the UV and FIR, so such a simple scenario of attenuation and re-emission is not physically plausible. From this analysis the system appears to be an on-going merger of two galaxies. `B' is apparently lower mass but is spatially coincident with the NIR emission. This is similar to some lower redshift ULIRG mergers Chapman:2004 with an optically obscured component that dominates the FIR emission and a lower L_ FIR component less obscured by dust.

sources.summary: WMH5 is presented as a z=6.0695 Lyman-break galaxy system whose ALMA dust and [C II] data reveal a compact core, tail sub-galaxies, and likely filamentary accretion.

quotes.quote: We present ALMA observations of the dust continuum and [ C 2 ] 158 m line emission from the z=6.0695 Lyman Break Galaxy WMH5. These observations at 0.3'' spatial resolution show a compact ( 3 kpc) main galaxy in dust and emission, with a `tail' of emission extending to the east by about 5 kpc (in projection). The tail is comprised predominantly of two distinct sub-components in velocity, separated from the core by 100 and 250 km s ^ 1 , with narrow intrinsic widths of about 80 km s ^ 1 , which we call `sub-galaxies'. The sub-galaxies themselves are extended east-west by about 3 kpc in individual channel images. The tail joins smoothly into the main galaxy velocity field.

We conjecture that the WMH5 system represents the early formation of a galaxy through the accretion of smaller satellite galaxies, embedded in a smoother gas distribution, along a possibly filamentary structure. The results are consistent with current cosmological simulations of early galaxy formation, and support the idea of very early enrichment with dust and heavy elements of the accreting material.

sources.summary: The paper identifies WMH 5 as an unambiguous, very luminous z~6 Lyman-break galaxy with Ly emission; a nearby foreground disk modestly magnifies it, but it remains intrinsically extreme.

quotes.quote: Strong \ emission was only found in two of the z 6 galaxies, WMH 5 and WMH 13. These are assigned QF=A.

WMH 5 has M_ 1350 =-22.65 , which is even more luminous than the lowest known luminosity z 6 quasar, CFHQS J0216-0455 ( M_ 1450 =-22.21 , Willott et al. 2009). WMH 5 is only 2.1 arcsec from the center of a galaxy in Figure fig:colorimages . This is an inclined disk galaxy with a photometric redshift of z=1.01 , rest-frame B band absolute magnitude of M_B=-21.5 and disk half-light radius 4 kpc.

Using the results of studies of the Tully-Fisher and similar relations for z=1 disk galaxies (Dutton et al. 2011; Miller et al. 2011), we find the circular velocity for this galaxy should be 160 km s ^ 1 . Modeling the gravitational potential as an isothermal sphere, the gravitational lensing magnification of WMH 5 due to this galaxy is a factor of 1.27. Hence the intrinsic absolute magnitude of WMH 5 is still extremely luminous with M_ 1350 22.4 .

gmw_00003245.name: Eye of Horus
gmw_00003245.overview: The Eye of Horus is a double-source-plane strong gravitational lens system [arXiv:1911.04805]. Its central lens galaxy is a massive early-type galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.795, which projects an Einstein ring and an arc from two background galaxies [arXiv:1911.04805].

sources.summary: The paper describes Eye of Horus as a double-source-plane strong lens with a massive early-type lens galaxy and evaluates cluster-scale mass structure affecting its lens image.

quotes.quote: The Eye of Horus is a strong gravitational lens object MTanaka that was discovered by the Subaru HSC--SSP survey HSC--SSP; HSC1stDR,HSC1styrOverview,Miyazaki18HSC,Komiyama18HSC,Kawanomoto18HSC,Furusawa18HSC,Bosch18HSC,Haung18HSC,Coupon18HSC . It is known as a precious double source plane (DSP) object. The lens galaxy of the Eye of Horus is a massive (stellar mass 7 10^ 11 M_ ) early-type galaxy which is located at (RA, DEC) = ( 14^ h 24^ m 49^ s .0 , 00 53 21 65 ) (J2000), and the redshift of the lens galaxy is z=0.795 . Since there are two background galaxies behind the lens galaxy whose redshifts are z=1.302 and 1.988 , respectively, an Einstein ring and an arc corresponding to these background galaxies are projected near the Einstein radii.

We observed X-ray emission around the Eye of Horus with XMM--Newton to evaluate the influence of cluster-scale mass structure on the lens image of the Eye of Horus . There are two clusters, the main cluster and the NE cluster which is located 100'' northeast of the lens galaxy, and we found that the center position of the main cluster is located 3.8 1.0'' south of the lens galaxy. The surface brightness distribution of the main cluster and the NE cluster is represented by a single, and a double model, respectively. We also revealed that the spectrum of the main cluster is represented by a one-temperature model, while that of the NE cluster needs a two-temperature model.

gmw_00003246.name: PKS 0048-097
gmw_00003246.overview: PKS 0048-097 is a strongly variable BL Lacertae object [arXiv:1604.05642, arXiv:1605.03599] located at a redshift of z = 0.635 [arXiv:1205.1215, arXiv:1605.03599]. The active galaxy exhibits strong optical beaming [arXiv:1205.1215] and displays variability across multiple wavelengths, including radio, optical, X-ray, and gamma-ray bands [arXiv:1205.1215, arXiv:1605.03599].

sources.summary: The paper reports X-Shooter spectra of PKS 0048-097, deriving redshift z = 0.635, a strong optical beaming factor, gamma-ray emission, and an EBL-absorbed high-energy spectrum.

quotes.quote: We identify the first two lines as [ O II ] ( _ 0 = 3727 ) and [ O III ] ( _ 0 = 5007 ) from which a consistent redshift of z = 0.635 is derived. The third emission line ( = 10751 ) is identified as H that corresponds to z = 0.638 . The small velocity difference ( 600 km s ^ 1 ) between the redshift derived from the narrow emission lines and the broad ones is not uncommon in quasars (see e.g. boroson05 ). We therefore assume that the redshift of PKS 0048-097 is consistently z = 0.635 .

In the case of PKS 0048-097 this ratio is 234 . The extreme value of this beaming (second only to OJ 287, see Figure   fig:beamfact ) is consistent with no detection of the host galaxy fal96 . PKS 0048-097 is a ray emitter observed with FERMI in the band 100 MeV - 10 GeV and it is well fitted by a single power law ( _ 0 = 2.38 0.24 ). Assuming the extrapolation of this power law to the TeV band we calculated the expected emission corrected for the EBL absorption domin11 adopting the derived redshift.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes nine years of multi-frequency data for PKS 0048-097, finding strong variability and no cross-band correlation outside optical bands.

quotes.quote: Nine years of multi-frequency monitoring of the blazar PKS 0048-097: spectral and temporal variability Multi-frequency monitoring of PKS 0048-097. Alicja Wierzcholska 1,2 . 1Insitute of Nuclear Physics, Polish Academy of Sciences ul. Radzikowskiego 152, 31-342 Krak o w, Poland 2Landessternwarte, Universit at Heidelberg, K onigstuhl 12, D-69117 Heidelberg, Germany alicja.wierzcholska@ifj.edu.pl A. Wierzcholska Blazars are highly variable, radio-loud active galactic nuclei with jets oriented at a small angle to the line of sight. The observed emission of these sources covers the whole electromagnetic spectrum from radio frequencies up to the high or even very high energy gamma-ray range. To understand the complex physics of these objects, multi-wavelength observations and studies on the variability and correlations between different wavelengths are therefore essential. The long-term multi-frequency observations of \ are analysed here to investigate its spectral and temporal features. The studies includes nine years of observations of the blazar, which is well studied in the optical and radio domain, but not in the other frequencies. Multi-wavelength data collected with OVRO, KAIT, Catalina, \ and \ were studied. The performed analysis revealed strong variability in all wavelengths that is most clearly manifested in the X-ray range. The correlation studies do not exhibit any relation between different wavelengths, except for the very strong positive correlation between the optical emission in V and R bands.

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 0048 097 as a strongly variable BL Lac object with uncertain redshift, variability from radio to optical bands, X-ray detection, and small R-band variability in this campaign.

quotes.quote: PKS 0048 097: is a strongly variable BL-Lac object. Its redshift is highly uncertain since its spectrum is basically flat; Stickel93 derived a lower limit of z 0.2 . A tentative value of z 0.63 was estimated RectorStocke01 and then confirmed at z=0.635 Landoni12 . This BL-Lac object, as most of its kind, shows variability at almost every wavelength range, from radio to optical bands Pica88 . It has also been detected in X-rays Brinkmann00 .

gmw_00003247.name: G1
gmw_00003247.overview: G1 is a foreground spiral galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.154 [arXiv:1205.1215]. It has a scale length of 5 Kpc and exhibits a disturbed morphology [arXiv:1205.1215].

sources.summary: The paper identifies G1 as the foreground spiral galaxy associated with the Mg II absorber, giving its projected distance, redshift, luminosity, disturbed morphology, and scale length.

quotes.quote: Moreover, a Mg II absorption system at z = 0.154 that is associated with a foreground spiral galaxy at 50 Kpc projected distance is found.

On the other hand, the spiral galaxy G1 ( m_ = 19.2 ) at 23^ North-West from the BL Lac corresponding to 50 Kpc projected distance at redshift z = 0.154 stickel93 is the obvious galaxy associated to the observed Mg II absorption. This galaxy ( M_ = -20.57 ) has a disturbed morphology (see panel (b), () and (d) of Figure   fig:fov ) after model subtraction and a scale length of 5 Kpc. This is a rare case-study of absorption system at low redshift (see e.g. kac11 ) in which the counterpart can be well investigated through imaging analysis.

gmw_00003248.name: NGC 4434
gmw_00003248.overview: NGC 4434 is an E0 elliptical galaxy [arXiv:1507.01336, arXiv:1105.4809] characterized by a large nucleus [arXiv:1507.01336] and perfectly elliptical isophotes [arXiv:1105.4809]. The galaxy hosts a supermassive black hole [arXiv:1803.08055] and features a uniformly old stellar population of approximately 10 billion years [arXiv:1105.4809].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 4434 as an E0 galaxy with a large nucleus, regular deep surface-brightness profiles, a profile bump, and a steep outer color decrease.

quotes.quote: NGC 4434 NGC 4434 (also known as VCC 1025) is an E0 galaxy where F+06 highlighted the large nucleus, derived from a ``break'' in the surface brightness profiles around 1'' . The ellipticity and P.A. profiles in Fig. ell4434 show strong variations within the first 20 '' , which are not mirrored in the shape parameters ( a and b high-order coefficients), which look very regular and featureless in the central 20'' , making this galaxy a quite perfect E0 system (there is a peak of 0.1 in the ellipticity at a 6'' while <0.05 everywhere). However, the shape parameters start to show strong variation outside, which are difficult to comment on because of the large errors.

Figure prof4434 reproduces the azimuthal SB profile in g and i bands. Even deeper than for NGC 4472, reaching 28 mag/arcsec ^2 in g band and 27 mag/arcsec ^2 in i band at a/a_ e \ 10 , they appear regular and very similar, and both show a bump in the profiles at a/a_ e \ 2.5 . This feature is evident as an excess of the residuals with respect to the best-fitting de Vaucouleurs profiles (Table tab:devauc ), which are again overplotted on the SB profiles and are better highlighted by the (O-C) curves (central panel of Fig. prof4434 ).

sources.summary: NGC 4434 is an E0 galaxy with elliptical isophotes, rotation, declining velocity dispersion, old stellar ages, and a radially changing metallicity gradient.

quotes.quote: NGC 4434 = VCC 1025 This E0 ( = 0.04) has perfectly elliptical isophotes. The rotation velocity reaches V_ max = 45 at 10 -- 15 arcsec ( 1 ) and remains constant afterward. In the region with a velocity gradient, the velocity dispersion decreases from _0 = 135 \ to = 70 . The age is uniformly old at about 10 Gyr, and the metallicity decreases monotonically with the radius. The slope of the metallicity profile changes at = 4 arcsec, being steeper in the inner part of the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 4434 with Gemini/NIFS and SAURON kinematics, finding a roughly 7.0e7 solar-mass black hole that is a large positive outlier from the relation.

quotes.quote: We present mass estimates of supermassive black holes in six nearby fast rotating early-type galaxies (NGC 4339, NGC 4434, NGC 4474, NGC 4551, NGC 4578 and NGC 4762) with effective stellar velocity dispersion around 100 km/s. We use near-infrared laser-guide adaptive optics observations with the GEMINI/NIFS to derive stellar kinematics in the galactic nuclei, and SAURON observations from the ATLAS ^ 3D Survey for large-scale kinematics. We build axisymmetric Jeans Anisotropic Models and axisymmetric Schwarzschild dynamical models.

Both modelling approaches recover consistent orbital anisotropies and black hole masses within 1-2 confidence level, except for one galaxy for which the difference is just above the 3 level. Two black holes (NGC 4339 and NGC 4434) are amongst the largest outliers from the current black hole mass - velocity dispersion relation, with masses of (4.3^ +4.8 _ 2.3 ) 10^7 and (7.0^ +2.0 _ 2.8 ) 10^7 M _ , respectively ( 3 confidence level).

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 4434 as a power-law galaxy and says the VCS pure model falls below the central data, producing a lower inferred cusp slope.

quotes.quote: VCS Models of Power-law Galaxies NGC 4434, 4464, 4621, and 4660 are galaxies that we have classified as power-laws that lf fitted with pure laws. The VCS models for all four galaxies fall below the data as 0 (Figure fig:acscomp2 ). Again, this behavior is echoed in VCS Figure 103, which shows that the fits fall below the observed domain ACS VCS data for <0 3 in NGC 4464, 4621, and 4660, and <0 15 in NGC 4434.

gmw_00003249.name: NGC 4474
gmw_00003249.overview: NGC 4474 is a nearby, fast-rotating early-type galaxy [arXiv:1803.08055]. Its nucleus exhibits a central velocity-dispersion drop within a radius of 35 pc, and its supermassive black hole mass is constrained to an upper limit of 7 million solar masses [arXiv:1803.08055].

sources.summary: The paper places a <7e6 solar-mass upper limit on NGC 4474's black hole and reports a central velocity-dispersion drop not attributable to cold stellar structure.

quotes.quote: We present mass estimates of supermassive black holes in six nearby fast rotating early-type galaxies (NGC 4339, NGC 4434, NGC 4474, NGC 4551, NGC 4578 and NGC 4762) with effective stellar velocity dispersion around 100 km/s. We use near-infrared laser-guide adaptive optics observations with the GEMINI/NIFS to derive stellar kinematics in the galactic nuclei, and SAURON observations from the ATLAS ^ 3D Survey for large-scale kinematics. We build axisymmetric Jeans Anisotropic Models and axisymmetric Schwarzschild dynamical models.

For two galaxies (NGC 4474 and NGC 4551) we are able to place upper limits on their black holes masses ( <7 10^6 and <5 10^6 M _ , respectively, 3 confidence level). The kinematics for these galaxies clearly indicate central velocity dispersion drops within a radius of 35 pc and 80 pc, respectively. These drops cannot be associated with cold stellar structures and our data do not have the resolution to exclude black holes with masses an order of magnitude smaller than the predictions.

gmw_00003250.name: NGC 4551
gmw_00003250.overview: NGC 4551 is an early-type elliptical galaxy [arXiv:1803.08055, arXiv:1105.4809] with a central core that is approximately 2 Gyr younger than the rest of the galaxy [arXiv:1105.4809]. Its nucleus exhibits a central velocity-dispersion drop within a radius of 80 pc [arXiv:1803.08055], which is not associated with cold stellar structures [arXiv:1803.08055].

sources.summary: The paper places a <5e6 solar-mass upper limit on NGC 4551's black hole and reports an 80 pc central velocity-dispersion drop not tied to cold stellar structure.

quotes.quote: We present mass estimates of supermassive black holes in six nearby fast rotating early-type galaxies (NGC 4339, NGC 4434, NGC 4474, NGC 4551, NGC 4578 and NGC 4762) with effective stellar velocity dispersion around 100 km/s. We use near-infrared laser-guide adaptive optics observations with the GEMINI/NIFS to derive stellar kinematics in the galactic nuclei, and SAURON observations from the ATLAS ^ 3D Survey for large-scale kinematics. We build axisymmetric Jeans Anisotropic Models and axisymmetric Schwarzschild dynamical models.

For two galaxies (NGC 4474 and NGC 4551) we are able to place upper limits on their black holes masses ( <7 10^6 and <5 10^6 M _ , respectively, 3 confidence level). The kinematics for these galaxies clearly indicate central velocity dispersion drops within a radius of 35 pc and 80 pc, respectively. These drops cannot be associated with cold stellar structures and our data do not have the resolution to exclude black holes with masses an order of magnitude smaller than the predictions.

sources.summary: NGC4551 is an E2 galaxy with a central drop, changing velocity dispersion and rotation, and a core about 2 Gyr younger than the bulk.

quotes.quote: NGC4551 = VCC1630 This is an E2 galaxy ( = 0.26) with a drop in the centre. The velocity dispersion increases from _0 = 90 to 100 at = 2 arcsec. The rotation velocity concomitantly reaches 30 to continue to raise slowly to 40 . The velocity dispersion declines to 80 at 1 . The SSP-equivalent age of the core is about 2 Gyr younger that the bulk, aged about 9 Gyr.

gmw_00003251.name: NGC 4578
gmw_00003251.overview: NGC 4578 is a nearby, fast-rotating early-type galaxy [arXiv:1803.08055]. Dynamical modeling of its galactic nucleus reveals a central supermassive black hole with an estimated mass of approximately 1.9e7 solar masses [arXiv:1803.08055].

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 4578 with Gemini/NIFS and SAURON kinematics and finds its roughly 1.9e7 solar-mass black hole lies on the scaling relation.

quotes.quote: We present mass estimates of supermassive black holes in six nearby fast rotating early-type galaxies (NGC 4339, NGC 4434, NGC 4474, NGC 4551, NGC 4578 and NGC 4762) with effective stellar velocity dispersion around 100 km/s. We use near-infrared laser-guide adaptive optics observations with the GEMINI/NIFS to derive stellar kinematics in the galactic nuclei, and SAURON observations from the ATLAS ^ 3D Survey for large-scale kinematics. We build axisymmetric Jeans Anisotropic Models and axisymmetric Schwarzschild dynamical models.
The black holes in NGC 4578 and NGC 4762 lie on the scaling relation with masses of (1.9^ +0.6 _ 1.4 ) 10^7 and (2.3^ +0.9 _ 0.6 ) 10^7 M _ , respectively ( 3 confidence level).

gmw_00003252.name: CTS C30.10
gmw_00003252.overview: CTS C30.10 is a bright, distant quasar located at a redshift of approximately 0.90052 [arXiv:1901.09757, arXiv:1909.05572]. Its broad magnesium II emission line consists of two kinematic components modeled as Lorentzian profiles [arXiv:1408.1520, arXiv:1909.05572], which exhibit variability that has been monitored to measure reverberation time lags [arXiv:1901.09757, arXiv:2201.11062].

sources.summary: The paper studies quasar CTS C30.10 with SALT spectroscopy, finding a type-B Mg II profile with two Lorentzian components and variability suitable for future time-delay monitoring.

quotes.quote: The object CTS C30.10 discussed in this paper is of type B class and requires such complex approach. CTS C30.10 is one of the quasars found in the Calan-Tololo Survey aimed to identify new bright quasars in the southern part of the sky. The source is located at RA = 04h47m19.9s, DEC = -45d37m38s. The quasar nature of the source was confirmed using the slit spectroscopy (Maza et al. 1993).

We performed five spectroscopic observations of a quasar CTS C30.10 (z = 0.9000) with the SALT telescope between December 2012 and March 2014, and we studied the variations of the spectral shape in the 2700 -2900 rest frame. We showed that the Mg II line in this source consists of two kinematic components, which makes the source representative of type B quasars. Both components were well modeled with a Lorentzian shape. Both components vary in a similar way.

sources.summary: The paper reports six years of SALT monitoring of the bright quasar CTS C30.10 and measures a Mg II reverberation time lag near 562 days at redshift 0.90052.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: The source is located at RA = 04h47m19.9s, Dec=-45d37m38s (J2000.0).

quotes.quote: We report 6 yr monitoring of a distant bright quasar CTS C30.10 ( z = 0.90052 ) with the Southern African Large Telescope (SALT). We measured the rest-frame time-lag of 562 2 days between the continuum variations and the response of the Mg II emission line, using the Javelin approach. More conservative approach, based on five different methods, imply the time delay of 564^ +109 _ 71 days.

Quasar CTS C30.10 has been found in the Calan-Tololo Survey, as a part of survey aimed to find new bright quasars in the southern part of the sky. The source is located at RA = 04h47m19.9s, Dec=-45d37m38s (J2000.0). Quasar identification has been confirmed by maza1993 . The source is bright ( V = 17.2 , as given by NED), the redshift ( z = 0.910 in NED) has been revised to z = 0.9000 by modzelewska2014 .

sources.summary: The paper monitors quasar CTS C30.10 with SALT, giving its coordinates and redshift, extracting MgII emission, and using its reverberation time lag for radius-luminosity work.

quotes.quote: Between December 6, 2012 and December 10, 2018, we monitored the quasar CTS C30.10 using the 10 meter SALT telescope in order to detect the time-lag of the LIL emission-line of MgII with respect to the quasar continuum. The hypothesis was to probe the consistency of the radius-monochromatic luminosity relation towards higher redshifts and higher absolute luminosities. The quasar CTS C30.10 was discovered as a bright source in the Calan-Tololo survey 1993RMxAA..25...51M among 200 newly discovered quasars with the visual magnitude of V=17.2 (NED) at the intermediate redshift of z= 0.90052 2014A&A...570A..53M . The equatorial coordinates of the source are RA= 04h47m19.9s and Dec=-45d37m38s (J2000.0).

The MgII line was extracted from the spectral energy distribution of CTS C30.10 in the wavelength range 2700-2900 by subtracting the two spectral components, namely the power-law component due to the accretion disc thermal emission, and FeII - line pseudo-continuum. The MgII broad line was found to consist of two kinematic components -- redshifted and blueshifted Lorentzian profiles the use of the double Lorentzian profiles is motivated from the study for this source in 2014A&A...570A..53M . The value of the ^2 obtained for the double Lorentzian is the lowest (see their Table 3).

sources.summary: The paper monitors quasar CTS C30.10 with SALT, measuring MgII and UV FeII reverberation lags and using wavelength-resolved mapping to probe BLR structure.

quotes.quote: We present the results of the reverberation monitoring aimed at MgII broad line and FeII pseudocontinuum for the luminous quasar CTS C30.10 (z = 0.90052) with the Southern African Large Telescope covering the years 2012-2021. We aimed at disentangling the MgII and UV FeII variability and the first measurement of UV FeII time delay for a distant quasar. We used several methods for time-delay measurements, and determined both FeII and MgII time delays as well as performed a wavelength-resolved time delay study for a combination of MgII and FeII in the 2700 - 2900 restframe wavelength range. We obtain the time delay for MgII of 275.5^ +12.4 _ 19.5 days in the rest frame, while for FeII we have two possible solutions of 270.0^ +13.8 _ −25.3 days and 180.3^ +26.6 _ −30.0 in the rest frame.

In our previous paper about the luminous quasar CTS C30.10, we reported the long-term measurement of MgII emission Czerny2019 . The reverberation mapping result using various methods revealed a time delay of 562 ^ +116 _ 68 days (rest frame) between the 3000 continuum and the MgII line variations in this source.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes CTS C30.10 reverberation data, reports its redshift and sky position, and finds it follows the broad-line-region size-luminosity relation for quasars.

quotes.quote: The studied source CTS C30.10 was found in the Calan-Tololo Survey as a bright quasar 1993RMxAA..25...51M of V=17.2 (NED magnitude) at the intermediate redshift of z=0.90052 2014A&A...570A..53M . The source is located in the southern hemisphere with the equatorial coordinates of RA = 04h47m19.9s, Dec=-45d37m38s (J2000.0). Between December 6, 2012 and December 10,2018 it was monitored by the 10-m SALT telescope in order to reliably detected the time-lag of MgII line with respect to the continuum, determine the size of the BLR, and finally to probe the radius-luminosity relation towards higher redshifts.

In conclusion, we find that the quasar lies on the broad-line region (BLR) size -- monochromatic luminosity power-law scaling, R_ BLR L_ 5100 ^ 1/2 , which was already confirmed for low-redshift sources.

gmw_00003253.name: NGC 5486
gmw_00003253.overview: NGC 5486 is a background galaxy characterized by relatively regular outer isophotes [arXiv:2105.05167]. A nearby emission source is likely a star-forming dwarf satellite of the galaxy, exhibiting a structure and luminosity similar to the dwarf irregular NGC 4163 [arXiv:2105.05167].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 5486 as a background galaxy with regular outer isophotes and argues a nearby emission source is likely its star-forming dwarf satellite.

quotes.quote: In the case of the source near NGC 5486, it is unlikely to be a part of the galaxy's outer spiral arms. The galaxy itself has relatively regular outer isophotes in our deep B and V band images (see Figure dwarf_detail ). Given that we can resolve the spatially extended nature of the M101 candidates in our images, but cannot for this source, it is unlikely that this is a star-forming object in the M101 Group, but likely more distant.

The source with the lowest H EW is the source associated with NGC 5486. If at the distance of NGC 5486, it has an H luminosity of 8.7e37 giving it an SFR of 4.7e-4 kennicutt2012 . It is interesting to note that its H luminosity and thus SFR are similar to that of NGC 4163, a nearby dwarf irregular galaxy kennicutt2008 . Their H EWs are similar as well; compare our source's 8 2 to NGC 4163's 8 2 EW kennicutt2008 . It is quite likely that this source is a star-forming satellite of NGC 5486 similar in structure and luminosity to NGC 4163.

gmw_00003254.name: J053408.41+085450.6
gmw_00003254.overview: J053408.41+085450.6 is an ambiguous active galactic nucleus flare candidate with conflicting photometric and spectroscopic redshift estimates [arXiv:2209.13004]. The source is associated with a 32.1 mJy radio source at 21 cm, suggesting it may be a blazar despite its quiet optical history [arXiv:2209.13004].

sources.summary: The paper flags J053408.41+085450.6 as an ambiguous AGN-flare candidate, with conflicting redshifts, inconclusive Keck spectroscopy, and a radio association suggesting a possible blazar.

quotes.quote: One of the sources, J053408.41+085450.6, has a quoted photometric redshift of z = 0.5 from MQC and a quoted spectroscopic redshift of z = 1.62 from GAIA DR3 GaiaDR3 , though the latter has flags indicative of an ambiguous redshift identification (i.e., FLAGS QSOC = 13). The higher redshift would place this source outside the LIGO/Virgo detection limit, though we still consider this candidate in our analysis given the low score of that redshift solution. We obtained a 600s spectrum of the source from Keck Observatory (see Appendix app:spectra ), finding a relatively featureless spectrum and an inconclusive redshift determination. The source is also associated with a 32.1 mJy radio source at 21 cm from the NRAO VLA Sky Survey Condon1998 , which suggests that this might actually be a blazar, although previous optical activity of the object is fairly quiet and it does not appear in any blazar catalogs.

gmw_00003255.name: J1636+47
gmw_00003255.overview: J1636+47 is an early-type galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.23 [arXiv:0708.2151]. It acts as a strong gravitational lens with a baryon-dominated core and a transition to dark matter dominance near one effective radius [arXiv:0708.2151].

sources.summary: The paper maps lens J1636+47 in total and baryonic mass, showing a baryon-dominated core and a transition to dark matter near one effective radius.

quotes.quote: Figure fig:map shows in greyscale the mass map of lens J1636+47 (z=0.23). The greyscale corresponds to the total surface mass density, and the projected scale is given as a fraction of the effective radius ( R_e=5.4 kpc). We divide the map into two regions. The panel on the left shows the region where M_ STAR /M_ TOT >0.5 i.e. mostly dominated by baryons, whereas the panel on the right shows the region dominated by dark matter. As expected, the core is dominated by baryons -- as seen in the radial profiles of figure fig:profs - and the transition takes place at around R_e .

gmw_00003256.name: WISE J064459.38+603131.7
gmw_00003256.overview: WISE J064459.38+603131.7 is an active galactic nucleus at a redshift of approximately 0.358 [arXiv:1810.01204, arXiv:1506.07373]. It has been classified as a radio-weak BL Lacertae candidate [arXiv:1810.01204, arXiv:1701.06067] or a weak emission-line quasar [arXiv:1404.5211, arXiv:1506.07373], though its exact nature remains elusive due to a lack of compact radio emission [arXiv:1810.01204].

sources.summary: The paper treats J0644+6031 as a radio-weak BL Lac candidate: optical data support the classification, but EVN observations find no compact radio source, leaving its radio quietness and BL Lac nature uncertain.

quotes.quote: J0644+6031 was selected as a blazar candidate counterpart of 2FGL J0644.6+6034 Massaro2013 , an unassociated ray source (UGS) in the second Fermi LAT source catalogue 2FGL, Nolan2012 . The nearest USNO-B Monet2003 optical counterpart to this WISE source is just 0 3 away http://vizier.u-strasbg.fr/viz-bin/VizieR-3?-source=I/284 . Follow-up optical spectroscopic observations by Paggi2014 revealed weak emission lines with REWs consistent with the threshold of 5 , supporting the BL Lac classification, and suggest a redshift of z = 0.3582 0.0008 .

In case of our other target, J0644+6031 , we searched for compact radio emission in the naturally weighted dirty map with a field of view as large as 2 2 , even though the a priori Gaia position was accurate to mas level (Table tab-1 ). However, nothing was detected above a signal-to-noise ratio of 6. The 6- brightness upper limit is 120 Jy beam ^ 1 . We conclude that any mas-scale compact radio source in J0644+6031 should be weaker than 0.12 mJy (Table tab-2 ).
Based on the data currently available, the nature of J0644+6031 remains elusive, and further efforts are required to draw a final conclusion.

sources.summary: The paper treats WISEJ064459.38+603131.7 as an intriguing gamma-ray blazar candidate with weak emission lines, redshift z = 0.36, X-ray and infrared counterparts, but no obvious radio counterpart.

quotes.quote: WISEJ064459.38+603131.7 This source is the gamma-ray blazar candidate counterpart of the UGS 2FGLJ0644.6+6034 proposed by 2013arXiv1303.3585M . The USNO-B optical counterpart lying at 0.4'' features magnitudes B1=19.44 mag, R1=19.03 mag, B2=19.33 mag, R2=18.23 mag and I2=18.28 mag, while the IR counterpart 2MASSJ06445937+6031318 ( 0.1'' ) features magnitudes J=16.923 mag, H=15.979 mag and K=15.371 mag. According to 2013ApJS..209....9P this source is positionally consistent ( 0.1'' ) with the X-ray Swift source SWXRTJ064459.9+603132 with a 0.3-10 keV unabsorbed flux 2.1 10 ^ 13 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 .

The spectrum of WISEJ064459.38+603131.7, in particular, is somewhat reminiscent of weak emission line quasar spectra 2006ApJ...644...86S,2009ApJ...696..580S , but the blazar identification for this source appears problematic. In fact the WISEJ064459.38+603131.7 is not detected by NVSS survey, so we can only put an upper limit on its flux 2.5 mJy .

sources.summary: The paper identifies WISE J064459.38+603131 as one of the first genuine radio-weak BL Lac objects, supported by mid-infrared colors, optical spectroscopy, variability, and Swift X-ray detection.

quotes.quote: On the basis of this analysis, we identify WISE J064459.38+603131 and WISE J141046.00+740511.2 as the first two genuine RWBLs, both potentially associated with \ sources. Finally, to strengthen our identification of these objects as true RWBLs, we present multifrequency observations for these two candidates to show that their spectral behavior is indeed consistent with those of the BL Lac population.

WISE J064459.38+603131.7, being selected on the basis of its mid-IR colors is definitively consistent with the BZB population on the 3.4 m - 4.6 m - 12 m diagram, while WISE J173052.85-035247.2 appears to have such colors more similar to the WELQs listed in the M14 subsample.
We collected 13 counts for WISE J064459.38+603131 in the merged XRT event file while there are 36 counts for WISE J141046.00+740511.2, making both sources well detected above the 5 threshold.

sources.summary: The paper revises the likely counterpart of 3FGLJ0644.6+6035 to XRT J064435+603850, confirms a blazar spectrum, and uses Mg II absorption to set z > 0.581.

quotes.quote: 3FGLJ0644.6+6035 : This object is an UGS in the 3FGL. The optical counterpart of this ray source was proposed to be WISE J064459.38+603131.7 and classified as a blazar at z = 0.358 paggi2014 . Based on the new 3FGL error box and the analysis of the Swift data, we propose the X-ray source XRT J064435+603850 as the likely counterpart for the Fermi emitter. Our optical spectrum confirms the blazar nature of the candidate and a prominent intervening absorption system of Mg II (2800 ) is detected at 4425 , setting a redshift lower limit of the object at z > 0.581.

sources.summary: The paper describes WISE J064459.38+603131.7 as a proposed IR/X-ray counterpart and weak emission-line quasar at z=0.3582, but doubts its association because no radio detection is known.

quotes.quote: Massaro et al. (2013b) propose WISE J064459.38+603131.7 as the possible IR counterpart to 2FGL J0644.6+6034, while Paggi et al. (2013) find that this WISE source is also detected by Swift /XRT. Recently, Paggi et al. (2014) have provided the optical spectrum of this possible counterpart, finding that it could be a weak emission line quasar at z=0.3582 . As is evident in Figure f1 , the positional uncertainties of 2FGL J0644.6+6034, 3FGL J0644.6+6035 and 1FHL J0644.2+6036 are partly overlapping. The error ellipse of 2FGL J0644.6+6034, apart from the source discussed by Massaro et al. (2013b) and Paggi et al. (2014) (source 2), contains also source 1 found in this work, while the error ellipse of 3FGL J0644.6+6035 contains only this XRT detection, which incidentally is also the brightest of the two X-ray emitters. The lack of any radio detection so far for source 2, combined with the above-mentioned characteristics of source 1, raise some questions about the reality of the association between WISE J064459.38+603131.7 and 2FGL J0644.6+6034, and suggest instead that source 1 is the only putative counterpart to the 2FGL, 3FGL, and 1FHL Fermi object.

gmw_00003257.name: NVSS J064435+603849
gmw_00003257.overview: NVSS J064435+603849 is a flat-spectrum compact radio source [arXiv:1506.07373] with colors compatible with the blazar strip [arXiv:1506.07373]. It features a strongly inverted spectral index between 5 and 7 GHz, which indicates that variability is occurring in the source [arXiv:1607.03415].

sources.summary: The paper favors NVSS J064435+603849/B0640.0+6041 as a radio and XRT source with blazar-strip WISE colors, rather than a previously proposed WISE quasar counterpart.

quotes.quote: The XRT counterpart found in this work (source 1 in Figure f1 ) is listed both in the NVSS (NVSS J064435+603849) and in the WENSS (B0640.0+6041) catalogues with 20 and 92 cm flux densities of 33.5 and 83 mJy, respectively (spectral index = 0.6 ) Here and in the following, we use S ^ , where S is the flux density, is the frequency, and the spectral index. . The WISE colours are W2-W3=1.97 and W1-W2=0.64 , making this object compatible with the blazar strip.

The error ellipse of 2FGL J0644.6+6034, apart from the source discussed by Massaro et al. (2013b) and Paggi et al. (2014) (source 2), contains also source 1 found in this work, while the error ellipse of 3FGL J0644.6+6035 contains only this XRT detection, which incidentally is also the brightest of the two X-ray emitters. The lack of any radio detection so far for source 2, combined with the above-mentioned characteristics of source 1, raise some questions about the reality of the association between WISE J064459.38+603131.7 and 2FGL J0644.6+6034, and suggest instead that source 1 is the only putative counterpart to the 2FGL, 3FGL, and 1FHL Fermi object. As a confirmation of our findings, we note that source 1 is also reported in the survey work by Schinzel et al. (2015) as a flat-spectrum compact radio source detected with a 6 cm flux density of 8.2 mJy.

sources.summary: The paper treats 1FHL J0644.2+6036 as a detected UGS/blazar candidate, matching its counterpart to X-ray associations and finding an inverted VLBA spectral index that suggests variability.

quotes.quote: Our proposed counterparts for the sources 1FHL J0644.2+6036, 1FHL J1223.3+7953 and 1FHL J0601.0+3838 are compatible with the X-ray associations proposed by Landi2015 and Paggi2013 , as indicated in the notes in Table tab_unassoc .

We note that for the UGS both the NVSS and VLBI flux densities are lower than for the associated ones (Fig. radio_histo_VLBA_detected ), therefore they may be weaker blazars not yet identified. Moreover, for eight detected UGS we calculated the spectral index values between 5 and 7 GHz VLBA data, which are either flat or inverted, indicating the presence of a self-absorbed region. In particular for the source 1FHL J0644.2+6036 the spectral index is strongly inverted ( =-2.7), exceeding the canonical value of -2.5 for a homogeneous synchrotron emission region. This is an indication that variability is occurring in this source.

gmw_00003258.name: PMN J1130--7801
gmw_00003258.overview: PMN J1130--7801 is an active galactic nucleus associated with a high-flux X-ray source [arXiv:1506.07373]. It exhibits a flat radio spectrum and infrared colors that are fully compatible with the blazar strip [arXiv:1506.07373].

sources.summary: The paper presents PMN J1130--7801 as the likely radio counterpart of the XRT/1FHL source, with high X-ray flux, flat radio spectrum, and blazar-strip WISE colors.

quotes.quote: This XRT source has a counterpart in the ROSAT Bright (1RXS J113029.0--780108, 7 arcsec error radius) source catalogue (Voges et al. 1999), as well as in a XMM-Newton Slew survey catalogue (Saxton et al. 2008) (XMMSL1 J113031.6--780112, 3 arcsec error radius); indeed, its X-ray flux, listed in Table tab4 , is relatively high. This X-ray source is most likely associated with the radio source PMN J1130--7801. The PMN object has a 36 cm flux density of 136 mJy and a flat ( =-0.4 ) radio spectrum from 3.5 to 6 cm (McConnell et al. 2012). The source is also reported as a WISE object with the following IR colours: W2-W3=1.93 and W1-W2=0.6 , which are fully compatible with the blazar strip. The source is discussed in a study of the Chamaeleon star-forming region (Alcala et al. 1995): it has been spectroscopically investigated, but unfortunately with no success. We also note that this X-ray/radio source falls within the positional uncertainty of the 3FGL counterpart (3FGL J1130.7--7800) of this 1FHL source.

gmw_00003259.name: 2MASX J12235831+7953279
gmw_00003259.overview: 2MASX J12235831+7953279 is a galaxy classified as a BL Lacertae object [arXiv:1506.07373]. It exhibits a flat radio spectrum [arXiv:1506.07373], blazar-like infrared colors [arXiv:1506.07373], and has been detected in both ultraviolet and gamma-ray wavelengths [arXiv:1506.07373].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 2MASX J12235831+7953279 as a galaxy and BL Lac counterpart, with flat radio emission, blazar-strip WISE colors, UV detection, and matching gamma-ray positions.

quotes.quote: This X-ray source is listed in the Two Micron All Sky Survey Extended sources catalogue (Skrutskie et al. 2006) as 2MASX J12235831+7953279 and was classified as a galaxy in NED. The source is also found in both the NVSS (NVSS J122358+795329) and WENSS (WN 1222.0+8010) catalogues with 20 and 92 cm flux densities of 31.5 and 43 mJy, respectively. A power-law fit between these two frequencies indicates a rather flat ( 0.2 ) radio spectrum. This is confirmed by the findings of Schinzel et al. (2015), who report parsec scale radio emission with a flat spectrum for this X-ray source. The WISE colours ( W2-W3=1.92 and W1-W2=0.48 ) place the source well inside the blazar strip.

This object is also listed in the Galaxy Evolution Explorer All-Sky Survey (GALEX, Bianchi et al. (2011)) as GALEXASC J122357.56+795328.7 with magnitudes of 24.6 and 22.3 in the far- and near-UV. Massaro et al. (2015) have recently identified it as a BL Lac of unknown redshift. We note that this 1FHL source is listed as 3FGL J1222.7+7952 in the third Fermi catalogue, and the XRT source lies at the border of its positional uncertainty confirming 2MASX J12235831+7953279 as the counterpart to both gamma-ray detections.

gmw_00003260.name: NVSS J131552--073301
gmw_00003260.overview: NVSS J131552--073301 is a weak radio source with a flat radio spectrum [arXiv:1506.07373]. It exhibits blazar-like WISE colors, ultraviolet variability, and high polarization, suggesting it may be a BL Lacertae object or a gamma-ray blazar [arXiv:1506.07373].

sources.summary: The paper treats NVSS J131552--073301 as the XRT/radio counterpart, citing flat radio emission, blazar-like WISE colors, UV variability and polarization, and possible BL Lac nature.

quotes.quote: In this case, the XRT source is associated with a relatively weak radio source (NVSS J131552--073301), displaying a 20 cm flux density of 23.8 mJy and a flat ( 0.1 ) radio spectrum (Petrov et al. 2013). Radio emission is also reported by Schinzel et al. (2015) with 6 and 3.3 cm flux densities of 40.2 and 38.4 mJy, respectively, confirming the flat radio spectrum. This source is WISE-detected with colours W2-W3=2.27 and W1-W2=0.88 , which are again compatible with those of gamma-ray emitting blazars.

In UV the source can be associated with a GALEX object (GALEXASC J131553.03--073302.4), which displays variability and high polarization (Bauer et al. 2009; Fujiwara et al. 2013). On the basis of these peculiarities, Massaro et al. (2013b) suggest that it could be a new gamma-ray blazar, while Hassan et al. (2013) conclude that it might be a BL Lac object. This 1FHL source is reported as 3FGL J1315.7--0732 in the third Fermi catalogue. Our XRT detection lies well within the 3FGL positional uncertainty, thus also confirming our proposed association for the lower energy gamma-ray emitter.

gmw_00003261.name: NVSS J213430--213032
gmw_00003261.overview: NVSS J213430--213032 is a flat-spectrum radio source [arXiv:1506.07373] that has been identified as a promising counterpart to a Fermi high-energy detection [arXiv:1506.07373]. The object exhibits WISE colors compatible with the blazar strip [arXiv:1506.07373] and is suggested to be a BL Lac-type blazar [arXiv:1506.07373].

sources.summary: The paper selects NVSS J213430--213032 as the most promising counterpart, citing flat radio emission, blazar-strip WISE colors, and literature suggestions of a BL Lac-type blazar.

quotes.quote: Source 1 has a radio counterpart (NVSS J213430--213032) with a 20 cm flux density of 22 mJy and a flat radio index of 0.23 (Petrov et al. 2013). It is also reported in the list of possible compact radio counterparts to 2FGL sources by Schinzel et al.
