(2015), where it is described as a flat spectrum source with 6 and 3.3 cm flux densities of 92.8 and 103.9 mJy, respectively. The WISE colours ( W2-W3=2.27 and W1-W2=0.78 ) are just compatible with the blazar strip and the source is discussed by various authors as a possible counterpart to 2FGL J2134.6--2130; for example, Massaro et al. (2013b) and Hassan et al.(2013) suggest that it could be a blazar of the BL Lac type. Source 2 is listed as a galaxy in NED, but it has no detection in radio, and it does not display WISE colours typical of blazars, so we consider it an unlikely association. As for source 3, the lack of any radio counterpart, as well as of WISE colours ( W2-W3=3.213 and W1-W2=0.481 ) that are not compatible with the blazar strip, suggests that this object is also an unlikely association. The third Fermi catalogue lists this object as 3FGL J2134.5--2131. Its restricted error ellipse excludes source 3 as a likely counterpart, but maintains 1 and 2 as possible associations. On the basis of our analysis, we consider only the first to be the most promising counterpart to this Fermi high-energy detection.

gmw_00003262.name: NVSS J225815--364433
gmw_00003262.overview: NVSS J225815--364433 is a galaxy classified as a BL Lacertae object [arXiv:1506.07373]. It is a bright radio, ultraviolet, and infrared source with mid-infrared colors that are compatible with the blazar strip [arXiv:1506.07373].

sources.summary: The paper describes NVSS J225815--364433/SUMSS J225816--364446 as a brighter radio, WISE, and UV source reported as a galaxy and classified as a BL Lac.

quotes.quote: The other detection (source 2 in the figure) is brighter and has a radio detection in the NVSS (NVSS J225815--364433) and in the SUMSS (SUMSS J225816--364446) with 20 and 36 cm flux densities of 10.6 and 14.1 mJy, respectively. The radio spectrum is quite flat displaying an index of 0.23 (Petrov et al. 2013). The source is also reported in the WISE catalogue with colours W2-W3=2.24 and W1-W2=0.57 , which are compatible with the blazar strip. It is also listed in the GALEX All-Sky survey (GALEXASC J225815.00--364434.6) with far- and near-UV magnitudes around 20--21. Source 2 is reported as a galaxy in NED and has recently been classified as a BL Lac by Landoni et al. (2015).

gmw_00003263.name: Sagittarius Dwarf Spheroidal galaxy
gmw_00003263.overview: The Sagittarius Dwarf Spheroidal galaxy is a dwarf spheroidal galaxy undergoing infall and tidal disruption around the Milky Way [arXiv:2303.13180]. This strong tidal disruption significantly affects the galaxy's dark matter density profile and halo structure [arXiv:2303.13180].

sources.summary: The paper models Sagittarius Dwarf/Sgr with a hydrodynamic simulation, finding tidal disruption lowers its dark-matter J-factor and weakens its usefulness for indirect dark-matter searches.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: centre of the simulated Sagittarius Dwarf, defined as ( , ) (284,-30.5)^

quotes.quote: The Sagittarius Dwarf Spheroidal galaxy (Sgr) is investigated as a target for DM annihilation searches utilising J-factor distributions calculated directly from a high-resolution hydrodynamic simulation of the infall and tidal disruption of Sgr around the Milky Way. In contrast to past studies, the simulation incorporates DM, stellar and gaseous components for both the Milky Way and the Sgr progenitor galaxy. The simulated distributions account for significant tidal disruption affecting the DM density profile.

Utilising this methodology provides a more accurate model of the Sgr DM halo than derivations utilising stellar tracers through more accurately accounting for the strong tidal disruption of the Sgr DM halo. The markedly extended nature of the J-factor distributions imply the extended nature of any ray source associated with the Sgr DM halo. [ ] The J-factor value for Sgr, J_ Sgr =1.48 10^ 10 M _ ^2 kpc ^ 5 ( 6.46 10^ 16 \ GeV \ cm ^ 5 ), was calculated by summing the pixel J-factor values within the core radius of Sgr reported in Majewski_et_al_2003 . To explain the recently observed ray emission from Sgr documented in Crocker_and_Macias_et_al_2022 with the derived J-factor value for Sgr would require a DM annihilation cross section incompatible with existing constraints. In conjunction with the recently observed ray signal from MSP sources in Crocker_and_Macias_et_al_2022 , this J-factor value militates against the use of Sgr for indirect DM detection experiments.

gmw_00003264.name: NGC 4194
gmw_00003264.overview: NGC 4194, also known as the Medusa, is a post-merger starburst and luminous infrared galaxy characterized by striking tidal features [arXiv:1404.1562, arXiv:2012.10054]. The system features a crossing dust lane [arXiv:1008.2095], stellar shells [arXiv:2008.12794], and a long southern atomic-hydrogen tail [arXiv:0808.3883].

sources.summary: The paper presents CO imaging of the Medusa merger, finding high central line ratios, an extended starburst, a crossing dust lane, and diffuse molecular gas.

quotes.quote: The Medusa (NGC 4194) is particularly interesting to study since its L_ FIR L_ CO ratio rivals that of ultraluminous galaxies (ULIRGs), despite the comparatively modest luminosity, indicating an exceptionally high star formation efficiency (SFE) in the Medusa merger. High resolution OVRO (Owens Valley Radio Observatory) observations of the ^ 13 CO 1--0 have been obtained and compared with matched resolution OVRO ^ 12 CO 1--0 data to investigate the molecular gas cloud properties in the Medusa merger. Interferometric observations of \ and \ 1--0 in the Medusa (NGC 4194) merger show the ^ 12 CO ^ 13 CO 1--0 intensity ratio ( ) increases from normal, quiescent values (7-10) in the outer parts ( > 2 kpc) of the galaxy to high (16 to > 40) values in the central ( < 1 kpc) starburst region. In the central two kpc there is an east-west gradient in \ where the line ratio changes by more than a factor of three over 5 \ (945 pc).

We have obtained a high resolution map of the Medusa merger, NGC 4194, in the \ 1--0 line with the OVRO array. The main conclusions we draw from this map are: The \ 1--0 emission is mainly distributed in the north-western part of the inner 8 \ region. There is also an off-centre \ peak in the tail-like distribution to the north, where the CO/ \ line ratios are similar to those of galactic type GMCs The / \ 1--0 line ratio, , varies on a scale of 950 pc from 16 (typical values for starburst galaxies) in the north-west to > 40 in the eastern part of the central region. We suggest that this shift between \ and \ emission is caused by the presence of diffuse gas in a prominent dust lane crossing the eastern part of the centre.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 4194 as The Medusa, a post-merger starburst with tidal features, central infrared emission, compact star-forming regions, and ionized gas showing solid-body rotation.

quotes.quote: NGC 4194 is a post-merger starburst known as The Medusa for its striking tidal features. We present here a detailed study of the structure and kinematics of ionized gas in the central 0.65 kpc of the Medusa. The data include radio continuum maps with resolution up to 0.18 (35 pc) and a 12.8 m [ NeII ] data cube with spectral resolution 4 : the first high resolution, extinction-free observations of this remarkable object. The ionized gas has the kinematic signature of a core in solid-body rotation.

The 'Medusa galaxy', NGC 4194 (a.k.a. Arp 160 and Mkn 201) is a starburst galaxy at 39 Mpc distance (1 = 190 pc) with striking tidal features; it has been variously classed as Magellenic, Sm(pec) and BCG. The central kpc of NGC 4194 is a powerful infrared source with 60 /100 1 , typical of starburst heating. Spitzer IRS spectra (from the Heritage Archive) show a spectrum typical of a starburst-dominated galaxy, with little if any contribution from an AGN. NGC 4194 is believed to be a merger remnant, with the favoured history being a small gas-rich spiral falling into an elliptical four times it mass MA08 .

sources.summary: NGC 4194 is treated as a minor-merger LIRG whose star formation occurs mostly in young super star clusters; aperture effects do not explain its optical/far-infrared N/O discrepancy.

quotes.quote: NGC 4194 is a LIRG and a minor merger. Estimates for its SFR are not in agreement, with SFR ranging from 46 \ M_ /yr by H_ observation 2006AJ....131..282H to 13 \ M_ /yr in far-IR 2014A&A...568A..62D . Most of the star formation happens in the super star clusters thought to be 5 to 15 Myr old 2014A&A...569A...6K,2007MNRAS.381..228P . It is in the GOALS samples.

In the case of NGC 4194, ISO [ N iii ] data is used which has a 75 arcsec aperture and could contain more extended flux than in the SOFIA field. But that would only contribute to < 30 [ N iii ] flux in ISO data given the small size of NGC 4194, and would lead to overestimation of N/O instead of underestimation.
However, for Haro 3, NGC 4194 and both components of Arp 299, the two methods arrive at values that differ by a factor of 1.5 to 2.5, at about 2 significance.

sources.summary: The paper presents Chandra observations of NGC 4194, a nearby high-SFR star-forming galaxy, and analyzes its X-ray point-source population plus extended nuclear X-ray emission.

quotes.quote: We examine the X-ray point source population and 2-10 keV luminosity for two galaxies with high star formation rates (SFRs), NGC 4194 and NGC 7541. The X-ray point source luminosity function (XLF) for these two galaxies is consistent with the XLF found by Grimm et al. \ (2003) for a sample of star-forming galaxies.

The Chandra observation of NGC 4194 (ObsID 7071) began on 9 September 2006 at 23:30:18 UT and had a useful exposure of 35.5 ks. The observation of NGC 7541 (ObsID 7070) began on 31 October 2006 at 21:35:08 UT and had a useful exposure of 39.0 ks. The Chandra data were subjected to standard data processing and event screening. The total rate on the S3 chip was below 1.5 /s for NGC 4194 and below 1.6 /s for NGC 7541 indicating that there were no strong background flares. For each observation, we constructed an image using all valid events in the 0.3--8 keV band on the S3 chip and used the wavdetect tool which is part of the CIAO version 3.4 data analysis package to search for X-ray sources. For NGC 4194, which has extended diffuse X-ray emission near its core, we also searched for sources in the 1.5--8 keV band.

sources.summary: The paper treats the Medusa as a nearby star-forming merger, mapping its long southern atomic-hydrogen tail and arguing that a minor disk-elliptical encounter reproduces its features.

quotes.quote: The Medusa (NGC 4194) is a well-studied nearby galaxy with the disturbed appearance of a merger and evidence for ongoing star formation. In order to test whether it could be the result of an interaction between a gas-rich disk-like galaxy and a larger elliptical, we have carried out optical and radio observations of the stars and the gas in the Medusa, and performed N body numerical simulations of the evolution of such a system. We used the Nordic Optical Telescope to obtain a deep V-band image and the Westerbork Radio Synthesis Telescope to map the large-scale distribution and kinematics of atomic hydrogen. A single tail was found to the South of the Medusa with a projected length of 56 kpc ( 5' ) and a gas mass of 7 10^8 M_ , thus harbouring about one third of the total mass of the system.

The simulations of the slightly prograde infall of a gas-rich disk galaxy on an larger, four time more massive elliptical (spherical) galaxy reproduce most of the observed features of the Medusa.Thus, the Medusa is an ideal object to study the merger-induced star formation contribution from the small galaxy of a minor merger.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC4194 as an advanced starbursting merger with filamentary ionized-gas outflows, central massive young clusters, stellar shells, and a confined central starburst.

quotes.quote: Noteworthy is the filamentary H emission in the south-west of NGC4194, the only galaxy where we observed filamentary H emission consistent with galactic scale winds and outflows of ionised gas. Indeed, these features are well linked with the extreme nature of the starburst occurring in NGC4194, as it will be discussed below, using the cluster properties as a tracer.

NGC1614 and NGC4194 show a significant enhancement in cluster formation between ages of 100 and 600 Myr and are still forming clusters at elevated rates. On the other hand, the massive clusters, within 1 kpc of the centre of NGC4194, are all very massive and with young ages ( <10 Myr), as already reported by previous studies w04, h06 . Morphological signatures, especially stellar shells in the southern part of the galaxy suggest that the massive progenitor of NGC4194 was an early type galaxy that has merged with a lower mass, gas rich component. Because of the confinement of the starburst to the centre of the galaxy, accompanied by a very young massive cluster population which shows no age gradient, the current starburst is very likely responsible for driving ionised gas winds observed in the H maps.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 4194 as the Medusa minor merger, detecting CO in its optical tidal tail and arguing the molecular gas was expelled during an elliptical-spiral collision.

quotes.quote: We have detected CO 1-0 emission along the tidal tail of the NGC 4194 (the Medusa) merger. It is the first CO detection in the optical tail of a minor merger. Emission is detected both in the centre of the tail and at its tip. The molecular mass in the 33 '' Onsala 20m beam is estimated to be 8.5 10^7 M _ which is at least 4 of the total molecular mass measured so far in this system.

We suggest that the emission is a molecular tidal tail which is part of the extended structure of the main body, and that the molecular gas was thrown out by the collision instead of having formed in situ from condensing atomic material.
The Medusa is very likely the result of an elliptical+spiral collison and our detection supports the notion that molecular gas in minor mergers can be found at great distances from the merger centre.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC4194 has the strongest line-region reddening, while sharing bright-BCG stellar populations dominated by young and old components and high recent star formation.

quotes.quote: We can see that it, even in the case of NGC4194, with the strongest reddening derived from its line-emitting regions, is not large.

For IC1586, NGC4194 and MRK499, the contributions coming from the young and the old stellar components are large, but that from the intermediate age component is small. It suggests that the rate of star formation during the intermediate age period is smaller than in the other periods, and the star formation process is not continuous in these galaxies.

The other result of our population synthesis is that while the observed properties of the bright BCGs (IC1586, NGC4194, MRK499) and the BCDGs are very similar, their stellar components and star formation regimes are generally different. There are many old and young stellar components in bright BCGs, and its recent star formation rate is very high.

sources.summary: The paper says NGC 4194 had few detected point sources, was analyzed using its D25 ellipse, lacked UV data, and had a high-SFR X-ray point-source ratio consistent with the authors' relation.

quotes.quote: In four galaxies (NGC 1427A, NGC 4194, NGC 4625 and UGC 05720) we detected a rather small number ( 4-6 ) of point sources that did not allow us to perform spatial analysis. For these galaxies we analyzed the D25 ellipse regions, as their CXB contamination was found to be in the 5 16 range.
For three galaxies of our sample (NGC 0278, NGC 4194 and NGC 7541) neither NUV nor FUV data were available.

2008ApJ...682.1020K examined the X-ray point-source population of two galaxies with high SFRs (NGC 4194 and NGC 7541) for which they found L_ X /L_ IR ratio consistent with our average relation, eq. ( eq:lx_lir_linear ).

sources.summary: The paper identifies Arp 160 as one of three barred merger remnants and reports a continually changing center with high A2 peaking at 10 arcseconds.

quotes.quote: Wozniak et al. 1995) is seen in only three cases, which we conclude have well-defined bars. These are: Arp 160, Arp 162, and Arp 163.
Arp 160: This shows a constantly changing centre (x0, y0) for the subsequent isophotes. The values of A _2 are also high with a peak of 0.45 at 10".

gmw_00003265.name: NGC 7541
gmw_00003265.overview: NGC 7541 is a nearly edge-on barred spiral galaxy of type SBc located approximately 37.1 Mpc away [arXiv:0805.0683, arXiv:2008.06039]. It has a companion galaxy, NGC 7537 [arXiv:0805.0683], and exhibits a high star formation rate with diffuse radio emission overlapping its disk [arXiv:0805.0683, arXiv:2008.06039].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 7541 as a nearby high-SFR SBc galaxy with a companion and analyzes its Chandra X-ray point-source observations.

quotes.quote: We examine the X-ray point source population and 2-10 keV luminosity for two galaxies with high star formation rates (SFRs), NGC 4194 and NGC 7541. The X-ray point source luminosity function (XLF) for these two galaxies is consistent with the XLF found by Grimm et al. \ (2003) for a sample of star-forming galaxies.
NGC 7541 is an SBc galaxy and has a companion galaxy NGC 7537. It was the host of SN1998dh which allows an accurate distance estimate of 37.1 1.5 Mpc Jha07 .

The observation of NGC 7541 (ObsID 7070) began on 31 October 2006 at 21:35:08 UT and had a useful exposure of 39.0 ks. The Chandra data were subjected to standard data processing and event screening. The total rate on the S3 chip was below 1.5 /s for NGC 4194 and below 1.6 /s for NGC 7541 indicating that there were no strong background flares. For each observation, we constructed an image using all valid events in the 0.3--8 keV band on the S3 chip and used the wavdetect tool which is part of the CIAO version 3.4 data analysis package to search for X-ray sources.

sources.summary: The paper notes missing Spitzer and UV data for NGC 7541, use of published IRAS data for IR luminosity, and a prior high-SFR X-ray point-source result consistent with its average relation.

quotes.quote: For three galaxies (NGC 7541, NGC 2139 and NGC 3631) neither MIPS 70 m nor 24 m data were available. For NGC 3034 (M82) Spitzer data are affected by saturation effects that limit our ability to extract reliable global flux densities. In these cases we used published IRAS data 2003AJ....126.1607S to compute the IR luminosity.
For three galaxies of our sample (NGC 0278, NGC 4194 and NGC 7541) neither NUV nor FUV data were available.

2008ApJ...682.1020K examined the X-ray point-source population of two galaxies with high SFRs (NGC 4194 and NGC 7541) for which they found L_ X /L_ IR ratio consistent with our average relation, eq. ( eq:lx_lir_linear ).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7541 as a nearly edge-on SB spiral whose 1.5 GHz image shows eastern spiral arms and diffuse disk radio emission, but no continuum halo.

quotes.quote: NGC 7541 NGC 7541 is an SB type nearly edge-on spiral galaxy. Our radio image at 1.5 GHz shows the spiral arms to the east very clearly. While we do not detect any continuum halo-like emission, we detect diffuse radio emission overlapping with the star-forming disk. detect finger-like structures protruding from the disk, which is seen in our images as well.

gmw_00003266.name: UGC 5979
gmw_00003266.overview: UGC 5979 is a faint, diffuse dwarf irregular galaxy that lacks a visible nucleus [arXiv:1406.2120]. Located at a distance of approximately 18.85 Mpc, the galaxy has a significantly sub-solar metallicity [arXiv:1406.2120].

sources.summary: The paper identifies UGC 5979 as a faint diffuse dwarf irregular host, gives its coordinates, velocity, distance, luminosity, and argues its oxygen abundance is significantly subsolar.

quotes.quote: The host galaxy, UGC 5979, is a low-contrast faint (with apparent B magnitude 15.93) dwarf galaxy without a visible nucleus.

UGC 5979 is a diffuse (dI) galaxy http://leda.univ-lyon1.fr/ , located at RA = 10:52:41.16 and Dec = +67:59:18.8 [ J2000 ] , with a radial velocity corrected for the Local Group infall onto the Virgo cluster of about 1376 km s ^ 1 (z = 0.0045). From the above value of the recessional velocity, we infer a distance for UGC 5979 of about 18.85 1.03 Mpc, resulting in an absolute magnitude of M _B = - 15.5 (distance modulus 31.38 0.27 mag, adopting H _0 = 73 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 ).

A rough estimate of the metallicity of the host galaxy can be obtained from the relation of : 12 + (O/H) = 5.80( 0.017) - 0.139( 0.011) M _B that links the integrated absolute B band magnitude with the average oxygen abundance of the galaxy, providing a value of 8, which suggests that the environment may have a significantly sub-solar metallicity. A direct measurement of the host galaxy metallicity confirms this result. We spectroscopically observed UGC 5979 with the Nordical Optical Telescope (NOT) equipped with ALFOSC + grism 4.

gmw_00003267.name: AGN J124942.3+344929
gmw_00003267.overview: AGN J124942.3+344929 is an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2006.14122, arXiv:2009.14247] at a redshift of 0.438 [arXiv:2006.14122, arXiv:2010.06056] that hosts a central supermassive black hole [arXiv:2006.14122, arXiv:2301.07111]. The galaxy hosted an optical flare, ZTF19abanrhr [arXiv:2010.06056, arXiv:2310.16025], which has been proposed as the electromagnetic counterpart to the gravitational-wave event GW190521 [arXiv:2009.14247, arXiv:2106.15075].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J1249+3449 as the AGN associated with flare ZTF19abanrhr, finds its light curve stable before a 0.3-mag flare, and argues similar AGN variability is rare.

quotes.quote: Alert ZTF19abanrhr (see Fig. 1), first announced 34 days after the GW event and associated with AGN J124942.3+344929 at z=0.438 (hereafter J1249+3449), was identified as potentially interesting. The AGN is located at the 78 spatial contour and 1.6 (0.7) from the peak marginal (conditional) luminosity distance.

From a fit to the H line profile of the AGN, using the QSFit routine , we find the mass of the central supermassive black hole (SMBH) spans M_ SMBH = [ 1,10 ] 10^8 M_ and therefore the pre-flare luminosity is L_ bol /L_ Edd = [ 0.02-0.23 ] relative to the Eddington luminosity. From the ZTF lightcurve, J1249+3449 varied by only a few percent of its mean flux level ( 19.1 mag in g band) over the 15 months prior to S190521g. A flare peaking 50 days after the GW trigger elevated the flux by 0.3 mag (equivalent to 10^ 45 erg s ^ 1 ) for 50 days, assuming a typical quasar bolometric correction factor Runnoe12 . The total energy released by the flare is therefore O(10^ 51 erg ) .

Using the DRW model parameters from the CRTS data, which characterize the overall variability of the source, we simulated the observed ZTF light curve 250,000 times and find an equivalent flare (i.e., matching the selection criteria described above) in four instances. The event is thus very unlikely to arise from AGN activity in this particular source (i.e., O(0.002 ) .

sources.summary: The paper treats AGN J124942.3+344929 as the candidate electromagnetic counterpart and host environment for GW190521, using its redshift and distance consistency to estimate H_0.

quotes.quote: The ZTF candidate counterpart was associated with AGN J124942.3+344929 with measured redshift z_ ZTF = 0.438 2020PhRvL.124y1102G . In Fig. fig:location we also show the distance of the ZTF candidate assuming Planck 2018 cosmology collaboration2018planck . We see that both e 0.7 and e=0 models are consistent with this distance, with somewhat higher probability density for the eccentric case.

Given the Gpc distance scale of the event, we neglected the peculiar velocity of the host galaxy. We also neglected any motion of the binary within the galaxy. Considering the mass of the supermassive black hole in the candidate AGN, M_ SMBH =10^8-10^9 M _ 2020PhRvL.124y1102G , and the characteristic distance 10^ 2 pc of the merger from the supermassive black hole, the rotational velocity of the binary is 10^4 km s ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper describes AGN J124942.3+344929 as a proposed GW190521 electromagnetic counterpart; DDOTI detected it at catalog brightness and treated prompt-flare recovery and chance alignment as limitations.

quotes.quote: They reported a rapid rise in the light curve of the AGN J124942.3+344929 see Figure 2 of Graham2020 detected by the Zwicky Transient Facility (ZTF) 34 days after the BBH merger. The photometry shows a rise from 18.9 to 18.6 followed by a fall over more than 100 days. DDOTI observed GW190521 and covered the LIGO probability peak in the northern hemisphere, including the position of J124942.3+344929. The source is detected in our image at a magnitude of w=18.9 , which is consistent with the catalogued value and therefore the source was not flagged as a potential counterpart by our transients pipeline. However, our search was limited to one night and could only recover prompt flaring events, rather than the month-long variability discussed in Graham2020 .
The probability of a chance alignment between AGN J124942.3+344929 and GW190425 is also not negligible Estelle2021 .

sources.summary: The paper analyzes AGN J124942.3+344929 as the site of flare ZTF19abanrhr, arguing its steady prior luminosity and distance/sky overlap support association with GW190521.

quotes.quote: The signal was followed approximately 34 days later by flare ZTF19abanrhr, detected in AGN J124942.3+344929 by the Zwicky Transient Facility at the 78 spatial contour for GW190521's sky localization. Using the GWTC-2.1 data release, we find that the association between GW190521 and flare ZTF19abanrhr as its electromagnetic counterpart is preferred over a random coincidence of the two transients with a log Bayes' factor of 8.6, corresponding to an odds ratio of 5400:1 for equal prior odds and 400:1 assuming an astrophysical prior odds of 1/13.

Approximately 34 days after GW190521, the Zwicky Transient Facility (ZTF) observed a flare, ZTF19abanrhr, in an active galactic nucleus (AGN) located within the spatial footprint of the detected GW event graham:2020 . The AGN in question, AGN J124942.3+344929, is reported by graham:2020 to have a very consistent luminosity for the year surrounding the event, making it unlikely that flare ZTF19abanrhr is due to the intrinsic variability of the source. Moreover, the observation of the flare showed little color variation, suggesting its cause to be a constant temperature shock to the AGN gas.

When the GW luminosity distance posterior distribution is conditioned on the sky location of AGN J124942.3+3449 The conditioned posterior distribution is obtained making use of the mathematical properties of the Dirichlet process Gaussian mixture model, as discussed in Section  sec:codes . , the AGN lies at the 49 ^ th percentile of the GW luminosity distance posterior.

sources.summary: The paper identifies AGN J124942.3+344929 as the redshift-0.438 AGN associated with the candidate GW190521 optical flare and describes inferred disk, gas-density, migration-trap, and central black-hole properties for that environment.

quotes.quote: Remarkably, the Zwicky Transient Facility observed an optical flare (ZTF19abanrhr), interpreted as coming from the kicked GW190521 BH merger remnant moving in an AGN disk . If confirmed, this would be the first electromagnetic counterpart to a BH coalescence (see, however, ). The flare occurred 34 days after GW190521 (the delay being ascribed to the remnant's recoil) in AGN J124942.3+344929 at redshift z = 0.438 .

If the flare is indeed associated with GW190521 and due to the remnant's recoil in the AGN disk, finds a total binary mass 150 M_ , kick velocity 200 km/s at 60 deg from the disk's midplane, a disk aspect ratio (height to galactocentric radius) of H/a 0.01 , and a gas density of 10^ 10 g/cm ^3 . The authors also argue that the binary is most likely located in a disk migration trap (galactocentric distance a 700 GM/^2 , with M 10^8 -10^9 \ M_ the mass of the AGN's BH), where gas torques vanish and binaries accumulate as they migrate inward .

sources.summary: The paper links SDSS J1249+3449 to GW190521 as a possible host quasar whose observed flare and 20-day delay fit the authors' BZ-powered ejected-blob EMC model.

quotes.quote: The GW190521 event has garnered special attention, not only because of the large masses of the two constituent BHs (85 and 66 ; Abbott2020 ), but also because the event was potentially hosted by the quasar SDSS J1249+3449 Graham2020,Palmese2021 .

With m_ 150 , the characteristic of an EMC driven by the BZ-powered blob is generally consistent with the flare of the quasar SDSS J1249+3449 monitored by the Zwicky Transient Facility Graham2020 . Moreover, there is a delay of 20 days (in the quasar's frame) of the EMC candidate (J1249+3449) with respect to the GW190521. This can be conveniently explained by t_ rise in the case of an ejected blob. We thus prefer case A of the EMC of GW 190521 associated with BZ-powered ejected blob, as shown in Fig. 2.

sources.summary: The paper argues that the optical flare toward AGN J1249+3449 matches quasar microlensing, with ZTF light curves, achromatic behavior, typical microlensing parameters, and a non-negligible event probability.

quotes.quote: We have found that the optical flare event detected about 47 days after the GW event S190521g towards the AGN J1249+3449 appears to be fully consistent with a quasar microlensing event, while the authors of discovery of the event attributed it to the more challenging possibility that it is the first electromagnetic counterpart to the binary black hole merger, immersed in the supermassive black hole accretion disk. Indeed, we find that the available ZTF data in the g and bands are perfectly in agreement with a microlensing Paczyński light curve, and show also a remarkable achromatic behavior, as expected in the case of a microlensing event. Also the other obtained microlensing parameters, t_E=17.6 1.2 days, u_0=0.92 0.03 and the peak magnification A_ max 1.41 , appear to be typical parameters for a microlensing event. Moreover, the microlensing rate has been estimated to be in the range 6 10^ 5 1 10^ 3 events quasar ^ 1 yr ^ 1 , implying that the probability of occurrence The rate gives directly the probability of occurrence of a microlensing event per year towards the considered quasar. of such events towards AGN J1249+3449 is absolutely not negligible, also taking into account that the quasar in question has been monitored for more than 15 years.

sources.summary: The paper evaluates AGN J124942.3+344929 as the proposed GW190521 optical counterpart, noting sky-map limitations but line-of-sight support and consistency with a background flare.

quotes.quote: The recent report of an association of the gravitational-wave (GW) binary black hole (BBH) merger GW190521 with a flare in the Active Galactic Nuclei (AGN) J124942.3+344929 has generated tremendous excitement.
The AGN J124942.3+344929 is not particularly well placed in the LIGO-Virgo GW190521 sky localization map. Nonetheless, the position on the sky has support in line of sight probability.

Clearly how one does the f_f calculation matters, but we argue that probabilities of 4 to 70 of a chance occurrence suggests that the flare in AGN J124942.3+344929 is consistent with being a background flare.

sources.summary: The paper uses AGN J124942.3+344929 as the proposed host environment of ZTF19abanrhr/GW190521, noting its redshift, persistent AGN flux, SMBH mass range, and Eddington-scaled luminosity.

quotes.quote: Graham20 reported that the optical transient, ZTF19abanrhr, from the AGN J124942.3+344929 at z=0.438 is possibly associated with GW190521. The optical flare in ZTF19abanrhr began to exceed the persistent flux from the AGN activity after 18 days (in the rest frame) from the occurrence of the merger event, GW190521. The peak luminosity of ZTF19abanrhr is 8 10^ 44 erg/s in the g and bands with the observed duration of 28 days (in the rest frame).
The mass of the supermassive black hole hosting the AGN J124942.3+344929 is M 10^8 - 10^9 , and the luminosity of the AGN in units of the Eddington luminosity is 0.02 - 0.2 Graham20 .

gmw_00003268.name: 3C 284
gmw_00003268.overview: The large Fanaroff-Riley II radio galaxy 3C 284 is hosted by a disturbed elliptical galaxy with a double nucleus, dust lanes, and a southeast tidal tail [arXiv:2101.07021, arXiv:0712.0595]. It features symmetric-length radio jets aligned with its central emission-line region [arXiv:2101.07021, arXiv:0710.3105], though its eastern radio lobe is significantly brighter than the western lobe [arXiv:2101.07021].

sources.summary: The paper presents GMRT and VLA observations of 3C 284, describing it as a large FR II radio galaxy with east-west jets, asymmetric lobe brightness, spectral ageing analysis, and no episodic activity.

quotes.quote: The galaxy 3C 284 has a redshift of z=0.2397 Ad09 . 3C 284 is an FR II type large radio galaxy with a projected linear size of 674.4 kpc. The radio luminosity of 3C 284 at 178 MHz is 167 10^ 24 W Hz^ 1 sr^ 1 https://3crr.extragalacticfo/cgi/sourcepage?103 .

For the galaxy 3C 284, the jet is elongated through the east-west direction. The length of the eastern and western lobe of the galaxy is nearly similar, making both of the jets symmetric. From Figure 3C284 , it is observed that the eastern lobe is much brighter than the western lobe.

sources.summary: The paper finds 3C 284's lobe X-ray flux matches IC/CMB predictions with near-equipartition fields, a 1 keV group-scale environment, and radio lobes near pressure balance.

quotes.quote: For the lobes of both sources (each modelled separately), the measured X-ray fluxes are in good agreement with the predictions for IC scattering of the CMB, with magnetic field strengths within a factor of 2 of equipartition. Both sources have group-scale environments (L _ X 10^ 43 ergs s ^ 1 ) with temperatures of 1.5 keV (3C 223) and 1 keV (3C 284). These results are in agreement with the predictions of L _ X /T _ X relations.

From our model for the lobe-related X-ray emission of 3C 223 and 3C 284 we find the internal energy of the radio lobes to be close to the minimum energy values. We can compare these values with the external pressure from the X-ray-emitting gas to study the lobe dynamics. We find that the lobes of both sources appear to be in pressure balance with their surroundings.

sources.summary: The paper reports asymmetric lobes for 3C 284, derives separate jet powers and ages for each side, and estimates its black-hole mass from bulge luminosity.

quotes.quote: 3C 284 shows asymmetry both in r_ h and A_ h . While r_ h and A_ h in the western lobe are estimated as 380 kpc and 6200 kpc ^2 , respectively, the corresponding values for the eastern lobe are 260 kpc and 4600 kpc ^2 . The obtained power and age are L_ j = (0.1 - 3.6) 10^ 46 ergs s ^ 1 and t_ age = 100 - 630 Myr for the western jet and L_ j = (0.03 - 1.8) 10^ 47 ergs s ^ 1 and t_ age = 32 - 260 Myr for the eastern jet. Since there is no estimate of the SMBH mass of 3C 284 in the literature, we derive the mass from the B-band magnitude of the buldge estimated in SRH05 . By using the equation in MCF04 which gives the correlation of the B-band magnitude with the BH mass, we obtain M_ BH = 8.2 10^ 8 M_ .

sources.summary: The paper models 3C 284 as one of four FR II radio sources with powerful jets, large cocoon energies, and substantial invisible energy components beyond minimum-energy estimates.

quotes.quote: We investigate the total kinetic powers ( L_ j ) and ages ( t_ age ) of powerful jets of four FR II radio sources (Cygnus A, 3C 223, 3C 284, and 3C 219) by the detail comparison of the dynamical model of expanding cocoons with observed ones. It is found that these sources have quite large kinetic powers with the ratio of L_ j to the Eddington luminosity ( L_ Edd ) resides in 0.02 <L_ j /L_ Edd <10 . Reflecting the large kinetic powers, we also find that the total energy stored in the cocoon ( E_ ) exceed the energy derived from the minimum energy condition ( E_ min ): 2< E_ /E_ min <160 . This implies that a large amount of kinetic power is carried by invisible components such as thermal leptons (electron and positron) and/or protons.

sources.summary: The paper says J131104.66+272807.2 remains above the Compton-thick column-density threshold under Brightman-Nandra modeling, even though its FeK line is small and detector-dependent.

quotes.quote: Source 22 - J131104.66+272807.2 Because of the high N_H , but the small EW of the FeK line, we fit the spectrum with the model of Brightman Nandra (2011). The result of the fit is a column density value of N_H=114^ +87 _ 29 10^ 22 , which is relatively lower than what is reported in the current study, but again above the limit that characterize CT sources. A strong FeK line is only present in the pn detector. Other useful values: p1/p2=0.003, cstat/dof=300/366, _ soft =2.6^ +0.2 _ 0.2 , _ hard =1.8 (fixed).

sources.summary: The paper applies a total-cocoon-pressure method to 3C 284 as an FRII radio galaxy, finding positron-free proton-electron plasma implausible and requiring electron-positron mixtures independent of jet power and cocoon size.

quotes.quote: We apply this method to four FRII radio galaxies (Cygnus A, 3C 219, 3C 223 and 3C 284), for which the total cocoon pressures have been already evaluated. We find that the positron-free plasma comprising of protons and electrons is ruled out, when we consider plausible particle distribution functions. In other words, the mixture of positrons is required for all four FRII radio galaxies; the number density ratio of electrons/positrons to protons is larger than two. Thus, we find that the plasma composition is independent of the jet power and the size of cocoons.

sources.summary: The paper reports that 3C284 has an emission-line region split into three components; its overall position angle is misaligned, but the central inner PA makes it count as aligned.

quotes.quote: 3C284 (Figs. 3C284-montage 3C284-overlay ) has and ELR which is composed of three separate components in our images. The PA along the overall structure is not aligned with the source. However, taking an ``inner PA'' of the central structure gives the alignment with the radio source, so we consider this ELR aligned with the radio source.

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C 284 as a disturbed elliptical FR II HEG radio source with a southeast tidal tail, several companions, a disky optical appearance, and dust lanes.

quotes.quote: 3C 284; z=0.239 An FR II HEG radio source, hosted by a disturbed elliptical with a SE tidal tail toward its most prominent companion 4 SE. Several fainter companions are also visible on the NICMOS chip. In the optical the source has a disky appearance and double nucleus, owing to this same tidal tail and associated dust lanes.

sources.summary: The paper compares its 3C 284 XMM-Newton fit with Croston et al., finding broad agreement and explaining apparent discrepancies through photon-index and calibration differences.

quotes.quote: 3C 284 The XMM-Newton data we use here were analysed by Croston et al. (2004). Their best-fitting model parameters are in reasonable agreement with ours, although they fixed the photon index at = 1.5 , rather than 1.7 as used in this work. Apparent discrepancies in the absorption column affecting the second power-law component and in the flux densities result from changes in the XMM calibration files, as for 3C 223.

gmw_00003269.name: SDSS J000441.24+000711.3
gmw_00003269.overview: SDSS J000441.24+000711.3 is classified as a Seyfert 1.9 active galaxy [arXiv:1412.6511]. It is X-ray unobscured, and its host galaxy follows the standard black hole mass to stellar velocity dispersion relation [arXiv:1412.6511].

sources.summary: The paper describes SDSS J000441.24+000711.3 as an X-ray-unobscured Seyfert 1.9 whose broad-line emission is partly hidden by host dilution while its galaxy follows the M-sigma relation.

quotes.quote: SDSS J000441.24+000711.3: it is X-ray unobscured. The SDSS public spectrum show a broad component in H _ but not in H _ , so it is a Seyfert 1.9.

After correcting for both host galaxy contamination and extinction, both objects show a broad lines in H _ , and also J000441.24+000711.3 show a broad line in H _ . We have analyzed the zone of H _ to determine the mass of the SMBHs.

J000441.24+000711.3 on one hand follows the M- relation. The dynamical mass of the galaxy is the expected in comparison with the M _ SMBH as well. Using the AGN emission after subtracting the stellar emission but without correcting for extinction we can calculate the Balmer decrement of the broad emission lines, and then we can calculate the N _H column derived from this Balmer decrement as in .

gmw_00003270.name: SDSS J025218.60-011746.3
gmw_00003270.overview: SDSS J025218.60-011746.3 is an edge-on Sb spiral galaxy classified as a Seyfert 2 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1412.6511, arXiv:2106.13255]. Its nuclear emission is outshone by host starlight [arXiv:1412.6511], and it is located near the galaxy NGC 1132 [arXiv:2106.13255].

sources.summary: The review discusses NGC 1126 as a spiral near NGC 1132 whose projected distance, velocity offset, and brightness may undermine NGC 1132 fossil-group classification.

quotes.quote: However, Dong-woo2018 , claimed that the second brightest galaxy is NGC 1126, a spiral galaxy located at 8.4 arcminutes or 230 kpc in projection. The virial radius of this group is estimated to be r_ 200 800 kpc, thus this galaxy should be inside 0.5 r_ 200 . The difference in the velocity space is v = 438 km s ^ 1 , as obtained using the Nasa Extragalactic Database (NED), so the two galaxies can be part of the same group, although a precise dynamical study should be done to confirm the membership.

In Dong-woo2018 NGC 1126 is described as ``seven times faniter in B''. We check in the SDSS DR16 the magnitudes of both NGC 1132 and NGC 1126: the former has m_r = 12.20 , the latter m_r = 14.01 , leading to a m_ 12 = 1.81 , rejecting it as a genuine FG. However, as we already mentioned, an accurate study of the membership of NGC 1126 to the group of NGC 1132 should be done and errors in SDSS magnitudes can not be excluded (NGC 1126 is flagged with ``unreliable photometry'', as many other bright galaxies, mainly due to an over estimation of the sky around bright objects).

sources.summary: The paper describes SDSS J025218.60-011746.3 as an edge-on Sb Seyfert 2 whose AGN signatures are hidden because host starlight outshines its nuclear emission.

quotes.quote: SDSS J025218.60-011746.3: is a Sb edge-on galaxy with low X-ray obscuration. 6dF public spectrum indicates a Seyfert 2 classification.

J025218.60-011746.3 falls below the M- relation (falls in the 2 3 range), the mass of the bulge is higher than expected compared to the SMBH mass [ 9 ] , meaning that the star light contribution outshines the broad components of the nuclear emission. We do not found a broad component of H _ . In conclusion, the AGN signatures are hidden because the galaxy contribution outshines them for two different reasons.

gmw_00003271.name: ESO 146-5
gmw_00003271.overview: ESO 146-5 is a giant interacting elliptical galaxy located at the center of the Abell 3827 cluster [arXiv:2208.08843]. It is a merger system with a total mass estimated from strong gravitational lensing, making it perhaps the most massive galaxy in the nearby universe [arXiv:2208.08843].

sources.summary: The paper uses ESO 146-5 as a giant interacting elliptical merger example in Abell 3827, citing strong-lensing mass evidence and a larger halo mass.

quotes.quote: An example of such a merger is the giant interacting elliptical galaxy ESO 146-5 (ESO 146-IG 005) in the center of the cluster Abell 3827. Its total mass is (2.7 0.4) 10^ 13 M_ within 37 h^ 1 kpc according to . This estimate was obtained from strong gravitational lensing. The total halo mass of ESO 146-5 is larger. It is perhaps the most massive galaxy in the nearby universe.

gmw_00003272.name: SDSS J115154.83 005904.6
gmw_00003272.overview: SDSS J115154.83 005904.6 is a quasar located at a redshift of z = 1.93 [arXiv:astro-ph/0512313]. Its carbon IV emission line has shown a bifurcated profile during a low signal-to-noise epoch, which was not observed in a spectrum taken 281 days later [arXiv:astro-ph/0512313].

sources.summary: The paper flags SDSS J115154.83 005904.6 as a z=1.93 quasar with an unusual C iv profile: a bifurcated low-S/N epoch, no matching sky contamination, and possible follow-up interest.

quotes.quote: SDSS J115154.83 005904.6 Fig. SDSSJ115154.83-005904.6fig shows the C iv line of SDSS J115154.83 005904.6, a quasar at redshift z = 1.93 . This C iv line was noticed as a part of the manual inspection for night sky and absorption lines done before line fitting. The low-S/N epoch line (light curve) appears bifurcated, while the high-S/N epoch line (dark curve) does not; it is thus unlikely to be the result of an intervening absorption system. These spectra were taken 281 days apart, corresponding to roughly 96 days' separation in the quasar rest-frame. The depression near the peak of the line at the low-S/N epoch lies at 4500 \ in the observed frame, where there is not expected to be a large contamination from night sky lines.

This dip in flux near 4500 \ is not seen in other objects observed simultaneously with the same plate. If this bifurcation is a real effect, this could potentially be an intriguing object for follow-up study.

gmw_00003273.name: II Zwicky 23
gmw_00003273.overview: II Zwicky 23 is a luminous blue compact galaxy currently undergoing a global starburst [arXiv:astro-ph/0603292]. The system exhibits a highly disturbed outer structure with long debris trails and interacting companions [arXiv:astro-ph/0603292].

sources.summary: The paper presents II Zwicky 23 as a blue compact starburst galaxy with disturbed debris trails, possible tidal dwarfs, a disky rotating center, and evidence for group interactions.

quotes.quote: II Zwicky 23 (UGC 3179) is a luminous ( M_B 21 ) nearby compact narrow emission line st arburst galaxy with blue optical colors and strong emission lines. We present a photometric and morphological study of II Zw 23 and its interacting companions using data obtained with the WIYN 3.5-m telescope in Kitt Peak, Arizona. II Zwicky 23 has a highly disturbed outer structure with long trails of debris that may be feeding tidal dwarfs. Its central regions appear disky, a structure that is consistent with the overall rotation pattern observed in the H velocity field measured from Densepak observations obtained with WIYN. We discuss the structure of II Zwicky 23 and its set of companions and possible scenarios of debris formation in this system.

II Zwicky 23 (Mkn 1087, UGC 03179) is a blue compact galaxy currently undergoing a global starburst. It was until recently thought to have one companion, KPG103a. However, recent work has identified several tidal dwarfs around this system, as well as one dwarf galaxy that appears to pre-date the tidal dwarf galaxies (TDGs).

sources.summary: The paper gives image-based morphology notes for KPG103B, including classification, color information, structural features, and its role within a binary-galaxy pair.

quotes.quote: KPG103B . The galaxy is classified as Sa. At our resolution, the (B-I) , sharp/filtered and unsharp masking images show a peculiar morphology. The arms appear radially distributed from a prominent bulge. Keel ( ) has extensively studied this galaxy. The (B-V)_ T ^ 0 colour is representative of a Scd-Sd galaxy.

gmw_00003274.name: KPG103a
gmw_00003274.overview: KPG103a is an Sb-type spiral galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0109231, arXiv:astro-ph/0603292] classified as an HII galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0603292]. It features a weak, two-armed spiral structure [arXiv:astro-ph/0603292], three central knots [arXiv:astro-ph/0603292], and diffuse, low surface brightness extensions that suggest a recent tidal interaction [arXiv:astro-ph/0603292].

sources.summary: The paper describes KPG103a as II Zwicky 23's companion, an Sb/HII galaxy with spiral structure, central knots, diffuse extensions, enhanced star formation, and signs of tidal interaction.

quotes.quote: KPG103a KPG103a is the less well-studied companion of II Zwicky 23. It lies 81 kpc to the southwest and has a very similar velocity ( cz = 8313 km s ^ 1 ) . It has been classified an Sb, HII galaxy and is characterized by the presence of a weak, two-armed spiral structure.

In optical images, there appear to be three knots in the center of the galaxy, possibly the nucleus and a circumnuclear ring, or multiple nuclei left over from an unrelaxed collision. There also appear to be some diffuse, low surface brightness extensions. These properties, along with what appears to be an enhanced SFR, suggest that KPG103a has recently undergone some type of tidal interaction.

sources.summary: The paper gives image-based morphology notes for KPG103A, including classification, color information, structural features, and its role within a binary-galaxy pair.

quotes.quote: KPG103A . The galaxy is classified as Sb. Its small angular size make the visualization of features a difficult task. At our resolution, both the (B-I) and sharp/filtered images shows a peculiar morphology. Keel ( ) has extensively studied this galaxy. The (B-V)_ T ^ 0 colour is representative of a Sab-Sb galaxy.

gmw_00003275.name: FGC 1540
gmw_00003275.overview: FGC 1540 is a superthin disk galaxy located at a distance of approximately 10 Mpc [arXiv:2002.09198]. It features two optical stellar disks [arXiv:2002.09198] and is embedded within a compact dark matter halo that likely regulates its superthin vertical structure [arXiv:1807.02992].

sources.summary: The paper describes FGC 1540 as a superthin Flat Galaxy Catalog object with high gas richness and compact dark halo, then models two optical stellar discs.

quotes.quote: FGC 1540 FGC 1540 is a superthin galaxy at a distance of D=10 Mpc. It is observed at an inclination of i=87^ and has an axial ratio b/a = 7.5 . It is classified as a superthin in the Flat galaxy catalogue karachentsev1993flat . It has M_ HI /L_ B =4.1 and is characterised by an asymptotic rotational velocity of about 90 kms ^ 1 and the mass modelling indicates a central dark matter density _ 0 =0.262 M_ / pc^ 3 and a core radius 0.69 kpc kurapati2018mass .

FGC1540 Unlike our other sample galaxies, FGC1540 has two stellar discs in the optical. The central value of the vertical velocity dispersion of the thick disc is 36.9 1.1 kms ^ 1 and falls off with an exponential scale length of 3.7 0.4 R_ d1 . The central dispersion for the thin disc is 13.1 1.2 kms ^ 1 , the scale length of exponential fall-off being 3.3 0.4 R_ d2 , where R_ d2 . The density averaged dispersion is reflective of the value of the thin disc component at all R .

sources.summary: The paper focuses on FGC1540, using GMRT H I, optical spectroscopy, Spitzer, and SDSS data to derive rotation curves, surface density, mass models, and a compact halo likely shaping its superthin stellar disc.

quotes.quote: We present high resolution H i Giant Meterwave Radio Telescope (GMRT) observations of the superthin galaxy FGC1540 with a spatial resolution of 10 '' 8 '' and a spectral resolution of 1.73 kms ^ 1 and an rms noise of 0.9 mJy per beam. We obtain its rotation curve as well as deprojected radial H i surface density profile by fitting a 3-dimensional tilted ring model directly to the H i data cubes by using the publicly-available software, Fully Automated Tirrific (FAT). We also present the rotation curve of FGC1540 derived from its optical spectroscopy study using the 6-m BTA telescope of the Special Astrophysical Observatory of the Russian Academy of Sciences. We use the rotation curve, the H i surface density profile together with Spitzer 3.6 m and the SDSS i -band data to construct the mass models for FGC1540.

We find that both the Pseudo-isothermal (PIS), as well as Navarro-Frenk-White (NFW) dark matter (DM) halos, fit the observed rotation curve equally well. The PIS model indicates a compact dark matter halo ( R_ C /R_ D < 2), with the best-fitting core radius ( R_ C ) approximately half the exponential stellar disc scale length ( R_ D ), which is in agreement with the mass models of superthin galaxies studied earlier in the literature. Since the vertical thickness of the galactic stellar disc is determined by a balance between the net gravitational field and the velocity dispersion in the vertical direction, the compact dark matter halo may be primarily responsible in regulating the superthin vertical structure of the stellar disc in FGC1540 as was found in case of the superthin galaxy UGC7321.

gmw_00003276.name: IC 2233
gmw_00003276.overview: IC 2233 is an isolated, bulgeless spiral galaxy [arXiv:0807.3560] with a gas-rich, superthin disk [arXiv:2002.09198]. The disk exhibits multiwavelength vertical corrugations [arXiv:0807.3560] and contains extraplanar dust emission at high vertical distances [arXiv:2207.11589], while showing no current interaction with its companion [arXiv:2210.09383].

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 2233 as an isolated edge-on spiral whose disk shows multiwavelength vertical corrugations linked to young stars, dust, H regions, velocity undulations, and possible star-formation regulation.

quotes.quote: We recently reported the discovery of a regular corrugation pattern in the \ disk of the isolated, edge-on spiral galaxy IC 2233. Here we present measurements of the vertical structure of this galaxy at several additional wavelengths, ranging from the far ultraviolet to the far infrared. We find that undular patterns with amplitude 5'' ( 250 pc) are visible in a variety of \ tracers in IC 2233, including the young-to-intermediate age stars, the \ regions, and the dust. However, the vertical excursions become less pronounced in the older stellar populations traced by the mid-infrared light. This suggests that the process leading to the vertical displacements may be linked with the regulation of star formation in the galaxy.

IC 2233 is a bulgeless Sd galaxy located at a distance of 10 Mpc. It has a moderately low surface brightness, ``superthin'' disk structure (Goad Roberts 1981) and a low current star formation rate ( 0.05 M_  yr ^ 1 ; MU08). The transparency and structural simplicity of IC 2233 make it well-suited to a detailed vertical structure analysis.

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 2233 as a nearby gas-rich superthin with dark halo, finding low optical and 3.6 micron velocity dispersions and a stable stellar disc.

quotes.quote: IC 2233 IC 2233 is a superthin galaxy with an axial ratio b/a = 7, observed at an inclination of 88.5^ matthews2007h at a distance of 10 Mpc. The galaxy has an aymptotic rotational velocity of about 85 kms ^ 1 . M_ HI /L_ b 0.62 and the M_ dyn /M_ HI 12 , the latter indicating that the galaxy is rich in HI. Mass models predicts a central dark matter density _ 0 =0.055 M_ pc^ 3 and a core radius 1.83 kpc banerjee2016mass .

IC2233 The central velocity dispersion of IC2233 in -band is 14.9 0.6 kms ^ 1 and a scalelength of exponential fall-off equal to 2.4 0.4. We have calculated the Q_ N as function of R which has a minimum value of Q_ N 2.2, confirming the stability of the disc against growth of axi-symmetric instabilites. The central vertical velocity dispersion of the thick disc in 3.6 m is 15.9 0.5 kms ^ 1 and falls off exponentially with a scale length of (2.2 0.4) R_ d1 ; the corresponding values for the thin disc are 3.9 0.2 kms ^ 1 and (6.0 0.2) R_ d2 . The density averaged vertical velocity dispersion does not seem to reflect any component in particular, but remains constant at 6 kms ^ 1 at all R .

sources.summary: The paper identifies IC 2233 as an Additional-sample edge-on galaxy with excess FIR emission at large vertical distances, interpreting the extraplanar dust signal as real rather than PSF or inclination driven.

quotes.quote: Six galaxies from the Additional sample (IC 2233, NGC 3592, UGC 07321, UGC 07387, UGC 07522, and UGC 09242) also show an excess of FIR emission compared to our models at large vertical distances. We note that this extraplanar FIR emission, which typically starts to dominate at heights 1--2 kpc above the mid-plane, cannot be explained by the scattered light of the PSF since we accounted for this effect while modelling. Also, variations in the galaxy inclination angle as described in Sect. sec:method cannot explain the observed excess of dust emission (the red spread along the model profile in Fig. fig:profiles shows a one- spread of the model profiles for varied inclination angles, as described in Sect. sec:3Ddisc ). This indicates that the observed extraplanar emission is real and produced by the dust at high distances above the mid-plane.

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC 4278 as having regular disks and no detected material between it and companion NGC 2537, arguing against current interaction.

quotes.quote: Besides the target galaxy we find one other source in the field that we deem to be real, the companion NGC 2537. We do not detect anything in between the two sources and their disks appear regular. This leads us to believe that there is currently no interaction between the galaxies. If they were dynamically connected their velocity structure is such that NGC 2537 would be counter rotating in the frame of UGC 4278 but in the frame of NGC 2357 the target would be co-rotating.

gmw_00003277.name: UGC00711
gmw_00003277.overview: UGC00711 is a superthin disk galaxy located at a distance of 23.4 Mpc [arXiv:2002.09198]. It has a high planar-to-vertical axial ratio of 15.5 and exhibits a highly stable disk [arXiv:2002.09198].

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC00711 as a high axial-ratio superthin at 23.4 Mpc, with modeled optical and 3.6 micron dispersions whose profiles appear to trace one disc.

quotes.quote: UGC00711 UGC0711 is a superthin galaxy with a planar-to-vertical axial ratio b/a = 15.5 and observed at an inclination of 74.7^ at a distance of D = 23.4 Mpc. The galaxy has an aymptotic rotational velocity of 100 kms ^ 1 mendelowitz2000rotation . Mass modelling predicts a central dark matter density _ 0 =0.033 M_ pc^ 3 and a charactaristic core radius 2.95 kpc banerjee2016mass .

UGC00711 The central vertical velocity dispersion in B-band is 18.4 0.9 kms ^ 1 , and falls off exponentially with a scale length of (3.2 0.4) R_ d1 where R_ d1 is the exponential scale length of the optical disk. We have calculated Q_ N as a function of R,we find that the minimum value is 4.5, indicating thedisc is highly stable. The central velocity dispersion in 3.6 m is 23.8 1.5 kms ^ 1 , the dispersion falls off exponentially with scalelength (2.4 0.3) R_ d1 . The minimum value of Q_ N is 4.3. We note that the vertical velocity dispersion as well as the disk dynamical stability profiles of the optical and the 3.6 m disc of UGC00711 match well well with each other, possibly confirming that they represent one and the same disc.

gmw_00003278.name: PKS 2136+141
gmw_00003278.overview: PKS 2136+141 is a radio-loud, flat-spectrum radio quasar located at a redshift of 2.427 [arXiv:astro-ph/0605134, arXiv:1804.03703]. On parsec scales, it hosts an extremely curved, spiral-like jet that bends over 180 degrees in the plane of the sky [arXiv:astro-ph/0605134].

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 2136+141 as a radio-loud quasar with an extreme, frequency-independent, spiral-like parsec-scale jet, interpreted as a helical Kelvin-Helmholtz mode driven near the jet base.

quotes.quote: We report the discovery of an extremely curved jet in the radio-loud quasar PKS 2136+141 . Multi-frequency Very Long Baseline Array (VLBA) images show a bending jet making a turn-around of 210 degrees in the plane of the sky, which is, to our knowledge, the largest ever observed change in the position angle of an astrophysical jet. Images taken at six different frequencies, from 2.3 to 43 GHz, reveal a spiral-like trajectory, which is likely a sign of an intrinsic helical geometry. A space-VLBI image, taken with the HALCA satellite at 5 GHz and having comparable resolution to our ground-based 15 GHz data, confirms that the bend is a frequency-independent structure. VLBA monitoring data at 15 GHz, covering eight years of observations, show knots in the jet clearly deviating from ballistic motion, which suggests that the bending may be caused by a growing helical Kelvin-Helmholtz normal mode.

In this paper, we present Very Long Baseline Array (VLBA) images from a dedicated multi-frequency observation and from the VLBA 2 cm Survey http://www.cv.nrao.edu/survey/ kel98 showing that PKS 2136+141 ( OX 161 ), a radio-loud quasar at moderately high redshift of 2.427, has a parsec-scale jet, which appears to bend over 180 on the plane of the sky, being -- to our knowledge -- the largest ever observed change in the position angle of an astrophysical jet (other sources showing very pronounced changes in the jet direction include e.g. PKS 1510-089 hom02 , 1803+784 bri99 , and NRAO 150 (I. Agudo et al., in preparation)). In VLA images, PKS 2136+141 is a compact source, showing no extended emission on arcsecond scales mur93 . Both 5 GHz fom00 and 15 GHz kel98 VLBA observations reveal a core-dominated source with a short, slightly bending jet.

sources.summary: The paper classifies 5BZQJ 2139+1423 as a high-redshift FSRQ with no significant monthly gamma-ray bins, probably because its IC component peaks below Fermi-LAT energies.

quotes.quote: 5BZQJ 2139+1423. This blazar is classified as FSRQ, with relatively high redshift, z = 2.427. A light curve with one month bins along 7.5 year showed no bins with TS > 3.0. However, we do not discard \ activity for this source. The IC component probably peaks at a frequency that is much lower than the bandwidth probed by Fermi-LAT, owing to its LSP frequency log( _ peak ) 11.0. In addition, because of its high redshift, the observed IC end tail gets even harder to probe.

gmw_00003279.name: SMCNOD
gmw_00003279.overview: The Small Magellanic Cloud Northern Over-Density, or SMCNOD, is an elongated stellar over-density located eight degrees north of the Small Magellanic Cloud [arXiv:1612.03938]. It is characterized by a low gas content and was likely removed from the Small Magellanic Cloud disk by tidal stripping [arXiv:1612.03938].

sources.summary: The paper reports SMCNOD as an elongated stellar over-density north of the SMC, with SMC-like stellar populations, low gas content, and a likely tidal-stripping origin.

quotes.quote: We report the discovery of a stellar over-density 8 ^ north of the center of the Small Magellanic Cloud (Small Magellanic Cloud Northern Over-Density; SMCNOD) using data from the first two years of the Dark Energy Survey (DES) and the first year of the MAGellanic SatelLITEs Survey (MagLiteS). The SMCNOD is indistinguishable in age, metallicity and distance from the nearby SMC stars, being primarly composed of intermediate-age stars (6 Gyr, Z=0.001), with a small fraction of young stars (1 Gyr, Z=0.01). The SMCNOD has an elongated shape with an ellipticity of 0.6 and a size of 6x2 deg. It has an absolute magnitude of M_V 7.7, r_h = 2.1 kpc, and _V(<r_h) = 31.2 mag arcsec ^ 2 . We estimate a stellar mass of 10^5 M_ , following a Kroupa mass function.

The SMCNOD was probably removed from the SMC disk by tidal stripping, since it is located near the head of the Magellanic Stream, and the literature indicates likely recent LMC-SMC encounters. This scenario is supported by the lack of significant HI gas. Other potential scenarios for the SMCNOD origin are a transient over-density within the SMC tidal radius or a primordial SMC satellite in advanced stage of disruption.

gmw_00003280.name: IC 2058
gmw_00003280.overview: IC 2058 is an edge-on Scd spiral galaxy [arXiv:2009.14731] located in the Dorado central compact group [arXiv:2206.01578]. It is interacting with the dwarf galaxy PGC 75125 [arXiv:2009.14731, arXiv:2206.01578], which has removed gas from the system [arXiv:2009.14731] and produced extra-planar far-ultraviolet features [arXiv:2206.01578].

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 2058 as an edge-on Scd spiral with disk-wide H-alpha regions and interaction-linked gas removal with dwarf companion PGC 75125.

quotes.quote: IC 2058 The galaxy is an edge-on spiral (Scd, Type=6.6 0.9). \ emission is revealed along the plane of the galaxy and no obvious bulge is visible (see Figure IC2058 ). Uncalibrated \ observations of this galaxy, performed by Rossa2003 , did not detect any extra-planar \ emission in the halo of this spiral galaxy. Figure IC2058 shows that \ regions are indeed distributed all along its disk.

The region of IC 2058, South-East of NGC 1549/NGC 1553, is \ rich Kilborn2009 with a mass of 17.1 1.2 10 ^ 8 M _ . Pearson2016 studied the \ distribution enclosing IC 2058 and the dwarf galaxy PGC 75125. On the basis of the low outer vs. total \ gas fraction they suggest that most of the \ gas, removed in the IC2058/PGC 75125 galaxy-galaxy interaction, is in an ionized state. Both IC 2058 and PGC 75125 show \ complexes see also for IC2058 Rossa2003 .

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 2058 as an edge-on Dorado compact-group member interacting with PGC 75125, with undistorted FUV imaging but smoothed extra-planar FUV features and disk-like profile.

quotes.quote: IC 2058 and PGC 75125 are part of the Dorado central compact group SCG 0414 -5559 Iovino2002 . A distortion of PGC 75125 is evident from the FUV.CaF2 image (Figure IC2058 ). The two galaxies, separated by V_ hel =28 , are likely interacting, as suggested by both the shape of the present FUV.CaF2 emission and \ observations by their Figure 14 Elagali2019 . The FUV.CaF2 image of IC 2058, seen edge-on, does not show distortions.

The smoothed image, shown in the bottom right panel of Figure IC2058 , shows the presence of some extra-planar features, not detected in \ both by Rossa2003 and Ram2020. The geometrical parameters of IC 2058, PA=17.9 ^ and =0.85, at R _ 25 in Table UVIT-sources are very similar to the average = 0.74 and PA =18^ obtained from the FUV.CaF2 surface photometry (Table UVIT-results ).

sources.summary: The paper treats IC 2058 as an unusually bright-profile outlier and one of two galaxies marginally consistent with a sharply truncated H surface-brightness profile.

quotes.quote: Although the average surface brightness scatters within only a factor of 3 between most of the galaxies, there are outliers with very faint or bright surface brightness values, whose contributions to the composite will be affected by the choice of weighting; in particular, the measured surface brightness integral of IC 2058 is almost three times as high as the second-brightest object.

The two cases that are marginally consistent with a sharp truncation are ESO 478-G011 and IC 2058. Visual inspection supports this for IC 2058; in the case of ESO 478-G011, the best-fit truncation radius is at the very large radius of 1.34 R_ 25 , so that the truncated profile is essentially indistinguishable from a single exponential shape.

gmw_00003281.name: ESO 478-G011
gmw_00003281.overview: ESO 478-G011 is a disk galaxy that is formally consistent with having an unbroken exponential profile [arXiv:1003.4872]. It is also identified as marginally consistent with a sharp truncation, though its truncated profile is essentially indistinguishable from a single exponential shape [arXiv:1003.4872].

sources.summary: The paper identifies ESO 478-G011 as marginally consistent with a sharp truncation and as one of the best candidates for an unbroken exponential H profile.

quotes.quote: The two cases that are marginally consistent with a sharp truncation are ESO 478-G011 and IC 2058. Visual inspection supports this for IC 2058; in the case of ESO 478-G011, the best-fit truncation radius is at the very large radius of 1.34 R_ 25 , so that the truncated profile is essentially indistinguishable from a single exponential shape.
Visual inspection does single out two of these objects, ESO 478-G011 and NGC 259, as the best candidates for an unbroken exponential profile; these are also the two objects best fitted under H_3 .
Of the objects that are formally consistent with an unbroken exponential profile, NGC 259 and ESO 478-G011 may be interesting cases for follow-up studies.

gmw_00003282.name: NGC 259
gmw_00003282.overview: NGC 259 is a bright Sbc-type spiral galaxy with a relatively low inclination [arXiv:0808.3878, arXiv:1003.4872]. Although previously classified as a warped galaxy, this appearance may instead be a continuation of a spiral arm [arXiv:0808.3878, arXiv:1003.4872].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 259 as a bright, relatively low-inclination Sbc galaxy whose supposed warp may be a spiral-arm continuation and lacks kinematic disturbance.

quotes.quote: NGC 259 (Fig. fig_ngc259 ) is among the brighter galaxies in this sample ( M=-20 ), an Sbc-type at cz=4045 km/s.
The acquisition images show that the supposed warp feature is in fact more suggestive of a continuation of a spiral arm.
The major axis spectrum samples its kinematics out to 1.2 R _ 25 , but shows no kinematic disturbance.

sources.summary: The paper singles out NGC 259 as a possible unbroken-exponential H-profile case, noting its warped classification may instead reflect a prominent spiral arm.

quotes.quote: Visual inspection does single out two of these objects, ESO 478-G011 and NGC 259, as the best candidates for an unbroken exponential profile; these are also the two objects best fitted under H_3 . This is surprising for NGC 259, which is listed as a warped galaxy; however, within our warped sample, this object is the least-inclined, and the classification as a warped object may be due to a prominent spiral arm, rather than to an actual vertical bending of the disk, which may explain why we can trace a regular, possibly unbroken H profile out to 1.35 R_ 25 .
Of the objects that are formally consistent with an unbroken exponential profile, NGC 259 and ESO 478-G011 may be interesting cases for follow-up studies.

gmw_00003283.name: UDFy-38135539
gmw_00003283.overview: UDFy-38135539 is a high-redshift Lyman-alpha emitting galaxy [arXiv:2402.06070, arXiv:1102.1726] with a systemic redshift of approximately 8.5 [arXiv:2402.06070, arXiv:1010.4312]. It is characterized by modest star formation [arXiv:1102.1726], young low-metallicity stars [arXiv:1102.1726], and dust enrichment [arXiv:1102.1726], with nearby ionizing sources likely aiding its visibility [arXiv:1102.1726, arXiv:1010.4312].

sources.summary: The paper identifies JADES-GS-z8.5-LAE as a z=8.5 Ly-alpha emitting high-redshift galaxy observed with NIRCam and NIRSpec, with strong rest-optical lines, measured systemic redshift, and Ly-alpha escape fraction.

quotes.quote: As part of an ongoing effort to build a large database of Ly measurements in the reionization era, we have identified a new Ly detection at z 8.5 , JADES-GS+53.15891-27.76508 (hereafter JADES-GS-z8.5-LAE), in the JADES Cycle 2 program 3215 Eisenstein2023b . The discovery of this Ly emitting galaxy will also be presented and discussed in more detail in a companion paper (Witstok et al. in prep.). NIRCam imaging observations of this galaxy have been obtained from the JADES Cycle 1 program Eisenstein2023a . It was selected as a candidate high redshift galaxy with photometric redshift z_ phot =8.5 using the dropout technique in Hainline2023a . NIRSpec observations of JADES-GS-z8.5-LAE was performed as a part of the program 3215 using the MSA in October 2023 over five sub-pointings.

Strong rest-frame optical emission lines (H , [ O III ] ) are clearly seen in both the prism and the G395M/F290LP spectra. We derive the systemic redshift z_ sys =8.4858 0.0004 by simultaneously fitting those strong rest-frame optical lines measured in G395M/F290LP spectrum with Gaussian profiles. Then we calculate the Ly escape fraction f_ esc,Ly =0.10 0.02 .

sources.summary: The paper models UDFy-38135539 as a z=8.6 LAE with modest star formation, young low-metallicity stars, dust enrichment, and nearby ionizing sources aiding detectability.

quotes.quote: The observational frontiers for the detection of high-redshift galaxies have recently been pushed to unimaginable distances with the record-holding Lyman Alpha Emitter (LAE) UDFy-38135539 discovered at redshift z=8.6 . However, the physical nature and the implications of this discovery have yet to be assessed. By selecting galaxies with observed luminosities similar to UDFy-38135539 in state-of-the-art cosmological simulations tuned to reproduce the large scale properties of LAEs, we bracket the physical nature of UDFy-38135539: it has a star formation rate 2.7-3.7 M_ yr ^ 1 , it contains 10^ 8.3-8.7 M_ of stars 50-80 Myr old, with stellar metallicity 0.03-0.12 Z_ .

For any of the simulated galaxies to be visible as a LAE in the observed range, the intergalactic neutral hydrogen fraction at z=8.6 must be _ HI 0.2 and extra ionizing radiation from sources clustered around UDFy-38135539 is necessary. Finally, we predict that there is a 70 (15 ) probability of detecting at least 1 such source from JWST (HST/WFC3) observations in a physical radius 0.4 Mpc around UDFy-38135539.

We have also bracketed the physical properties of UDFy-38135539: the SFR range between 2.7-3.7 M_ yr^ 1 , it has 10^ 8.3-8.7 M_ of stars with a mass weighted age of 50-80 Myr and a mass weighted stellar metallicity between 0.03-0.12 Z_ . It is dust enriched with dust masses 10^ 5.7-6 M_ which translates into A_V 0.75 mag and a color excess of E(B-V) 0.16-0.19 using the supernova dust extinction curve.

sources.summary: The paper reports UDFy-38135539 at z=8.5549, selected as a z~8.6 candidate and observed with SINFONI, while arguing nearby fainter galaxies helped ionize its surroundings.

quotes.quote: UDFy-38135539 ^5 is at a redshift z=8.5549 0.0002, which is greater than those of the previously known most distant objects, at z=8.2 ^ 6,7 and z=6.96 ^8 . We find that this single source is unlikely to provide enough photons to ionize the volume necessary for the emission line to escape, requiring a significant contribution from other, probably fainter galaxies nearby ^9 . UDFy-38135539 was selected as a candidate z 8.6 galaxy from deep Wide Field Camera 3 observations of the Hubble Ultra Deep field ^5 . Its red Y _ 105 J _ 125 colour is one of the reddest in the parent sample of z =8--9 candidates, and, together with the sensitive upper limits in the optical through the Y _ 105 band, make it the most plausible z 8.6 galaxy ^ 5,10 . To search for its Ly emission, we obtained sensitive near-infrared integral-field spectroscopic observations of UDFy-38135539 using the SINFONI spectrograph at the ESO Very Large Telescope, with an integration time on the source of 14.8 h in the near-infrared J-band (1.1-1.4 m).

sources.summary: The paper compares UDFy-38135539 to its simulated LAE, citing its z=8.6 discovery, line flux, luminosity, and model-implied mass, star-formation rate, and escape fraction.

quotes.quote: The recent discovery of the most distant LAE, UDFy-38135539, at z=8.6 by Lehnert10 marks a milestone in observational cosmology that galaxies form less than 600 million years after the Big Bang. The detected line flux of this object is 6 10^ 18 ergs cm^ 2 s^ 1 , which implies a luminosity of 5.5 10^ 42 ergs s^ 1 . This is close to that of our galaxy at z=8.5 . Our model suggests that this LAE may have a total mass of 10^ 10-11 , a stellar mass of 10^ 9 , a SFR of the order of 10 , and f_ esc ^ 0.18 . We note that suggested a lower SFR of 2.7-3.7 for this galaxy by assuming a escape fraction of 100 per cent .

gmw_00003284.name: SDSS J142507.32+323137.4
gmw_00003284.overview: SDSS J142507.32+323137.4 is a type 1 quasar at a redshift of 0.478 that displays double-peaked narrow optical emission lines [arXiv:1701.04572, arXiv:1011.3365]. While initially proposed as a dual active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1011.3365], radio observations suggest it is a single low-luminosity active galactic nucleus with a two-sided jet or lobe-like features [arXiv:1701.04572].

sources.summary: The paper treats SDSS J142507.32+323137.4 as a proposed dual AGN, but e-MERLIN data favor one low-luminosity AGN with two-sided jet or lobe-like radio features.

quotes.quote: SDSS J142507.32+323137.4 (hereafter J1425+3231, z=0.478 ) was reported as a promising dual AGN candidate by based upon its double-peaked narrow optical emission lines. found two compact radio emitting features at a projected separation of 2.6 kpc at 1.7 GHz in a European VLBI Network (EVN) observation. At 5 GHz, only the brighter feature was detected with EVN. Below we report on our e-Multiple Element Remotely Linked Interferometer Network (e-MERLIN) dual-frequency observations of J1425+3231 (project: CY2205), which were aimed at finding out whether the two detected radio features are the cores of two radio-emitting AGN, or they belong to one AGN, and represent core and extended features.

J1425+3231 was proposed to be a dual AGN candidate because of its double-peaked optical spectrum ( ). High-resolution radio observations with the EVN showed that it has two compact radio emitting feature. Our follow-up e-MERLIN observations revealed a rich morphology in the source at 1.6 GHz, but detected only one radio-emitting feature at 5 GHz. While the scenario involving dual radio-emitting AGN cannot be completely ruled out, our results are more indicative of one low-luminosity AGN with two-sided jet and/or lobe-like features in J1425+3231.

sources.summary: The paper describes SDSS J1425+3231 as a type 1 quasar with double-peaked narrow lines and argues that its blue and red components are best explained by a dual AGN system.

quotes.quote: The peculiar emission-line spectrum of SDSS J1425+3231 is noticed in the course of searching for dual AGN candidates in the SDSS QSO sample. SDSS J1425+3231 shows all its strong narrow emission lines with double-peaked, it is hard to make conclusions on the structures of weak emission lines due to the signal-to-noise ratio (S/N).

A dual AGNs system The final picture we want to suggest for SDSS J1425+3231 is that this is a dual AGN system. The primary black hole in this system has a mass of 10^8M_ , the observed blue narrow component in 4959, 5007, the broad \ component and the wing of 4959, 5007 are generated in its NLR, BLR and the region in between (this region is usually referred as intermediate line region in literatures) respectively. The consistency between the black hole masses estimated from broad \ component and of the blue component supports this idea. We have not observed the broad emission lines from the BLR of the secondary black hole ( 10^6M_ ), the possible reasons for this includes: (1) the S/N of SDSS spectra is not high enough for us to separate the broad emission line of the secondary black hole from primary one; (2) the secondary black hole is a type 2 AGN in which the BLR is obscured by the dusty torus.

gmw_00003285.name: BG1
